input: rating 5.0. I am very happy with this serum I am very happy with this serum. In the past, any retinol product has caused irritation and burning. So far, this does not. I have not been using it long enough to see any visible difference, but the ingredient list is very good, and I feel confident in continuing to use it. I use if after OZ's tonic and it seems to absorb quickly into my skin.... time will tell !!
rating 5.0. This serum feels so wonderful on my skin This serum feels so wonderful on my skin, it goes on smoothly and is absorbed well. It leaves my skin feeling so soft and hydrated.

rating 5.0. Wonderful I love this product!  It goes on smooth without a tacky feel.  It absorbs quickly in the skin.  I pair this with the Oz Naturals Retinol Moisturizer and I wake up with the softness skin imaginable.  It doesn't irritate my skin or cause it to dry out either.  I'll def keep using this product.

rating 5.0. A true powerhouse in a small bottle : After washing my face for bed, one pump is more than enough to lightly cover completely, and my skin drinks it in. A few minutes later, I follow up with the OZNaturals Super Youth Retinol Moisturizer. No reaction or drying of my skin, and my pillowcases aren't discolored like prior products I've used. It's best to use this product at night, and with the cold pressed technology in this serum, I get the true and highest concentration of the ingredients. I have noticed a considerable difference in the tone in my complexion with a nice radiant look. I could not be happier, and look forward to the future results from using this serum, as it will be another product in my arsenal of skincare from OZ Naturals.

rating 5.0. Noticeably smoother skin and improvement in problem areas I found this product to be effective at smoothing out my skin.  I've been using it for about a week and see a change in my skin.  It seems smoother and more supple.  I am hoping that it improves the absorbsion of the topical antioxidents I use, especially Oz Natural's C serum products.  I found this especially effective on the parts of my skin that have psoriasis, reducing flaking and peeling, and making my skin much smoothier and less irritated in the problem areas.  Recommended, with sunblock for protection.

rating 5.0. Another Great OZ Product! Like all OZ products, this one is a winner too. You can feel the serum working within 2 minutes after application. After 5 days off use I can see a difference in the tone of skin-brighter, tighter, and a noticeable reduction of fine lines. Great product, great price=win win.

rating 5.0. Love this new Cold-processing Product! I am a huge fan of the OZ Natural product line, and this product is yet another great one. The OZ Naturals people have developed a new line of cold-processing products, and as hard as it is to believe, these products are even better. As instructed, I use this product at bedtime, and my skin is extremely soft and smooth in the morning. I especially like the pump applicator - one small pump and there is enough serum to cover my entire face and neck area. Strongly recommend!

rating 5.0. Easy to apply! I have been using Oz Naturals for a while and I love this serum. The pump makes application easy and I get just the right amount. It spreads easy and absorbs right into my skin. I does not irritate my sensitive skin and I don't notice a smell with it. I apply the serum right after washing my face before I apply the Retinol moisturizer. I feel like I'm really taking care of my face and fighting aging using these products. I also love that they are 98% natural. Great product!

rating 5.0. Excellent product OZ Naturals provides another great addition to their skincare line with this anti-aging Retinol serum.  It contains powerful ingredients that help smooth your skin without being overly harsh like some Retinol products.  Used in conjunction with their Vitamin C and Hyaluronic Acid serums you will see much more beautiful skin.  Great customer service and very importantly cruelty free!
rating 5.0. FEELS AWESOME!! Use this at night and you can feel it absorbing. Wake up with no oily skin. LOVE it!!!

rating 5.0. I am so happy I ordered the retinol serum I am so happy I ordered the retinol serum! I have been using it as part of my nightly routine. I use the vitamin c serum first and add the retinol serum. My skin feels wonderful and it is never dry! I have noticed that the lines around my mouth are far less noticeable. I will order it again and would recommend it highly!

rating 5.0. Love, love, love!!! I love this!! I have  it experienced any increased skin sensitivity (and I have really sensitive skin)! My skin appears more clear and I have noticed my grouchy line between my eyebrows seems less noticeable! I would definitely purchase this again. I also really love the way my skin feels after applying. I also use under makeup and everything looks better than ever.
rating 5.0. This is a great retinol serum This is a great retinol serum! I do however recommend using it at night or in the morning only if you are not going to be subjected to the sun.

rating 5.0. Another great Oz product!!! I've added this to my nightly routine, and love this product. This, along with the Oz toner, is helping my skin tone stay balanced and brighter, as well as getting rid of my horrible dark spots. Every summer my spot comes back from being in the sun, so I can't wait to continue using this through the summer to make sure those pesky spots stay away!
rating 5.0. Five Stars Love this serum! Worth every penny, it has only been a few weeks and I can see a difference!!

rating 5.0. New Anti Aging Retinol Serum! Another great product from OZ Naturals! Have been using this product for about 10 days and it is an improvement over the over serum - but then I like the other serum too! I haven't found any of the products I don't like! The new cold processed Vit C serum seems to be a little drying for my skin, but I solved that issue by using it at night and the retinol serum during the day. I love these products!
rating 5.0. I love love, love the serum I love love, love the serum, great to use when your skin is acting up.

rating 5.0. really making a difference on my skin I'm really loving this serum. I have noticed a big difference in my skin since adding retinal to my skin care regimen. It leaves me a little flaky the next morning, but only on my more sensitive areas (around my nose specifically). But that goes away after a day and a good wash with my clarisonic brush. Only wish the bottle provided better control of how much product comes out. It's hard to get less than a full pump, so I feel like I waste a little.

rating 5.0. Love it! I've been using the OZNaturals Anti Aging Retinol Serum every night for a few weeks now and my skin seems to look brighter and smoother. Hoping that over time this will also help to lighten a couple hyper-pigmentation spots. Another great product from OZNaturals.

rating 5.0. Another five star product I've been using as products for a few years now and like all their other products I have yet to be disappointed their products work exactly like it states and after a few times of use you can actually see your skin  improve I tend to have a lot of breakouts I also have sensitive skin so I'm always trying to find more natural ingredients to use I also suffer from a lot of redness and one thing I've noticed with awes natural products is because they are all natural they don't irritate my skin anymore and actually diminishes the redness and reduces breakouts

rating 5.0. I've purchased this before and found that it did make ... I've purchased this before and found that it did make a difference in the problem areas.  Does it erase lines and wrinkles?  No but it does make them less noticeable.  You don't need a lot and it doesn't dry out your face after applying.

rating 5.0. Love the Whole Line In my opinion, what puts Oz Naturals Pro-Retaxional way ahead of other retinol serums is that it is 98% Natural. I do not have to be afraid to put it on my face and neck (i.e. my thyroid gland). I know that I'm not poisoning my body with a lot of chemicals and for it to be reasonably priced, what a find.
I'm in love with the whole line.

BTW - I easily pass for 10 -15 years younger than I am.

rating 5.0. Great serum Love these products and this line. My skin has been doing so much better since I got into the habit of using serum under my moisturizer and sunscreen every day. I find this particular serum very plumping and hydrating and love to use it both day and night.

rating 5.0. Incredible Results - Love this product line! I've been using OZ Naturals products for a couple years now. As I approached my late 30s I started noticing fine lines around my eyes. I tried other natural products from my local health food store but wasn't seeing the results I wanted. After doing a bit of searching I came across the OZ Naturals line and I'm so happy that I did! Their products are the only thing I use on my skin now (except for makeup) and I've gotten remarks from several people that I look years younger than my true age. Now I'm 40 and look younger than I did a couple years ago! I'm definitely a lot more confident and happier with the way my skin looks. The Anti-Aging Retinol Serum is something that I use regularly. I apply it at night after washing my skin and using the Ocean Minerals Tonic. I also use the Super Youth Retinol Moisturizer. I have less lines around my eye area and better overall skin tone. Part of the reason I was attracted to these products is that they're all natural, so I love them even more now that they're cold processed!

rating 4.0. I have used OZNaturals Retinol Serum before and liked the effects I have used OZNaturals Retinol Serum before and liked the effects. The newly formulated product is really good as well. I use it every night after cleaning my makeup and I have seen great results. I did see improvement in the dullness on my skin

rating 5.0. Fantastic product! This is a fantastic product! I have been using for 3 weeks every night and have noticed great results! The best results are in the thicker wrinkle/crease on the bottom of my jaw line. It has definitely minimized the crease. It has helped with the fine lines around my eyes. I would say the combination of a few products have helped. I am using from OZNaturals the spray mineral tonic, followed by Vitiman C with AHA then hyaluronic acid, then another product of niacinamide (vit B3)- helps minimize pores- followed up with the retinol moisturizer from OZNaturals. I think this combination has given me great results over the past month. I do use a mask a couple times a week Vit. C hydration mask from OZNaturals and a bentonite clay mask. I have seen brighter skin, tighter pores, moisturized skin and the wrinkles are a bit diminished!

rating 5.0. So awesome for the skin! I am a huge fan of this serum. First of all, I love the pump dispenser, It gives you just enough serum to completely cover your face and a bit of your neck. It glides on really smooth and absorbs into the skin after a few minutes. It makes my skin look really nice and smooth, and I am definitely going to continue to use it in the future.

rating 5.0. I like to use this after my vitamin C Have been using OZ naturals Pro-Retaxinol 2.5 Retinol Serum for the past few weeks and it really works well with all their other products. I like to use this after my vitamin C. Works great. Happy with this purchase!

rating 5.0. Great alternative to prescription Retin-A This is definitely one of my favorite products and I routinely use it every day. I love the consistency and dispensing option. A little goes a long way and the price is certainly affordable.  With repeated and consistent use, you will see an improvement in the appearance of your skin and a reduction in fine lines. Adding in the other OZ products such as the Vitamin c serum or hyluraonic acid  definitely compliments this product.  Love it!

rating 1.0. Changed ingredients - now acids and fillers New ingredients 4/2017 = altered effect. Used to be 7 ingredients, all of which were recognizable to a consumer ('rose hips'). Now it includes at least 20, including 'glycerin' and 'phenylpropanol'. P-anisic acid, anyone? I experienced insomnia and an upset GI system after using. A once trustworthy company going for the cheap.and hopefully losing lots of us.

Retinol used to be the first ingredient...Now this:

AQUA, GLYCERIN, 1,3 PROPANDIOL, RETINOL, CAPRIC CAPRYLIC TRIGLYCERIDES, HAEMATOCOCCUS PLUVIALIS EXTRACT, SODIUM HYALURONATE, HAMAMELIS VIRGINIANA (WITCH HAZEL) EXTRACT, XANTHAN GUM, ROSA CANINA FRUIT EXTRACT, CAMELLIA SINENSIS LEAF EXTRACT, CHAMOMILLA RECUTITA (MATRICARIA) EXTRACT, POLYSORBATE 20, P ANISIC ACID, PHENYLPROPANOL, PROPANEDIOL, CAPRYLYL GLYCOL , TOCOPHEROL, CITRIC ACID, BIXA ORELLANA SEED EXTRACT. I

rating 5.0. Good product so far! I began using the OZ Naturals anti aging Retinol serum a little over a month ago. I haven't noticed anything different in the overall appearance of my skin yet. I'll update my review once I've used the entire bottle. It doesn't have a scent and a little goes a long way.. I apply at night after toner and typically need just 1 pump to cover my face and part of neck. I wish I had more control in the dispensing since I don't need a full 2 pumps, maybe 1.5. It goes on very slippery and dries quickly. I love the ingredients, customer service, discounts, pricing and overall experience in using OZ Naturals products. I look forward to trying more products.. they run out of stock quickly so grab them while you can!

rating 5.0. As an Esthetician I have tried everything. I love this line & am addicted. My skin glows As an Esthetician I have tried everything. I love this line & am addicted. My skin glows & I receive tons of compliments on my complexion - for a 49 year old - I'm very happy to hear that! I also don't need to wear as much make-up anymore since using OZNaturals.

rating 5.0. one application was all it took... I've been looking for something to help with preventative aging for some time, I'm a male, early 30s, who started noticing early signs of aging. I've tried everything from OTC stuff at super market, to strange diets consisting of bones broths and callegen.... maybe it helped, maybe it didn't... but it took only one application of this product to notice considerable difference. My skin feels softer and toned. I'm going to start ordering more from OZ from now on--- and??? It's only 17 bucks! Can't beat the price!

rating 4.0. It's okay (I actually paid for this myself.) This is good, not great. There are a lot of 5 star reviews on here, written by people who got this for free. This is not a 5 star item. Pros: It is nice and sturdy. It shipped extremely quickly. The large drawers are nicely sized for lip glosses and eye pallets. Cons: One of the top drawers is out-of-square, so it hangs weird. The top piece (which is removable) is not flat on bottom, so it doesn't sit flat. I took the removable organizer from the bottom drawer and put it on top instead. The compartments are too short for brushes or pencils, which is a disappointment, and they are too wide for the average size lipstick. The black padding sheds badly, so I threw that out.

Overall, I like this organizer. I would like it a lot better if the compartments better fit my needs.
rating 5.0. Looks great Love these cosmetic organizer. The drawers are three different sizes. The bottom drawer is the deepest. My bathroom is so much cleaner

rating 5.0. Well Constructed and Looks Pretty After seeing reviews on YouTube as well as reading them here, I decided to go for it.

It is only a little bit smaller than I expected, however, it is larger than one I purchased earlier of similar design but from a different company. I love that you get an extra piece nestled inside the bottom drawer. I'm using the extra piece for my base and top coats as well as small bottles of polish remover and my hand creams. That piece alone holds as much as the Glossybox box I was using but takes up less room and makes it easier to see and get my hands on the things I use most ofter. If I could buy the drawers separately, I would so that I could organize the rest of my manicure stuff.

I also love that the bottom drawer is nearly twice as deep as the middle drawer. I don't have to worry about thicker compacts or powder boxes tripping up the drawer.

This organizer is made of very durable and substantial material. It feels substantial as well and it holds a lot more than the other organizer. Now I can have a drawer for each type of makeup. The only things I can't fit in it are the W7 eyeshadow palettes and the NYX makeup palette I have.

Really happy I took the plunge and got this. I would recommend you get this before the sale price disappears but it is well worth it even at the full price.

rating 5.0. Perfect for those who love cosmetics! No need to look further if you own and collect cosmetics!  Over the past months I searched and even purchased make-up organizers which did not meet up to its descriptions and photo displayed.  Yes this one is more costly than other make-up organizers, but it delivers on space saving organization.  The top detachable organizer offers good amount of space to adequately store everyday essentials such as base, primer, lipstick, glosses, mascara, eyelash curler and small compacts.  The drawers however are awesom--very deep for a compact organizer.  The drawers are very easy to pull out and I was able to store several pots of cream eyeliners, pencils, large compacts, and small tools.  The bottom drawer contains a removable jewelry organizer which is perfect for earrings (I don't use it, but it's well constructed for use).  My only regret was allowing the price tag in dictating me not to order, however I spent money on cheaper organizers only to end up giving them away, or throwing out due to poor quality.  This organizer although pricey is money well spent.

rating 5.0. Works like a charm. May need more than one application. Smells horrendous but WORTH IT. Wowwwwww this product is the bomb!! I colored my hair a purple ombre back in October of 2020. It looked great for abouuuut 2 weeks. After about 1-2 months the color was so dingy and faded and I hated it. I knew I wanted the color out but didn’t want to dye it, I really just wanted my original color back. I searched and searched and found this product, read the reviews and went for it. I am SO happy with this product. It smells God awful but who cares. I mean it smells bad, not so much while you’re using it, and you must have a window and door open when using it, but more so, it smells horrendous after using it and the smell stayed strong for a few days then lingered for a few more days. I did two applications about 10 days apart. I could care less about the smell, cause the product worked so well. I use herbal essence color save shampoo and conditioner (only cause it smells like absolute heaven) and I feel like that helped with the smell. I also used the conditioner twice while in the shower and left it in for a few minutes  and came out with ridiculously soft hair. I don’t think it damages hair at all. Would HIGHLY recommend this product if you want your old dye out of your hair. This product will NOT take out bleach as bleach strips your hair of its color. I had box dye in my hair and it took it out to reveal my natural hair plus sun in ends (yeah I’m old school, I use sun in to lighten my hair). Do not go over the 20 minutes leaving it in your hair. After your hair is saturated, wrap it in foil as heat activates the product a bit more. Buy this product! It is worth the price! Because of the smell, I do not suggest using this product when you have some sort of event or gathering coming up cause you WILL smell like rotten eggs =)

rating 5.0. It actually works on blue hair dye!!!!! Read the reviews and everyone says it smelled like donkey poop but i didnt Mind the smell, my child on the other hand hated the smell. I love This product wayyy more than ColorZap it works in a faster amount of time and worked sooooo well getting the blue out! If i ever do any crazy colors again this will be my go to to get back to my natural color! It also left my hair feel less damaged than the stupid color zap! A++++++++

rating 1.0. Damaging, doesnt remove color. I'm sorry. This stuff sucks. I had bleached hair and 4 months later dyed it dark brown. I decided to take it out because it showed up really dark. I usually use color fix but decided to try this one this time. It turned my hair dark auburn  and damaged my hair beyond repair all the way up to my ears. I either have to shave my head or get a pixie. Those are my options.

rating 2.0. Wouldn't recommend Edit: the lightness somehow faded after a few washes!! Thought this product was great, but it isn't in my opinion.

rating 4.0. Color comes back At first this was a MIRACLE worker, I've dyed my hair vivid for 18 years and NEVER had anything like this.  That said....some things are too good to be true....throughout the day my color magically reappeared.  :/  that said. It's significantly lighter.  Besides blue is a notoriously stubborn color.  And yea it smells weird, the 2 smells nice but the 1 kinda smells sour at first and then your hair smells like Nair for a few days after.  It went away quick, really NBD if you seriously want your hair color out.  Would I recommend?  Sure, it still worked.  Just remember some things really are too good to be true!!

rating 5.0. Very impressed! As a hairstylist , I’m very impressed with this product!! I’ve had black professional dye in for awhile now , I haven’t touched my color in 5 months & am looking to go a more natural brown to match my new growth. My hair has a history of damage & compromise so I have stayed away from bleach , but decided to give this product a try. I was skeptical at first because I’ve used other color removal products and they haven’t worked well , but this did the trick!! Lifted my hair evenly with no brass. A few notes for this products- the smell is not that bad! It has a very faint perm odor but not overwhelming & the outcome left my hair just a little bit dry , definitely recommend a conditioner treatment! 10/10 will use again

rating 5.0. Great for stubborn colors Started with a suborn blue over bleached hair. Starting color was 2 month old Arctic Fox Hair color in: Aquamarine, Periwinkle, and Poseidon (picture provided). I used this color remover 2 times and the last 3 pictures are my results. 1 bottle managed 2 applications however the second was not enough for a full head (I didn’t need my full head anyways). I have very thick and long hair but I have the sides shaved. The smell is not terrible and I have smelt worse. My hair also isn’t damaged and feels great! It is very soft and there is not left over residue after washing it out. Overall this product is great for removing stubborn colors.

rating 5.0. Works good for product without bleach or ammonia Hair dyes work as deposit on your natural hair so if your natural hair has been bleached prior to coloring then chances are your natural hair is also dry & damaged under the color dye. Salermo Color Reverse removed most of the demi-permanent color I've been using. It works pretty good for having no bleach or ammonia-(however it does have an ammonia smell, so expect that). I'd recommend not using it more than twice depending the condition of your hair. For my hair, I'm using it just twice. The 2nd time should remove all of the rest of demi color in my hair. I'm very satisfied with this product.

My intent is to do an overall color much lighter but I had to remove the hair dye in order for the next color to hold. I'm very satisfied with the amount of color it removed.

rating 1.0. It's bleach, it will effect your natural color It's bleach... It claims it won't change your natural color underneath but I never lightened my hair, then dyed it dark red. After using this product my hair is lighter than my roots! Not only did it stink but this listing is very misleading. One of the ingredients is bleach. I'm honestly not sure what I'll do with this orangey hair that is lighter than the roots coming in... The whole point was to stop dying my hair. Not impressed.

rating 5.0. Great product This product lifted my red violet after one use . After the second use , my hair was more copper. It definitely does what it says . The smell isn’t great , but it isn’t horrible .

rating 2.0. Hair fell out after using So, I will start off by saying I had extremely compromised hair prior to using this product. There is a chance that the results I had were because of the state of my hair. I opted for a color remover instead of a bleach in order to limit further damage done to my hair, so if that's what you're also looking for then I'd probably skip this product. Honestly, since I've used it, I've been kicking myself for not just doing a bleach bath on my hair. I've been dying my hair on my own for years; I'm no professional but I do think I have a pretty decent understanding of how products work and how far you can push your hair. The overall application process was pretty simple...it wasn't too messy and wasn't too smelly as far as a color remover goes. I had the fan on and the window open in my bathroom and was completely unbothered by the scent, which is way more than I can say for other color removers. There was a scent that stuck on my hair for about 6 washes after though, and that was pretty unbearable. I had a faded demi-permanent black on top of permanent chestnut brown and this color remover brought me to a light orangey-brown color that was an okay base for a deep purple. I'm not mad at the color my hair was after using, but I've never seen breakage like this in my life. My hair looks so frizzy when in actuality the top of my head is filled with little baby hairs that were left after breakage. If you have strong hair, I'd say do it. If you've been dying and bleaching and you're worried about how your hair will react, then skip it.

rating 2.0. It's just okay I've used so many color remover products. This one does a good job at lightening a color but has never fully removed the color from my hair. If I simply want to lighten so as to reapply color this will do the trick. If I'm trying to go back to blonde this is not the brand I reach for.

As far as the smell it's about the same as all the other color removers I've used. It's not the worst but it definitely has that sulfur scent that will stay in your hair for days (unless you are bleaching after using this product.) As of now it's the price we pay to remove color deposits quickly.

I can't say I'd recommend this product but that's just my opinion for my hair. I keep a platinum blonde when I'm not coloring. For someone who has a darker shade this product may be all they need.

rating 5.0. Not for blue/green/purple fantasy colors It will lift oxidative hair dye (the type you mix with peroxide) but will not damage your hair. Do not expect your hair to look the way it was before you dyed it- nothing will. This will only shrink the color molecules of the dye to be then washed away. Fashion colors (neon, hot pinks, blues & greens) are NOT oxidative colors. There are color removers for those (usually citric acid based). Hair processed will always take (absorb) more color because it's porous. Be careful re-coloring your hair after.

input:
rating 5.0. Atomic Pink, Great for Pastel Pink/Candy Colored Hair Ok, first off: a review of the seller. Then, a small tutorial on how to achieve pastel pink/candy colored hair!

Review:

You are probably not going to find Special Effects dyes cheaper from any other seller on amazon. This place is great, I got standard shipping and my dye got here within about 3-4 days. The packaging was great, my dye was bubble-wrapped and in a BOX not an envelope, so it wasn't roughed up from shipping at all. Inside the box, was their cute business card and for some reason....a Swedish fish candy...I don't know why they gave me a Swedish fish, but that was a cute/odd touch. So overall:

Shipping 5/5
Packaging 5/5
Price 10/5 (Atomic Pink for $11? Crazy cheap!)

Now, here's how I dye my hair:

Disclaimer: I AM NOT A PROFESSIONAL. However, I bleach and dye my own hair at home on a budget! These are all tips and tricks that I learned through trial and error and what I have found through hours upon hours of research. Until just a few weeks ago, the thought of bleaching my hair terrified me, too. But I spent a LOT of time realing posts from the LiveJournal Community PinkHair (go check it out, it will save your hair the first time you dye) and now I'm fully confident in dying my own hair and getting it exactly the way I want.

First of all, I want to say that if you have light brown hair at your roots that you haven't dyed, this dye WILL soak into them and give them a pink tint. Aside from that, YOU NEED TO BLEACH YOUR HAIR. If you want to get the color in the bottle exactly, you will have to bleach your hair, there's no way around that, your hair needs to be WHITE. The only reason to skip bleaching is if your natural hair color is very light blone (almost white) or you just want to tint your hair.

Trust me, if you do it slowly (over the course of 2-3 weeks) bleach is not scary and should NOT burn. It may be slightly itchy, but it should NOT ever burn or hurt! Ideally you should not wash your hair the day before or the day of bleaching. This builds up enough natural oils on your scalp to protect it from the bleach and keep it from hurting. Yes it may be gross and oily until you bleach it, but when you come out without hurting your scalp, it will be well worth it.

Now, the reason why bleach is because you want the color to be exact to what is in the bottle, or close to it. Think of it like this: take a white sheet of paper. On one side, color with a brown/black/red crayon (this is your natural hair color)...now take a light pink crayon and try to color over that brown/black/red. At best it turns into a nasty mud color, at worst, the pink doesn't show up at all, does it? Now color with the pink directly onto the white paper...the color is vibrant and true to the crayon, isn't it? The white paper is like bleached hair.

If you're looking to get a pastel pink/light candy pink out of atomic pink...you will need to dilute it, and that's because the color IS STRONG. The best way to dilute special effects dyes are with really thick conditioners or deep conditioning hair masks (think pantene, or those conditioners that come in jars, just avoid anything with dimethicone in it, as it separates the color from your hair).

If your hair is already bleached, then mix tiny amounts of Atomic Pink with your conditioner to get the exact color you want. Don't make it darker, because your hair will turn the EXACT color your conditioner/dye mix is. Then, just put it all over your head like you would with normal conditioner, and let it sit in your hair as long as humanly possible! I mean at least half an hour if not more! 15 minutes will only give you a tint, it won't dye your hair correctly. And don't worry, with all that conditioner, plus the natural ingrediants in the dye, your hair will be baby soft by the time you wash it out. =)

And then just dry your hair and you're done! If you're like me and went for a lighter candy pink, then this bottle will last you a LONG TIME. My hair is very thick, curly and down to my waist, and I only used about a teaspoon of Atomic Pink and about 2-3 cups of conditioner.

Have fun,
Wulufii

rating 5.0. Amazing color that lasts! Have some application tips, too! I had my hair highlighted at the salon with pink highlights and I noticed it started to fade almost immediately. My stylist pulled me aside and told me that she uses Special Effects at home and it lasts a long time! She was right! I left it in for about 2 hours (instead of the recommended 15 minutes) and the parts of my hair that were bleached because a vibrant pink. The rest of my head (which is dark brown) became a lovely violet color. I love it!!!

A couple tips:
- Wear gloves when applying. You can get a box of cheap gloves from a drugstore.
- Use a mascara brush to apply it. I had a mascara wand that I
I had gotten from Sephora that I had not used for mascara. You can dip it into the bottle and carefully apply it to your hair. It helped me control where I wanted the dye to go AND it kept it off my scalp and ears.
- Use clips to separate your hair. If you do your hair in sections, you won't get any on your clothes, ears, forehead, etc.
- Put a plastic tarp (like one from a hardware store to use when painting) on the counter and floor. Trust me, it's easy clean up and can be reused every time you color.
- After rinsing your hair in the bathtub, spray some Lysol bath & tub cleaner or similar immediately on the tub and let it soak. I did that every time I rinsed my hair for the first couple days and my white tub is not pink. No scrubbing needed.

Good luck and have fun!
rating 2.0. did not come out pink at all i bleached my bangs and the color came out bright red instead of pink =/ i really was looking forward to pink hair

rating 5.0. Atomic Pink is fantastic After reading many reviews on Atomic Pink, I went with this color for the first time ever dying my hair pink. I found some tips online that I followed before I dyed it and I'll relay some of them here.

One thing that is pretty common knowledge is that the more damaged or processed your hair is, the EASIER the color will take to your hair.

So knowing that, I bleached my hair (Using Manic Panic's amplified bleach kit) to get my hair as light as color (It made my hair near-white but it would probably still give a decent color even at the light orange stage) and after rinsing the bleach out, I avoided conditioning my hair as I usually do after a bleach job.

Tips I was given:
1- Make your hair damp. it helps the dye to cover evenly and considering the last time I tried dying my hair a funky color it ended up splotchy despite being in a similar bleached state, I think it definitely helped.

2- Mix the dye with conditioner in a small tub (I personally rinsed the little tupperware that came with the bleach kit since it has a lid and I could cover any I didn't use). Conditioner was said to make the color last longer and it also helps to repair your hair since you've likely just bleached the hell out of it.

3- If mixing with conditioner (It didn't dillute the color at all for me but if you use a signifigant amount it just might), start with just a couple of teaspoons of dye at a time to make sure you don't waste any. I ended up making two "batches" and still had some leftover, and barely used any of the bottle, but my hair is very short.

4- Cover your hair with a plastic bag (or shower cap) when waiting for the dye to take. It'll keep your hair from drying out and also keeps the mess off your neck and clothes

5- A tip I was given but didn't try was to blast your hair with hot air with a hair dryer right before you wash it out. Highly suggested if you use a red or blue.

When washing out your hair the first time, don't use shampoo. Even the second time you wash your hair, don't use shampoo. Only use shampoo when your hair looks dirty. Invest in some dry shampoo to use between washings. Less washing means a longer lasting color! That said, I dyed my hair on Valentine's Day and I've washed it three times since then and the color is still as vibrant as when I first dyed it.

REMEMBER just because color is washing out of your hair doesn't mean your hair still has dye in it. Your hair is likely to bleed for awhile. Don't use your best towels or pillow cases or wear light colored shirts for awhile.

If your color does start to fade, just mix up some more dye and conditioner to refresh it. You can also mix a tiny bit of dye into your conditioner and shake it up and use that regularly.

rating 5.0. Vibrant, long lasting and you DON'T have to bleach your hair beforehand Wow, this is awesome stuff. I ordered Atomic Pink and my hair stylist laid it over my medium brown hair (panels on my bottom hair, my top is light blonde) and did not even need to lift any of the brown out of my hair. Here I am, 2 months later and the color is still vibrant. It's great because since she didn't need to bleach my hair it is still shiny and healthy.
If you have lighter hair it can still bleed into your blonde, but my personal results have just ended in looking like a cute pink melt. Make sure that you rinse will cool water and only wash your scalp, not the treated hair directly.
This is superior to products my stylist has purchased in the beauty supply stores. When I am ready to be done with the color she told me that it is actually going to be more of a process to gradually remove the pink than she thought. Normally it fades so quickly that they never have to do a gradual process to lift it out.
That's how much staying power this color has (Given you treat it appropriately.) Given the quality of it, I can't imagine it's very forgiving to mistakes. I would not trust myself it self-color, but that's just me.

rating 5.0. best, highly recommend This is the best pink hair color I've ever used. Most pinks don't even stay in my hair well to begin with, but the quality of Atomic Pink is impeccable. As soon as I rinsed it out, I saw how deep and concentrated the color was. I didn't dilute it so it was kind of a deep pretty magenta. I've had the color in about four weeks or so now, and I swear, it hasn't faded; hardly at all. And the small amount of fading that has happened has only made the color a brighter pink instead of the deeper magenta I started with. But for the most part, it has hardly even faded. I've also used their brand of green in the past, I think it's called iguana green, and although it isn't as heavy duty as the pink, (ie it didn't last as long) I still had amazing and vibrant results. I will continue to use special effects in the future.

rating 5.0. Vibrant even on unbleached hair Used this on my daughters hair. She has light brown hair naturally, and I've only ever used SFX on her. We leave it in overnight before rinsing out. This is definitely the most vibrant color we've used so far...it's beautiful and shows up great on her unbleached hair.

rating 5.0. I put it over some light blonde hair (not platinum blonde) and it came out super bright. It's long lasting Ohhhhh my gosh this stuff lasts forever if you don't dilute it. I put it over some light blonde hair (not platinum blonde) and it came out super bright. It's long lasting. At first it would bleed when I showered but I put some after color treatment it bleeds only if it rubs up against thing but the water runs clear. I went swimming and it did not bleed in the pool! I went hot tubbing and it didn't bleed! I was out in the sun all throughout spring break and it barely faded. It's been a month and it's starting to fade but barely. It's still super bright for all that I've put it through. The photo with my hand over my face is about a month after I initially dyed it and the other is the day I dyed it. Not much of a difference!

rating 5.0. Great little dye I love this product. I've tried other brands and by far this is the best one.

The colour LASTS, after one month it is just as bright as when I dyed it. The colour doesn't bleed onto everything which is a big plus. I highly recommend this product

rating 5.0. Would recommend! Definitely buying again! I've used four brands of dyes in my life, and this one is the best! Manic Panic and RAW hair dye has NOTHING on Special Effects! I dyed my hair almost three weeks ago and the colour hasn't faded from what I can tell! This colour is so vibrant and bright, I think I'm going to be sticking to pink for awhile haha. It covers orangey spots really well and even if your hair didnt come out even after bleaching, the dye looks really even! ALSO it looks so cool under blacklight!

Pictures: I used cupcake pink by Special Effects on the ends of my hair.

rating 5.0. It worked fantastic and was easy to use I adore this dye! It worked fantastic and was easy to use, even to a newbie to the world of bright colors. I used Atomic pink, wildflower, and a mix of Blue Haired Freak and Fishbowl. I love how it turned out, my hair is soft and shiny, and I get complements whenever I go out. I can't wait to try some more colors.

rating 5.0. SO PINK! Had my light/med brown hair bleached professionally Tuesday of last week and dyed Atomic Pink as part of my powerlifting costume for Nationals. Hair is still a gorgeous, hot pink today on the Thursday of the next week. I shampoo every day in hot water, which is the worst thing to do. It also made it through several trips through the hot tubs and pool in the Las Vegas sun. I've seen lesser dyes disappear entirely after a couple hours in a chlorinated pool.

The dye does bleed for a bit, so have a pillowcase and towel on hand that can be stained pink from use. As of today (thursday), shampoo suds are still pink, but the towel isn't showing any additional staining.

rating 5.0. Great pink for price - bought atomic pink. It seems to be lasting longer than manic panic so far. To get my color, i had to bleach to light blonde and then dilute the atomic pink with conditioner. :)

rating 5.0. Lasts forever on bleached hair! This stuff is amazing. I don't know what they put in my hair at the salon, but even with only washing my hair in cold, and not every day, it still was fading almost immediately. I mixed just a little of this half and half with conditioner and left it on the pink part of my hair for half an hour, and it boosted the color right up! At first I did that every other week, but right now it's been a good 2 and a half months (and I gave up on cold washes a month ago - I visited Peru where it is winter, and wanted the hot showers!), and while the ends have faded a bit, most of my hair is still vibrant hot pink. I think when I get my roots done I'll just have them bleach it and put this on myself, since it seems to work and stay far better than what they used.
Also, there's plenty in this bottle! I only have about a third of my hair pink, but it's long and thick, and I've still only used less than a quarter of a bottle after 3-4 uses. It doesn't need to be full strength to work, in my experience, so I expect this bottle will last a good long time.
As with all fun color dyes, this will stain everything! Be careful, wear gloves, put vaseline around your hairline, put a shower cap or something on while you're letting it set, or you'll have pink where you don't want it!

Edit - it's now been over 10 months since I last touched up the color, and 10 months since I gave up taking special care of it. That's nine months of HOT showers, and not doing anything special, just plain shampoo and conditioner as I would usually do. There were parts at the ends that were lighter bright pink with hints of peachiness, but the majority was still a very bright hot pink! Not as dark as freshly dyed, but still very bright and vibrant. I had a ton of roots obviously, but the color was still fantastic. Last night I bleached my roots and re-dyed, and now my hair is even more super vibrant bright pink. I'm very pleased. I've finally got to the bottom of the bottle, so I'm re-ordering. Atomic Pink is my color. (Also, highly recommend Manic Panic's Dye Away wipes. They do a great job getting the stray dye spots off your ears and arms, and help a bit with surfaces too.)

rating 5.0. Fantastic Color! I love this brand and the brilliant color it always gives us. Both my teenager and I love to dye our hair and we have used many brands. This is one of the ones we trust to give us great color, not damage hair, and is pleasant to actually use. I actually like the scent of this dye, unlike others. I think we get more coverage with a bottle of this than with manic panic. If I had any advice it would be to go for a share darker than what you think you will need if your going for deep color and not bright color and you have light hair.

rating 5.0. Special Effects Atomic Pink made me fall in love with dying my hair! ((Excuse the old dorky photo))
Several years ago I bought 2 things of Special Effects Atomic Pink Hair Dye. I absolutely loved how the color turned out on me and it was my first adventure into dying my hair unnatural colors. Since then, I have felt the most comfortable when my hair is dyed (usually pink or red) and I vastly prefer Special Effects brand compared to others.

rating 5.0. Stays in for months and is a beautiful, bright pink Stays in for months and is a beautiful, bright pink. I mix it with purple to get a strong orchid color. Just put in in your hair, leave in for an hour or so, and you're good to go! Just take case in not getting it anywhere because it will stain things easily. When rinsing out, make sure you've completely rinsed out as much as possible, or else your dye will get all over your pillowcase when you sleep.

rating 5.0. Atomic Pink is the best pink hair color there is Atomic Pink is the absolute BEST hot pink hair color on the market.  It's super bright. It goes on evenly and it stays!  I've included a photo of my pink and purple hair.  This is after at least 7 washes. It's still as bold as day 1. (The purple is not the same brand)

rating 5.0. omg yissss this stays in my hair so much longer ... omg yissss
this stays in my hair so much longer than the salon's Pravana

rating 4.0. I love, love the color in this little bottle and ... I love, love the color in this little bottle and the fact that it doesn't damage your hair at all (the bleach takes care of that, if your hair requires it). The only problem is that it goes EVERYWHERE. I'd recommend wearing dark tops for the first couple of weeks and using dark pillow cases or some you don't mind getting a bit stained. If it gets on the counter, it eventually comes off. I read recently that nail polish remover - or was it alcohol? - removes dyes, so I'll be trying that soon. I bleached my hair only a little (from dark to light brown/dirty blonde) and used the Atomic Pink. It looks more red than pink, but I still like it. I get lots of compliments, and I assume if you have lighter hair it will actually look hot pink. I have short hair and can make this little bottle last for about three touch ups.

rating 5.0. My go-to dye now This dye has great lasting power. I like how the pigment is rich enough to where I can mix it with white conditioner to get a great pastel tone. If you're going to mix it like this, remember to measure how much of each you're using lest you not to be able to recreate the proper percentages to get the same results. Been there, done that. ;) As always, definitely test your mixture out on a piece of hair to make sure you like the results so you're not stuck with a head full of results you don't like!

rating 5.0. The Atomic Pink has lasted me 4+ months! I was using Manic Panic's Hot Hot Pink when I did a little research and discovered people saying that SFX Atomic Pink lasted longer than Manic Panic. I figured I had nothing to lose and took the leap into a new product.

For the first few months, it was very obvious that the color lasted longer than Manic Panic's did. Still, I stuck to my regular touch ups of every four weeks, just because I absolutely love the color straight from the bottle. It's so very bright. I never wanted to actually test how long the color lasted because I was scared my hair would look gross if the color washed out too much.

Well, I've not had the motivation lately to keep up with my intense hair regimen. The last time I touched up the color in my hair was September. It's been over 4 months. And let me tell you, my hair is still super bright. You can see in my pictures that there hasn't been a lot of fading. The picture in which my hair is fluffier was taken only a few hours after I put the color in my hair. The picture in which my roots are way too long was taken today.

I think that a big reason why my hair hasn't faded a lot is because I never shampoo it. That's right: no shampoo. The ONLY time that I shampoo my hair is when I'm about to touch up the color. After that, I only use conditioner. I don't even have any dye in my conditioner, which is what I did with Manic Panic.

So, yeah. SFX lasts forever.

One slight downside: it doesn't bleach out very well. I'll add another picture of when I wanted to do blue underneath the pink and had my stylist bleach that portion for me. You can see that my roots turned blond, but where my hair had already been dyed pink, it just basically faded it to a peachy kind of color. If anyone is wondering, the pink that didn't get bleached out didn't affect the blue dye when I put it in. I used SFX Velvet Blue. It actually turned out pretty cool. The blue lasted a really long time as well, although the roots did turn slightly green. Half of the blue portion did turn purple, but the rest just became a lighter blue.

rating 5.0. This product is a lot of fun, easy to use Really lived up to my expectations! I barely used any and it has lasted many washes and kept the vibrancy I craved. This product is a lot of fun, easy to use, and doesn't seem to damage your hair. It also fades far less than Manic Panic. The picture is 3 weeks old after the first use. I highly recommend this product.

rating 5.0. SUPER long lasting bright color I have been looking for a bright pink hair color that would stay in longer than one month with normal washing. This product has FAR exceeded my expectations! I have naturally blonde hair that is also highlighted. I dyed my hair in August and it's December and my hair is still almost as bright as the day i did it. I think the only reason it's slightly lighter is that i'm starting to get tired of it and have started washing once a week with Head & Shoulders to try and take some of it out. I'm starting to think that i'm either going to end up having to grow out of my hair until i can cut it all out, or just start highlighting it more heavily. Don't get me wrong, i wanted a color that would last and this DEFINITELY has. Although i was looking for a slightly more coral colored pink, this has turned out great. I added some dimension to it when i did it by putting some on normally, and mixing some with conditioner to make it lighter. I love how it turned out and have gotten nothing but compliments about it. I've also read that it glows under black light, but i haven't had the pleasure of experiencing it, i'm still looking for one to try it out! The pictures i've included go from the day I did it Aug 28,  Nov 14, Dec 5, & Dec 27th, that's 4 months and just as bright!

rating 5.0. Stain ALL THE THINGS! So far this is my favorite brand. I bought atomic pink and burgundy wine, mixed them 50/50 and ended up with something close to virgin rose (which was sold out). I have virgin dark blond hair, about a Lvl 6, and this dye takes very well to my hair. I wash my hair BUT DONT CONDITION, blow dry it completely dry, apply dye all over (two bottles covers my mid back length hair), put a disposable shower cap over it and leave it on for about three hours. I rinse my hair over the side of the bath until the water runs clear, vigorously apply conditioner in my hair and rinse again until the water runs clear (the conditioner should dislodge more dye). Then I blow dry and style as desired. It lasts a solid 4 - 6 weeks before I have to re-dye.

Wash your hair only when necessary with a color safe shampoo and conditioner in COLD water (and I do mean cold, not luke warm. welcome to the world of crazy hair, get used to it)

Some things you may want handy are gloves, shower cap, paper towels, cotton rounds, rubbing alcohol, acetone, Vaseline and dry shampoo ( I like batiste). Line your counter with paper towels because it does make a mess, but rubbing alcohol and acetone will help remove stains and I use the cotton rounds because they are small and disposable. Before you start put your hair up away from your face and neck, put a generous amount of Vaseline on all exposed skin, hair line, neck, shoulders, forearms etc. it will help to prevent stains on your skin, rubbing alcohol and a cotton round will remove stains on your skin. Also use paper towels to touch things like turning the faucet on/off. When you are ready to rinse put on a fresh pair of gloves (or rinse the previously used gloves to be more frugal) so your hands dont stain. to extend your color more wash less often by using dry shampoo. Having fun-colored hair is a lot of well...fun, but it does take a lot of maintenance work so be prepared.
rating 5.0. ... dye with conditioner and my hair is now a beautiful pastel I diluted the dye with conditioner and my hair is now a beautiful pastel pink/peach

rating 3.0. Green gone blue I was a bit upset with the sonic green, I've used Special Effects before, but I wanted to try sonic green. It didn't turn out how I wanted it to, it turned a blue/teal. I've used Atomic Pink and so far its been my favorite, as for sonic green. Not so much.

current_product_reviews: rating 5.0. Lasts and lasts I have atomic pink. I lightened my dark blonde hair to lightest blond and this is so bright! It also glows in a black light. This is THE best pink I have ever used and I have used many. Ion, manic panic, Pravana and any other pink I can find. This is it!. I've had this in my hair for months now and only have had to retouch the regrowth a few times and comb through refresh a little. This stays. Everything else I tried would be gone in a week. Not this. It will stay until I bleach it out, so be warned if you are looking for temporary. This is permanent and I am thrilled. I use this and Pravana Vivids Violet for my purple and I never have to worry about faded out hair anymore. The blonde in the photo is how light I bleached my naturally medium blonde hair before applying this color. The photo is the pink after I colored over some of it with purple and retouched 3 months ago! This is 3 month old color!!!! I don't even wash in cold or use any special conditioner. I cannot rave about this pink and the Pravana Violet enough. Now if I can find a teal this good I'll be in heaven.

input:
rating 2.0. got my hopes up, but not my lashes :( I wanted to believe all the hype, but alas, this was no good for me. It didn't curl my lashes any better than the drugstore model.

I have very straight and somewhat sparse lashes -- they do take mascara very well though (too faced better than sex is a great buildable mascara, btw)...if only I could get the lashes to curl up they'd be pretty good all things considered. This curler got my hopes up but not the lashes -- in a side by side comparison my old curler (I think it's a Revlon from CVS) did as good if not a little better. Oh well :(

rating 4.0. The good, the bad and the ugly... My review consists of the good, the bad and the ugly. First,
the good - I finally found a curler that 'fits' my eyelid. Huh, you say? Well, it appears that most of the manufacturers believe I have golf balls instead of human eyes. The tool's shape is long and shallow enough of a curve to catch most of the eyelashes. It is comfortable to use, but takes a bit of practice. Once you are comfortable with the scissor motion (sorry, no other way to describe it) used to make the curl, you are rewarded with a nice, non-crimpy curl. The pad in this one is the best I have used. This tool also opens wide enough that even the most nearsighted or lush lashed amoungst us can get it close to the base of the upper lashes. The design over all is very well thought out. I recommend using an magnifying mirror if you have to use bifocals (I do). All that said, now...
the bad - there is no replacement pad and I am questioning how sanitary this is going to be in the long run. I agree completely with folks here who advise to REMOVE ALL EYE MAKEUP before use. Ain't nothing pretty about an eye infection. I am wondering out loud how to properly clean this curler to be certain it remains safe. If you are prone to sties and other types of infection, I would be asking the same questions. Which leads to...
the ugly - until you practice and are used to it (won't take long) the results are a bit odd looking. The black pad is plushier and looks to be made of silicone and not the usual hard rubber. I have found that the hard rubber pads are chiefly responsible for that weird ugly crimp you can get. Not so with this one. I mentioned before the squeezy scissor action your thumb and index finger have to use can be tricky. If you have coordination issues, well - things could get ugly.
SUGGESTIONS for use...wash your hands before use. I keep the little plastic clip to keep the tool closed. You could follow the advise of one person here to heat the tool with a dryer, but I think I may skip that. Use a makeup mirror to assist you. Practice when you have some time. Enjoy, Ladies - get out there and be lovely.

rating 1.0. Broke immediately It doesn't matter that the packaging is really nice if the product doesn't work. I went to use it and the pressure put on it from normal use is enough to pop the plastic finger part right off. rating 5.0. I wish the rubber would stay in better when not in use Didn't think I could find a curler that wouldn't pinch, but here it is. I wish the rubber would stay in better when not in use, but honestly this is great.

rating 2.0. Not for me This product is well built, nice to look at, and will last. However, it does not work for me. The top horizontal piece of the curler is too wide for it to fit comfortably under my brow bone. Still looking for a great curler.

rating 2.0. At first I loved this curler At first I loved this curler.  It did elevate my lashes to new heights.  After a couple of months, however, the metal support pieces got all wonky with the result that the bar the rubber rests on is crooked.  I am now missing 1/4 of my top eyelashes.  No longer a fan.

rating 5.0. Works GREAT! Since I got older, my eyelashes started growing downwards. My current lash curler didn’t work on them. THIS ONE DOES WONDERS without pinching! Curls them nicely BEFORE you put on your mascara! Perfect!

rating 5.0. LOVE!! I've used so many different brands of eyelash curlers over the years and have been going back to the Revlon eyelash curler time and again for over 10 years. It recently broke so I decided I'd browse around for a new one on Amazon. With all the great reviews, I thought I'd give Bella & Bear a try. The first couple days I had a hard time getting this new tool to work with my eyes. When you've been using the same eyelash curler for over 10 years, using something different is obviously going to feel a little off. However, after 2-3 days, I finally adjusted to the new feel of this curler and it's been great in giving me beautiful curls. The thing I love most about this curler is that I never feel it tug or pull at my lashes as did my Revlon at times.

Overall, I am completely satisfied with this purchase. Like other reviewers commented, the curler comes in a very cute box!

rating 5.0. Awesome. Happy lashes I hate to admit it but I had the same maybelline eyelash curler for the past 15 years. I really didnt use it much in those 15 years because everytime I used it it really didnt do much of anything that I could tell. So I would say. out of those 15 years I might have used it 50 times. But i also was never a big makeup user either. I probably only put makeup on a couple of times a week if I was going out. Fast forward to today and at 37 years old, and a mom of 2, I am wearing makeup alot now to hide the dark circles from no sleep and the beginning signs of age..sigh.., well anyway I tried that old eyelash curler for the last time and threw it out since i have seen so many you tube videos and it looks like their eyelashes curl when they use it. So I said to myself, lets try this again but also dont break th bank either in case it doesnt work again. So I ordered this one. It came super fast and is in a cute package and little bag. Holy eyelashes batman!!!!! I freaking love this thing!!!! It curls so easily! No pinching or having to try and get all the lashes in there. It is like it was made for me. I love it, buy this, dont make a 15 year long mistake like I did. Juat get this nd have happy lashes!!

rating 2.0. This eyelash curler looks great but didn't do a great job with my lashes This eyelash curler looks great but didn't do a great job with my lashes. rating 4.0. Not the best curler--but close I bought this one because I have deep set eyes and I cannot get most eyelash curlers to actually reach my lashes.  They are too curved.  I bought this one because it did not look as curvy as most. It works and it works really well.  I gave it four stars rather than five for three reasons--it does not come with a replacement pad, it is overpriced and pinches.  I have had other curlers with that black silicone pad and they do not last forever.  For the price, there should be some way to replace the pad.  It does pinch a bit too- even though it is advertised as no pinching.  The best working eyelash curler I have used was a small cheap pink plastic fold up one--but it did not last very long.

rating 5.0. Best Eyelash Curler Ever! I recently had wonderful luck with other products from the Bella and Bear line, so I decided to try this eyelash curler.  I have small eyes and have spent many, many years trying to find an eyelash curler that I could use effortlessly and without discomfort.  This eyelash curler is the only one I have ever found that actually curls my lashes easily and with no discomfort.  Anyone else like me that has tried them all . . . your search is definitely over!

rating 5.0. Good Quality - Works Great So far, this eyelash curler seems to be doing the trick.  It's very sturdy and seems to be a good quality.  It doesn't pinch my eyes and curls them perfectly.  After my mascara application, my lashes stay curled throughout the day.  This is my second Bella and Bear product I have ordered and I have to say, I am very impressed with their packaging.  Items come in the prettiest little boxes and bags.  Little things like this can leave a lasting impression especially when combined with a quality product.  Huge thumbs up for presentation and a great working eyelash curler.

rating 5.0. Love this eyelash curler. Will check out other Bella and Bear products. I just recently purchased this eye lash curler and wasn't sure I would like it. rating 1.0. One Star Did not curls my lashes with or without mascara.

rating 2.0. Reject? I bought one of these a few years ago and loved it until it finally broke. I just ordered another one and I think I got a reject because when you squeeze the handle, the curler closes on an angle. rating 2.0. Wanted it to work for me... I've been using this curler for the last few weeks & as much as I wanted to love it, I don't. The curler itself is very well made, it is durable, the spring works great, & the finger grips make it easy to grip without fear of it slipping. The only design flaw that I've found is that the rubber pad that curls my lashes already has a slit in it from where the metal & rubber meet. The other issue which is why it doesn't work for me is the curve of the actual curler. It is very wide which, but the curve of the curler is too strait for my eyes. I have almond shaped eyes & I guess a slightly smaller eye socket. In order to get all of my lashes in the curler I have to press the curler into my eyelid & then adjust the angle to get them in a few times to get the curler close enough to the base of my lashes. If I only had to press the curler lightly on my eyelid that would be one thing, but I have to really press & almost flatten out my eyelid for it to work. Even after that I get maybe a creased "curl". My lashes are quite long & bone strait & even pumping the curler a few times up my lashes doesn't give much of a curl. By the time I put on mascara they are almost strait again. I've been using curlers for 20 some odd years now & honestly the drug store ones work better for me personally.

If you have short lashes & more oval eyes this may be a great curler for you & I do recommend it for the quality. Good luck!

rating 5.0. Where have you been all of my life?! This thing is awesome! I had never heard of the brand and frankly bought it because of the reviews. Well they weren't wrong!  Probably the most sturdy and well made curler yet! My lashes are rockin!

rating 2.0. Too straight I bought this curler based on reviews, however, I can't say that I am satisfied with it. I find the curve of the curler too...straight? rating 5.0. ASIAN approved! OMG! These are a dream. I have been blessed/cursed with the typical straight as a poker stick, Asian eyelashes. I bought this due to the rave reviews and I am so glad that I did! This lash curler curls my short and stubby lashes beautifully! Don't be alarmed by the curvature of this curler, as they do not pinch or pull at all, which is a huge plus.

*My current lash curler is the Revlon curler and it does not work at all for me. The Revlon curler was a huge waste of money and they hurt too. If you are looking for a fairly inexpensive pair of eyelash curlers that will do it's job, then you will be pleased with this purchase.

*Comes with a cute little storage bag and an extra rubber refill.

rating 5.0. One of my favorite eyelash curlers I have tried many eyelash curlers and while most are sufficient and get the job done I can honestly say this is my favorite eyelash curler along with the whiplash curler (by crave naturals also available on amazon).

I read once on in a beauty magazine that eyelash curlers tend to wear out after about a year and a half. No way!! A good eyelash curler with proper care (not getting dropped or crushed in a makeup bag) should last a lifetime, the pads are another story...but not with this one!.
I have tried the famous Shu Uemura and while that did a very nice job of getting a natural curl I could tell statement about wearing out after a year and a half as the metal connection did not seem very strong. This curler however is rock solid in it's build! The metal is thick and reinforced as is the connection. Another thing I preferred over the Shu Uemura is that this is strongly spring loaded where as Shu Uemura isn't.

The finger holds are very comfortable and smooth plus, also importantly, the curler looks classy! The pad on this curler, as said by the company doesn't need to be replaced. This is impressive as you can tell by looking at it and feeling it the pad is thick and does not have a lot of give to it but is still gentle enough not to harm your lashes.

As for curling. I get the curler as close to the roots as possible by about half opening my eye and the no pinch design allows you to do this easily. Then I hold for about 7 seconds occasionally pumping the curler. I then raise my eyebrows and move the curler a little farther down my lashes and pinch for a second for two while bend the curler backwards and raising the handle up a bit. The main issue with certain eyelash curlers is the over curled and bent lashes effect which also isn't good for your lashes. This curler, when not over pressed, gives a natural curler that is noticeable on it's own without even using mascara.

And a shout out to the wonderful company Bella and Bear with their wonderful customer service and wonderful products that they wholeheartedly back up! Can't go wrong here.
rating 3.0. Three Stars Not a great deal of difference in me, seems ok

rating 3.0. Good but not great It's pretty good, I'm not really sure if it's the best product I've ever used though, I needed a new eyelash curler and this gets the job done but you would be fine using a cheeper one.

UPDATE
It looks like Bella and Bear have backed down on the claim that they had made in the past that the pad *never needs replacing" which makes me wonder if a. The quality of the product has gone down. Or b. they have decided to be honest with their buyers. Also they now include a travel bag for your lash curler which is a nice touch (too bad they did not have that when I bought mine last month) all that being said I still enjoy using my curler but you still could just as easy get the same results with a cheeper one.

rating 3.0. Initially I loved this product Initially I loved this product...my eyelashes really took to the curl and look beautiful when I applied mascara. But, I began to notice that one the left corner of each eye...my lashes looked like they had been cut.  Not falling our but literally cut as if someone took itty bitty scissors.  After some thought I realized that the culprit was the new eyelash curler. I stopped using it and my eyelashes finally grew back in.  Very weird. Don't know if mine was just a defective curler but I'd be scared to try again.

rating 2.0. Eh.... I REALLY wanted to love this curler, especially after I read a review that seemed just like my situation. I use Latisse so I have pretty long lashes and I love the natural look if I am able to get a good curl but I do use mascara also.

This curler does get a great curl and I like that you don't have to squeeze too hard or apply too much pressure (actually I found if you do, I get too much curl, but it's nice that there could be some variation). In any case, I try not to squeeze too hard so I can keep my lashes in tact.

So all that said, I guess my beef with the curler is that it just doesn't fit the curve of my eye. It feels very awkward when I go in for the initial approach and then when I go to "squeeze" it misses half of/ my outter lashes. When I try a different angle I miss or don't get enough or am unable to get close enough to the lid so the curl is not good.

I'll try it a couple more times but alas, I think my quest for the perfect or best eyelash curler continues

rating 5.0. Very impressed and would recommend to everyone! I used to have a revlon curler and barely used it. Over the years, the silicon pad eventually became too worn out and I decide to get a new curler and ordered new pads for my revlon curler and have it as a back up. It was hard at first, deciding on which one to order. There are over 6 eyelash curlers on Amazon, all with over 4 star ratings, having hundreds of reviews and all within the same price range too. How did I decide on this one? I don't remember honestly. But I can tell you that every time I put on mascara, I always use this curler. It seriously makes a huge difference. No wonder why I never used my revlon one. It is absolute crap compared to this curler. I have straight sparse Asian eyelashes and this curler effortlessly curls all of my lashes and keeps them curled. I can't currently comment on the longevity of this curler since I've only had it for a few months so maybe in a few years, I'll try to update this review if I remember. I honestly don't think anyone will regret purchasing this item. If you do, you really would be in the very small small minority.

rating 5.0. Best eyelash curler i have ever used First let me tell you about my lashes. My lashes stick straight out and the ones on the outer corners curve down :( and I have small eyes so it seems the other eyelash curlers that I have used never got my eyelashes on my outer corners very well. I have used lots of different eyelash curlers and they have the same problem which is they did not curl my lashes on the outer corner and the ones that did get curled everywhere else only held the curl for a few seconds so I would have to immediately apply my mascara. I got the Bella and Bear curler yesterday at work and I had to use it as soon as I got the package, I opened the pretty box the curler comes in and I took it to the bathroom I did not have any mascara with me but I really wanted to see how good it curled my lashes. After I used it on one eye there was a huge difference, it curled every eyelash even the ones on the outer corners then I did my other eye it came out beautifully also and sense I did not have any mascara with me I could not put any on so I decided to see how long the curl would hold. I checked 30 minutes later and the curl was still there and not only was it still there it was a much better curl then any other curler had given me even right after I would use them. When I got home I decided to see how it looked with mascara and it was the best my eyelashes have ever looked. In fact my 6 year old has very pretty dark curly lashes and when she seen me after I curled and applied mascara with the Bella and Bear she said mom your eyelashes look so pretty like my eyelashes.
When I seen this curler on Amazon I was hesitant to buy it because of the price and I was not sure if it would really work like the description and reviews said but I was so tired of how my eyelashes were before so I decided to give it a try and I am so happy that I did. I will never use any other curler again.

rating 5.0. The curl of the Bella and Bear is GREAT. The pads of the Tarte curler need to be replaced every 3 months and the curler itself gives out after 6 months, typically the "spring" mechanism snaps.  The curl of the Bella and Bear is GREAT.  It curls quickly and the curl lasts.  It seems to be very sturdy and it says it will last so time will tell but I am optimistic. input: rating 5.0. It works! I just can't believe that this makes true magic when it comes to whitening teeth.  I could tell a difference and I don't smoke nor drink coffee but wow what a difference on my husband.  He used it three or four nights in a row brushing two minutes, per instructions, and it is amazing stuff.  I highly recommend following the directions to the T, because I was so excited I opened it and made a mess, but if you're careful and use the dark towel by the sink as recommended you'll be fine.  Even when it splatter everywhere, as I said I didn't read the directions to spit out as close to drain as possible, because it is natural it was a breeze to clean up with a wet cloth.  Make sure you floss after use because I cleaned my gums and black residue was still in gums even after brushing so just FYI.  I can honestly say conservatively speaking, this little jar will last me and my husband using once a week for at least a year, you don't need a lot.  Comparing the price of whitening treatments and how painful they're (if sensitive teeth) this is a no brainer, I didnt feel any difference on my teeth, sensitivity wise, at all.  Love will recommend to friends.

Yes, you will see black when brushing.. and yes... there really is no taste or smell to it (even though it says "mint")... but the results are absolutely amazing.  NO GMO, NO poisonous fluoride... just a couple of safe ingredients.  The way mother nature intended.  Why support giant corporations and chemical companies when there is someone out there doing it right without all of the things we don't need in our bodies?  Try this product.  I believe you will be glad you did!

rating 5.0. This is the best teeth whitener on the market WOW! Buy this now. And while you're at it buy a few more for the people you love. This maybe the single most impressive product I have ever bought. I have been chewing tobacco and drinking 2 pots of coffee for 20 years, and this immediately whitened my teeth to almost perfect color. You dont get any fake looking white, you get natural teeth looking white.

For those of you having trouble with the "mess" give me a break. Even if you get a little on the counter, it will clean it better than you would have anyways. Just put a napkin under the jar. Tap the top, and sides of rim a few times with your toothbrush and slowly unscrew the cover. It's really not a big deal. When you are spitting during brushing make sure you get your head fairly low (to avoid splash). And remeber even if you do splash a bit it's no big deal the clay will actually help clean your sink anyways.

This stuff is simply amazing. Please support this product and keep them in buisness.

rating 5.0. Whiter Teeth! This stuff is great!  Noticeable improved whiteness after the first use!  I love the clean senstation afterwards.  I do not make a paste out of it. rating 5.0. Has multi uses This is just something I wanted to try out for whiter teeth, since mine are too sensitive for the chemical whiteners on the market or even a good dental whitening. After the first couple days use my teeth were shinier & slightly not as stained. I'm not expecting dramatic results over night.
One big issues I had with my gum was an abscess that was hard getting rid of with antibiotics (8 days, 1500 mg a day) but after 3 days of packing this on the gum where the abscess was it cleared up (after the antibiotics ran out & the pain was still there). I wasn't expecting this product to clear out the rest of a gum infection, but it did.

Some of the others uses I have found for this are as a facial wash (quarter size amount of castor oil & a tsp of charcoal make a good facial), for insect stings (gets rid of the itching), cuts, the list goes on.

I thought the price was expensive at first, but compared to some of the other brands this one has less ingredients, also a better quality of them. I'm not disappointed.

To use this as a toothpaste is very messy & I still have not mastered how not to get it all over the sink or myself when brushing, but that's ok. It does clean up easy. There is no taste or smell, just a gritty feeling to the charcoal.

Don't expect miracles over night though, because as with all natural products they lack the chemicals that provide extreme results in a short amount of time. Natural products take time to work with repeated uses.

rating 5.0. This is really good stuff. After one use This is really good stuff.  After one use, there was an immediate improvement. I use it about once a week and I should probably use it more.  I drink a lot of dark things (coffee, soda, and so on) and more regular use would have a big impact.  For a few days after I got it I was using it once a day and the results were really dramatic.  You'll love this but you have to be careful of the mess.  It won't stain anything but it can be a huge pain to clean up.

rating 3.0. 5 Magic Mud tips UPDATE: Reduce from 5 stars to 3 after using 1/3 to 1/2 of a jar during a 5 month period:

I believe this product diminished stains and lightened my tooth color. However, the powder molded, so I had to throw away at least half the jar.

Go ahead. Try it. What have you got to lose? Here are five Magic Mud tips as you get going:
1) Take selfies. See before & after for yourself.
2) Give it time. Don't expect pearly whites over night. Keep using.
3) Brush your front teeth, which people see.
4) Waterpik afterwards to get stubborn grit gone from between teeth.
5) Wet wipes take care of any minor messes.
One last note: Magic Mud is tasteless. Orange and mint are in the ingredient list, but, as advertised, they don’t contribute flavor.

I was amazed how much this lightened my teeth, plus, they feel and look incredibly clean. You can run your tongue over your teeth and tell that there's no plaque there. It's as if you've been to the dentist. Plus, it really reduces the sensitivity.
