rating 3.0. Fine for Special Occasions Overall, this works okay. I cannot do winged eyeliner for the life of me, so this pen was very appealing. I liked that it had eyeliner on the other end, so the two colors/sheens of the eyeliner and stamp are uniform. I did have to try the stamp a few times, as my eyes are big and every now and then, the stamp did not get my whole eye (so there were spaces it did not get to). I'd recommend stamping it with your eye closed. You also have to practice so both sides are at the right angle. I thought the end of the eyeliner brush was pretty thick (making the line more dramatic than usual) but it easily glided on and was very easy to apply. The stamp also was fairly light at times, so I had to go over it again (but it was helpful to have an outline). It looks nice overall and will be good to use during fancier events where I want my eyeliner to be more dramatic. Still helpful overall and makes the winged look more attainable, just not ideal for my everyday look.

input:
rating 5.0. Turned my clipper brand new and even sharper than new I had ordered a new clipper but after checking the review of this blade, I cancelled my order
But after I received and installed this new blade, it turned my clipper brand new and even sharper than when it was first purchased.
I am happy about this order

rating 5.0. Great set of replacement blades! The blade on my cheaper set of wahl clippers became dull ( the precision marked ones), so I decided to replace them with the ones found on the wahl seniors. Comes with oil, screws and two well lubed blades (stationary blade, and moving blade). I did however used my older screws because the ones that came with theses weren't wide enough.

These blades love to grab a hold of hair, and cut it off. I noticed the difference in the cut quite easily. I actually think they are a lot better then the one on my oster fastfeeds.

Comparing my fastfeeds ($50) set of clippers to a wahl $20 with a $10 blade on, i actually like the wahl combo a lot more. The magnetic motors in the cheap clippers move the blade very fast, paired with a sharp blade you get a clean cut. A good way to improve the potential of a cheap pair of clippers.
rating 5.0. Purchase met and exceeded expectations This was an OEM Part, so I had no doubt that I would be satisfied.  Easy to replace, fast shipping, and back to shaving my own head.

rating 5.0. Crazy If You Don't Buy Them The Gist of it: This replacement blade set (2-hole) revitalized my Wahl adjustable clippers purchased at a certain big chain box store back in 2000. Definitely worth the cost, as opposed to buying an entire new clipper set.
As other customers have noted, it does take a bit of patience to align the blades and keep them that way while tightening the screws. However, it is far from rocket science. As at least one other customer noted, I also had to use my old screws as the new ones would not thread properly.
As long as your Wahl is USA made, and has two screws in the blade head, then these should be okay. I dare you to try it. It's kind of fun and you may just find yourself adjusting your blades routinely. I know I do now.

rating 5.0. Alternate blade for the Wahl 9590-210 Pro-series Clipper with rechargeable battery I bought this replacement set to replace the blade that came in the Wahl 9590-210 Pro-series Complete Pet Clipper Kit (Cordless Operation, Blue) that I bought a few years ago. The original blade worked well for a few years, but after giving a new summer hair style to one of the cats, it dulled the blade and this year it was painfully slow to trim the cat's hair again. The main replacement blade, which is held in place with two Torx 20 screws is identical to the original blade. The smaller blade replacement (which is driven off the tool's motor) is very different to the original blade. However, the registration holes are in the same position. I was able to assemble the blade, but it would not fit inside the tool. A quick snip of the plastic housing made the new blade set fit perfectly. The smaller driven replacement blade (it is larger than the original) just needs a little more space to be able to move left and right to cut hair properly, since it is slightly wider than the original blade.

rating 5.0. Great clippers and this replacement blade brought it back to ... I have a set of Wahl clippers that I use to buzz my own hair. Great clippers and this replacement blade brought it back to like new performance.

input: rating 1.0. LOVE IT ! I am 63 and have been noticing my eyebrows getting more sparse as time goes on.  I have tried many different brands of powders including expensive brands from Sephora. I have also tried eyebrow tinting but I looked like a clown as you have to be careful to ONLY tint the hair, not the skin.  Thank God it wears away in a few weeks.  I  go to the gym 4 days per week and sweat a LOT.  This product is fantastic !  No smudges and NOT itchy at all.  It looks very natural, not "clown-like."  Would highly recommend !

rating 5.0. which I know works on me but didn’t feel like paying $30-40 for an eye cream As I have gotten older I have been struggling with swelling in the eye area, and face overall. When I wake up my eyes are very puffy. I have tried several other eye creams that claim to take away the puffiness but most of them didn’t work at all. I bought this cream because it has some of the same key ingredients as Mary Kay eye cream, which I know works on me but didn’t feel like paying $30-40 for an eye cream.
This one makes a big difference. It is visible. I can actually see my eyelids. The skin texture has improved in firmness; the skin is moisturized and the little wrinkles and rough areas have disappeared. I notice continuous results with this gel. I apply it morning and night all the way around the eyes and work it in in the outward motion. I do recommend this. It's my life saver! I only regret I didn't find this cream years ago.
You get a lot of product in the container. You can even apply it over the entire face if you need to.

rating 2.0. so it can get pretty hot down here I live in the South, so it can get pretty hot down here. I also have some skin problems (mild to moderate acne, redness, dry flakes), so I like to wear base makeup whenever I go out. I bought this because I was hoping that combined with a primer and fixing spray, the skin prep pro would help my makeup stay put and not get swept away by the flood of sweat that comes crashing in every summer. The primer and fixing spray I use work well for me (or at least are the best I've tried so far) and sweat is still an issue for me. When this came in, I had high hopes, but unfortunately this is not a miracle product. It did make my face feel tight, so I applied a small amount of moisturizer over it after letting it set but before applying my makeup. I'm a college student, so I walk to class all the time, and after a few classes I could tell that my face was fading and melting as usual. It wasn't a pretty sight. So the next day, to see if it was due to the moisturizer, I just applied the skin prep pro before my makeup. Sadly, I got the same results. I break it out every now and again to see if it's a certain product that it won't work with, but after going through all of my routine taking one product out at a time, I've determined that this just doesn't work for me. I gave this two stars because while it didn't do what I wanted it to, it also didn't make any of my skin problems worse. I also want to give it the benefit of the doubt in case it is helping, just not significantly enough.

rating 5.0. This is the perfect skin prep for the summer months If you don't have this in your life, you are missing out.  This is the perfect skin prep for the summer months.  Try it, you would like it.

rating 5.0. For Sweaty Betty's This stuff really works. If you have a major event to attend and you sweat profusely under your makeup; I highly recommend this product by Mehron. If you can put it on at least a half hour before you put your make up, it will set better. I would use a combo primer/moisturizer over it to make sure that your makeup applies smoothly. It helps with preventing my makeup from melting off my face. When I get home, I use micellar water to remove as much of my make up as possible then I wash with my clarisonic to clean my face thoroughly after using this skin prep.

rating 5.0. This product is amazing, and worth every cent This product is amazing, and worth every cent. To put this into context, I live in Southern California and we experience high heat and high humidity.

- Makeup stays put and looks fresh for 12+ hours WITHOUT any touch up
- Makeup adheres better to skin - I ended up using less foundation, bronzer, and highlighter. This primer ENHANCES color payoff in other products
- Primer does not feel thick or uncomfortable on the skin throughout the day

Only issue: this product dries very quickly to a tacky texture. I got the best results when I applied moisturizer before the primer. After the primer dries, liquid foundation should be applied with a very quick hand. Otherwise, the foundation will begin to collect unevenly in blotches. You can apply liquid foundation by hand or brush, but the best method is probably stippling with a brush or beauty blender. Stippling will prevent the foundation from sticking to the primer in blotches

rating 2.0. Use With Caution! pros:
it truly makes your make up stick to your skin and doesn't melt off on the hottest of days. i wore this to a concert festival during the summer and by the end of it, my face wasn't melted and oily.

cons:
taking the fact that it's for high-performance conditions, i would NOT recommend anyone to use this daily. i wore this twice on separate occasions because i couldn't find a primer that really worked at the time (update: found a product that works amazingly and can be used everyday--MAKE UP FOR EVER Step 1 Skin Equalizer) what ended up happening after i used it, probably 1-2 days after was that my face broke out in large pimples as well as a weird rash on my cheeks. this is just my personal experience, but i would not use it again. my skin did not react kindly to this and my poor clogged pores were probably suffocating under it. so if you must, use with caution!

tips:
apply this LAST, after any other creams, lotions, sunscreen. i made the mistake of applying it before my sunscreen and it became flaky and i had to wipe off crumbs from my face.

current_product_reviews: rating 5.0. Very good I have something called Horner's Syndrome, it's some kind of crazy bizarre nerve damage in which part of my face is really dry and doesn't perspire and other areas perspire way too much. Crazy. It also makes one pupil appear small and the other is noticeably larger, so creepy, but the product can't do anything to help me with that. I decided to try this Mehron Skin Prep Pro, to see how well it could help me with the areas that perspire too much. I poured out a very small amount onto a stainless steel makeup palette that I have and decided to use an eye shadow brush to apply it, I only apply the Skin Prep only to parts of my face that have always had the problem. Generally, I start to apply my makeup and need to stop 3-4 times during process to gently blot the same 4 overly sweaty sections, lightly dust powder, sit with a fan, think breezy thoughts, etc. I was careful not to paint the Skin Prep on the alternate, dry areas, those areas do not perspire and can develop a parched, peely appearance. I waited a few seconds for the Skin Prep to dry on my face before continuing with my makeup. I've only used it 4 times so far, but, touch wood, it seems to be working. My makeup has had a more even application and appearance, even before I was done putting makeup on, I feel like my tone was more even and looked better. When it comes to the mirror, we notice things in so much greater detail than anyone else ever would, I realize no one else may ever notice the difference I notice. It certainly has helped a bit with my bizarre harlequin sweaty vs dry issue I've got going, even if no one else ever notices it, that little improvement means something to me.

Deciem produces active ingredients and holy cow they are active indeed. i m using this one among 4 other substances and my skin has gone from dull to glow and healthy within the forst 2 weeks. rating 2.0. Magnetic eyelashes I purchased these lashes, and was so excited. I wore them the first time and loved them. They were easy to put on, and the liner was easy to remove with my makeup remover cloths. On my third attempt to wear, my mind changed drastically. One of my lashes would no longer stick. I added more liner, etc, nothing. I attempted to stick several times. I took both lashes off, and noticed one lash has 5 magnets, and the other only has 3. The one with 3 magnets, is the one that does not stick anymore. I've only worn twice, and the area that has magnets on the lash with 3 magnets does not stick either. Not sure how long these lashes were suppose to last, but I expected to get more than two wears out of them. I always put them back in the case after every use and tried to take good care of them. I will reorder another pair, from somewhere else, as a last attempt before going back to using the regular lashes with glue. I do like them, and they saved so much time applying my lashes, so I hope to have better luck with the next pair, but I will not order from same vendor.

rating 5.0. Must have one for thinning hair My first experience with boar brush of any brand. This brush is really a good one to begin with. With pure BB brush, the key to using is by sectioning your hair and brushing otherwise you wont feel the brush on the scalp.I have thinning hair and an Indian woman with black hair, I ordered this recently and have been using it over the past 3 days.I can say that it straightens the hair naturally which is very wonderful to me as mine is a bit wavy. Plus without sectioning my scalp, I don't find this brush massaging my scalp so I section in 3 parts or so and do it. I am not losing much hair too but will comment later post regular usage on hair growth parts. Also it has a petrol kind of smell to the brush which I didn't mind and I started using it as is. The wooden comb also massages the hair well and find it soothing to my itchy scalp. Really recommend to people with thin hair.

I extremely happy with this come and brush.

rating 4.0. Works Beautifully Received this today.
It's receiving four stars instead of five because it took over a month to reach me, but it didn't get three stars because customer service handled it beautifully so thank you guys.

I was so excited to test it out so I want to leave some tips because everyone doesn't know how curing works believe it or not. So the lamp works, it is an LED lamp not UV so it's not harmful to your skin. It's cheap so it's a really good deal.
How to Use:
•There are two settings 30 & 60 seconds, press quickly for 30, long press for 60 seconds.
IMPORTANT: DO NOT *TOUCH* THE NAIL POLISH DURING THESE STEPS.
1. Apply thin base coat; cure for 30 seconds.
2. Apply first coat of nail polish, very thin *but* pigmented; cure for 30 seconds.
3. Apply second coat of nail polish, thin; cure for thirty seconds.
4. If needed apply a third coat then cure for thirty seconds.
5. Apply top coat, thick or thin is your choice; cure for 60 seconds.
IMPORTANT: Still don't touch it with your fingers
6. Take a lint free cloth with some alcohol on it and wipe the nail clean.

(These steps were posted previously in a unique manner however I don't think enough people seen their post. Touching the nailpolish will ruin all your efforts. Please trust me.)

input: rating 5.0. Great! I love this product. I have extremely oily skin and this is very gentle and doesn't leave you with that nasty squeaky clean feel, instead it leaves you hydrated soft clean. I don't know how it does on its own I use and oil cleanser before hand*. This Came as described and was wrapped in packaging with a seal inside.

rating 5.0. I was skeptical at first.. Jumped on the Asian Beauty train after following the Asian Beauty subreddit on reddit. This came highly recommended as a first try, since it's inexpensive and straight forward. I was fairly skeptical how well it would work since I very rarely have acne, but this stuff is amazing! My skin doesn't feel stripped of oils, I like the tea tree scent, and it heals up what few breakouts I have.

It does an OK job at removing makeup, but I would use a true makeup remover before using this.

rating 5.0. This is the perfect followup to any cleanser and keeps my skin balanced I knew nothing about the importance of having a low ph cleanser until I read up on it.  This is the perfect followup to any cleanser and keeps my skin balanced, clear and healthy.  Definitely a holy grail item in my skincare line up.

rating 5.0. Wonderful Basic Cleanser... I cannot say enough about this product. It is a wonderful cleanser that I use day and night as part of my skin care routine. I love the fresh scent, how clean it makes my face feel, the price, and how it is great for my sensitive skin. Cosrx is a wonderful brand, I use quite a few Cosrx products for the last few months now as part of my Korean skincare routine and this along with a few others has changed my skin. It doesn't break me out and is just a great general cleanser. No complaints here, would definitely recommend this, it's not for removing makeup but you can use it as many times as you want throughout the day, and it doesn't dry my skin out...just feels fresh and clean. Use it at night before my night time cleanser, and in the morning as my only cleanser before makeup. Also use this after I workout as well. Cannot say enough about this, but if you need something stronger for pimples just use this as your base cleanser and pair with toners, pimple pads or salicylic acid, or the blemish cream. For example I am oily on my nose and chin so I pair it with toner, essence, ampuole, and sunblock during the day. For night, I pair this with salicylic acid wash, pads and blemish cream etc.
rating 5.0. Excellent Quality and Price This is my wife's favorite face soap. She's tried many others but always comes back to this one as her go to.
rating 5.0. Excellent Quality and Price This is my wife's favorite face soap. She's tried many others but always comes back to this one as her go to.
rating 5.0. One of my favorite cleansers. It's not overly drying / stripping for my combination skin. And my SO, who has oily / acne prone skin, likes it as well (which is a big win in my book)!

rating 5.0. Great for sensitive and acne prone skin Great for sensitive and acne prone skin. It does clean but without takina all your natural oils away which helps reduce breack outs and improves your skin's ph level.

rating 5.0. Holy grail cleanser This is my holy grail cleanser. I use it as my second step in my 2 step oil cleansing routine. For background, I have combo, dehydrated skin that is more prone to dryness in the wintertime. This cleanser rinses clean without any residue or drying/over-stripping my skin. It has a scent of tea tree oil but it is not overwhelming, and I haven’t had any reactions or breakouts from the cleanser. I’ve found that since using a lower ph cleanser, my skin has actually become less oily and less prone to breakouts. The ph of this cleanser is 5 and it really does make a difference. I truly believe my skin was overcompensating for the stripping cleansers I was using that had a way too high ph.

rating 5.0. Stole It From Wife I Liked It So Much I love this face wash. My wife got it and I stole it from her. It doesn't feel like much when you're actually applying it, but once I dried my face I felt that it was significantly cleaner. I'm generally a fan of the thicker moisturizers that foam up a bit more to let me know they are doing the job, but this one foams up enough (especially when I get to my beard). I also shave my head and use this cleanser afterwards because its both gentle and effective. I have notice a significant decrease with the ingrown hairs as well.

Pair with a good moisturizer and you're golden (I use Triumph & Disaster Gameface, which is fantastic).

rating 1.0. Hair on the inside of the plastic cleanser cap I have used COSRX products for over two years and I really love this brand. However, this time around I think I received a fake product. rating 5.0. Really helpful for acne Like many others, I have started following the Korean skincare routine. I first tried double cleansing with the rice water oil wash and cleanser from the face shop as it had good reviews, but quickly started looking elsewhere as it broke me out like crazy. It could have been purging, but regardless, I didnt feel it was doing a good job for my skin as it left it red and angry. Not wanting to upset my skin too much, I opted for this cleanser as the water based portion of the routine. I am already seeing results after short use. My skin looks and feels better immediately after washing with this. It has a very light lather and feels very luxurious on your face. I can definitely smelll the tea tree oil in it, and I feel that's a big part of why my face is finally clearing up. I haven't had any new breakouts since switching to this cleanser, which is really comforting since I've been working so hard for clear skin. I'd definitely give this a shot if you suffer from acne. This is a really good deal for two bottles and I feel like it will last a long time

rating 5.0. Worth it !!!!! I always struggled with an oily T-zone and dry cheeks, so I did my research and found out my past cleansers were too stripping and acidic for my skin to handle; thus stripping it of the natural oils and then producing even more to offset it. This was a lifesaver for my skin honestly, I feel squeaky clean without that apparent tightness you get from those ‘exfoliating’ cleansers, plus it doesn’t contain any microbeads that might irritate my skin. Skeptical of the hype at first, but now I’m totally convinced !

current_product_reviews:
rating 5.0. Simple & gentle everyday cleanser For reference: My skin type is combination/oily and acne prone. I rarely get cystic acne (though I have in the past with the scars to prove it), but I get a lot of whiteheads and clogged pores around my cheeks, jawline, chin, and edges of my mouth.

I have used the Purity by Philosophy cleanser for the last few years because it never lets me down. It's gentle, doesn't sting my eyes, and removes all my excess makeup. But it is expensive and as I began developing my own k-beauty skin care routine, I was looking for a gentle foaming cleanser that was more affordable. This Good Morning cleanser is perfect for everyday. I currently only use it in the morning because I still have more than half a bottle of the Purity left, which I use in the PM. But once my purity runs out, I look forward to using the CosRX cleanser both AM & PM. It foams really nicely and my skin doesn't feel excessively dry or tight after usage. It has a very light and pleasant medicinal smell.

rating 5.0. Aromela Aroma Diffuser Wins! The aromela aroma diffuser is a good deal for a good product. Simply and efficiently built. Easy to put together and operate. The bundled essential oil is good quality and smells nice (not overwhelming or chemical smelling). Combined - this unit and the oil does an effective job of adding some humidity and scent to any room in your home. It would be a nice xmas gift treat.

rating 5.0. A nice bonus is the variety of colors the diffuser has ... I ordered this diffuser because I wanted something small that I could use essential oils in. Attach the power cord (which is decent length), add water (I use distilled), a few drops of essential oil and it's ready to go.  A nice bonus is the variety of colors the diffuser has available.  Set it to a color that suits your mood or let it change through all the colors.  Nice little diffuser for an affordable price.

rating 5.0. Glad I purchased this! When I purchased this, it actually was not what I was looking for at the time. I searched humidifiers and came across this and decided to buy it thinking it was a humidifier that could also do aromatherapy.
I live in Colorado where the air is extremely dry and to get real benefit from a humidifier you need a much more hefty machine that cranks out much more moisture than this item does; so I don't recommend this item if that is solely what you are looking for. However, the benefit this product offers from aromatherapy may help with some of the reasons you are looking for a humidifier. We wanted a humidifier because the dry air leaves us with dry skin and frequent sinus and respiratory infections.
I read the little booklet that came with the product and saw that eucalyptus is supposed to be beneficial for a decongestant and helping with respiatory problems. I happened to have some eucalyptus on hand so we decided to give it a try and it was the first night in several that my husband did not wake up coughing every couple of hours.
So we now have both this and a more powerful humidifier set up in our bedroom and we are very happy with them. We are actually both considering buying additional units for our offices!

We also love the soft glow of the light at night, we find it to be soothing. If that is not something you like you are able to turn the light off by pushing the button several times, cycling through the color options.

rating 4.0. Great Value I used this for the first time today and my review of it is a positive one, with comments:

First, the box showed up, it was not plain or unmarked. And the plug looks completely american.

However the temp is in CELSIUS. the max being 230. Which is 446 degrees F. Which more than how high an average flat iron goes.

I have naturally wavy/curly hair, which is butt length on me and i am 5 ft 10 inches tall. Its usually very thick also. So in the real world, i do not have time to blow dry my hair and flat iron it. However for this first use i was excited and had my daughter blow dry my hair as straight as possible then test out the brush. I have added photos. one after blowdrying, one half and half, the last fully brushed.

This is definitely worth the money paid!!! i look forward to calming the frizzies that dry naturally and using this brush as a quicker way to look "natural" rather than a pin straight look anyway. I just dont have time to flat iron my hair and this is a great compromise for $23 rather than $300.

input: rating 5.0. The best stamper ive ever used! Awesome stamper! I bought 2 more because this makes stamping so easy! Also I was worried it would be too firm but it's very squishy!

rating 2.0. Perfect Placing Stamper! (updated) The clear stamper came just as described. I haven't actually used it on my nails yet but I have played around with it on some paper and it picks up the images perfectly. After reading some concerning reviews on other clear stampers I was hesitant to purchase this one. However, I have had no problems as of yet. The only flaw is that there are two little dent/scratches on the stamper head and I am afraid eventually it will lead to bigger dents/scratches. With it being so inexpensive I wouldn't mind purchasing another one for back-up, just in case. I do not use any scrapers that come with the stampers, as I use gift cards because they are much easier to use and swipe with.

The stamper head is squishy but not marshmallow squishy. It is possible to squish on your nail without rolling, so long as you do not have extremely wide nail beds. It is a little hard to get used to being able to see through and it takes some practice getting it lined up exactly where you want, but it's easier once you do it a few times. As I stated earlier, the images are very crisp and clean when being picked up. Instead of squishing down like I did with my other stamper I instead try to roll/flick the stamper across. This is more for the fear of damaging the stamper, as I've read about others who did not have success with clear stampers.

Make sure that when you use it you do not clean it with acetone/polish remover as it will eventually make the stamper head cloudy and no longer see through. Also, do not buff or anything before first use, I found that it picks up perfectly without having to do any of that.

I highly recommend getting this stamper if you love to stamp but have problems with perfect placing, such as myself.

*****UPDATE******

I have no idea what happened but I've recently been trying to use this stamper and it will just not work. I've used so many different polishes, different brands and nothing! I had no problem at all and picked up images very well, however now it picks up less than half of the image which is extremely frustrating. I spent about 2 hours this morning doing all kinds of different things, using different polishes, using different techniques, different stamping plates and nothing is working! I've tried priming it like you would a non-clear stamper which includes wiping the head with acetone and buffing it with a file.

rating 5.0. Over all really good, once you get the hang of it. The scraper is plastic so you don't have to worry about it scraping up your plates. I've seen people using just old credit cards to do scraping but because it's free why not use it, plus it fits in your hand easily so it's easier to grip, which is great if you have small hands like me.

The stamper itself, is made up of two parts. The squishy thing to actually transfer the stamp to your nail, and the tube thing that holds it. They do come apart so it's easier to clean (or whatever).

And now for some things I wish I knew sooner:
1. You do not have to press as hard as possible when scraping the plate. Just as long as you move the excess polish out of the way you're good.
2. Newer polishes work best. I know there are specifically polishes for stamping but I don't have those. I have tried to stamp with several different polishes and I've found that newer polishes are easier to pick up with the stamper because they are more liquid like.
3. Move quickly-ish, no you don't have to race to get the polish on your nail but remember that it's a very small, thin amount of polish that dries very quickly, so don't wait a millennium.
4. Roll the stamper over the plate. Start with one side and roll to the other, I've found that this works best as opposed to just straight down on the plate.
5. Remember to use the clearness of the thingy, it's easy to forget that it is clear so remember it is the diffrence between a straight design and crooked design.

rating 5.0. A Must Have I purchased this product because getting my eyebrows tinted at the salon was costing me $12 every two weeks.  When you add in the $8 for wax, that is $20 every two weeks...or $40 per month or $480 per year.  Way too much to be spending on my eyebrows.  Due to the good reviews, I purchased this product.  It comes with EVERYTHING you need including an application brush.  Its about 30 capsules, and 1 capsule makes plenty.  FOLLOW the directions! I was watching YouTube and the women left theirs on for like 10 minutes...even the lady at the Salon did the same thing...maybe that's because she used a lighter tint...I am not sure...but the directions are clear and concise.  I followed them and I LOVED the results.  I was so afraid of breaking out in an allergic reaction, I was putting everything over my eyebrows at that point.  Its day 4 and its still there.  Using Vaseline around the brows and qtips to help wipe away the access tint was the best advice I received on YouTube.  I will continue to use this product...would definitely recommend!

rating 5.0. Leave the color longer than 2 mins! The items are easy to mix, not as thick as I'd expect it. I added a picture with 4 series.
1. Before any product
2. Eyebrows with product- let is sit for 10 mins
3. Removed color with face wash & water
4. Removed the stain skin color with skin color remover from Sally's ($4 for a small- lasts about a year)

There wasn't that much of a vast different. It did color all my baby hairs just fine. Instructions recommend leaving it on for 2 mins. That would've done nothing. Next time I plan on leaving it for 20 mins. It stains your skin pretty bad. I highly, highly recommend going to Sally's and buying hair color remover first. It removes the color from the skin only. Otherwise you'll look like you have insane tattooed brows.

I also added some on my lashes by the lash line, my eyes started to burn in about 5 mins. That's when I took it off. Don't recommend it, but it did leave some good color.

Also, I recommend getting a few people together. The amount of color one capsule produces is good enough for maybe 8 pairs of eyebrows. But probably not enough to cover an entire mans beard.

rating 5.0. Salon eyebrow-tinting is now a thing of the past I have brown hair but was born with naturally light and sparse eyebrows. I used to spend obscene amounts of money getting my brows done for up to $20 at the salon (oftentimes they didn't get it as dark as I wanted), but since purchasing this product I haven't been back! Since I know how I like my brows better than any esthetician would, I apply the dye as I would fill in my brows with a pencil or eyeshadow. I let the dye sit for about 2-3 minutes, then wipe it off with a rag (caution: the dye DOES stain rags!). The dye is inevitably on my skin for a day or 2, but since I fill in only where I'd actually put makeup, it's like I have makeup on and they actually look fuller (just use caution when applying). The box comes with at least 20 or so capsules of dye (which are the hardest to open without getting powder in my sink), so that means I can literally dye my brows 20 different times. The dye lasts about 2 weeks at a time. Overall, if you have lightly-colored eyebrows and want them to be darker, I highly recommend this. It's saved me time, money, and hassle!

I attached 2 photos to this review: Picture #1 is my natural brows, before dying, and picture #2 is shortly after dying, with my brows brushed upwards with some drugstore brow gel. The first is taken with my iPhone and 2nd picture with my Canon DSLR, but the sparsity of my natural eyebrows should definitely be apparent in the first photo despite the lower-quality.

rating 5.0. LOVE IT (not a sponsored review) I use this approximately once every two weeks. I think its great! Easy to set up & put away.

I use the included wedged brush to draw my eyebrow EXACTLY how I would when applying my makeup & the result is perfect. It works so fast that once i finish the eyebrow i wait only 10 seconds and immediately take a wet face cloth and wipe it off. You can use remaining dye in the cup to re-apply if its not dark enough for you. Its very simple!

The dye only lasts on skin for around 3 days but the hair of your eyebrow stays dyed for a little over a week. Honestly the whole process takes no more than 10 mins so its fine to do it weekly in your routine.

Works well for sparse eyebrows and helps stain skin where there are no hairs ;) The longer you keep this on, the darker this gets (brown/black). If you have very sparse eyebrows though i.e. areas where there is really no hair, the dye will not last more than 3-4 days because it is on skin. The powder capsules can be opened and used in smaller amounts since you won't need a full capsule for each use, so this is a very economical buy. Carefully open the capsule, place the powder in the included grey cap, or on one end of a plastic soup spoon (easier to clean) and mix with the creme. That's how little you need.

rating 5.0. Amazing, Real Feet after 15 Years This thing is amazing. I've had cracked heels for 15 years, so bad sometimes the cracks were like deep painful knife cuts. I tried every cream and buffer and professional pedicure. Some would work for a few weeks, but nothing ever got through the layers of tough skin. Then, for less than the cost of a pedicure, I bought this callus remover.

Believe the reviews that say you need to be careful. This is a powerful unit and, even on the low setting, can burn if you leave it on one spot for more than a second or two. Just take your time and apply it lightly to the rough areas. I used it every other day for 2 weeks and now only need it once a week. I'm still on the first disk, so the supply that came in the package will last me a few years.

rating 4.0. A LOT of power for really bad feet. I am going to confess something, my feet are hideous. My family make fun my feet all the time and call them barney rubble feet. I basically walk around barefoot almost 24/7 and never go for pedicures. I would go for a pedi but I am too embarrassed at how bad my feet were. I will be the first to say I neglect my feet, they are (or should I say were) very callused and stained. My heels had deep cracks from neglect and three years of being barefoot almost exclusively. Today my callus remover came and I have to say I am impressed. I shop on amazon ALOT and it takes a lot for me to go out of my way to write a review but this gadget deserves one.

First, out of the box it looks to be a heavy duty machine. Thick power cord and a decent weight but not cumbersome. It looks built to last. It has enough power on low to do a major overhaul on the hardest and deepest of calluses. User beware this thing packs a punch. Don't spent more than a couple seconds on an area or you will feel the burn quick. Try just tapping the head on your calluses. Go against the direction of the spin because it will run off on you. It's strong enough to sand down thick toenails too. My feet have never looked this good.

No worries for lack of power on this thing, if you are looking for a gentle buffer this isn't it so if you feet are in decent shape look for something a little less aggressive. If your feet look like they walk over hot coals from time to time, this baby will take care of that in one quick session. My feet are super smooth and look like brand new, albeit a little sore.

rating 5.0. Best Buffer I've Ever Used My original thought after I used this was to come here and berate all the people who gave it a one star review because they thought it was too powerful. But then I realized that the one star reviews are the reason I decided to give it a try. I get big, thick, tough calluses on my feet that are very painful. I've tried multiple types of buffers and other means to remove them and smooth them down, all to varying degrees of success. I figured there had to be something out there that could handle the job better and I hoped this was it.

As soon as I got this, I put the rougher of the two discs on, started off on low to test it out, and within a few seconds I had it cranked all the way up as high as it could go and was buffing away. It made short work of my calluses. It sanded them right down to a smooth finish. Yes, if you go too far, it will burn, but that's the sign that you've gotten down to skin, which is where you want to be. That's happened with any buffer that I've used, but it gets there much quicker with this one. It just takes a little bit of practice to get the feel for it and know when to stop.

If you have the types of calluses that I have and you have had a difficult time trying to remove them, then this is the product for you. It does exactly what it was designed for, which is to remove and smooth out tough calluses. If you find that it's too powerful, then you don't have those types of calluses and you have the wrong tool for the job.

Return it and find the right one for you. But don't give this one a one star review because it wasn't it. It does exactly what it's supposed to do. So don't be so "callous" with your reviews (see what I did there).

rating 5.0. One of the best Amazon puchases EVER! I live in the desert. I hate what the heat and constantly wearing flip flops does to my heels. I have tried everything to stop the deep cracks that would often lead to bleeding; nothing worked. I got pedicures every 4 weeks; nothing. I exfoliated and moisturized every night, but there was so much skin, I couldn't make a dent (literally). I was getting really frustrated. I considered buying the Clarisonic foot exfoliator, but everything I read about the product told me it was too weak to treat what I was dealing with. Then I got a plantar wart on my heel. Ick. No matter how much I filed, I couldn't get the acid to reach the source of the virus. I turned to this product and the wart was gone in a week! My heels have never looked better. My feet look like I just got a pedicure, EVERY DAY!

There are some things you need to know before you use this:
1. It is super powerful. It could probably sand wood, if you turned it up high enough. Leave it on the lowest setting.
2. Wet your feet before you use this. If you apply it to dry skin, you could burn yourself. After you get used to it, you can use it on dry skin, but I still like to soak my feet first so the dead skin is nice and supple.
3. If you can, use this outside. The machine blasts off a ton of dead skin, but it leaves a fine dust all over everything in the process.
4. Be extra careful around toes and between the toes. Do not be adventurous the first time you use it; stick to your heels until you get used to it.

Enjoy! You will be so happy you bought this. It is worth every penny.

rating 5.0. This thing is amazing. I've had several others and they were so ... This thing is amazing. I've had several others and they were so weak that the slightest pressure would stop them cold. This bad boy is like having a 400 hp hemi on a push mower. Expecting it to be similar to all the others I had used before I put one of the coarse pads on and attacked the thick callus on my heel. Not having experienced anything like this before I made the mistake of holding it in one spot for almost a second. The intense heat from the friction started at about 1 second in. I'm not sure if it was skin debris flying off or smoke from the friction. Being just a tad stubborn I thought maybe it was just a fluke and perhaps the burning sensation was false input from my nerves. So I repeated my same tactic a second time. Nope, no mistaken signal. This time I am sure it was smoke. I'm surprised the house smoke alarms weren't blaring away. Fortunately I had a cold glass of tea that I promptly placed on my foot and with a hiss like a red hot horse shoe hitting a water tank my foot quickly cooled. If I were a Naked and Afraid contestant I would take one of these for a fire starter. Oh did I mention I had it on the lowest setting. I can only imagine that the high setting is for removing your skin down to the second layer of muscle. If you have dragon scales or calluses tough as a tortoise shell, this thing on high might be for you. After a few more experiments I found that I found that very light strokes and very little pressure is all that is needed. Without a doubt there is a technique that the user must acquire to use this device properly. The Professional part of this devices name is not an overstatement. My feet are in wonderful shape now and are as smooth as when I was a kid. So if you have tough calluses and you have given up on wimpy removers I would highly recommend this one.

rating 5.0. Excellent product... There are plenty of really great reviews for this product already. Nonetheless, this is the absolute best callus remover product I have ever used. I therefore feel obligated to at least point out any pros/cons.

Pros:

- It works fast.

- It works on even the most difficult and stubborn dried, cracked and/or damaged skin.

- It works with very little effort on the user's end. No need to apply pressure...it will work just as well without doing so.

- It doesn't get absurdly hot. If you start to feel the heat is a problem, you can just swap the pad for a new one and resolve this issue. (Of course you can go back and swap back later so as not to waste an entire pad).

- Replacement heads are cheap and easily obtained here on Amazon - both the coarse and polishing options.

- The unit works immediately, right out of the box. No need to pre-charge or assemble anything.

- For those with fragile hands, this is a lightweight product with a comfortable grip.

- Works well even on dry skin that is *not* pre-soaked or treated with any creams, etc.

Cons:

- Although I mentioned this in the pros, the fact it does get hot and *could* require a swap of the pad mid-job, makes it a con as well.

----------------

The above is my personal assessment of this product. I have yet to encounter any other cons and would highly recommend this to anyone in need of such a product. My days of scraping, scrubbing, etc. are over. For years I have tried every approach, product, method, and what not. I have even used straight sandpaper at times. I wish I had discovered this product sooner, but am pleased to have it now at least. In my opinion, this is worth every penny.

I was also a little afraid to try it after receiving it since the small print states that it is being sold as a "solvent". In the art world, that means to me that it will eat away other things (think turpentine LOL), and the warning stated that it could damage the skin. Yikes!

I reviewed this one some while ago. I've now been using this for at least a year and a half and it is the best pain relief cream I've ever used, and I've tried a lot. First, it doesn't have a lot of the medicinal odor that many pain relief creams use due to the camphor and menthol in the others. I don't have to use much before I get relief (although I'll admit that when I start hurting in bed I also use a heat pad)...but it's the use of so little that is well worth the price and recently it was marked lower so I made sure and bought two to stock up on. Every time a friend complains of pain I share some and then they remark how great it was for them as well. I don't ever want to run out of this since it works fast, uses very little, and lasts a long while.

Unfortunately, or fortunately, however one wants to look at it, I have been in extreme pain for years now. I have Plantar Fasciitis on the right foot, a swollen Achilles Tendon (an MRI showed it being strained and my ankles as being torn but the doctor only had me go to 3 months of PT for that), spondylosis in both the cervical and lumbar spines, and sciatica. I was beginning to feel hopeless at ever feeling good again. So then, I thought, with as many people who have touted the wonders of this particular jar of cream, what do I have to lose. Maybe a little skin, but maybe, just maybe I could lose some of the pain.

WOW!!! Am I ever glad I finally gave in! I opened it up 3 nights ago, and almost immediately felt the pain easing away from the areas I rubbed the cream in to. I had a pretty decent night's sleep and the cream seemed to keep the pains at bay for at least 5-6 hours. Yesterday, however, I had to go to town and didn't get around to putting anything on before going. Going to town out here in rural American is a half day affair. The closest grocery stores and such are at least 35-40 mins drive away. So I wound up being back in extreme pain by the time I returned, put groceries away, cooked and ate dinner, and cleaned up from dinner. But I put some more on before settling down to watch a movie last night and voila, it seemed to ease the pains again, so before I fell asleep I put some more on and again, had another more restful night's sleep. This morning, before I even got up and started my day, I put some on my feet and my neck and they feel pretty good. I didn't get any on my lumbar spine and that's beginning to hurt, so as soon as I get this posted, I will be putting some on my lumbar spine area. I am definitely sold on this and will buy more when I start getting low, however, a little goes a long way, so I think it will probably last me for quite a long while. I highly recommend it and will be telling family and friends about it, as well. I am definitely sold!!!

rating 5.0. Adds circulation for crusty diabetes feet! The aroma is pleasant. Only with the first use did I experience a slight garlic taste but I never noticed it after that. I didn't find it effective enough to eliminate my aspirin/Tylenol regimen for arthritis pain. But what I found amazing is when I tried it on my dried out crusty diabetes feet...  I instantly had great relief when I applied this cream!!  It was as though I had circulation in my feet that I didn’t even know I was missing for the first time in years. Although my feet aren’t entirely normal again, I haven’t had to apply bandaids with neosporin for the cracked feet infections for more than a month now!  I gave half my jar to someone else and have just ordered my second jar..  I honestly don’t use this more than once every few days because I didn’t initially have much more improvement from the first couple of weeks by doing so after that burst of circulation that seems to be ongoing with 1-2 uses per week. The skin isn’t fully healed but feels so much better..  again - zero bandaids!!  And the appearance of my feet is continuing to improve so maybe I’ll write again if this truly becomes the cure!!

rating 3.0. they're ok but make sure you get multiple packs These extensions are nice HOWEVER one package is not enough for a full head which is why I purchased 3. I have medium length hair, an inch or two past my shoulders. I bought these in 14inch (natural black) which is more of an espresso black. I should have purchased jet black. Normally, with high quality extensions like Bellami brand (for example, i do have these extensions as well which are close to 300$ at 200grams of hair, however they are VERY long which is why I found these because I needed a 14inch length) you can dye them darker if you NEED to. I would never trust dying these. They are soft, but don't feel durable and the strips are really thin. They do feel like real hair, and they are soft and shiny and look very very real, but using heat styling products on them and coloring them I would NOT do it. Also, I would recommend buying 4 packs if you have thick hair like I do. I'm going to keep them since I can use spare parts and lengths to go with my several other extension sets, but these just are hard to blend with your hair because they are just thin. I plan to use these to add length to my hair if I plan to put it up or something also.

Overall, they are good extension to have but I wouldn't rely on them to be your "go to full head" set. And DON'T order one package. Be sure to get 2 or more depending on the thickness of your hair. Make sure you also take extra care of them since the quality seems appropriate but they aren't top quality like some of my other sets. For the price you can't complain but I just have such a familiarity with extensions that I wasn't overly impressed with these.

They came quickly and were packaged nicely. The plastic case is reusable with a snap to store them. They were true to the picture and they come with the clips already sewn in. The wefts themselves could use more hair, maybe a second strip sewn to the clips.

rating 5.0. BEST FOR DIY [[VIDEOID:2db16890ec8bb0f2d95c2e73d39a0ce8]] Video is finished look and pic is a week later!

This is definitely the best glue for DIY.
My eyes are extremely sensitive and water with any glue, when someone else does my lashes (so my eyes are closed and will water from the flumes). I don’t water at all with this glue and can keep my eyes opened. Throughout the week, I was able to fill in a sparse area or add more whenever I wanted to.
The retention is pretty good for home use. Obviously it’s not professional glue and won’t last 3-4 weeks. I want to say ~30% of them fell off by the end of the first week, but it still looks good overall and I can still go out. I also wash my eyes and face quite often and I do not avoid trying to get them wet (besides the first 24 hours).
It does take awhile for the glue to dry (6+ seconds). I usually dip the lash in glue, isolate a lash, and then put the lash on. Isolating the lash takes a couple of seconds and gives the glue time to dry. A lot of the time, I’ll still have to hold the lash up while it dries on mine. The process was hard at first, but I got really used to it.
And of course with all glues, you should do a spot test on your skin to see if you’re allergic before using it on your eyes. It looks like a lot of people assumed it would be okay since it says sensitive and their eyes burned.

I recommend everyone try this glue if they like to do their own lashes. I’ve tried many “sensitive” glues. Only this and the ardell individual glue do not burn my eyes, but this one definitely lasts longer.

It’s the best feeling waking up with lashes on and feeling pretty :)

rating 5.0. Love these! I was absolutely happy with these nail polishes! They are a metallic type finish but with the really cool chameleon color shift. They look different colors in different lighting. They can be used without magnet too but magnet gives them a very neat even more shifty effect. I do reccomend using a black or darker colored base coat for optimum effect, but you can also pull it off as is. You just need a few coats. Also, I recommend giving the bottle a little shake before opening and applying and holding the magnet over the nail for a few seconds to really get the magnetic effect. I like that it comes with 6 different colors. Some of them did look similar in the bottles but they are definitely different once you apply them. I will note that I did not have a proper magnetic wand but I used a regular round magnet I had at home and still got the magnetic effect. I would definitely reccomend these.

rating 5.0. Extends time between washes I don't wash my hair everyday.

As a matter of fact, I don't wash my hair AT ALL! Not with shampoo/conditioner, anyway. So when I'm getting to day 3-4 in between "washes" my roots get a little oily and my ends get a little dry. This brush eliminates that "you need to clean your hair" look! I like to section my hair and use this brush from roots to ends. It redistributes my hair's natural oils very well and leaves my hair looking soft and clean and even adds a little more volume. Great for extending the time between washes! I like to use a little dry shampoo on my roots after I brush through.

rating 2.0. More than just Boar I should have heeded the less than favorable reviews on this product.  Near as I can tell, this brush is not 100% boar bristle.  When I fist got the brush, I ran my hands over the bristles.  About ten of them fell out in the sink, they were un-naturally v shaped, folded in half.  A natural hair, cannot be made to hold this shape, unless this is its natural shape.  Last I checked, boar bristles are straight.  Then there was the burn test, hair should ignite and go to a soft grey ash.  This curled back from the flame into a ball, that when crushed was very black like charcoal.  The brush makes my hair go very flat to my head and have a lot of static when touched.  The rubber pad, could be contributing to this.  The wood handle is nice.  This may have some boar bristles, but this benefit is lost due to the "other" bristles.

rating 4.0. Glad I Purchased ~ This is an excellent hairbrush and I'm glad I purchased it. It had maybe a dozen bristles fall out when first used and then no more. The bristles (which do not have those horrendous balls on the end) are dense and leave my hair with zero static and a great shine. My scalp is massaged very well and the brush, with its wooden handle and dark bristles, is beautiful to see and display. My reason for not giving this brush 5 stars is that I would want the bristles to be a 1/16 inch longer, the brush handle to be about 1/2 inch longer so it's easier to hold with the paddle itself about 1/2 narrower. (I'm a woman with short hair which has changed texture as it has become 70% silver)

rating 5.0. worth every penny I am in the process of growing out my hair. I hated washing it so frequently, and when it became tangled constantly, I was at a loss. Finally my friends with long hair told me to get a boar bristle brush. Now, I really can't help BUT brush my hair when I am at home.

It makes my hair impossibly soft, manageable, and it really does evenly distribute the oil over my hair. I absolutely love this brush and I have no idea how I grew my hair so long and never got a brush like this.

rating 3.0. Disappointed User The first thing I noticed when I bought the brush was that it had a terrible smell. I hoped the smell would dissipate but it's been over a week and it still reeks. To make matters worse, the smell transfers to my hair.
With regard to how it brushes, I have to say I'm disappointed. The bristles only reach the very top layer of hair. I have very thick, waist-length hair and this brush just isn't working for me.
I didn't notice that it distributed that natural oils in my hair at all. I've had better luck in that department with the Tangle Teezer.
I'm disappointed so I'm going to return the product.

rating 5.0. Using a boar bristle brush requires a little research and practice to get it right. I've never used a boar bristle brush before. At first I was slightly dismayed at how short the bristles are. I didn't think they would get through my thickish wavy hair...and I was right. But I did a little research and found that they aren't supposed to. This type of brush is not intended for detangling. For that I use a very wide-toothed comb. What this brush is for is to distribute oils from the scalp down the length of the hair. This it does extremely well. The best way do accomplish this with my hair is to separate it into sections, and just brush one section at a time. I can't get over how fantastic my hair feels after brushing in the evening and all the next day. I am attempting to get away from shampooing my hair daily which has left my hair in worse and worse shape the longer it gets. So the brush gets five stars. And so does the service. The brush arrived well packaged and in good shape in a much shorter time than I expected.

rating 3.0. Everything's great except the smell The bristles and shape are as pictured, which is good. My only complaint is the smell. It doesn't transfer to the hair while brushing thankfully, but I get a whiff of it every time I pick it up to use it. I can handle it but I don't have any allergies or a super-sensitive nose, other's may find it too much to bear.

rating 4.0. Rethink your brush! It's a hairbrush that's made out of hair. Holy moly! Want to know what it's like to brush your hair with hair? Well buddy boy have I got a brush for you, it's this one! Its hair that the fine people at GranNaturals cleverly affixed to a piece of wood. But they didn't stop there, they then took that piece of hairy wood and carved a handle at the bottom. And talk about ergonomics, that sucker feels almost as good in your bald hand as it does on your hairy head! This isn't your grandpa's hairbrush, RIP. This is the new face of hair. Flat, lifeless hair? Buy this double hairbrush. Split ends and a receding hairline? That's entirely your fault and you deserve it chrome dome. Get the only/best/original GranNaturals hairy brush. And if that's not enough to convince you listen to this. Sick of all the brushes in your life. Who can keep track? Well throw out that old brush barrel and make the last brush purchase you'll ever make. Hair feels great on any hair. Heres a LLH(lady life hack) comb down from the top of your head with the GranNaturals Hairbrush Hairbrush, down the sideburns to the beard, over to the neck, around the back down the chest and straight down the "Happy Trail". Lord loves a hairy woman and a hairy woman loves a hairy brush.

rating 3.0. Don't look for a "grip" from these bands... offers added friction only. This does not keep a lace front from blowing away from your edges. This is not a substitute for glue. In the wind, sis, you will be embarrassed without something else to keep those edges down.

These bands make it a little harder for the wig to slide backwards but the wig has to be tight in the first darn place. It's really just added friction. You'll be disappointed if you buy this expecting a "grip".

The velcro closure is cheaply made, big and bulky but manageable. I'll use what I brought but am not sure about a repurchase. I'll update after I can gauge the durability of the bands.

rating 5.0. Very cool effects Where do I start to describe the dimensions of this nail polish?!  I've attached 4 pictures, 2 in natural light and 2 in the artificial light of my bathroom.  In artificial light, you can really see the 3D effects. In natural light, the chameleon color shift really shows. You get 6 colors in the set, all beautiful.  This set does not come with the magnet but, I already had one so it was no big deal. First,  I polished all my nails with a black base color, 2 coats, and I let it dry thoroughly. I found it was best to work with one nail at a time. Give the bottle a good shake each and every time just before you apply it to the nail. That way, the magnetic particles stay mixed because they tend to settle to the bottom of the bottle quickly.  After applying one coat, hold the magnet as close as possible to the nail for about 30 seconds. Then, apply your favorite top coat and hold the magnet over your nail for another 30 seconds before moving on to the next nail. The effects are gorgeous! Love these!

rating 5.0. Beautiful Nails at home In an effort to save money I began doing my nails at home.  I’ve had my eye on this set for sometime and I’m so glad I purchased. rating 5.0. Great variety set of magnetic multichrome polishes This set comes with 6 polishes, all different colors of multichrome shifts.
The shade names are Fairbanks Art, Lightning, Greenland Night, Aurora Borealis, The Gravity, and Astral Realm. I applied all of the polishes onto clear nail swatch sticks. I started with an opaque black base and did two coats of each of the polishes. I used a magnet I got from Born Pretty store over each of the coats. Afterwards I applied a topcoat and held the magnet over the topcoat for ~30 seconds per the instructions up above.
As far as the color descriptions, Fairbanks Art shifts between rose gold, yellow, and lime green at extreme angles. Lightning shifts between orange, yellow, and olive-y green. Greenland Night shifts between pale blue, purple, pink, yellow, and lime green. Aurora Borealis shifts between aqua, blue, purple, pink, and rose gold at extreme angles. The Gravity shifts between emerald green, pale blue, pink, and gold. Astral Realm shifts between purple/pink, rose gold, gold, yellow, and even a bit of green. I’d say my favorite out of the selection is Aurora Borealis.
The only downside of magnetic polish in general is that it is more layers than just throwing on a solid creme color. However, the effect is sure to impress. I wore it for the first time today and actually got a compliment on my nails which always feels great. :)

rating 5.0. Northern Lights ON MY NAILS! [[VIDEOID:b13de6121f6a1bdb45c0cf46a995ace5]] I purchased this product for the purpose of review.
I really loved the effect I got from this polish. It does take a bit of practice though, so, as a beginner, I wouldn't expect to get the same results as the promo pics.
The magnetic particles only react while the polish is wet so you have to be really fast and hold the magnet as close as you can to the nail, without touching. The first coat dries so fast you won't really see much result, but each layer sort of reactivates the previous one so the effect will get a little stronger each time. You MUST hold the magnet over it until each layer is dry to the touch. Then you need to use a top coat and it will really pop!
I am no pro at this so I couldn't get a crisp line but I think if I had a stronger magnet it would have been more detailed.

This set does not come with a magnet.

It took 19 days to arrive, which is typical for this type of item, coming from China.
The product packaging is very nice and the ingredients are listed, in English, on the back of the box. All items were also placed in individual zip lock bags and wrapped together, snugly, in foam sheeting for safe shipping.

I am very pleased with the quality of this product and the obvious care that was taken to ensure that my order arrived undamaged.

rating 5.0. They actually work! I absolutely love these!

I love that these are a polish and not a gel. I don't use gel on natural nails. Removes very well with pure acetone and reacts well with regular top coat.

Highly highly recommend for every nail lover out there trying to spice up their collection!

rating 4.0. Takes a little practice, but pretty in the end Very excited to get my first magnetic polishes. They came packaged very securely in foam and individually boxed. My first attempt didn’t go well, just couldn’t get it to work. Rather than constantly removing polish from own nails, I switched to pop sticks.

I started with a base coat of black. Once dry I painted one thick coat of the magnetic polish and immediately held the magnet over it, (in my experience it appears coat need to be thick and you need to work quickly) worked great!

So I attempted my own nails again, and it didn’t come out as well. I just couldn’t get as sharp of a line. It could be my nails are just to short with small nail beds, if the coat is to thick it floods my nails. I still love what I did get, when moving my fingers the line I did get appears to move, it’s very pretty and I got several compliments on them.

I did try using different magnets, stuff I had on my refrigerator as well as the Frcolor Double-head Stick Wand for 3 D Magnetic CAT Eye that I purchased on Amazon. It was a strong magnet, I had to really pull to get it off my fridge when I tested it. That magnet work best, As it was the strongest of what I had.

Overall, I like this polish and will enjoy using it.

rating 5.0. 10/10 Absolutely Beautiful Nail Polishes! I highly recommend these nail polishes! They are absolutely gorgeous and are great quality. The polishes are pigmented and smooth. Definitely worth the money! It may be a bit tricky for a beginner to use magnetic polishes, but it's worth the patience. The way that I like to use them is with gel polish. Here are the steps that I do: 1 coat of gel base coat, cure for 60 sec. 1 coat of black gel polish, cure for 60 sec. Apply 1 coat of the magnetic polish and while the magnetic polish is still wet, hold the stick magnet over your nail until the coat of magnetic polish is dry (for me that takes about 40-60 seconds, so you definitely want to go one nail at a time). For a bolder effect, do another coat of magnetic polish and repeat. Then I finish with a gel top coat, cure for 60 sec. The photo attached is of shade Aurora Borealis. I topped my ring finger with a scattered holo top coat to get an easy galaxy effect. I'm absolutely in love! I own a bunch of other Born Pretty nail art products and none of them have let me down yet!

rating 5.0. Very pretty cat eye effect! [[VIDEOID:5805559d998217b36ec87dea0c9e0296]] These Magnet cat eye polish are exactly as described. First of all MAKE SURE TO BUY  A MAGNET as these polishes don't provide one. Second of all you will need to apply a black or dark base for the effect to show. All in all I had a really great experience and can't wait to show off my mani!

rating 2.0. Weak construction, have repaired twice already This mirror arrived in perfect (we thought) condition. After just a day of use it fell apart. The nut on one side that connects the mirror to the stand continually becomes detached and the mirror falls off the stand. Luckily the mirror itself has not shattered or cracked (so far). I have been able to reattach the nut and reassemble the mirror. And then after just a few times the same thing happens. I have now applied "Loctite" to the threads and reassembled again. The problem seems to be with the nut assembly itself. The outside nut is metal (appears to be stainless steel) and the inside part it attached to is made of nylon. It appears to me that the nylon part is the weakness in this product as it flexes and gives way when the mirror is flipped from the magnifier side to the standard side and then the nut snaps off and the entire mirror falls from the stand. If the Loctite does nor solve the problem I am contacting the seller to replace or refund.[[ASIN:B01DYXQQBQ Ovente MPT85BR Multi Touch Tabletop Makeup Mirror with 3 Tone LED Light Option, 8.5 inch, 1x/10x Magnification, Brushed]]

rating 4.0. pretty great! before you even try out the product, toss the brush. it's too small to even be considered a full on eyeshadow brush.
however, the selection of colors is pretty great. they fit all skin tones. some are shimmery, some are satin, some are matte, and there's a couple with glitter flecks. all are very pigmented and last a long time. impressive options.
the blush does not go on very choppy, but it isn't as smooth as it could be. a bit of blending is necessary, but not enough to be a pain.
before purchasing, i would have liked to know that the palette is split in to 2 smaller palettes. while it isn't a big deal, it bothered me that in the photo they were trying to make it look like it was one large palette.
this palette set is DEFINITELY worth the price. both are small enough to fit in a purse, and contain a small thin mirror for touch ups.
upon arrival, the package was not damaged in any way. it even came 2 days before i expected! needless to say, i was pleased.

7th and Final: these would also be great as eye shadows. :)

rating 5.0. Love This! My sensitive skin absolutely loves Rejuve Naturals 100% Organic, Hexane  Free Castor Oil. It goes over my face smoothly  without leaving that "icky" feeling. rating 4.0. Test Small Amounts Sparingly First I decided to purchase some castor oil at the recommendation of a friend to promote hair/lash growth.  The order was prompt and packaged well.  I love the bottle that it comes in, and it serves as a bonus cute decoration in my bathroom.  I applied the oil to my top and bottom lashes with a q-tip at night to promote hair growth.  After a few days of this, I noticed that I started to develop several chalazions in my eyelids which form when oil glands in the eyelid become blocked/clogged.  I also tested a little bit out on my face as a moisturizer and noticed that a small pimple formed the next day.  After giving the regimen a rest, I have slowly begun to try to apply it again and it doesn't seem as though any new chalazions are forming, so maybe it was purely coincidental.  I kept the four stars because the quality of the oil is good and it seems to be a fair price for what you get.  I would just encourage anyone to use sparingly and test small areas of skin first before liberally applying anywhere.

input:
rating 5.0. Volume, definition, without lots of product - multiple days of fabulous looking wavy hair! I've never used a diffuser until now and I can't believe what I've been missing out on. My hair dried so fast, with a fraction of the amount of product I usually use, and was not frizzy at all. It was bouncy and beautiful and I got so many compliments on my hair the first day I used it. I usually let my hair air dry with a bunch of product in it but in NYC Winters - that just doesn't work. And between not really using any product and the bounce and definition it gave me, I took a nap mid day and my hair still looked great! AND, I never ever get a second day out of my curly hair without washing it again. It still looked good when I woke up the next morning.

Aside from that, it's well constructed, fits easily on my dryer, and Iove the feel of the soft silicone. So glad it's collapsible because this will be coming on my many trips with me. Super happy with this product! And because it's so affordable, all my friends and family with curly hair will be getting one as a gift this year.

rating 5.0. This Diffuser Works Wonders! I don't have the curliest hair, but this diffuser has done serious wonders for these locks! Seriously. It is so easy to slip on my blow dryer, and it stays put! My hair was dry in no time with next to NO frizz! I cannot tell you how happy that makes me!! Plus I loved that little Curly Co. Guide - so cute and such helpful tips! Can't wait to get more Curly Co. products! #frizzfreelife

rating 5.0. I honestly wasn't 100% sure that I would love it. It has absolutely exceeded ALL of my ... When I ordered this diffuser, I honestly wasn't 100% sure that I would love it. It has absolutely exceeded ALL of my expectations! I couldn't be more in love with it now!

I needed a diffuser that would be easy to store in my small bathroom and just as easy to take with me when I travel. This one fits the bill. It has fit every blow dryer that I've tried it on, and it's so easy to get on and off. The best part is my hair has honestly never looked better. I've never gotten such great results from a hair diffuser before! Can't wait to see what other products The Curly Co. comes up with!

- Russell's wife :)

rating 5.0. Great for travel!! I've been looking a long time for a good diffuser to take when I travel.  Most were way too large and never fit the hotel hair dryers, and the sock diffusers never worked well for me.  This collapsible one is great!  I couldn't believe that it actually fit my small travel hairdryer, it was a little too snug at first, so on the inside of the rubber rim I trimmed off a little of the black rubber tabs and it fit perfectly!  I especially like that the diffuser nozzle is rubbery and clings to the end of the hairdryer instead of shooting off the hair dryer like a rocket. I'm also thrilled that it's collapsible and doesn't take up a lot of room in my travel bag.  This was definitely a good purchase  and one that I will get a lot of use out of on my travels!!

rating 5.0. I love the way this made my hair not as frizzy I love the way this made my hair not as frizzy!!! The o my downside is when it gets hot it does tend to collapse a little bit not a huge issue.... All in all it's pretty good!!!! ****

rating 2.0. I wanted to like this... I really wanted this to work for me as I love to travel and this diffuser would take up much less space than my regular one. Alas, it isn't going anywhere with me.

This unit suffers from the same issue as others of its kind: it collapses too easily against your head when you're trying to use it. The merest touch against your hair causes it to buckle, so you have very little control over the diffuser. Additionally, the "fingers" that protrude from the bottom don't protrude nearly far enough, nor are there enough of them, to adequately hold/lift the curls or to help separate them into a soft, piece-y style. The diffuser DID diffuse the air stream from the dryer, but it didn't facilitate styling like my regular one does. Lastly, the opening that fits over the dryer barrel is very small, so it wouldn't fit over 3 of 4 dryers I tried. (I'm also in the market to replace my dryer.)

I hope someone does come up with a workable travel diffuser. Unfortunately, for me anyway, this one doesn't cut it.

rating 5.0. Works well, stays on the blow dryer without falling off. So far I am enjoying the Curly Co. collapsible diffuser. This fits nicely on my hair dryer and collapses when I want to store it, and I don't even remove it from the dryer. The diffuser works well and my hair seems to be fluffier ,with defined curls,  which is always my goal. I look forward to being able to take this with me when I travel, so that is a nice bonus.

rating 4.0. makes sense, and worth it!!! i gotta, say, before buying this i saw all the other reviews saying how terrible it was being flimsy. well, i'll admit it's not the sturdiest of diffusers BUT, you gotta consider the fact that it IS made out of silicone AND collapsible. those two combined are not necessarily the strongest. THAT BEING SAID, i dont have a problem with it because i'm not pressing my diffuser up to my scalp to let it do its job. only if your roughly diffusing (which i don't know why that's necessary) will you have a problem with the flimsiness.

in all honesty, i don't think its weak at all. it's great for travel, does the job, and very well made. a very good purchase in my opinion.

rating 5.0. amazing...totally blew me away.i was very skeptical.but...glad I tried it & yes I use the diffuser upside down 4 seconds This stuff is totally amazing, I have used the most expensive sulphate free shampoo/conditioners on my hair, nothing, nothing has ever came close to this.
I have long color treated layered curly/wavy very healthy hair, thick & coarse, NOT DRY.I use this a nickel size or quarter size, huge amt of suds, same with conditioner..
I am getting the curl cream, & I am going to try the mild hydrating shampoo/conditioner.to change up...the sea salt spray has always been an issue, it's junk.this VERB sea salt spray is amazing, wash/condition, small amt of curl whatever, quick spritz of sea salt spray, upside down for 4 seconds with hair, LEAVING IT WET IS A MUST FOR ME WHEN PUTTING A PRODUCT IN, I FLIP MY HEAD BACK & DONE.
VOILA..I AM SERIOUSLY THINKING OF GIVING AWAY MY FAV 2 PRICE SULPHATE FREE SHAMPOOS..THE SECRET TO THIS STUFF.NO PARAGONS.MAYBE ALSO GLUTEN, PARAGON COATS YOUR HAIR, Makes it not shiny.
try a small size .hope you love it as much as me :)

rating 5.0. Best product I’ve found in years! (But maybe buy through different seller) I love this product. Sometimes paraben free, sulfate free products leave my hair dull and dry, but not Verb Ghost products. My hair has transformed from dry to silky since I’ve been using it (4 months now) ...and my hairdresser asked what I’d been doing differently! It has a very light, pleasant fragrance (it does have added fragrance so if you are looking for completely chemical free this might not be for you), and feels rich and moisturizing. Highly recommend.

Update: Last time I ordered through this seller the product did not come in a box, and instead the bottles were TAPED together at the top, clearly used. Eew.

rating 3.0. You Get What You Pay For.... My hematite magnetic bracelet arrived today, one day sooner than expected which is a good thing. The early arrival is the only reason this product got an extra star, I would have only given it 2 stars, not 3, but the early arrival helped saved its rating. I know the old adage "you get what you pay for" and I guess I will have to learn to deal with that in regards to this bracelet. Don't get me wrong, it's attractive, very masculine and has an awesome color. But the quality of it all, it would have been nice for them to put a couple more hematite pieces onto the bracelet. I'm not a very big guy, my family jokes about my girl looking wrists all the time. But even with my petite wrists the black stretchy bands that make up the bracelet are showing. I have like a half inch gap of missing material, meaning a half an inch of my skin and those two black cords are showing! It would have been great for them to put at least two more of those block shaped pieces and maybe 4 more pearls into the bracelet, then I wouldn't have this embarrassing gap on the back side of the bracelet. Oh well, I will wear it from time to time, but not as many times as I was was planning to before making this purchase.

(If you got big wrists, this bracelet may not be for you, choose another one from Amazon.com, maybe one this is bigger!)
