input: rating 5.0. This curler is Wonderful! I never use eyelash curlers much because mine are so short. I always pinch myself, which is very unpleasant to say the least. With this curler I had no problem what so ever hitting every last and reaching the base, while missing my eyelid. Great product all around! Would definitely recommend this to my friends as a must have.

rating 5.0. WOW! I have never had an eyelash curler make this much difference! I have to admit...I did not use a curler regularly because I never saw the difference. Well now I know why. I have inset eyes so my curler wouldn't even fit right. I could only grasp the very end  of my lashes and in the end I couldn't even notice any difference. But that is certainly not the case with this one! I love it! Even my husband noticed the difference! Very happy!

rating 5.0. Best Curler I've Used I just received this, and already LOVE it! I was previously using a Sephora brand curler that just did not do the job. I have short, straight eyelashes that don't hold a curl without a coat of waterproof mascara. My last curler barely got them curled and it was really difficult to get the right angle without pinching my eyelid. This curler is GREAT. It feels really sturdy and seems to be pretty good quality. I'm interested to see how long it curls my eyelashes well since it says I should never need to replace the pad on it. Received it on time and in the cute box, pictured. So far, I'm loving it!

rating 5.0. Best eyelash curler i have ever used First let me tell you about my lashes. My lashes stick straight out and the ones on the outer corners curve down :( and I have small eyes so it seems the other eyelash curlers that I have used never got my eyelashes on my outer corners very well. I have used lots of different eyelash curlers and they have the same problem which is they did not curl my lashes on the outer corner and the ones that did get curled everywhere else only held the curl for a few seconds so I would have to immediately apply my mascara. I got the Bella and Bear curler yesterday at work and I had to use it as soon as I got the package, I opened the pretty box the curler comes in and I took it to the bathroom I did not have any mascara with me but I really wanted to see how good it curled my lashes. After I used it on one eye there was a huge difference, it curled every eyelash even the ones on the outer corners then I did my other eye it came out beautifully also and sense I did not have any mascara with me I could not put any on so I decided to see how long the curl would hold. I checked 30 minutes later and the curl was still there and not only was it still there it was a much better curl then any other curler had given me even right after I would use them. When I got home I decided to see how it looked with mascara and it was the best my eyelashes have ever looked. In fact my 6 year old has very pretty dark curly lashes and when she seen me after I curled and applied mascara with the Bella and Bear she said mom your eyelashes look so pretty like my eyelashes.
When I seen this curler on Amazon I was hesitant to buy it because of the price and I was not sure if it would really work like the description and reviews said but I was so tired of how my eyelashes were before so I decided to give it a try and I am so happy that I did. I will never use any other curler again.

rating 4.0. Not the best curler--but close I bought this one because I have deep set eyes and I cannot get most eyelash curlers to actually reach my lashes.  They are too curved.  I bought this one because it did not look as curvy as most. It works and it works really well.  I gave it four stars rather than five for three reasons--it does not come with a replacement pad, it is overpriced and pinches.  I have had other curlers with that black silicone pad and they do not last forever.  For the price, there should be some way to replace the pad.  It does pinch a bit too- even though it is advertised as no pinching.  The best working eyelash curler I have used was a small cheap pink plastic fold up one--but it did not last very long.
rating 2.0. Two Stars does not curl any better than my revlon eyelash curler.

rating 2.0. got my hopes up, but not my lashes :( I wanted to believe all the hype, but alas, this was no good for me. It didn't curl my lashes any better than the drugstore model.

I have very straight and somewhat sparse lashes -- they do take mascara very well though (too faced better than sex is a great buildable mascara, btw)...if only I could get the lashes to curl up they'd be pretty good all things considered. This curler got my hopes up but not the lashes -- in a side by side comparison my old curler (I think it's a Revlon from CVS) did as good if not a little better. Oh well :(

rating 1.0. Overpriced curler, not worth it, doesn't work for my lashes... I was drawn to this eyelash curler because of all of the perfect reviews (at the time about 10 five star reviews). In hindsight, I should have been wary as they were SO enthusiastic about the product---too good to be true, right? The curler itself seems sturdy and yes it came in a "cute" box but the function is lacking. They curl my lashes after a very hard press and even after "pumping" the curler a few times, once I apply mascara, my lashes are straight again. And this is with Benefit's They're Real! mascara, which can give my lashes a slight curl even without using a curler first. I even tried curling them post-mascara, but to no avail. Disappointed with this curler, especially at its price point-- will go back to my old trusty Shu Uemura again, and toss this one into the bin.

rating 1.0. Broken eyelashes I loved this the first few days I used it and then it happened, my eyelashes broke right in the middle of my eyelid. I also noticed that the part with the pad on it is loose and wobbly. Very disappointed after reading all the great reviews.

rating 3.0. save your money eh curls like a $2 curler....only difference is that the spring leaves curler open and they seem harder to press. btw i did get pinched and i have long eyelashes. save your money. given too much credit and over priced for what it does tbh

rating 5.0. I didn't have high hopes that this curler would be any better than all of the fancy expensive brands of curlers ... I am a lash aficionado.  I don't leave the house without curling my lashes and putting on mascara..  I didn't have high hopes that this curler would be any better than  all of the fancy expensive brands of curlers I've owned, which  always left me with  2 problems.  One- they never got all of my lashes in one attempt (especially the outer corners) and two- they always crimped my lashes instead of curled, leaving me with wonky lashes going every which way.  This curler  is  AMAZING.  It gives the most beautiful curl (not crimp!)  perfectly even across every single lash that stays all day and it gets every lash from the inner corner to the outer lashes in one attempt.  It is exceptionally well made and the spring-load makes it much sturdier than the other high end brands I've tried.  I've only had it a few weeks so i don't know how long it will hold up without a replacement pad, but i will happily pay to replace this if I ever lose it.  If you think all lash curlers are the same, try this one and you will definitely see that they are not.  This is a superior product.

rating 3.0. Worth a try Doesn't seem to be any better than a a standard eyelash curler. I was seeking a replacement for my beloved Smashbox curler (since apparently Smashbox doesn't make them anymore!), and hoped this would wow me, but I didn't notice any real major curl going on. Pretty much the same so-so results I get from a Revlon curler. Bella and Bear really do stand behind their product, though - as soon as I contacted them to let them know, they issued a full refund. Impressive. So would definitely give this product a try - there are a lot of other positive reviews for this curler. Maybe my eyelashes are just stubbornly loyal to Smashbox! I'd definitely be willing to try Bella & Bear products again, based on my overall experience.

rating 3.0. Could be better I bought it after the great reviews, but this doesn't curl my eyelashes as well as if I were to get a eyelash curler for $3 at a makeup or clothing store. I was really expecting a good eyelash curler but this just didn't do my long eyelashes any justice. Its strong and doesn't pinch my eyelashes as much, but after three crimps, and mascara, it just doesnt seem to hold the curl as long as I would like.

rating 1.0. I would love to have loved this eyelash curler so charmingly raved ... I would love to have loved this eyelash curler so charmingly raved about and packaged, but I can't. As a matter of fact, after curling my eyelashes with over-the-counter eyelash curlers for over 40 years, this is the first curler that actually ripped my eyelashes out!!! I didn't put mascara on before curling. I didn't pull on them. I wasn't pushed. I didn't feel any pain. The lashes are and were my own - not false or artificial. I used the curlers exclusively once I had bought them. They performed about as well as the previous curlers that I had probably bought at Walgreen's or maybe even the 99 Cent Store. So I wasn't thrilled, but I'd grin and bear it if they hadn't done any damage, but they did. I noticed that a chunk of lashes were missing in the center of both swathes of lash on both eyelids. What? Yes.  Cut to the quick as though chopped by a scissors to about 1/4" when my eyelashes are probably 3/4" to an inch long. They're just beginning to grow out. I'm being patient. Am told by a professional make-up artist that it will take a while. With 365 days a year of curling my lashes almost every day - sometimes more than once a day - I'm nearing somewhere in the vicinity of 10,000 curlings. Do the math. I did. I hope I didn't throw them out since I just noticed the money back guarantee. Good luck and be careful.
rating 3.0. Three Stars This doesn't seem to curl very well.

rating 2.0. Its a sturdy eyelash curler Its a sturdy eyelash curler, but honestly it doesn't curl very well. I find myself having to squeeze the curler extremely hard to get a little lift. Once I add mascara the curl falls.

rating 2.0. Too straight I bought this curler based on reviews, however, I can't say that I am satisfied with it. I find the curve of the curler too...straight? Not sure how to describe it, but whereas for one of the reviewers, it grabs corner hairs, on me, it misses them. I feel like I can't get close enough to my lid to have the curl be in the appropriate spot. I switched the curler with my daughter's to see what she would say and she doesn't like it either. She ended up bending the metal to make it curvier, so it is better, but not great. I do not recommend that you try bending the metal...i think she was just lucky.

rating 2.0. Wanted it to work for me... I've been using this curler for the last few weeks & as much as I wanted to love it, I don't. The curler itself is very well made, it is durable, the spring works great, & the finger grips make it easy to grip without fear of it slipping. The only design flaw that I've found is that the rubber pad that curls my lashes already has a slit in it from where the metal & rubber meet. The other issue which is why it doesn't work for me is the curve of the actual curler. It is very wide which, but the curve of the curler is too strait for my eyes. I have almond shaped eyes & I guess a slightly smaller eye socket. In order to get all of my lashes in the curler I have to press the curler into my eyelid & then adjust the angle to get them in a few times to get the curler close enough to the base of my lashes. If I only had to press the curler lightly on my eyelid that would be one thing, but I have to really press & almost flatten out my eyelid for it to work. Even after that I get maybe a creased "curl". My lashes are quite long & bone strait & even pumping the curler a few times up my lashes doesn't give much of a curl. By the time I put on mascara they are almost strait again. I've been using curlers for 20 some odd years now & honestly the drug store ones work better for me personally.

If you have short lashes & more oval eyes this may be a great curler for you & I do recommend it for the quality. Good luck!

rating 1.0. Cuts off your eyelashes!! Do not buy I purchased this eyelash curler four months ago and it worked great. A little more curl than I typically like, but it was fine. Then, a few days ago, part of the black plastic pad on the curler seemed like it was a little cut -which seemed odd, but I didn't pay a lot of attention. Until I saw that I had cut off 1/3 of my lashes on each eye! Seriously! About 1/2 way to my lid. I couldn't believe it but there's no question that it was this eyelash curler that cut them. Steer clear! I had to buy fake lashes so I don't look like a freak....

current_product_reviews:
rating 4.0. The good, the bad and the ugly... My review consists of the good, the bad and the ugly. First,
the good - I finally found a curler that 'fits' my eyelid. Huh, you say? Well, it appears that most of the manufacturers believe I have golf balls instead of human eyes. The tool's shape is long and shallow enough of a curve to catch most of the eyelashes. It is comfortable to use, but takes a bit of practice. Once you are comfortable with the scissor motion (sorry, no other way to describe it) used to make the curl, you are rewarded with a nice, non-crimpy curl. The pad in this one is the best I have used. This tool also opens wide enough that even the most nearsighted or lush lashed amoungst us can get it close to the base of the upper lashes. The design over all is very well thought out. I recommend using an magnifying mirror if you have to use bifocals (I do). All that said, now...
the bad - there is no replacement pad and I am questioning how sanitary this is going to be in the long run. I agree completely with folks here who advise to REMOVE ALL EYE MAKEUP before use. Ain't nothing pretty about an eye infection. I am wondering out loud how to properly clean this curler to be certain it remains safe. If you are prone to sties and other types of infection, I would be asking the same questions. Which leads to...
the ugly - until you practice and are used to it (won't take long) the results are a bit odd looking. The black pad is plushier and looks to be made of silicone and not the usual hard rubber. I have found that the hard rubber pads are chiefly responsible for that weird ugly crimp you can get. Not so with this one. I mentioned before the squeezy scissor action your thumb and index finger have to use can be tricky. If you have coordination issues, well - things could get ugly.
SUGGESTIONS for use...wash your hands before use. I keep the little plastic clip to keep the tool closed. You could follow the advise of one person here to heat the tool with a dryer, but I think I may skip that. Use a makeup mirror to assist you. Practice when you have some time. Enjoy, Ladies - get out there and be lovely.

rating 5.0. The most economical treatment for dogs with recurring skin problems My older Maltese has recurring skin problems for many years. The cause was common bacteria that occur everywhere but my dog lacks the robust immune system to fight it off. So every time there is breakout, the vet gave her antibiotics, steroids as treatment. The dog would recover but only to have another breakout 3 months later. Not only the vet bills added up, it is also bad for the dog to repeatedly taking antibiotics and steroids over long term.
The active ingredient of this this antibacterial soap for human is the same as medicated shampoo for dogs sold by the vet, but at 1/5 the price. I tried it after giving her a regular puppy shampoo bath first then sprayed over her body except the area around the eyes. Gave it a good 5 min rubbing and rinsed off. On the third day, I could see significant improvement. The rashes and redness was gone in a week. No more antibiotics and steroids, it is a life saver.

rating 5.0. Help yourself & your loved ones fight off germs and dangerous infections! I CANNOT LIVE WITHOUT MY HIBICLENS!!! A few years ago, my then boyfriend was set to jail. When he came home, he had horrible boil like, red, inflamed bumps under his arm pits. He eventually went to the doctors & was diagnosed with MRSA!!! Well, being that we shared the same bed, I then ended up with MRSA.  I got it treated and it went away.  But, a few months later, it came back out of nowhere! This same cycle happened about 3 or 4 times. Finally, I was referred to an infectious diseases physician.  He put me on a super strong antibiotic & heartily recommended that I begin to use this soap when simply washing my hands, as well as all over my body while taking my shower.  This soap is amazing!  It has kept the MRSA away from me & my loved ones for a few years now. When I first began using it, after I ran out, there was a lapse of time before I got more. In that short amount of time that I did not have Hibiclens to use, I  had a flare up of MRSA. Ever since then, I make sure that my household NEVER runs out of this liquid gold.  Now, while it's not the best smelling product, it's definitely not the worst.  And for the piece of mind & protection it provides my loved ones & myself, I wouldn't care if it smelled ultra horrible because the benefit & safety it provides greatly outweigh any unpleasant fragrance it may have.  I have also found that the larger the volume of Hibiclens you purchase, the cheaper it is per ounce.  Lastly, I have come across some very competitive prices for this product here on Amazon.

rating 5.0. Effective, but can cause permanent eye damage. I used Hibiclens while I had an active Staph (MRSA) skin infection. Hibiclens, in addition to 10 minute bleach baths, gave me peace of mind that I could sanitize myself and not risk transmiting the infection to others.

Please read the directions very carefully!!! This product can cause PERMANENT eye damage if it enters the eyes and is not immediately flushed out.

It is not meant for long term use over large areas of the body. After the infection responded to treatment (debridement, followed by oral antibiotics, Bactrim and Clyndamycin simultaneously) and stopped oozing, I switched from Hibiclens to regular antibaterial liquid handsoap from the grocery store. Those products contain benzalkonium chloride, which has been shown to be effective against MRSA.

Hibiclens is a highly effective product, but it contains powerful active ingredients, and must be used with caution.

rating 5.0. Best. Lotion. Ever. I love this lotion! I ordered the salve 2 years ago when I had contact dermatitis on my hand from using a very strong cleaning spray. Nothing helped the dermatitis until I tried the salve and it cleared it up in less than 24 hours! 2 months ago I had a bad case of cellulitis on my chest was put on 3 different types of VERY STRONG antibiotics-they didn't work! I thought of the Fay Farm salve and remembered they also had a lotion and ordered it. Within DAYS the cellulitis started to ckear up. I had even had a skin biopsy taken, 3 doctors were concerned it was cancer when they 3 antibiotics did nothing. This lotion is PURE GOLD... After moving to a northern state this summer my hands are so dry and cracked my fingertips were cracking open by my nails--so painful! I'm now slathering this lotion on my hands. And I also had a head cold a couple weeks ago--my nose was so red I started putting the lotion on my nose at night before I got in bed--and my nose didn't peel! My husband isn't crazy about the smell if the lotion but I LOVE it!  When I ordered the lotion I also ordered another tin of the salve--also amazing stuff!

rating 4.0. Revised review If anyone read my review that said these were too tight, I apologize. That review was for a different set of headbands, which I accidentally put here. So forthwith is my review for these headbands.

Firstly, they are decidedly not tight. They are really very comfy.

Secondly, they took forever to get here. I ordered them April 24, and they finally arrived on July 23. Talk about a slow boat from China. So if you need them in a hurry, you are out of luck.

I am not convinced that they are very sturdy or will last long. The very first one I took out of the bag fell apart in my hand. Apparently, it had not been sewn well (or sewn at all). I thought about trying to sew it back together, but I saw that it also had no piece of elastic, and I was just holding two separate twisted pieces of material, and neither my sewing skills, nor my spatial relationship skills (I couldn't figure out how it was supposed to go together) are all that great. I wouldn't have felt so bad if it weren't one of the few colorful ones in the pack - the green one. The headbands seem to be what is pictured in the listing. It would have been nice if they had more colorful materials - these seem to be on the dark side, even the flowered ones. (Those aforementioned tight ones had gorgeous patterns compared to these). The rest in the package all stayed together when I stretched them a little, but somehow they still felt a little fragile.

But they are a good value, and are so comfortable that I am sure I will wear them more than the prettier/tight ones, at least until I feel safe going to the hairdresser again.

input: rating 5.0. New favorite This is my new favorite deodorant! It smells amazing and I love that there's no aluminum

rating 5.0. Best from MANY Aluminum free deodorants We all heard that Aluminum deodorants were related to cancer..... So I started looking for Aluminum free deodorants and it was hard.... Tried Toms, Alba Botanica, and others... They all smelled bad, or did not smell at all, but mainly - did nothing. I could smell sweat even if using them 5 mins earlier.

Nivea does not help with moisture much but it smells great, leaves no white spots and very gentle! Trust me, it is hard to find a perfect one but Nivea did it right! Love it! Going to buy another one for my gym bag.

rating 4.0. sweet floral for me This does last a really long time.  Unfortunately, I don't care for the smell - too sickly, sweet floral for me.  I'm really sensitive to smells though.  If they came out with a different scent, I'd definitely give it a try. I gave it 4 stars because it's so hard to find a really good non-aluminum deodorant.  This works well.

rating 5.0. Fresh Pure scent is terrific! Personally, I prefer the deodorant-only products that are easy to wash off, whereas I find that antiperspirants seem to stick to me in the shower and have to be scrubbed off or else they build up over time. On me, antiperspirants also darken my armpits.

Keep in mind if you are ordering [[ASIN:B00NQ2CCOQ Genuine Authentic German Nivea Deodorant Fresh Pure Aluminum Free 1.69 fl.oz / 50ml]] deordorant, it is *just* a deodorant, and *not* an antiperspirant. Which means your armpits can sweat. Which means that while *you* won't smell (because you are wearing a deodorant), your clothes *will* after the sweat dries on them. So you will only get a single wearing out of a shirt if you sweat, but my clothes are washable, so that is OK with me. If I had to pay for dry-cleaning, I might have a different opinion.

I've tried this scent (Fresh Pure) and another scent (Fresh Natural) and the Fresh Pure scent is terrific. The other one I did not like, although another reviewer did, so your experience may vary.

rating 5.0. What a find!!!! Works as well as antiperspirant without a strong smell!! This is seriously the millionth non-aluminum deoderant that I've tried, all of them failing and falling short of working... until now! I realize that everyone's body chemistry is different, but the smell of this is not over powering, as some non-antiperspirants can be, and it still actually WORKS!!! Maybe even a BETTER! It doesn't over dry the skin surrounding my arm pits like the antiperspirants (Nivea included) tend to do, and doesn't leave me even the slightest bit sticky or sweaty feeling, even after physical activity like skiing and working out. I think I just found my forever product!!

rating 4.0. It works, but not for 48 hours!!!! I was not very sure about this deodorant, but I bought based on the reviews. I sweat, I stink. The only deodorant without aluminum that would work temporally, not making me stink was Milk of Magnesia, however, once you sweat you have to wash and reapply again. It works, but the white stuff gets on your clothes and makes a mess. This one does not last 48hrs for sure, however, it does last until I start sweating on my workout. So I reapply a little bit more, before working out and holds well. My 12 years-old daughter, it is a mini-stinky-me, and it works pretty well for her and she is happy, because she does not want to have aluminum on her deodorant! I would recommend. It has a nice scent, and if you are not all day outdoors in a high humid environment such as in South Florida, you will like the results!

rating 5.0. Has a fresh, clean smell and works. This deodorant has a fresh, clean smell and it works! The shipping takes a little while, so I make sure to order a replacement ahead of time. rating 5.0. Wonderful Product This is a great product especially if you are concerned about your health. At first, I didn't like the smell as I am not big on floral scents (I'm more of a fruit person), however, I grew to like the scent very much.  This product rolls on which is wonderful because it reminds me of my childhood.  If you are wondering if the scent will be too powerful or what not because of headaches or sensitive sinuses, it's not too overpowering if you only use a little which is all you really need.  Plus you can use this also as a perfume because otherwise, the smells might clash.  I find myself thinking of honeysuckle flowers if anyone knows what that is.  It's strong at first but after a second or two a sweetness will come into the mix.  This product dries very quickly and works very well unless your extremely busy and sweating or in the middle of a heat wave then you may find yourself reapplying.  The only problem I had was I live in the U.S. and I believe this product was shipped here from Germany and I believe it took about two months to get here.  I was starting to worry I had wasted money but I think that it was well worth it!  Also, this travels very well.  Of course, this is a glass container and you should be more careful than other containers, but it's made really well and I don't have to worry if I drop my bags a little harder than I should.  In short, wonderful product!

rating 4.0. 3-peat customer with no issues. I'm allergic to aluminum so I have made my way through dozens of aluminum free deodorants and antiperspirants. Some I've liked and some I've hated but this German Nivea, glass bottle, is legit. Most all natural deodorants can't carry me through my day and into the gym but this one can. With past brands I've told my friends at the gym, "I'm using hippy deodorant today so if I smell like Pig Pen- that's why." Another bonus is that it's clear so I've never had an issue of it staining my clothes.

This will be my 3rd purchase so I'm curious to see what I get because the first two were not the same label. Both glass bottles but different scents. I could really care less because they both worked the same and smelled pleasant.

Just like I thought. I received my 3rd order today and they get a 4 of 5 just because I've never got the same labeling. Weird.

rating 5.0. Great deodorant, I wish they would sell them in the US This is one of the best non-aluminum deodorants that I've tried. Delivery was great, took about two weeks, packaged nicely. The scent is fresh and light, sweet flower-y, very pleasant. These scents seem to be popular in Western Europe, since I just came back from my vacation in Europe and I was surprised with similar scents in local soap and shampoo. I did not see anything similar to this in the U.S. yet.

Deodorant lasts all day. It does provide decent sweat protection, but you will get sweaty if you are going to work out or stay outside during hot summer day. The scent is very long lasting, it will definitely last throughout the day.

I thought it was like the regular threads you sometimes find in human hair bundles but it's like the hair strand starts out black and halfway it's bright red. I just don't like persons lying about what their product actually consists of, hence the 3 stars. The curly picture was after having rods in it overnight. Cracked in 2 days of receiving i purchased all 3 Torino brushes.  hard (red), medium (blue), soft (teal).  within 2 days  of receiving the brushes, the soft brush cracked down the back and small stress cracks formed along the bristle holes.  I am very disappointed and perplexed.  I cannot explain why this happened to a brand new brush.

rating 5.0. Best Brushes on market I’ve been a waver for some time now using wave enforcer brushes, original diane brushes, crown brushes exc. but these torino pros are awesome. my wave depth has improved after using for a week. the soft brushes bristles do a fantastic job laying your frizz down. a must need brush after a 1.5 cut and great finisher brush after a good brush session. the curve is good for maintaining your connections and the pointed tip is great for working in and around your crown area. what impresses me the most is the paint job on this brush and the print on the handle doesnt look like it will fade for a long time. this is an outstanding product and the price is very reasonable.
rating 5.0. My soldier loves it, so I love it!! My soldier loves the brush... He's getting waves!!!
rating 5.0. Five Stars One of my favorite brushes

rating 5.0. Want a quality brush? Just get a brush from Brush King and You will be happy with it! As a coarse hair waver is difficult to find a brush with noticeable pull but the Torino Pro line is a excellent choice. My orders arrived on time and in great condition. This one came with a very small chip probably from shipping but it’s nothing major or anything to complain about. I love these brushes. Keep producing quality products Brush King and you will have a repeat customer in me.

rating 4.0. My Go To Soft Brush This is a medium soft brush. When I first recieved it I found it difficult to distinguish between this and my medium Torino Pro. For the medium version I  wrote a review even though I purchased it direct. Comparing them side to side for firmness of the bristles was a bit eye opening for me. Perhaps I just can’t personally distinguish but, it’s still a good brush. It’s just doesn’t seem as soft as I had imagined it would be.
rating 2.0. brush Had to return original brush because of peeling after two days and bristles were too soft and falling out.

rating 5.0. Worked just as expected. I bought both this soft brush and the blue medium brush. I had previously been using the Palm Annie brushes faithfully. I realized that I could not get my crown to where I wanted it to be with those brushes. I had already been well informed on wave products since I’ve been a consumer of the YouTube wave community since soul seeka was top dog. After seeing the endorsements by some of my most frequently visited wave channels, I decided to give these brushes a try. Needless to say, I’ve only been using for about a week and my waves are already at another level. My swirl has made tremendous progress over this past week also. I rate both of these products a 5 out of 5. And I recommend all serous wavers to purchase at least one of these brushes.

rating 2.0. Paint chips off of brush The bristles to this brush were ok but I will not buy this again. I bought three of these brushes the hard, medium and soft. On all the of them the paint chipped on the brush a lot! These are just about the most expensive wave brushes on the market so I expected more. The company should be ashamed. It chipped after only 4 months.
rating 5.0. Perfect Perfect for my tender scalp.

rating 5.0. Perfect Soft Bristle Brush Great coverage, perfect curve, great bristle quality. I swear by Brush King! It’s really fully of bristles so it feels closer to a medium but definitely a soft brush. A little big for a travel brush but it’s a must have!

rating 5.0. I Can’t Stop Brushing!!! I actually tried to not like this brush. Being an OG waver since early 2000’s, I was stuck in my ways thinking ANY decent brush was good enough for your waves. Maaannnnnn listen, I’m glad my girl put me on to these. Quality is fire, the curve makes it easy to hit all my angles, the brush is super soft but it’s still strong enough to get down in your new growth even if you’re wolfing. The tip makes it easy to get my crown together too. I noticed a difference in my waves in just two days of use, no bs. Overall this is probably the best brush I’ve ever owned. I’ve already bought a couple more and I’m sure my collection will grow over time. Only negative is because it’s so big, it’s hard for me to take it everywhere(and trust me, I want to take it EVERYWHERE).

If they tried to pay me to write this review, I would have given them their money back and still wrote it.

rating 5.0. You Need This In Your Rotation!! For my semi coarse wavers... this is a must buy! rating 5.0. Great product! This stuff is great for Making Cannabis Tincture. Great price for a gallon anywhere online and also anywhere in store. I filled 3 huge jars and still have a bunch left for next time. This is also Kosher.

My tincture instructions:

Fill jars with leaf matter
Fill with Glycerine Tincture
Leave a little space at the top.

Screw on Caps. Mark dates on top of caps.

Leave for 60 Days.

Put jars in the over for 20-60 Minutes on 100 degrees Fahrenheit ( some people argue to do this first. )

Filter out Leaf matter with cheesecloth and funnel into dropper bottles.

I am happy that this glycerin serves as a multipurpose product. You can use it in your body care formulas, cook with it, great replacement for those alcohol based products, can be used for burns, and in terms of taste & body -- it doesn't cause tooth decay (so I've heard), and it provides a light sweetness and moisture. The fact that it is USP -- tested to be safe for food usage and effective-- it's a great buy for a gallon, for $25, what more can I say? I only took out one star because I know they make organic glycerin, and I kind of prefer organic vegetable glycerin for homemade body care products.

rating 5.0. This serum feels so wonderful on my skin This serum feels so wonderful on my skin, it goes on smoothly and is absorbed well. It leaves my skin feeling so soft and hydrated.

rating 5.0. Wonderful I love this product!  It goes on smooth without a tacky feel.  It absorbs quickly in the skin.  I pair this with the Oz Naturals Retinol Moisturizer and I wake up with the softness skin imaginable.  It doesn't irritate my skin or cause it to dry out either.  I'll def keep using this product.

rating 5.0. A true powerhouse in a small bottle : After washing my face for bed, one pump is more than enough to lightly cover completely, and my skin drinks it in. A few minutes later, I follow up with the OZNaturals Super Youth Retinol Moisturizer. No reaction or drying of my skin, and my pillowcases aren't discolored like prior products I've used. It's best to use this product at night, and with the cold pressed technology in this serum, I get the true and highest concentration of the ingredients. I have noticed a considerable difference in the tone in my complexion with a nice radiant look. I could not be happier, and look forward to the future results from using this serum, as it will be another product in my arsenal of skincare from OZ Naturals.

rating 5.0. Noticeably smoother skin and improvement in problem areas I found this product to be effective at smoothing out my skin.  I've been using it for about a week and see a change in my skin.  It seems smoother and more supple.  I am hoping that it improves the absorbsion of the topical antioxidents I use, especially Oz Natural's C serum products.  I found this especially effective on the parts of my skin that have psoriasis, reducing flaking and peeling, and making my skin much smoothier and less irritated in the problem areas.  Recommended, with sunblock for protection.

rating 5.0. Another Great OZ Product! Like all OZ products, this one is a winner too. You can feel the serum working within 2 minutes after application. rating 5.0. Love this new Cold-processing Product! I am a huge fan of the OZ Natural product line, and this product is yet another great one. As instructed, I use this product at bedtime, and my skin is extremely soft and smooth in the morning. I especially like the pump applicator - one small pump and there is enough serum to cover my entire face and neck area. Strongly recommend!

rating 5.0. Easy to apply! I have been using Oz Naturals for a while and I love this serum. The pump makes application easy and I get just the right amount. I apply the serum right after washing my face before I apply the Retinol moisturizer. I feel like I'm really taking care of my face and fighting aging using these products. rating 2.0. Research the company and product before you buy!!!!! I just ordered a product from this company and when it arrived it looked very generic and the packaging of it was concerning. Instead of a normal seal on the bottle, the cap was taped in with a white plastic tape. I was able to unscrew the cap with ease and access the contents inside and replace the cap without removing the tape. That raises red flags for me. Could someone have accessed/contaminated the product after it left the facility and then tape it in this way or is this how the company "seals" its products? I wanted to find out more info on this company and whether I should be worried about the "seal" before using the product. Just concerned. I believe it is a newer company and would be glad to continue using their products if I can be reasonably assured they are legit.

rating 4.0. 4 stars. First of all, this product really is great. I also love the fact that it's organic! However, if you have a pretty big make up brush collection, then this product won't last you a long time. I used it for the first time today and it did the job; it really does clean your brushes perfectly. One pump, even half a pump will do the trick, which is what I love about it. So today, I decided to use it and when I looked at the bottle, almost half of it was gone already! (Which was a bummer, but I can't really complain considering I cleaned 28 brushes and 2 beautyblenders). The brushes in the picture aren't even half of my collection. Therefore, this product most likely won't last a long time. Also, I noticed I had this white dry stuff all over my hand afterwards. It did go away after I washed my hands though.

rating 5.0. Great solution to a discontinued product. I have used the Windex Touch Up Pumps in my bathrooms for quick cleanups. Unfortunately, they discontinued the product and the pump bottles are not refillable. I was getting a pedicure recently and the cosmetologist used a bottle like this for nail polish remover. Lightbulb moment: I can fill them with windex and keep one in each bathroom to replace the touch up pumps that I used to buy. I have to fill these bottles more frequently but in the long run, they will save me money because they are refillable.

rating 1.0. Not a good match for acetone I bought this to use with 100% acetone nail polish remover. The first problem was that one pump was insufficient to soak a cotton swab enough to take off polish, but a second pump would cause the acetone to spurt all over. This lead to a second problem, which was the spilled acetone would then drop down the outside of the bottle and stain/cloud it. Ultimately, I replaced this with a glass bottle with a steel pump.

rating 1.0. Waste of money I bought these to use to dispense mineral spirits and denatured alcohol in my woodworking shop. I figured that if they could be used with acetone nail polish remover, they would certainly hold up for the less volatile solutions above. In both cases, they worked the first time I used them on the first day. But the next day, both of them where frozen so that the pump would not spring back to allow the liquid to be dispensed. I couldn't even get them to pop back out by pulling on the pump pad.  Now they are just bottles that I need to open and pour the liquid unto the rag, since the pump is useless. I would not recommend these.

rating 4.0. Useful for non-beauty related applications, too No one in my family uses nail polish (on actual fingernails--we have a few bottles as ad hoc varnish/threadlock), but we've found these bottles quite useful to dispense iso alcohol during crafting/electronic repair projects, especially when we're working with resin and have hands covered in gunk. Having the pump bottles on the desk, we can grab paper towels/cotton pads with our gunky hands and dispense some cleaner without having to spread the gunk. These are plastic, so they're a bit light, but I didn't want to risk a glass bottle with the shenanigans I'd be performing with gunky hands. Also a bit difficult to get the lid open if I forget to have it already opened before gunking up my hands, but that's not really a product design issue. And it's still easier than trying to open/pour a regular bottle of iso with gunky hands.

rating 5.0. The best gel base and topcoat set I’ve used!! This is the best gel base and topcoat set I’ve found so far! The gel basecoat prevents any products I use from staining my nails, and it protects my nails from anything I put on them! The topcoat cures quickly and protects my polish and nail art under a thick, glossy layer of gel, which has a beautiful hard shiny finish. I also like that these are no wipe, which makes them easier to use since there is no sticky layer after curing!

rating 5.0. Great The bottom coat and top coat both went on smoothly. Not lumpy, not too thick. Very easy to use. Exactly what I was looking for. Any imperfections were my fault because I was a little lazy taking off the previous polish and with the application.

rating 5.0. I can see my reflection from the top coats glossiness! [[VIDEOID:7e314eb68d3d14994f418f96e03962ca]] Okay so besides 1 gel top coat I've bought ages ago and used before I've never played with gels all that much because they've seem complicated and messy and I was more accustomed to normal nail polish. However! These are phenomenal, they are rubbery enough that they don't glide all over and over fill my cuticles. The base coat does a great job at adhering to the gel polishes I used (also from LagunaMoon) and the top coat is so unbelievably glossy I could see the clouds reflected on them! Spectacular product!

rating 5.0. No Wipe Top Coat! REALLY WORKS! This nail polish was awesome to use, I cured it for 90 seconds each time with a LED light. The base coat wasn't gloopy or gross, it applied easily. The smell wasn't overpowering either.
The top coat on the other hand was a bit thinner, and it smelled. It wasn't too bad but it was very weird for a nail polish lol. I loved that it's a "No Wipe" Top Coat. I feel like having alcohol is just too much stuff when I do my nails. My polish wasn't sticky or tacky when I finished.
I haven't had the polish peel off yet. I really love this!
And the price can't be beat! You get the Top and Base Coat for less than $10!

rating 1.0. Cheap, gummy, doesn’t cure correctly. The polish itself stinks pretty bad even after curing under the UV.
The Base coat never completely cures. I did a thin layer and sat under the UV longer and longer even with multiple sessions to see if it got better....and it still remains gummy and sticky to the touch.
Adding gel polish is a waste as it never sets up correctly and the manicure falls apart after just a day or so.
Very disappointed considering the reviews. I find this cheap and worse on the nails as peeling screws up the nail.
A good base coat and gel polish is solid, it chips after weeks of wear and is hard, not quite as hard as acrylic but very protective on the nail.
This garbage peels within the first few days of application. Has nothing to do with it touching the sides/skin as mine peeled from the tops as well as sides and bottom, which the parts that peel, the polish is rubbery when it peels off no where near hard.
Do NOT recommend this and it’s a waste of money to use it with nicer gel polish as it’ll be falling off way before it should.

rating 5.0. Finally - use the hair dryer you want and the diffuser you want! Love this thing! I am particular about the hair dryer I use, and particular about he diffuser I use. I don't want to change one to accommodate the other. I have wavy hair that is fussy about how I dry it to get to most volume and curl out of it. The black orchid diffuser works like no other to hold up my waves, gently dry them, and help coax volume at the crown of my head where I need it.... but it doesn't fit the dryers I like to use. This little adapter actually works to make that a problem of the past. It even works on my very short-snouted Sedu 4000i dryer (see pictures). It holds very securely, even though it may look like it won't. In the first picture I am not holding the dryer+diffuser vertical, it is standing like that on its own. It takes a little bit of coaxing to get it on both devices, but once you figure out how to do it, and with how much pressure and finesse, it's good to go.

rating 5.0. UPDATED... I really love this palette holder. I was using letter sorters, but they didn't really fit my palette and I had to use too many. This keeps them all in one place, and pretty compact (for 10 palettes) It is sturdy acrylic, that will probably last forever. It fits the Large Z Palette perfectly and the MAC Pro Palettes (older slim version.) I wish it were about an inch wider so I could put my Viseart palettes together, but overall I am very happy. I read on the Alegory website they were coming out with an oversized & small palette organizer, so those might be worth picking up also to organize by size better. I love to look at pics to see what will fit into certain organizers, so I included a pic of mine. In case it is hard to tell, these are what palettes that are in it: (Front to Back) 1.Viseart, 2. Becca Champagne Glow, 3.Viseart & Hourglass Ambient Edit, 4.ABH Contour & Becca Afterglow, 5,6, & 7 are MAC Pro Palettes (old version) & Large Z Palette in last row. Hope this was helpful!

Update: Added some new pics of some palettes people have been wondering if they fit. I honestly haven't found a palette that WONT fit. Morphe palettes DO fit, they just have to lay on their side. I have found that if you put all of your palettes on their side you can fit WAY more in this organizer. I currently have 15 palettes fit in mine, so this has been very handy! I love having all my palettes at my fingertips, it really encourages me to use all of my palettes more instead of being in a drawer forgotten about. The new palettes im the pictures not listed above are the Anastasia Glow Kit, Too Faced Sweet Peach, MAC Pro Large single sided palette, Violet Voss Holy Grail Palette & Morphe Palettes in the back., the rest are on the list above.

rating 4.0. Like, it's an acrylic brick This thing is HEAVY! Like, it's an acrylic brick. I have a glass vanity and so I am slightly concerned with the heft of this. With that said, it's an excellent product. I would never, ever use it as picture, however. Even though it is quite weighted, that just looks like a disaster waiting to happen. Your eyeshadow would shatter for certain with the weight of the organizer hitting it should it fall.

I use mine like a "drawer" of sorts. My glass vanity has a shelf and I put this on it and then pull it out when I need to select a palette. It works great. If the palettes are slim, you can fit two to a row.

rating 1.0. What's all the Hype? This thing is Terrible! What's all the hub-bub about this thing? It sucks! I got it based on all the great reviews - my mistake. The photo makes it look DEEPER than it really is - but when you put the palettes in it (Urban Decay, Kat Von D et al) they HANG OUT by about 40% making it very unstable...(I should have measured better I suppose)...also, the acrylic is fairly thick but not thick enough to be "stable" unless you sit the thing right on your counter - I had planned to stack it on top of something else, but I could not - wanted to fall right over. Arrrgh! Some 5-star review. I thought to myself, what am I gonna do with this stupid thing - it was not cheap either!
Solution: I am using it to store my Oversized Music CD's!! Many titles today come in the oversized cases with the "bonus blu-ray", etc. (such as the new Iron Maiden Book of Souls and Rush R40 Set!) - these guys sure don't fit into the regular CD caddies I had so had to store them in shoeboxes (nice!)...but not anymore. Now they are all nice & organized for easy access. SO all it not lost - BUT I am disappointed it did not work for the makeup collection. Oh well, back to the drawing board!

rating 5.0. Price Matces Quality This is a lot heavier and more sturdy than I expected. It is perfectly sized for the naked palettes. If your palettes are any taller than the naked palettes, I think it's best to lay the organizer horizontally so that the palettes are more stable in the way that I have pictured. Something to note is that the edges are beveled so that the base is a bit wider than the opening. Had the edges not been beveled it would have been possible to have 3 ways to display the organizer as opposed to the 2 options pictured. The third option would have been to position the organizer so that the palettes stood upright in a vertical position. The beveled edge makes this impossible. Hope that makes sense.

rating 5.0. By far the BEST palette organizer I have found! This palette organizer is amazing!  I have been looking for something to compactly contain and organize my eyeshadow palettes for easy access and this is absolutely perfect - much better than the DIY cd racks and letter holders I have seen others using.  However, to get the most storage from this organizer I would recommend using it lying down, not standing up.  Instead of placing the palettes in horizontally as shown in the item's picture, after laying the organizer down I was able to place multiple palettes vertically in each compartment.  I can fit 24 eyeshadow palettes of varying sizes using the 9 divisions provided.  The dividers are also removable if more space per section is needed.  Also, since the organizer is made out of very heavy duty, thick acrylic, I was concerned about it getting knocked over, which could be prevented by backing it against another vertical surface, but laying it down horizontally completely eliminated this issue.  I highly, highly recommend getting this organizer if you've been looking for a better way to store your palettes, and this seller also has several other makeup organizers (for compacts, lipsticks, etc.) that also look great.

rating 5.0. More than I could hope for! I took quite a few picures so all of you can see what I recived and I must say I love all the colors.  In the first picture the pastle colors there is one bottle that leaked but that doesnt bother me because on the piece of paper I tested each color and it still works just as good.  I have seen several reviews that its really thin and you need more than one coat.  But you always need more than one coat.  You never want nail polish to be too thick.  I have a huge variety and I did not know there was a "Fractured" set and on the paper you can see how it works and I am in love with it.  I also tried to see if the other nail polishes would work for the fractured but it didnt thats what the red smudge is next to the three purples.  I have no issues with these and for the price you are getting a great deal.  They are pretty decent size bottles and when i run out i will be buying more from Beauty Brags.  Even though one bottle leaked I am still giving 5 stars.  You can see each color and decide on your own but i say treat yourself and get these awesome sets.

rating 5.0. To summarize, the price alone makes them worth it! Came in yesterday. Well cushioned in bubble wrap. Then in 5 tubes of 6 ea. Finally inside convenient storage trays with individual slots (works for me!) Not likely to break.
The colors really are wonderful! Can’t speak for durability having not yet worn any. I’m hoping 2 regular or a gel topcoat will help them last?? If not, great for learning practice for someone? At $20. for 30 full size bottles! What a deal!!
Polish was separated so before trying, I wore my shoulder out shaking & laying them on all sides between shakes.
I’m not familiar with fractured polish, so just guessing the 3 streaked examples should have been shaken more?? They are the first ones I used. Each (only those) have a white base and one slightly heavy polish coat... they fracture on contact!
The other colors are 2 thin coats and all 30 have topcoats. Although a few are very similar in color, no 2 are exactly the same. Not complaining mind you but In a perfect world I would exchange a couple look a-likes for a Christmasy green, & a good clear glitter, possibly black (I think) to complete the set😉
I hope this info helps you decide if you want!

rating 5.0. Love it I use to do my nails all the time growing up. My mom taught me how to do acrylic nails back when I was in high school. I stopped doing them myself for a while and my husband asked why I don't do them anymore. No really reason I just stopped. I recently started back doing them and I always wanted a drill so I finally bought me one. I wanted something simply not too pricey that I could relearn and properly use. I have some clue on how to use it. But everything is still new to me thank heavens for youtube and other reviewers. I know it is going to take some time to master it but overall I am really happy with my purchase. I haven't had any issues. It work perfectly fine. I tend to not go so high just so that I can be more accurate and precise. I will update if anything change but I am very satisfied with my drill

rating 4.0. Does the job for DIYers I try (albeit poorly) to do my own nails at home and got really into doing structure gel over nail tips. This drill does the job, but you will need to buy different drill bits because the ones it comes with are almost useless (except for buffing). I bought a couple Kupa Elite nail bits and they fit perfectly in the drill. After owning this roughly 2 years, I have been experiencing trouble with the cord. It makes a weird high pitched beep when plugged in, and sometimes stops when the cord is a certain way. My application has gotten better over the past couple years, but still lumpy / uneven :( so the drill spins off the nail and has cut me at times, so be careful!  Too stubborn to give up even if I suck haha. Not sure if I would buy this again when mine conks out or if I will invest in a better drill.

rating 2.0. A decent drill...while it works I bought this to file down acrylic nails I was doing on myself with dual nail forms (which I don't recommend if you have high c-curve nails, by the way). At first I had to watch a video to figure out to put on the bits, but once I got the hang of it, it was quick and easy. To turn it on, there's a little wheel towards the bottom of the drill where you can control the speed. It worked well enough for filling, though I mostly used the barrel bit for buffing and the sanding for taking down length. The other tapered bits weren't too useful, they didn't work well for filing underneath the nail. Overall, compared to a professional drill I'm sure this is much slower, but also much safer.

That was about 8 months ago, and since then I've used it about 10 times. I used it one more time for acrylics and the rest of the time to (very gently and slowly) file off top coat from gel nails for easier removal. Just a couple of days ago while doing the latter, the drill wouldn't turn on. I moved it around, and it would start and stop in short bursts as I would move it around trying to use it. That means the adapter cord is already damaged, which is ridiculous considering how little use I got out of it. For this reason I'm taking off three stars, no product should being to fail so quickly. Look elsewhere for a drill, unless you don't mind dealing with a faulty adapter.

rating 1.0. Even at this cheap price I do not recommend it. This was so difficult to use. I felt as though my fingers were going to break trying to change the tips on this machine. I got extremely frustrated with the item and after only being able to get the two to change out just one time, I returned it. Even at this cheap price I do not recommend it.

rating 5.0. A good buy My big toenails were thick and partially black from fungus. I tried to sand them down with a regular nail file but that was way too much work and too little progress. I was a little doubtful about this drill because of the price but it really does work. If you press down too much the drill does stop but the whole point is to really not do that, lightly sweep it over your nails and let the sanding do the job. I like the speed dial and the fact that I can control it. For the price, it does what its supposed to do. I didnt have any overheating issues or anything. Now my toenails look as good as they are going to get while i do my treatments.

rating 5.0. Rivals My Pet Dremel File! Worked well for me without overheating.  I used this on my husbands thick toe nails to thin them out and stop splitting.  Worked great but be careful not to stay in one spot for too long, as it generates heat on the nail quickly! Still a bit too noisy for use on  my skittish little puppy's nails.  He runs! Hopefully in the future he will warm up to it.  I'm using the pet Dremel for now on the low setting.  I originally started to use it for my husbands nails as well, which is what led me to this one.  This nail file is easier to hold since its slimmer, and it also has a wider range of speed settings. Another great feature is that it works with the same system of tips as the Dremel, so they are somewhat interchangeable. The tips it comes with have a skinnier post than typical Dremel ones do, so you can only use these tips on your Dremel BUT NOT your Dremel tips on this machine!  I like it better than the Dremel though, just hope it holds up well.  You can't beat it for $8.  I just saw one in Bed Bath and Beyond for $20 which clearly is not as good of quality as this one which also was battery powered.  This one uses a cord, which I prefer.

rating 5.0. As expected I use these for witch hazel in the bathrooms.  It makes it easy to dispense and I would say it reduces waste.  Usually only need two pumps per cotton round. Would recommend.

Easy to use with one hand and the size is just right - I use them for storing/dispensing witch hazel and toner.

rating 5.0. Decluttered Vanity very nicely! These great dispensers are pretty and work perfectly.  Water tight lids are perfect for watery liquids. If there is any leftover liquid after dispensing,  it slowly drips back into the bottle.  I use these for witch hazel, however, I would not recommend for use with Creams and lotions.

rating 5.0. Great solution to a discontinued product. I have used the Windex Touch Up Pumps in my bathrooms for quick cleanups. Unfortunately, they discontinued the product and the pump bottles are not refillable. I was getting a pedicure recently and the cosmetologist used a bottle like this for nail polish remover. Lightbulb moment: I can fill them with windex and keep one in each bathroom to replace the touch up pumps that I used to buy. I have to fill these bottles more frequently but in the long run, they will save me money because they are refillable.

rating 5.0. Works great looks great I bought these to put makeup remove and toner in. They’re pretty big and make it super convenient to get product out. They look really nice on my counter and work great.

rating 1.0. Not a good match for acetone I bought this to use with 100% acetone nail polish remover. The first problem was that one pump was insufficient to soak a cotton swab enough to take off polish, but a second pump would cause the acetone to spurt all over. This lead to a second problem, which was the spilled acetone would then drop down the outside of the bottle and stain/cloud it. Ultimately, I replaced this with a glass bottle with a steel pump.
rating 5.0. Great quality Bought for my facial water.  I love them, I only needed one but a back up is always a bonus

rating 1.0. Waste of money I bought these to use to dispense mineral spirits and denatured alcohol in my woodworking shop. I figured that if they could be used with acetone nail polish remover, they would certainly hold up for the less volatile solutions above. In both cases, they worked the first time I used them on the first day. But the next day, both of them where frozen so that the pump would not spring back to allow the liquid to be dispensed. I couldn't even get them to pop back out by pulling on the pump pad.  Now they are just bottles that I need to open and pour the liquid unto the rag, since the pump is useless. I would not recommend these.

I think the plastics used just don’t work the best with pure acetone, but everything else I’ve put in it has worked great and hasn’t leaked at all.

rating 4.0. Useful for non-beauty related applications, too No one in my family uses nail polish (on actual fingernails--we have a few bottles as ad hoc varnish/threadlock), but we've found these bottles quite useful to dispense iso alcohol during crafting/electronic repair projects, especially when we're working with resin and have hands covered in gunk. Having the pump bottles on the desk, we can grab paper towels/cotton pads with our gunky hands and dispense some cleaner without having to spread the gunk. These are plastic, so they're a bit light, but I didn't want to risk a glass bottle with the shenanigans I'd be performing with gunky hands. Also a bit difficult to get the lid open if I forget to have it already opened before gunking up my hands, but that's not really a product design issue. And it's still easier than trying to open/pour a regular bottle of iso with gunky hands.

I typically get baby wipes as they are softer but these are cheaper than other organic brands and the packaging is cute! I can leave them sitting out since they arent an eye sore like most wipe packages. I highly recommend.

rating 5.0. Subtle fragrance and gentle.......... Just when I was about to give up on wipes altogether due to the fact I break out even on sensitive ones, and most of the big name brands always feel slimy.  These were incredible, they were perfect wipes and I'm so glad I discovered them.  I just placed a second order and I will continue to buy these for as long as I need them.  Great product!!!

rating 5.0. Great for sensitive people! I love the whole L. line of products because I seem to have issues with many other brands in the area of feminine hygiene. These are a pleasant smell with good durability and a convenient, reclosable pouch. I don't have any issues "down there" when using them and would recommend this to anyone.

rating 5.0. Not your average wipe - thank goodness! I just bought the L. pads for my daughter and figured I would add these to the cart.  We don't typically "do" wipes in our house - not even for makeup, because I don't want the harmful chemicals hurting any of us.  These are a healthier option though (thank you L.) and sometimes it's just really nice to be able to freshen up without a full blown shower.  The packaging is nice and they have a fresh clean scent to them that is pleasing.  Happy I found them.

rating 1.0. Caused a yeast infection I am pretty sure these gave me a yeast infection. The only time I have ever gotten them was when I took antibiotics, but the only thing I have done differently is use these wipes.

rating 5.0. Refreshing and Healing Lady Wipes - Best New Product I've Bought These are the best. I am astounded at the healing factor and lack of prickly pain when used.

rating 5.0. Love it I really like them ordering again it has a really clean feeling I choose wipes over toilet paper any day smells really good

rating 5.0. Excellent I like that there isn't any ingredients that aren't really identified or that you can't pronounce. The scentn is pleasant but neutral. I have fairly sensitive skin, and these are really great at gently cleansing the areas that need it.

I keep a packet in the bathroom, and a packet in my purse.

Also, I love that they support girls & women less fortunate.
rating 5.0. Simply the best! Great to use after the gym if you don't have time to shower. Or if you just aren't feeling all that fresh down there and need a quick little cleanse!

rating 5.0. It truly fits the cord! Perfect for my Dyson hairdryer!! input: rating 5.0. Great for pain relief! My husband loves this product! He says it gets deep into the painful area and relives the soreness. We will be ordering this again.
rating 5.0. This product works very good product, and it works! My  thumbs and wrists feel much better.
rating 5.0. This stuff works I have been using this for along time.  It works.  I put it on my shoulders, neck, back & hips. It takes the pain away.  This stuff is the bomb.

rating 5.0. Definitely sold on this!!! I originally bought another highly rated cream that has DMSO in it, but there are also a lot of other strong smelling scents and it doesn't really do a whole lot. I was also a little afraid to try it after receiving it since the small print states that it is being sold as a "solvent". In the art world, that means to me that it will eat away other things (think turpentine LOL), and the warning stated that it could damage the skin. Yikes!

I reviewed this one some while ago. I've now been using this for at least a year and a half and it is the best pain relief cream I've ever used, and I've tried a lot. First, it doesn't have a lot of the medicinal odor that many pain relief creams use due to the camphor and menthol in the others. I don't have to use much before I get relief (although I'll admit that when I start hurting in bed I also use a heat pad)...but it's the use of so little that is well worth the price and recently it was marked lower so I made sure and bought two to stock up on. Every time a friend complains of pain I share some and then they remark how great it was for them as well. I don't ever want to run out of this since it works fast, uses very little, and lasts a long while.

Unfortunately, or fortunately, however one wants to look at it, I have been in extreme pain for years now. I have Plantar Fasciitis on the right foot, a swollen Achilles Tendon (an MRI showed it being strained and my ankles as being torn but the doctor only had me go to 3 months of PT for that), spondylosis in both the cervical and lumbar spines, and sciatica. I was beginning to feel hopeless at ever feeling good again. So then, I thought, with as many people who have touted the wonders of this particular jar of cream, what do I have to lose. Maybe a little skin, but maybe, just maybe I could lose some of the pain.

WOW!!! Am I ever glad I finally gave in! I opened it up 3 nights ago, and almost immediately felt the pain easing away from the areas I rubbed the cream in to. I had a pretty decent night's sleep and the cream seemed to keep the pains at bay for at least 5-6 hours. Yesterday, however, I had to go to town and didn't get around to putting anything on before going. Going to town out here in rural American is a half day affair. The closest grocery stores and such are at least 35-40 mins drive away. So I wound up being back in extreme pain by the time I returned, put groceries away, cooked and ate dinner, and cleaned up from dinner. But I put some more on before settling down to watch a movie last night and voila, it seemed to ease the pains again, so before I fell asleep I put some more on and again, had another more restful night's sleep. This morning, before I even got up and started my day, I put some on my feet and my neck and they feel pretty good. I didn't get any on my lumbar spine and that's beginning to hurt, so as soon as I get this posted, I will be putting some on my lumbar spine area. I am definitely sold on this and will buy more when I start getting low, however, a little goes a long way, so I think it will probably last me for quite a long while. I highly recommend it and will be telling family and friends about it, as well. I am definitely sold!!!

rating 5.0. Finally!! DMSO without Stinky Scent!!! Finally!! This cream is amazing!! As a firm believer in the healing power of DMSO, I can’t not stand the smell of plain DMSO. We use it for everything, any ache, pain, arthritis, bursitis, temples for headache, sinus issues, burns, acne plus so much more! If you use it consistently you WILL definitely be able to tell a difference and see improvement no matter what ails you!! I have used this product religiously for a few years now and hope it is around forever!!! Buy it, you will be glad you did!!

rating 1.0. Made the pain so much much worse. Made the pain much much worse.
I have used DMSO on two separate occasions. Fist time I used it years ago for a period of a week and this time once only. Both time it made my pain worse. During my last experience with DMSO, the pain started the next day after the application before bedtime. At the time most of my hip and shoulder/neck pain was in remission but after a single application of DMSO, I have had a flare up that has lasted so far for 3 days. I then looked into online forums and realize that subgroups of people experience increased pain on DMSO and there is no explanation as to why this happens. It may work for many but it is not for everyone.

rating 3.0. Gave me dandruff I used this product for about 3 months and everything was ok. I didn’t love it but it worked. I am trying to avoid parabens and sulfates. It got my hair clean and even though I didn’t love the smell it worked.

Then I noticed dandruff. I conditioned more often but it just seemed like more and more dandruff was everywhere. I had to go back to normal shampo for awhile because my head was full of flakes and with dark hair it became noticeable.

I have since then switched to OGX tea tree shampoo which is sulfate free and the dandruff went away. After a month I went back to the Okay Black Jamaican Castor oil shampo and immediately the dandruff came back.

So I am on the look out for a better paraben free sulfate free shampo but wow there is some nasty stuff out there. OGX claimed they were paraben free but I’m not so sure based on the PEG ingredients. Herbal essences has a paraben free shampoo but per the reviews still causes dandruff (and has sulfates). Alba shampoo got ok reviewed but contains so much fragrance that it makes me gag.

At this rate I might just stop washing my hair altogether ha!

rating 5.0. Pretty Diva Rocks!!! I have had this product for two days...and I have had sooooo much fun with it I almost used it all!!! I am the "Gramma" that does everybody's nails...and I can't wait!!! Hard to photograph in the sun but I want to share some of my pictures. Sorry not better...but hard to capture the beauty of this powder. It has been many years since I have been this excited about something new for manicures, and I have been doing them since I was 14...right now I am 57. I rarely write reviews but this product I need to share my excitement!!! I have also enclosed some photos from another product I ordered from Pretty Diva...they are my "go to" from now on!!! Their other metallic powders are the best of the best...I could go on and on...and I am a pro at it now so if anyone needs any tips just add to comment and I would LOVE to assist...5 stars...no TEN STARS!!! Thank you, thank you, thank you for igniting some new excitement on something that is so important in a woman. When you meet someone for the first time it is a proven theory they notice your hands almost first thing. I know that to be true now because everywhere I go I do get compliments on my nails and they want to know where to get it!!!
Thanks for taking the time to read...after I reviewed the product had to take a couple more pictures of their chrome powder to share...the one wheel with other colors showing is the powder OVER those colors instead of just black. Putting it over coral was absolutely beautiful!!!

rating 5.0. Holo powder I absolutely love this holo powder.  I've used it on different colors of polish and each time the results are gorgeous.  I've also used it on "regular" nail polishes( that are not gel and are not soak off).  You DO need the no wipe top coat that requires curing with either the led or uv light.  I've used it on "cream/enamel" polishes, on glitter polish (although the effect is not as pronounced) and on the gel polish that does not require curing (Sally Hansen).  It looks great on dark, medium and light colors.  I've also used it on my nails without any colored polish - just a base coat, the no wipe off top coat (make sure to cure this top coat) and then the powder.  I do use a regular top coat (non cure) over the powder to keep it from getting scratched.

rating 5.0. YES it works on regular polish too! Does this work on regular polish?  YEP.  Not as perfect as over gel, and you really have to know how your topcoat feels at what stages of drying you're at, but it's possible.  It looks better in person than the photograph, which is taken with a flash (which magnifies quite a lot especially when all these small particles are everywhere).  Photo was taken with the powder on top of nude nail polish which is giving me a pink-toned holographic glow.  It's a bit of work to get it over regular polish and it isn't as even & perfect but it's workable.

rating 5.0. I was honestly so doubtful when I bout it because I'd never used gel polish before and I felt like this wouldn't work out Let me just start off with saying that this stuff is BOMB. I was honestly so doubtful when I bout it because I'd never used gel polish before and I felt like this wouldn't work out, but I was wrong!

I used the Aimeili Base and No Wipe Top Coat set for my holo nails and the powder buffed on perfectly! I put my nails under my LED light for 30 seconds (timing might vary for you depending on the light you are using/the top coat you use) and barely even touched the buffing tool to the powder and it was enough to fully cover my nail in ~holo~.

My only complaint about this product are the instructions. I'm not sure if this is coming from China (it probably says somewhere on the packaging but I'm too lazy to look), but the instructions were really hard to read and honestly did not benefit me in the process of doing my nails. It made the process kind of scary because I did not feel confident that I was using the powder properly because the directions were so poorly written. Make sure that you read and watch some tutorials on how to do these nails beforehand like I did, just to ensure that it all comes out right!

Definitely buy this, it's amazing!

rating 5.0. Exactly what I was wanting I like this and it does work but it can be tricky. The first time I used it with the no wipe top coat it came out with a frosted/glittery look. I cured it about 30-35 seconds the first time. I tried it again and cured the no wipe top coat longer (closer to 60 seconds) and it came out perfectly! Also I found that it looks better to rub/buff it in with the eye shadow applicator instead of your finger. I think the natural oils in your finger dulls the finish. For my nails I did a gelish base coat, gelish ocean waves (a medium blue color), no wipe top coat (cured for 60 seconds), the holo powder, and then finished with the no wipe top coat.

rating 5.0. Great deal! Great product! They took a little more than three weeks to get here, but it was worth the wait! I ordered two so I can use one in each hand. They are lovely and look like the photo advertised. They definitely are real jade! They are so beautiful, smooth and have some weight to them. Gua sha is an interesting technique that I've been researching. I haven't used it on my face yet, but plan to. If anything, they are amazing when giving a massage. Just use a little oil and these beauties do all the work so your hands don't have to. My boyfriend can't use the excuse anymore that his hands hurt during a back massage! It removes the knots in your back better than any hand or finger could ever do. They feel nice to hold and the contour is perfect for sliding over different body areas. For the price I paid for these, it was so worth it! Other gua sha tools can be pricey and they aren't even made out of a semi precious stone! You won't be disappointed in these!

A warning though, too much pressure will result in slight bruising. I would recommend this tool to anyone who cares about their aging skin.

rating 2.0. Lighter than expected. Almost a Nude/Lightpink versus a Pure Peach The color was much lighter than advertised. I've included a photo of what I mean. The photo is of the product applied liberally, no gradient or wiping it off. This was the full color that I was able to achieve. I personally wanted something a bit more like the color in the advertised photo. Its a nice color, but it blends in with my skin tone way too much, so it ends up looking like a nude/pink color.

If that is the look you are looking for then its good or if you have a very pale/ivory complexion it might pop more.

Other than the color the product was good. It had a nice pleasant smell and didn't dry up. I actually ended up purchasing another color (Peak Rose) that was darker and I loved it!

rating 5.0. I like it! The limited lip color selection and the reviews that many of the colors are similar or very bright were the only reasons I took months to make this purchase. I have no regrets finally buying this product!

I wanted a natural pink-peachy toned lip stain and this one fits the bill! I have sensitive lips that peel and crack easily. This makes it difficult for me to wear lipsticks (liquid, creamy, matte, etc,). I really like this particular formula. It has a mousse-like texture when first applied (in a thin layer). It dries down slightly and is very light on the lips, feels like nothing is there. I can use my balm over it with minimal transfer throughout the day.

I made the initial mistake of applying it like a lip gloss or liquid lipstick and ended up with goopy neon like lips! A little bit of product goes a very long ways. If you want a more intense color, I suggest thin layers and building up to your desired color. I would buy this product again.
