rating 4.0. Amazing value! While I don't think that these are of quite the same quality as the Konad plates, you certainly cannot beat this price! For how many plates and designs you get, it was worth every penny. Just be careful when removing the plastic, they are very sharp! I just filed the edges of mine with an emery board and that took care of the problem.

rating 5.0. Great Product - Great Price! I bought these plates because my daughters spent the night at a friends and came home with the cutest nails. When I asked about them their friend showed me her 'name brand' set that she paid about $50 for. It had two plates some polish, a stamper & a scraper. I thought it was adorable but was not going to pay that kind of money. Bargain hunter than I am I searched for a better deal. Which led me to Bundle Monster and another 'off brand' that I found on ebay for similar value.

I bought both sets. The received the ebay ones first. They were a great value came with some extras & offered cute plates (a few with the same designs from one plate to the next). They even offered the same designs and our neighbor girl had on her 'name brand' plates (none of which were 'full nail' or 'french tip' images). I was quite satisfied but have to say when I received the plates from Bundle Monster I soon realized I preferred their images better. They were larger for the most part & a much nicer selection over all (I hope they continue to add to their designs).

The ONLY Con I would mention is that the edges seem a bit sharp (compared to both the name brand & the other off brand I purchased) so I would keep them out of reach from young fingers even if they are just 'looking at the designs'. My teenage daughter cut herself on one not paying attention.

For those of you who are having problems with them here are some tips & step by step instructions I use (mentioned by others but with so many reviews I will post it as well).

Tips to Know Before you get started:

1) REMOVE the plastic film: They come with a plastic film when you first receive them that sometimes can not be seen. I used something sharp (tweezers) to cut a spot & then removed the rest with my fingers. If you do not remove the film the plates do not work.

2) Use THICK but NOT Quick Dry Contrasting Nail Polish for Images: If you do not have a 'thick' polish & do not want to spend $10+ on the 'name brand' let one of yours dry out a bit or go to your local salon that does nails & see if they have some 'old stuff' that you can buy for pennies on the dollar.

If you use quick dry you may not get the transfer done quick enough before it drys.

I have tried a few variations of colors but for obvious reasons white & black work best. A dark color with a white or light pink print or a light color with a black or similar dark will have the best results. Do some practice ones on something to see what you really like.

3) Use one coat cover for Bottom Coat & Top Coat: I recommend using nail polish that IS quick dry & only takes one coat to cover for both the Bottom & Top Coats (not images). This is because the more nail polish you have on your nails the more likely it is to smear your images. Using quick dry also helps with this.

4) Use a Good Acetone nail remover: It seems this works best for cleaning the plates. Some remover seems to thin out the polish & make it more difficult to transfer.

5) Let the Plates DRY completely: After each transfer you will need to clean the plate (if there is polish in the image you will not get a good transfer). When you wipe the polish out let the remover dry before you put polish on it. Again it will thin out the polish making for a poor transfer.

6) Use Cotton Rounds instead of Balls: Cotton Rounds make it a lot easier to manage the nail remover without messing up your nails. I use a nail polish remover with a pump so (one touch remover) I don't have to do too much with my hands.

7) Between EACH STEP let your nails dry for the best possible results.

8) If you don't have a plate holder (which I highly recommend) use double sided scotch tape to hold the plates in place on whatever you are scraping the extra paint onto.

9) I also recommend the two sided stamper with the smaller stamp. It is much easier to ensure the best image placement for smaller images.

10) If you have larger nail beds you may want to overlap designs. Everyone has different nail bed sizes so it would be difficult to accommodate everyone. With practice one could learn to line up the designs for a fuller image but I would recommend either using more than one design or just overlapping them. It can create a nice effect as well.

10) Clean EVERYTHING thoroughly when you are done: This will be important so that your future painting will be more successful & so that the plates, stamper & scraper last longer.

My personal steps:

1) Wash hands & gently push back cuticles that might be up on nail bed.

2) Get out all needed products & items.

3) Paint nails with bottom coat. Let dry completely (VERY important for best results)

4) Prepare image plate, image nail polish & cotton round with polish remover.

5) Working Quickly - Paint a nice coat of image paint over your image, scrape away extra, stamp with stamper, line up with nail bed, roll on fast, firmly & carefully ensuring you get the entire image to transfer. (If you have never seen this done check out a youtube video on it).

6) After each image you will have to clean the plate completely (especially if you are using the same image for each nail). Be sure to do so carefully not to smudge your other nails. Some recommend using tweezers - I find if I use a Cotton round instead of a ball I don't need to. Again let the polish remover dry on the plate before adding the polish again.

7) I recommend doing all your images on one hand BEFORE putting on the top coat. Just be gentle not to 'bump' them.

8) Once your first hand is complete put on your top coat & let this dry Completely before starting your second hand.

9) Repeat for second hand!

Once you get the hang of it use multiple designs & colors. We have used it to stamp our phones & more (and change when we get bored)

Hope you enjoy this as much as I did! All the little Girls in my life (age appropriate of course) will be getting this for the next 'gift giving' occasion!!

rating 1.0. Completely unsatisfied. Can't decide if I just want to return it or exchange. I was super excited to receive this in the mail because I love Konad but the brand name plates are insanely expensive. So I came across this Bundle Monster set and looked through the reviews. I thought it was too good to be true that such good quality could come from something so cheap, and boy was I right

The plates came in one thick stack in a plastic bag inside a mailer. They were not separated by any protective plastic or papers or anything, much like a stack of new mirrors would come packaged. They were just all in one big stack of plates. I tried peeling at the plate, hoping some protective plastic would peel off.

I grabbed some special polish, applied it to the design and tried to stamp. When I scraped off the excess, hardly any polish remained in the plate, and it didn't go smoothly. When I scraped it down, it sounded like it was being scratched. I looked at all the plates before using, and they were COVERED in thick, nasty scratches, as if someone took sharp rocks and scratched up the plates.

Either I didn't receive a new set, or they were damaged by scratching against each other in transit. Even the plate on the top was scratched, so it couldn't have been scratched by another. I am very VERY disappointed. I have always gotten good products from Amazon, but this was not one of them.

I never return products cause it's too much of a hassle, but these aren't even in good enough condition to resell, so I went to Bundle Monster's storefront page to see their return policy. I don't mind paying for shipping for it to be returned, but if I am charged a crazy restocking fee, my bad review will stay on here. I will delete this and write a good review if BM isn't a pain in the butt to return or exchange this item.

rating 3.0. CHEAPER KONADS I like this product, not the greatest but good for the price.

PROS:
-Cheap
-You don't feel bad about scratching them
-A lot of cute designs that are non floral unlike KONAD (a lot of floral)
-Full finger design fits me just fine except for the thumb (it's a bit small)
-Plenty of designs to choose from

CONS:
-Edges are really SHARP
-The bigger designs tend to smear
-Disregarded some of the larger designs because it just looked like a blob even after I scraped it thin
-The metal is a lot thinner
-The designs are not as detailed

rating 5.0. So much cheaper I was skeptical of the quality of the bundle monster plates vs. the konads.  These plates are equal is quality but are so much cheaper!  They are a bit smaller than the konads, but I have small fingers to it's great for me! :)

rating 5.0. Excellent Bargain This is such an excellent bargain, you really can't beat it! The plates arrived in a very timely manner and they were well packaged. The plates are exactly the same size as the Konad brand plates and they are the same thickness as well. I mention this because I read some reviews saying the plates are a little smaller/thinner than the Konad plates, but they aren't. They're the same size. I've used several of the plates now and I love them. I do find that sometimes I have some trouble with getting some of the images to pick up on the stamper but the same is true when using the Konad plates. Sometimes just trying a different color of special polish seems to help with this - I have no idea why but it's something I noticed. So if you have trouble getting one of the designs to transfer to the stamper, try a different color and see if that helps. I do agree with some of the other reviewers that the full-nail designs could be a little wider, I think they could be a bit longer too. The French tip designs could be wider, as well. But overall these are great, and the price is fantastic. I hope Bundle Monster is planning to make more plates in the future - I'd definitely buy them!

rating 3.0. Quantity Over Quality While I am not disappointed in the quantity of images you get when you order the Bundle Monster Image Plates, I am quite sad with the quality of the images. Some of the images refuse to transfer regardless of what kind of polish I'm using. I've had mediocre luck with them at best. Also, I have pretty normal-sized nail beds, but the full-nail images don't cover them at all. This, in my opinion, was a major oversight in production. Or they did it on purpose to fit more images on each plate. Either way, they don't work, so there was no point in it. A metal scraper doesn't really work with these, which is no big deal, but it's nice for people to know what they'll be getting. The plastic film is really hard to get off, so remember to be careful as these are just little discs of sharp metal.

PROS:
=lots of images to choose from
=good price compared to Konad
=tip designs and single-image designs work well

CONS:
=full nail is WAY too small for normal nails
=some images are hard to pick up properly
=plastic film is really hard to remove
=edges are sharp

+/-
=metal scraper doesn't work at all with these plates
rating 4.0. cool They have some really nice images, easy to use and a lot cheaper than the konad ones. worth the money

rating 4.0. Would Recommend! PROs:
- fast shipping
- variety of designs
- really CHEAP, 2 Konads compared to 21 of same price
- some designs have same quality with Konad plates

CONs:
- some designs are not etched deep enough, like the spider web, i was soo excited for that for Halloween but much to my dismay it wont transfer even with special nail polish. I tried it 10x with different polish, i always end up being disappointed
- very sharp edge
- even after taking the plastic shield off, some plates are still scratched. No biggie..
- the full nail designs are not wide enough. Considering, I have a small nail, but it still wont cover my thumb and middle finger.

TIPS:
- when you take the plastic shield off, use an emery board. Works better than tweezer. Just buff the edge towards the center and peel it off..
- to avoid getting cut with the sharp edge, glue it with a thin cardboard before using. I just use a Cherios box. Lol.
- I didnt use the Konad scraper because plates scratched more easily compared to Konad plates but an old credit card cut in 2, works just fine..

Would I order another one if they have 2nd batch? YES
Would I recommend it to friends? Already Did!

rating 3.0. You get what you pay for These plates are just cheaper (price-wise and quality) versions of Konad plates. You get 21 plates for about $18, and shipping is free, so that's quite an irresistible deal, but I have to say, these plates CANNOT compare to the Konad plates.
First of all, there is no safety backing on the plates, as many people have written already; the plates are very thin, and can be very sharp. I resorted to pasting the plates to index cards so it's less likely to slice into my fingers, and to prevent the plates from scratching each other.
Secondly, the designs of the plates are not as intricate and well done as the Konad plates. I have a bunch of Konad plates, and the BM designs definitely don't come close. The BM designs are not as detailed, and just are not as "pretty" as the Konad designs (but of course, you get what you pay for). Also, some of the plates I got, seems like there were manufacturing flaws. The designs on the plates appear smeared, or smudged, and when I tried to stamp them, the images do not transfer properly, if at all.
Thridly, the full nail designs don't actually fit on your whole nail (unless you have really small hands, or are a child). I learned that if I'm very precise on how I stamp, I can get away with stamping the "full nail" designs from my index to my pinky. Thumb is definitely out of the question, lest you end up with a half stamped thumb, or you're good enough to stamp in two halves (you have to really match up the designs). I've never had this problem with Konad designs.
Fourthly, some of my plates arrived with water and rust stains. I don't understand how this happens, since there is a thin Saran type film ontop the plates when you first get them. This must mean that the plates have somehow gotten wet during manufacturing process, but before the packaging process. This doens't interfere with the stamping process, but I just find it annoying to have rust and water marks on my plates.
Overall, these plates are dirt cheap (under a dollar for each plate), compared to Konad which are between $4-7 per plate, but you get what you pay for, and Bundle Monster plates definitely do NOT compare to the quality of Konad's.

rating 5.0. Excellent quality! The designs are diverse and the system is simple to use. I will never buy another Konad plate again. I really do agree with the other reviewers. BundleMonster needs to make more plates!

rating 4.0. stampy I was really excited to try this product because I think nail designs are cute. I've never done anything like it before and didn't want to spend a lot of money on Konad only to find I didn't like it. Well, I think that the variety of designs you get with this are fab.

However, the full nail images are small. They may cover my full pinky nail, but that's it. When testing them out I found it impossible to use regular nail polish for the image. I tried a nail art polish, as well, and it worked a bit better but I feel like stamping polish is probably best. Overall, I think it's a really great deal for the money.

UPDATE 5/16/11
I'm changing my rating from 3 to 4 stars. I find that these stamps do work well and print with regular polish. However, the regular polish must pass the 1 coat to opaque test. Sally Hansen Insta-Dry works really well. So, it really is a great deal. I'm not making it 5 stars because the full nail images are still small.

rating 5.0. Well duh. I was so close to returning these today after having them a month and getting more and more irritated that they weren't picking up the nail polish or working. Peeled it off and all the scratches came off too of course. Now I'm itching to get back to painting. So please, peel off that layer.

rating 2.0. Great transfer, but full nail images are way too small. Once I figured out the film cover over the plates, I was really happy with how well they transfer. I've been trying to learn how to double stamp to cover the whole nail, but the trial and error is a serious process and I simply don't have that kind of time. It's a shame, too, as I prefer the whole nail designs over the smaller designs. Half of the images are useless to me thanks to the small size.

I see my entire Konad system collecting dust simply because I can't do what I want to with them.

current_product_reviews: rating 1.0. I WANTED to like these but... These just don't work well...  In theory, I LOVE them, but the acetone evaporates before gel polish will dissolve... I'd buy the silicone "Soakies" instead and see if those do a better job at preventing the evaporation.  I haven't used these on regular nail polish because it comes off so easily anyway... so, If they don't last long enough to dissolve gels, I can't endorse them... I also tried wrapping the fingers in foils and using a regular thick cotton round ( both cut and folded).  Still, the acetone evaporated before the gel was coming off... and the very thin pads included wouldn't work AT ALL... I even took a bottle of acetone, warmed it  (carefully) in a pan of hot water & soaked cotton pads, wrapped fingers in foils & continually re-wetted with additional acetone... THAT finally worked, but by that point, I could have just done it without the clips.  And, for self-use they are less than worthless...  trying to get them on and keep working is comedic. Additionally, a lot of toes won't fit at all because the clips just aren't large enough.

rating 5.0. I am reviewing BOTH the curling iron and the 2-in-1 straightening iron/brush REVIEW FOR THE CURLING IRON:  The curling iron is THE BEST ever. It gives my hair the most amazing curls. You can brush out your hair countless times and the curls will remain for 3 days (without hairspray). It is such a great iron, way better than very expensive irons I've owned! Don't let the inexpensive price fool you- this is a GREAT iron! I don't know how I lived without this iron! My hair has the most incredible volume and body after I use this iron. It makes my hair look so THICK, shiny, and bouncy. The small clip is genius, it makes the iron so much easier to use than a clipless wand. You can make such a variety of curls too depending on how and where you wrap the curls around the iron. My hair is long, so some curls I will start at the ends of my hair and some curls I will start at the root of my hair (the shaft of the iron is a little short, so you need to wrap hair tightly in order to fit long hair strands). I love that the temperature locks too (so many irons I've used in the past, I end up hitting the buttons accidentally and I shut off the iron). I recommend this iron to everyone who compliments my hair. Giving this iron a very enthusiastic 5 stars!!

REVIEW FOR THE 2-IN-1 STRAIGHTENER/ BRUSH:

I liked the ceramic hair straightener/ hot brush, but it seemed defective because it made a loud electrical buzzing sound while turned on. Wanted a replacement iron, but I didn't see that option, so I returned the iron. Customer service was super fast, responsive, and excellent. Giving the iron 3 stars, but I am tempted to order another one and see if I have better luck because I absolutely love the other curling iron.

rating 3.0. If it wasn't for the sprayer tops this would be a really great product! There are many pros to this pack of Cobalt Blue Glass Bottles:
- The bottles themselves are a great value, very sturdy, and exactly as the seller described them.
- I love that the pack comes with the chalk board labels. I'm in love with anything rustic and chalk board labels are very high on my list. This being part of the listing was what made me click to buy this pack. The labels are waterproof, very sticky, and over all exactly how I would expect them to be quality wise.
- This pack also comes with a pipette. The pipette is large which is perfect for transferring lots of essential oil, carrier oils, and other natural cleaning concoctions into the bottles. Its a little flimsy and a feel like my EOs are going to eat it for lunch but all in all exactly as I would expect.
- Also, the bottles come packaged very nicely. You do not have to worry about these little guys breaking in transit because much care is taken to package them.

The Cons to this pack:
-The big disappointment for me on these bottles was the sprayer tops. They are a fine mist sprayer which is nice if you want to spritz yourself with a smelly yummy but if you are trying to clean anything larger then a pair of glasses this can be quite annoying. I tried to clean my outdoor furniture cushions and it took me FOREVER and I thought my finger was going to fall off! Then when I went to clean the furniture again I pushed down on the sprayer and the sprayer stuck. I had to raise it up manually and then squirt, raise it manually and then squirt. Needless to say I was not so thrilled with that.

So in conclusion I think these bottles are still a great value, however, I recommend buying different tops to go with them.
(I took a picture of the stuck annoying sprayer top.)

rating 5.0. This is a great toner, it broke me out a little at ... This is a great toner, it broke me out a little at first because my sensitive skin wasn't use to it, but after a week or so my skin is fine.

rating 5.0. Highly recommend this company'S products. First, I want to say I bought this at full price....period!

I've been very lax over years, at any kind of skin care routine. My skin isn't really showing any effects of bad skin care, because I was blessed with really good skin, but....I'm 52 now and decided I really shouldn't push it any longer. So; I purchased Tree of Life Beauty Vitamin C wash first. I was very impressed with the ingredient list, the amount used to wash my face (neck, chest, and shoulders as well), and the way my skin felt afterwards.

So when I saw the toner was 93% organic, I decided buy the Tree of life Beauty Toner, to go along with the wash. First of all, I really am impressed with the ingredients this company uses. I grow all organics herbs, berries, vegetables, etc. and use as many organic ingredients that I can. I would love to be a purist, but my budget says "NO!" This company doesn't use a lot of needless filler ingredients to stretch the product. So when you think of the product being only 8 oz, you can be assured that 8 oz (when used properly), is a good amount of product that will last. I can't say how long yet, as I've only been using it a week.

Secondly, being an herbalist has taught me that your skin is your largest organ! You should be careful what you put on it. Your pores soak up every last drop of whatever you put on your skin. Parabens are absolute poison and should be avoided like the plague! (Sorry all of you Vicks and vasaline lovers out there) From what I've read about Tree of life Beauty, we're on the same page and I don't feel I need to be concerned about them hiding any in their products.

And finally, Vitamin C is essential to your body in too many ways to go into. But I take a vitamin C daily to help with the absorption of Collagen 1&3 (Which is important for hair, skin, nails, bones, joints, etc). I had not realized the benefits of vitamin C for your face. I absolutely love the way my skin feels after using the cleanser and toner. I let it dry well, then follow up with moisturizer both morning and evening.

So far, I'm quite happy with Tree of Life Beauty products and will probably transition to more of their products as I use up what I already have. We'll see how it goes after using these items for 30 days.

The gel basecoat prevents any products I use from staining my nails, and it protects my nails from anything I put on them! The topcoat cures quickly and protects my polish and nail art under a thick, glossy layer of gel, which has a beautiful hard shiny finish. I also like that these are no wipe, which makes them easier to use since there is no sticky layer after curing!

rating 5.0. Great The bottom coat and top coat both went on smoothly. Not lumpy, not too thick. Very easy to use. Exactly what I was looking for. Any imperfections were my fault because I was a little lazy taking off the previous polish and with the application.

rating 5.0. I can see my reflection from the top coats glossiness! [[VIDEOID:7e314eb68d3d14994f418f96e03962ca]] Okay so besides 1 gel top coat I've bought ages ago and used before I've never played with gels all that much because they've seem complicated and messy and I was more accustomed to normal nail polish. However! These are phenomenal, they are rubbery enough that they don't glide all over and over fill my cuticles. The base coat does a great job at adhering to the gel polishes I used (also from LagunaMoon) and the top coat is so unbelievably glossy I could see the clouds reflected on them! Spectacular product!

rating 2.0. No shrinking but came lumpy/hardened When I opened the bottle, there was dried pieces of polish around the edges (maybe it had opened a little?). I thought the polish itself was going to be okay but when I used the top coat it had little clumps/crystals in it and was hard to put the top coat on smoothly. Besides that issue, I like this brand a lot more than the last one I tried because it doesn't shrink away from the edges at all as it cures.

rating 1.0. Disappointing waste of money Very disappointed in this item.  Everything seemed OK until I put the top coat on.  No matter what I did, it seemed too thick and never cured all of the way.  For at least two days it felt tacky and smelt and tasted like I had rubber cement on my fingers. It also lost its shine in less than a day. Waste of money.  Ended up ordering another brand that works much better.

rating 5.0. No Wipe Top Coat! REALLY WORKS! This nail polish was awesome to use, I cured it for 90 seconds each time with a LED light. The base coat wasn't gloopy or gross, it applied easily. The smell wasn't overpowering either.
The top coat on the other hand was a bit thinner, and it smelled. It wasn't too bad but it was very weird for a nail polish lol. I loved that it's a "No Wipe" Top Coat. I feel like having alcohol is just too much stuff when I do my nails. My polish wasn't sticky or tacky when I finished.
I haven't had the polish peel off yet. I really love this!
And the price can't be beat! You get the Top and Base Coat for less than $10!

rating 1.0. Cheap, gummy, doesn’t cure correctly. The polish itself stinks pretty bad even after curing under the UV.
The Base coat never completely cures. I did a thin layer and sat under the UV longer and longer even with multiple sessions to see if it got better....and it still remains gummy and sticky to the touch.
Adding gel polish is a waste as it never sets up correctly and the manicure falls apart after just a day or so.
Very disappointed considering the reviews. I find this cheap and worse on the nails as peeling screws up the nail.
A good base coat and gel polish is solid, it chips after weeks of wear and is hard, not quite as hard as acrylic but very protective on the nail.
This garbage peels within the first few days of application. Has nothing to do with it touching the sides/skin as mine peeled from the tops as well as sides and bottom, which the parts that peel, the polish is rubbery when it peels off no where near hard.
Do NOT recommend this and it’s a waste of money to use it with nicer gel polish as it’ll be falling off way before it should.
rating 2.0. Not the best I have used! Base coat works fine but top coat stays tacky and lack luster. One of the worst brands I have used yet.

rating 3.0. Overall it's a great product and made drying my brushes after washing so ... Very strong smell upon opening. The protective covering is quite difficult to get off of the pieces. I had to soak the pieces in warm soapy water to even attempt to get it off and wash it several times and soak it some more to get the smell off. The smell is weak now and didn't transfer to my brushes when I used them. Overall it's a great product and made drying my brushes after washing so much easier and organized. Once you get the covers off and get rid of the smell it'd very useful. It doesn't stay together perfectly but still helpful.

rating 5.0. Works great!! Sturdy Works great!! Sturdy, easy to assemble and can be stored flat. All brushes of every size fit on here with no problem thanks to the silicone grips. Highly recommend. Definitely beats laying flat on a towel to dry.

rating 5.0. I love this item because I can hang a lot of ... I love this item because I can hang a lot of brushes on it and leave it to dry. I like the idea that it is hanging upside down so that it will dry faster. So far I'm happy with this item. The only tip I can give is that evenly distribute the brushes because it does have a tendency to fall over if you have heavy brushes that are only placed on one side.

rating 4.0. Just like the other reviews said to do I soaked it ... When I first opened the brush stand I was a little confused at the brown paper coating that covered both pieces of the stand. Just like the other reviews said to do I soaked it in soapy water for a few minutes and it all peeled off so no harm done. It is a little wobbly but was very impressed that my huge tarte brush fit perfectly!

rating 5.0. Wonderful brush tree! I was initially looking at the benjabelle brush tree, but found this one for cheaper. Yes, there is brown paper stuck to the item, and you do need to run it under water and then it peels off.I would actually pay extra to not have to do that, but once the paper comes off, the brush tree sets up easily. It is fairly sturdy and hasn't fallen apart. I have used big heavy brushes in the larger holes and it has worked out great. A wonderful option if you don't want to spend $20+ on a brush tree. I have been waiting to buy this for a long time because when is a brush tree necessary? I am glad I did, because I think it will help extend the life of my more expensive brushes.

rating 5.0. Very handy little product! Love this brush tree! It's a hard plastic, and comes wrapped in a sticker-like coating that looks like brown paper and peels off. It's difficult to find a brush tree to hold this many brushes and still store away neatly. The tree is in 3 pieces, and easily comes apart. It stands just tall enough that my larger brushes aren't touching the counter when sitting upside down in the tree. It's really a simple product, very functional, a great price, and exactly as described. It's also sturdy when standing. I would buy another one in a heartbeat.

rating 3.0. Meh... The idea is cute, but I'm not sure it's worth all the work. I felt like I spent more time in my wash process using this holder. I won't say I'll never use it again, but I wouldn't jump to pull it out (one pro is that it's collapsible so putting this away is a breeze).
I also felt it needed more smaller holes for smaller brushes. They should also manufacture this as a set, with one solely for large brushes and a second that is designed for smaller brushes. This way, more variety is offered, essentially increasing efficiency of the product.

rating 4.0. Works well This works as advertised. It holds my brushes of all sizes. My only complaint is I wish it was a little taller. rating 5.0. I LOVE this tree I LOVE this tree!! How did I not know about these until recently? Very convenient and efficient use of space. Easily holds all my brushes with a few spaces to spare. Stand doesn't wobble or appear uneven. Definitely recommend.

rating 4.0. Great to dry brushes I always put off washing my makeup brushes because o never have an easy way to dry them that won't deform the brush heads....until now!  I ordered this after some research and reading the good reviews.  It's incredibly easy to assemble and had a variety of sizes of slots.  It worked well and i am happy with it but if touched the wrong way the brushes will swing to the side and not stay straight down but if you set it up and put it where it won't be touched or bothered it's fine!  It also needs to be on a stable surface, i tried to put it on a my sink but it had a slight curve and kept collapsing.  When i placed it on my shelf and no problems.

rating 5.0. Absolutely Love! This thing is amazing! I used to have to lay my brushes on a towel to dry after I washed them and that was always a pain because they would roll off and it took forever to dry. And some brushes would get damaged drying that way because some of water would stay at the base. With this holder they stay in place and they dry so fast!! I washed my brushes today at 10am and it is now 7:25pm and about 90% of my brushes are completely dry!!!! That's the best part for me. The only down side to this is that pesky covering that it has that you have to run under water in order to peel it. But it kept the holde from getting scratched and it has a very sleep finish once it's off. The other thing that I wasn't fond about is that it's a little wobbly. Since it is a collapsible item that makes it super convenient for storing it is going to be a little wobbly. At least that is what I told myself. But overall I love this holder and I a sourly recommend it.

Oh I had to double up some slots because I ran out of space for my brushes that is why there is more than one brush in a slot. I have to purchase another one 😄

rating 5.0. Great for anyone who washes multiple makeup brushes! This brush tree came quickly and as described. It was a little bit smaller in person than I expected, but that's probably because I did not read the dimensions very carefully, but it's not a problem for me. Like others said, it comes covered in brown paper, which easily peels off if you follow the directions (it even comes with it) to run it under warm water, then peel it off at a corner. Yes, it may be annoying to some, but it keeps the acrylic black shiny and scratch-free until you use it. It has multiple holes of different sizes, and is sturdy when put together (putting it together takes about 2 seconds). It is a good size as it can fit many brushes, but it is still a compact product, so it will not take up your entire bathroom counter while using it. It could also be a cool way to store/display your makeup brushes and have them keep their shape (versus throwing them into a cup/holder/drawer). Overall, I think it is a good value for anyone who uses several makeup brushes and washes them, as I never had a place to put all my wet brushes and the shapes sometimes got messed up- but now I don't have that problem!

rating 4.0. Easy Solution for Drying Brushes I blight this drying rack because it had a good price and good reviews.

When you take this out of the packaging, you need to need to take off paper tap from the pieces. Luckily for me, they came off easily. If you have any issues, use a bit of water. I did wash the pieces before using because they had a bjt of a smell (plastic?).

I washesban assortment of brushes. I put them all face up while I was cleaning and when I finished, I picked up the top piece and flipped it so the brushes were upside down.

The brushes took a bit of time to dry (12+) but the long time could be because of where I live and the lack of heat/light.

Overall, I like this product a lot and will be using it every time I clean my brushes. My biggest problem with the rack is the size of the brush holes. I have some thin brushes that could use smaller holes. Also my oval brushes had some issues in here.

My favorite thing is that it's easily to transport or put away. This can be assembled/disassembled in 30 seconds. It also doesn't feel like it's going to fall apart any second when it's filled with your brushes on the table.

rating 5.0. Awesome Inexpensive Brush Tree! After finally investing in a couple of professional brush sets, I decided to get a stand that would hang them to dry properly. Normally I prop my cleaned brushes up at an angle to dry, but I figured my Wayne Goss and Billy B's needed to hang upside down. Was going to bite the bullet and spend 3X as much on a name brand tree but happened upon this little gem here on Amazon. What luck! Full disclosure: I have not used a Benjabelle drying tree or seen one up close so I can't speak to it's relative size or dimensions. What I can say is that this stand holds a great variety of brush brands securely. I own Goss, Billy B, Real Techniques, MAC, It Cosmetics, Morphe, Sonja Kashuk, Sephora, Luxie, Crown, Ecotools, and a few Wet 'n Wild. All hang nicely in the stand and dry like champs. Even the super skinny travel sized brushes and eye brushes don't slip out. The base is basically a folding "X" configuration and the flat "table" of the hanger sits atop. It's sturdy enough to do the job but it definitely won't stand up to a hard bump of the vanity, a wayward cat, or a curious kid. Stick your brushes in, set it up where it won't be disturbed, and enjoy your perfectly dried makeup brushes. YAY

This stand comes with a sticky brown overlay on all exposed parts. Takes a couple minutes to remove. Plastic surface beneath is nice and shiny. This stand also cleans up well. Great functional item, I will likely buy more just for hanging storage. Fast shipping, excellent seller communication. Highly recommend.

input: rating 4.0. Smells good.  I reckon that's the point... I'm on this crazy nostalgia kick so I figured I'd try one of the oldest colognes/perfumes (it's unisex, ya know) around.  Gotta say, this has a very pleasant smell and is not overpowering at all.  In fact, if anything, it might fade just a tad too quickly for some.  It's cheap.  Give it a try!

rating 5.0. This stuff is great I use this Florida Water during our hot Louisiana summers while playing golf.  We also use it at the course when we host other clubs and everyone really enjoys the refreshing, cooling effects it gives.  I just wish it was a different scent.  The smell is a bit strong.  I have used other products like this that were fruity smelling which is better for me.  I do highly recommend this stuff....just don't use too much of it.  It will take your breath away.
rating 5.0. No Zombies!! My luck has improved, and I haven't seen a zombie in months...

rating 4.0. Interesting old formula Like someone else, I purchased this because I read that Lucille Ball's last words were "bring me my Florida Water". Personally, I like douse my pillows and blankets with it more than wear it on my body.  I like it a lot but it's not a substitute for perfume or cologne.

rating 5.0. Great, just as described Great, just as described. First time trying Florida water and its everything i hoped it would be. A nice mixture of medicinal, spice, and citrus with just a hint of floral. Would've been nice in a glass bottle but i ain't complaining. Will be buying more.

rating 1.0. There's only one thing this stuff is good for - sprinkling it in the toilet after a ... There's only one thing this stuff is good for - sprinkling it in the toilet after a healthy #2 to cover up any offensive odors that might be lingering.

rating 5.0. Just like I remember it... My grandparents are from Puerto Rico and used to keep this stuff around for all sorts of things.  They put it on bug bites, burns stings and occasionally my grandfather used it as aftershave.  I myself am using it as aftershave and have loved it so far!  The smell is great and hasn't caused any allergy issue with anyone.  The smell doesn't last for ever but that is not that big of an issue with me.

rating 5.0. Interesting scent - grows on you and is addictive Interesting scent - sweet/citrus/floral with a spice dry down (cloves and something I can't quite place). Doesn't last long, but I didn't expect that for the price and formulation. I added some witch hazel and menthol to make it into a nice aftershave - I've heard many use this for ceremonial uses as well. I find myself reapplying in the middle of the day to get a boost.

rating 5.0. A perfectly refreshing facial and body splash. I used ... A perfectly refreshing facial and body splash.  I used to buy this at Walgreen's or CVS in the "hispanic" section, but I haven't seen if for several years.  Found it on Amazon, ordered a few bottles, and I am enjoying it during the hot Chicago summer.  Very citrusy and cooling, I keep some in the fridge in a plastic spray bottle and just spritz it on, and it actually makes me feel a few degrees cooler, not to say cleaner.  As it is not a "heavy" scent, you can use a lot and it's not overpowering.  Enjoying my "Florida Water Experience"...!!!

rating 5.0. Good Stuff - Doesn't have an old fashion smell at all. Good stuff.  I have never bought any of the "classic" aftershave products like Murray& Lanman or Clubman or Old Spice.  I thought I would give M&L Florida Water a try after many years of using various modern stuff.  I have to say that M&L Florida Water is pretty good stuff.  It has a very nice smell.  Actually kind of mild.  Not overpowering at all.  Not an old-fashion smell either.  The formula for this cologne/aftershave is nearly 200 years old - but the smell seems to be current.  It actually could be a little bit stronger, as the scent seems to fade after a few hours.  Overall I am happy with M&L.

rating 5.0. I Imagine Ernest Hemingway Used This Stuff For starters, I live and play in Florida and just for kicks, I bought this as a novelty item.

Much to my surprise, I became quite fond of the scent and invigorating freshness of the cologne. It has a sweet orange citrus base with hints of lavender and clove. The name refers to the fabled Fountain of Youth which was said to be located in Florida as well as the "flowery" nature of the scent. After all, Florida means "Feast of Flowers." So, the name of the cologne is certainly fitting.

Large quantities of Florida Water were used by barbershops as cologne and aftershave. In the 1880s and 1890s Murray & Lanman Florida Water was advertised as "The Richest of all Perfumes" and "The most Popular Perfume in the World"

Like other colognes of the era, Florida Water was valued for its refreshing and tonic nature as well as its scent, and could be used as a skin toner or as what we would now call a "body splash". This is my number one use of Florida Water- as a body splash; particularly during the hot summer months in Florida.

My second means of using this product is by mixing it with iced water and dipping a clean rag in and wiping the neck and body for a refreshing cooling effect.

It's not just aftershave. It's also a refreshing body splash with that sweet citrus, yet manly smell.

rating 5.0. Outstanding scent and refreshing as claimed... I have been on a recent kick of purchasing classic scents. It all started when my last bottle of Caswell Massey Lime aftershave ran out. I then started the hunt for more of it and other classic barbershop/old time scents.This is with out a doubt one of the finest. It has a scent thats hard to explain. Its clean, fresh and full of citrus spice. I wish the scent would hold a little longer, but at 5$ for 7.5 ounces who cares. Just splash on some more. It has an amazing cooling effect and makes you feel awesome when trying to beat the summer heat. Just ordered 3 more bottles.
rating 5.0. It's all true! The reviews on this product are dead on. This stuff has a wonderful citrus/clove smell and is subtle and refreshing. Definitely a steal for the price.

rating 5.0. Love the smell Love the smell!  Very refreshing when using after shaving.  There are some reviews where people mix it with ice water and spray it on themselves in the summer, I will have to try that if it ever warms up here.

rating 4.0. Fun stuff! I bought this stuff just for the label...it looks so old-timey and weird, plus it came highly recommended as a skincare aid from a girlfriend.

It's mostly just scented alcohol...but I've been using it as an astringent toner, and it does make your face feel fresh and tingly. I don;t know that it's had any real effect on my complexion, though...I sitll broke out when I got my period :/

rating 5.0. You either love it or hate it If you like bay rum, then you'll like Florida Water. If you don't like bay rum after a hot shave then something is wrong with you. Turn in your man card immediately.
rating 5.0. Time travel in a bottle A light, refreshing scent.  Unfortunately doesn't last long; however, if it did then it wouldn't be light!  I will buy again.

rating 5.0. GREAT OLD-SCHOOL STUFF ! Manly-man scent which seems to be strong at first but dissipates quickly.  I find this stuff invigorating on several levels: 1) the label artwork is very cool;  2) love the shape of the bottle;  3)  that first splash gives a great ZING!

rating 5.0. Good product I have always liked this fragrance, especially in the summer.  It's cool and refreshing, and it's not too heavy.  It also makes a great facial astringent.

rating 2.0. Like a granny's bathroom Like a granny's bathroom. Not offensive but not a smell I want on my body. More like a dollar store deodorizer or stale 7up.

I've used it though for everyday wear.  It's not too feminine or masculine.  It's just perfect and very reasonably priced here.

rating 5.0. HEAVENLY This is the most awesome smelling cologne, perfume, water, which ever title you personally wish to give this item, that I have bought in a long time. My husband used it after shaving, my grandaughter uses it on her face, I splash it on my hands throughout the day. Need to get more soon.

rating 4.0. and smells like a mixture of camphor I don't know quite what to think of this fragrance. It's VERY strong, and smells like a mixture of camphor, clove, orange, and a subtle floral hint. It's not that it smells bad--- quite the contrary. But it will open up your sinuses for sure! :)
rating 5.0. Just the thing I love this! Light, cooling remains gently on the skin, a perfect summer eau de cologne in the old fashioned sense. Perfect for men or women

rating 1.0. Good price but not for me I bought this based on other reviewers comparing this to Bay Rum. There is a slight resemblance in the smell, however this product smells much more like a perfume than an after shave, I could still smell it on my hands a couple of days after using. I ended up pouring it out and given the price I was fine with this. Stick with the classic barber after shaves and the original Bay Rum.

rating 5.0. Chase away evil spirits and smell good while doing it! You get a lot more than what you pay for with this product. It smells awesome! And, after doing some research, it's also used in many religious ceremonies. Nice! It must be in part to the nice, light mixture of citrus and floral scents. It's great.
rating 2.0. Strong The scent is much stronger than I expected it to be and a bit spicy for me. Some will like it though.

rating 5.0. which I like very much After receiving the Murray & Lanman Orange  Blossom product, which I like very much, I received the Florida Water cologne and soap.  I haven't tried the soap yet, but based on fragrance alone, I'm giving it 5 stars.  I became interested in the Florida Water after I read that the last thing Lucille Ball said before she passed on to her reward was "Bring me my Florida Water."  Well, having read that, I was naturally quite interested in knowing how she smelled when she died.  I'm quite satisfied that she turned a few heads when she passed through the Pearly Gates.  5 Stars.  I love this stuff!!

rating 5.0. Mild and not overpowering I love Florida water by Murray and Lanman it has such an apealing fragrance its mild and not overpowering. I have gotten many complements and people ask me all the time what I have on. For me, Florida Water is my everyday go to item. Wont leave the house without it. I even carry a little spray bottle of it in my pocket so I can re apply when ever I want a pick me up.

rating 5.0. Mmm... Florida Water. This stuff smells great! I'm no old man, either. It's fresh, clean, & unique. The smell reminds me of someone from my childhood, but I can't place exactly who... It just brings me nice, but vague memories. I have gotten quite a few compliments since I started wearing it. I now put it on each morning & plan on ordering the smaller bottle to keep with me in my backpack so I can freshen up on the go. I think this is officially the only cologne I will buy from now on... Why spend fifty plus bucks on something that makes you smell like a chick?! Be a man & invest (hah!) in some good ole Florida Water!

rating 5.0. Yes I would recommend this to friends and family the product came early and the water has a peppery/spicy cent to it. Spicier than "Bombay rum" aftershave and last lounger as well. Doesn't have that aftershave sting however...... keep in mind that it isn't marketed as an aftershave, rather it's a cologne.... so the sent  DOES  last longer but doesn't have that stinging clean of an aftershave. Yes I would recommend this to friends and family.

rating 4.0. beautiful product mostly got to love it because it's such a classic product.  you can definitely smell the cinnamon, neroli and rose.  some orange blossom and lavender as well.  highly recognizable as a cologne, i find it works best to dilute it when using on the person.  works well in bath.  a must have for shamanistic ceremonies, it's reputed to clean the body and area of negative spirits and bring positive energy.

rating 4.0. Not quite perfect but very good I've had more alcohol heavy florida waters that are crisper as an aftershave. This is the do-everything product we latinos have kept secret for years. Can be used as an aftershave, deoderant, cologne or cleaning product.... and supposedly it brings good luck too...but I've had plenty of good and bad luck without it so don't rely too heavily.

This one is okay, but there are better out there. Inexpensive and ships right to your house...what more can you want. Smells better than some $50+ USD colognes. Give it a try.

rating 4.0. Try it as a pre- or after-shave with your electric razor. I use the stuff as pre- and after-shave with my electric razor. As a pre-shave, it provides a little lubricant and adds a little comfort to the process. As an after-shave, it doesn't sting or irritate the skin like most after-shaves do. The scent is very pleasant (my wife likes it). It's spicier than I expected, which is fine with me. This is not a cologne, though, as far as I'm concerned; the scent doesn't linger like true cologne would. It's just a refreshing, spicy addition to the clean-up routine.

rating 5.0. Glad I found Florida Water I just plain like this stuff. The smell doesn't overwhelm, or last too long, but talk about astringent after shower and shave.
I hose off on my patio after yard work about 3 times a day, and I keep a bottle of this Florida Water on the back porch to use after each hosedown.
You'd have to be a guy to understand.
rating 3.0. Not for me I got this product after hearing so many great reviews about it. I assume it service its purpose but the smell is too masculine. I gave it to my brother.

rating 5.0. A fresh, bracing scent Smells very strongly at first, but smells so refreshing when it dries. Smells like juniper, lemon, rosemary, oranges, & clove to me. I really like it, but it's definitely more a masculine scent.

current_product_reviews: rating 5.0. Clean and happy My wife says It smells clean; like soap.  I bought it for that reason so I couldn't ask for more.

input: rating 5.0. It works!  Non-kink oxygen tubing. People who depend on portable oxygen will know how difficult it can be to find tubing which does not "kink"--tangling in on itself, blocking the air-flow, and really becoming a nuisance.  The manufacturers of this product describe it as "non-kink" and they tell the truth.  It is refreshing to find tubing which behaves itself, even when extended to the maximum medically recommended length of 50'.  When, after a while, it does begin to tangle, it's time to turn to new tubing anyway.  I was skeptical when ordering this,  but it really does work.

rating 4.0. Good quality I like to change out my hose often - I can get free ones from my medical provider but the quality of this one is much better - fewer tangles and fewer problems and that's a good thing. I'll keep buying these.
rating 4.0. good item I like this item was exactly as described the only problem was I had was is still a little excessive in the tangled up area. I truly believe is more my fault the air hose

rating 2.0. It's cheap and works It's a great price but not the quality I was looking for. It kinks easily and isn't as thick as higher quality tubing. It does work just a little of a hassle when rolling it up and you have to be careful to lay it out so it won't kink on you.

rating 2.0. green? its not that the tubbing does not work it does. but i did not want green.and no wherewas it listed that it might come in that color.for if it did i would not of ordered it. does any oneeven wonder how this effects a person who is on oxygen?i think not.

rating 1.0. Won't get this again if I don't have to Too narrow a gauge for starters, crushes enough to obliterate flow with the least pressure, and kinks and knots up like crazy even though I'm using swivel connectors. Starts to stiffen up at as high as 60 degrees F and I hate to think what it will be like when it's exposed to even colder temps come Fall and Winter months. If you have the choice to not buy this, don't.
rating 3.0. gets caught on everything i need to use the o2 24/7 and the tubing goes with me wherever i go. it seems to get caught on all the furniture. wish there was a larger tubing.

rating 1.0. Poor Quality This tubing is very poor quality. It is yellowish I color and looks aged. It is also thin walled and kinks very easily, so much so that I had to discard it. Would not recommend unless you like throwing money away.

rating 4.0. American Bantex Hose Does It's Job Product was wrapped and coiled neatly. Had no problems with hooking it up. I will continue to use this style of tubing due to ease in use and availability.

rating 2.0. Worry free oxygen tubing Having been on oxygen 24/7 for 5 years now, I have always had difficulty with the tubing kinking. This product said it did not kink so I bought it. Sadly, it does kink, not as bad as the other, but you realy must keep a close eye on it.
rating 5.0. Sturdy tubing My mom is on oxygen and needed longer tubing for her large machine. This gave her a lot of freedom around the house and even to go outside

current_product_reviews: rating 5.0. Good hose unless you have cats This is a decent quality hose that stands up well to normal use going around the house for a constant oxygen wearer. I will say that if you have cats that like to paw at things that move or chew, they do fall victim to that pretty hardcore and will need replacing. And then from the EMT point of view, there's always the fact that when you filter oxygen down 50ft of hose, you're not getting the high-quality oxygen you're looking for, nor are you getting the pressure you have your concentrator set on by the time it travels that far to reach you. But the hose itself is good for the money you pay for it, and mom is happy with hers, so will probably be ordering some more.
other_product_reviews: rating 5.0. Very awesome! These are awesome tats. Very pretty and great quality. Customer service is awesome and they did get them to me a day before their expected arrival when I chose expedited shipping. My friend loves her share of them as she's a huge Sugar skull freak.

rating 2.0. Well made but HUGE! This may be a high quality brush, but personally I have problems with it. First, it's huge! Way bigger than any other mens hair brush I have ever seen. Maybe it would soften a bit over time but it's not likely to get that time since I won't be using it. Remember this is a comment on my personal opinion, not on the quality of the brush. It may be very well made, but it's not what I want in a hair brush.

Idk if it's just this brand or if all boar head brushes have these issues but I probably won't re-order.

rating 3.0. Everything's great except the smell The bristles and shape are as pictured, which is good. My only complaint is the smell. There's a strong burnt rubber smell, presumably from the bristle backing, that hasn't aired out, so I'm going to try washing it. It doesn't transfer to the hair while brushing thankfully, but I get a whiff of it every time I pick it up to use it. I can handle it but I don't have any allergies or a super-sensitive nose, other's may find it too much to bear.

rating 4.0. Works well I have never had a boar bristle brush and this takes some getting used to. The bristles are too soft to really penetrate my thick curly hair (and I think they are too densely grouped), but I'm just brushing firmly and in sections and it's working fine that way. It is very smoothing and works to distribute the oils. Seems like a quality brush, though hard to use. It MAY grow on me.

rating 2.0. Does not work well with long or thickish hair Does not work well with long or thickish hair. The bristles are VERY short, barely touches my scalp. Definitely does not get the tangles out of mine or my girls' hair. On the bright side, it does make your hair very smooth. I would like to try another sometime, with longer bristles, but I'm still not too sure it would help at all with tangles...
rating 1.0. This is a terrible product. It doesn't get the tangles out of ... This is a terrible product. It doesn't get the tangles out of my hair, it just slips over them. Do not buy this product!

rating 3.0. I enjoy the scalp stimulation I wanted to replace the Boar Bristle Paddle Hair Brush that I had purchased a few years ago.  The new brush looks the same but the bristles are soft and not the same as supplied by Gran Naturals.  How can I get the "stiffer" version?  I enjoy the scalp stimulation.

rating 2.0. Would not recommend for long straight hair or pets Brush very very dense. Too dense to grab anything. Creates a lot of frizz and static when used on long hair since it only brushes the very surface. Bristles are unevenly cut: some are 2-3x longer than the rest. Some bristles fall out especially with washing. Don’t know what type of hair it could be used for: definitely not long straight hair. Tried it on my dog- just smoothens the top, still doesn’t get rid of any tangles & still creates a lot of static. Smells bad- Tried washing it a few times and still smells bad. Tried spraying on other scents and the boar smell is very overpowering and makes hair smell that way too.

rating 4.0. Rethink your brush! It's a hairbrush that's made out of hair. Holy moly! Want to know what it's like to brush your hair with hair? Well buddy boy have I got a brush for you, it's this one! Its hair that the fine people at GranNaturals cleverly affixed to a piece of wood. But they didn't stop there, they then took that piece of hairy wood and carved a handle at the bottom. And talk about ergonomics, that sucker feels almost as good in your bald hand as it does on your hairy head! This isn't your grandpa's hairbrush, RIP. This is the new face of hair. Flat, lifeless hair? Buy this double hairbrush. Split ends and a receding hairline? That's entirely your fault and you deserve it chrome dome. Get the only/best/original GranNaturals hairy brush. And if that's not enough to convince you listen to this. Sick of all the brushes in your life. Who can keep track? Well throw out that old brush barrel and make the last brush purchase you'll ever make. Hair feels great on any hair. Heres a LLH(lady life hack) comb down from the top of your head with the GranNaturals Hairbrush Hairbrush, down the sideburns to the beard, over to the neck, around the back down the chest and straight down the "Happy Trail". Lord loves a hairy woman and a hairy woman loves a hairy brush.

rating 2.0. Great for cats, not so much for humans. I should have returned this as it does not really work great on human hair and it has a really bad odor. rating 4.0. I have fine, wavy shoulder-length hair I have fine, wavy shoulder-length hair. Likes to get especially tangly around the back. Anyone else in this camp knows that finding products/brushes/styles for this type can be borderline excruciating, but this brush works very well!

Pros:
The "naturally conditions oil" claim seems to be a valid one, i do feel that i need to condition less frequently and oils are more evenly distributed from root to end using this product
Experiencing significantly less tearing, snarls and general despair than when brushing with other types
My hair is shiny and smooth, and I have to spend less time styling if i use this!

Cons:
Has less "grab" than non-bristle brushes; I often have to comb first or separate sections more than i would have to with a standard brush with longer "teeth." That being said, the end result is worth it!

rating 1.0. Not as advertised Not 100% boar brustle brush. It very obviously has plastic bristles. No way I coumd use this, it would likely rip my hair out. I offered it to my daughter. She looked it over and said, "No thanks." It's definitely a return.
rating 2.0. I didn't like it, I didn't like it,.  It did not work on my hair.  Since I used it on my hair i kept it and did not return it.  It will be stored in my drawer of unused brushes.

rating 5.0. but they feel great on my scalp The bristles on this brush are a bit more rigid than other boar bristle brushes I've owned, but they feel great on my scalp. I've seen other reviews that said the bristles aren't natural- I assure you, my brush is natural. There is a slight chemical odor to the brush, it comes from the mesh used to anchor the bristles. I imagine after a few washes the smell will fade- but even if it doesn't, it's not transferring to my hair...and since I don't particularly enjoy sniffing my hair brush I'm not too worried about this. I think the thing to remember is that although this cost a little more than a standard drugstore hairbrush- this is a boar bristle brush. You could easily drop $250 on a hand crafted boar bristle brush that is perfect in every way. Or... You can spend $13 on a boar bristle brush that has a faint odor. I've had several brushes in this price range and I assure you, none have been perfect and this one is just as good if not better than the others I've used.

rating 3.0. Too Soft and Too Big Although the bristles are fine, I ordered this item because "boar hair bristles" are supposed to be quite stiff. In fact, they were not included in the 'About The Product' section but further down the page in the 'Production Description' section. Meanwhile, I am very pleased with this brush regardless as to whether or not it can help with the thinning issues.

rating 3.0. Cool concept, but not as easy as it looks I looked through a lot of magnetic eyelashes, and these seemed to be the best reviewed for the best price. The packaging was in good shape when I got them, but it took me forever to figure out how to put them on

PROS:
- the double magnets on each end make it so neither of the ends flare out
-they are short so you can place them at the outer edge of your lashes to make them look more natural
-they are tapered which gives them an even more natural look
-they are not as cheap looking as some of their competitors
-they slide off easily without pulling out your lashes

CONS:
-even after practicing for over an hour, I still could not get the lashes to sit flush to my eyelid. No matter how hard I tried, there was always a 3 or 4 millimeter gap that was pretty noticeable. I tried having my friend put them on for me, and even with an extra pair of hands, it is still pretty impossible.
-I bought these because I thought they would be an easier, less messy alternative to traditional fake lashes. It's true that they aren't as messy, but they definitely are not easier. It takes me 10 times as long to get these things on compared to fake lashes.
-they don't look as natural as traditional fake lashes

Overall, if you can somehow figure out how to put these on accurately, I think they would be a good alternative. I just don't have the patience to spend an hour every day trying to get these to sit correctly.

The top and bottom are the same fullness so once on (joke) you look like you have an animal on your eyelid.  The magnetic strip is after all, metal so it is only on each end and is rigid. Trying to bend it to the curve of your eye makes it crack and eventually peel off.  I took my practice pair and removed the magnetic strips, trimmed them and used it with glue.  Honestly, stick with mascara.

input:
rating 3.0. Maybe a little harsh... My previous review was pretty scathing, so I'm going to revise it a bit here.

1) It's still true - after 4 applications, I've gotten nowhere with the Bare Balm as far as removing pubic hair. Interestingly enough, I accidentally removed some of my leg hair with the product in the process of working on the package. So the stuff does work, just not if you've got heavy, coarse hair down there.

2) Customer service did finally get back to me - apparently there was a breakdown of sorts delivering the message through the Amazon system. We're resolving the issue now, and their folks are quite personable once you get to talk to them a bit.

3) The Calm Balm is still a nice product, not a bad moisturizing cream at all.

Short version: Don't buy this if you have very coarse hair down there - it won't work, period.

rating 3.0. Remove product doesn't work as well. Lotion however is great Remove product doesn't work as well. Lotion however is great!

I'm giving 3 stars because of the lotion but im fairly disappointed that the removal cream doesn't work as I thought it would.

It removes hair but not all of them.

Nair Extra Strength seems to be a stronger and better product then this one.

Not to mention this product is highly overpriced i paiy 5 dollars for Nair 10 oz

rating 2.0. Rather use my wife's Veet... Even when you follow the instructions (wash the area, apply a thick layer, wait less than 15 minutes, "jump in a lukewarm shower", remove gently with a washcloth) it doesn't work as expected.

I tried it first in my chest, and even when I'm not a hairy guy I decided to trim it down with scissors. In my first session I left it for 10 minutes and it did a pretty mediocre job, so I reapplied it and waited for 15 minutes more. To make the story short, I ended up shaving with my razor because, in addition, the areas where it worked where not smooth, instead they felt like on the third day after shaving.
In a second session two weeks after the first (and with a lot less hair) I left it for 20 minutes and the story repeated.
If you consider that to get the foretold results I ended up rubbing it hard with the washcloth, I don't want to imagine what would happen when applied to "my pair".

Some months after, I decided to finish the product applying it to my glutes. This time it worked fine, but only after I waited ONE HOUR and scrubed with a back scrub brush.

The only PLUS is the "Calm balm". It feels great and really sooths your skin.

rating 1.0. Prepared my hair for shaving, little else. I've been using this product now for a few weeks and have had poor results. With each use, I've applied a generous amount on the areas I wanted to see hair removal. The first time I used it, I left it on for 10 minutes. Very little, if any, hair came out. It should also be noted that the hairy area had been trimmed maybe two weeks prior, so I started out with my hair already under pretty decent control.

The second time I left it on for 12-14 minutes. A little more came this time, but the result was odd. Despite the fact that I coated my hair with the product all the way down to the roots, it didn't come out at the roots. Instead, it more or less dissolved the hair above the roots, leaving a ragged, inconsistent look over the area where the product was applied.

The third time I left it on for 15-17 minutes, applying a much more generous amount than ever. Once again, the hair dissolved in patches, never all the way to the root. Some areas were completely unaffected. Pretty much at this point I realized that I wasn't going to get a better result than this, and, noticing that the Balm had brought my hair length down enough to do a decent shave, shaved in the shower immediately after scrubbing the product off with a washcloth.

Accusations have been levied that the positive reviews for the Bare Pair were written by employees of the company who produced this product. This may be true, but I'll give them the benefit of the doubt and believe some people out there probably have had good results with this product. I did not, this did not work for me as advertised and was a complete disappointment. I do not have excessively coarse or unusual hair where the product was used. I don't know what kind of hair you need to have for this to work on you, but know that your mileage may vary, so buy this product with caution!

rating 1.0. Not good. Didn't really work at all. First time i used it worked worse than shaving lots of stubble. i followed the instructions. Second time didnt remove any hair and i left it on for 15 mins. im sure i applyed enough. the nair for men works much better.

rating 3.0. Solid product for some I don't love this product. Good for sensitive skin areas with fine hair. The quick breakdown:

The hair remover smells better and is much gentler than other products. Using as directed it removed most hair readily. The only problem is that if you have very coarse hair on "sensitive" areas the directions say not to exceed 16 min. This didn't quite do the trick; it simply left the hair brittle, matted, damaged, and blonde. I plan on trying again later and waiting a full 20 min as the product is so gentile on skin that I felt absolutely 0 discomfort.

Update: I retried the product and I'm not a big fan. It left a lot of medium coarse hair as stubble. I'm amending my original review and going to say it really just good for fine hair.

rating 2.0. Process takes too much time, Doesn't work that well I bought this product a couple of weeks ago and wanted to try it as soon as I received it. For me, it did not work as well as others have noted.

Some of the main problems that I have with this is that it takes too much time for it to work and doesn't work very well. I tried it on my legs because I thought it would be the best place to start with since the hair is not too thick and I would be able to spread it out fairly even. At first I waited 10 minutes after applying in the removing lotion and barely anything came out. I wiped it with a towel and then went to the shower and tried to take out the hair with no luck. Then right after I did it on another area and that was better since I kept it on for 16 minutes. Still not all the hair came out. Anyways, this whole process took a long time, longer than I had expected since you have to wait for at least 15 minutes, wipe it, then go shower, once done you realize that not all the hair is removed so you have to apply more lotion and repeat.

The product has a very distinct smell that isn't necessarily bad but will give you a headache if you're at it for a long time because of all the chemicals.

For me I never got a clear and easy result and at the end had to just shave because it just looked weird that some places had hair and others didn't. I don't think I would recommend. I'm thinking of just shaving or getting waxed for a better result.

rating 4.0. Packaging I really like the product and it works well. I do have two minor complaints though. First, this stuff smells terrible, its very chemically. Second, this does not come in the standard Amazon box. It comes in a package that says Balls Balm on the front. Just be aware incase you are getting this shipped somewhere like work or something. My roommates got a good laugh at me gettin "Ball Cream" as they put it.

rating 4.0. Works Great, ON THE SECOND ATTEMPT! I was just about to write a scathing review about how I had fallen victim to the clever marketing and the fake reviews posted here, when I decided to try one more application.

My first attempt achieved NOTHING. I had already trimmed my scrotum and applied a generous portion of Bare Balm to it. I waited 15 minutes, the maximum it said to wait, and jumped in the shower and rinsed off the family jewels. To my dismay, I noticed that it didn't appear to remove a SINGLE HAIR! However, it did seem to bleach the hair, as it was decided lighter colored. I was so upset. I got dressed, came downstairs and hopped on Amazon to write this review. But then I read some other reviews that said they had tried it more than once and gotten better results a second time. So I said, "Heck with it" and ran back upstairs, 20 minutes after my first application, and dropped my drawers and grabbed the bare balm again. This time I gooped it on like schmear on a bagel. It was thick and I waited a FULL 20 MINUTES. I jumped in the shower again, and rinsed off, and AT FIRST, it seemed like the hair was still there. Then I grabbed my washcloth and started rubbing it across my sack, and each spot I hit with the wash cloth, the hair rubbed off my sack! So I'm sitting there and rubbing and buffing my sack with my washcloth and the hair is coming OUT! It was amazing!

So here I sit, at my PC, writing this review of Balls Balm, and I'm sporting my new pair of shiny, polished nads! Couldn't be happier! For those people having problems, I suggest trimming first, then putting it on thick and waiting 20 minutes, then rinse and REPEAT! Use a washcloth to remove the stubborn hair when you rinse. It will pull right out!

rating 4.0. Great...but smelly. First off let me say that this is the first hair removal product I have used and I am 90% pleased. Which is why I gave the Balls 4 stars. The only thing that is holding the product back from 5 stars is the rotten egg smell. Now I was expecting this because I read the reviews and I know it's a chemically based product but it kind of blew me away when I first smelled it.

Also, Balls is pretty messy if you live in a college dorm like I do. You have to cover up with a towel which gets covered in the chemical which then smells obviously like rotten eggs. Then causing your roommates say, "What the F*** is that smell." Which then peaks a funny yet some what awkward conversation about removing ball hair.

With that said, it did the job fairly well the first time. There were still bits that didn't fully come off, but with round two I was extremely smooth and felt very satisfied. No bad reaction, no pain just smooth balls.

I'll definitely be using this product in the future again and again.
rating 1.0. Does not work at all Do not waste any time or money on this product. Does not work at all. Hair is not removed. Anywhere.

rating 2.0. Doesn't remove hair This stuff stinks and does not seem to remove much hair. Nair (womens stuff) seems to work much better and you can pick it up at Target. This stuff is more gimicky and probably meant as a funny gift.

rating 5.0. Works as advertised Product worked well for me. I have coarse hair and left the product on for 15 minutes, at least 80% of the hair was removed. After the second application at a later time the product worked great, nothing left. I typically have sensitive skin and had no issues. Any of these kinds of creams have a bad smell but I did not think this was any worse than nair. The included lotion works well and I will definitely purchase this product again.

rating 2.0. didnt work ill adimt, im half monkey, but thats why i looked to find this type of product.  i used it on my back, my chest and my arms, and well...  anyway, it didnt seem to work.  if you have light amounts of body hair, and just want a clean look, maybe this will work for you.  if you have thick body hair, dont expect much!!

rating 4.0. The Bare Pair I received my order in a couple of days, very fast. Yes I would recommend this product if you are tired of shaving your stuff. I have also sent questions to the company and had very fast reply. The only thing I don't like is the smell of the first one you use, but it goes away. I'm looking forward to the new product coming out the first of the year because of the new smell. Thanks BallsBalm !!

rating 5.0. Hirsute men can rejoice I'm a member of the Perennially Hairy Club. I have coarse hair all over which, while indicative of my inherently virile nature, is a bit of a put off for women who've been weened on celebrity lady-boys. I've searched (in vain) for a product that does what it advertises, and does it without making me want to scrape my skin off. The Bare Pair delivers. First, it's got a completely inoffensive smell which is kinda nice considering that you have to wear the stuff for 15 minutes or more at a time. Second, it doesn't leave me with burning, ingrown-hair bumps that make me feel even more disgusting than my natural, gorrilla-like state.

The key is to remember that for truly manly-type hairy guys you're probably gonna have to go two or three rounds with this stuff before you see the full effect. You're also going to want to invest in something like the [[ASIN:B0037HP9OA Philips Norelco BG2040/34 Philips Norelco Bodygroom Pro Grooming System, Black / Silver]] in order to keep maintenance to a reasonable minumum.

I've used this stuff on my back, arms and nether regions, with complete success in all areas (though again I caution that two or three applications were required to achieve the desired effect). I've left my chest and legs untouched because I'm not going to entirely deny my Nordic/Viking hairy awesomeness, but I don't doubt that a similarly patient approach to those areas would leave me as smooth as a very smooth thing.
