rating 2.0. I bought these as a stocking stuffer but unfortunately the ... I bought these as a stocking stuffer but unfortunately the box came crushed in the corner so I had to return them.
rating 2.0. Two Stars Disappointed- Doesn't cut well or open wide enough. Hard to squeeze

rating 2.0. it's meant for men, not women. my nail ... it's meant for men, not women.  my nail beds are natuarlly curved instead of flat.  after i put my nails in and applied small pressure, layer of my nails are peeled off before it's getting cut.

rating 4.0. He has used them a few times now and really likes how sturdy they are Bought these for my youngest child who was always complaining that he could never find any nail clippers when needed.  I didn't tell him I bought them so when the package arrived I just handed him the clippers and said that they were his.  He chuckled and said thanks.  He has used them a few times now and really likes how sturdy they are, he says he can use them and not feel as if he is going to break them.
rating 5.0. Nice design! Nice well-balanced design.  Solid and sharp with wide opening.  Work great!

rating 2.0. Not good Cuts cleanly. The end of the clipper is too bulky to get underneath the nail to get a "bite". This product will only be useful to you if you want to cut nails that are grown out long and you don't want them clipped close.

I modified them to work better. (see customer images before and after)
rating 5.0. super quality clippers Woow sharp, super quality clippers. For a lifetime.

rating 5.0. Well made clipper that works great on cutting toenails straight across Well made clipper that works great on cutting toenails straight across.  If you have problems with ingrown toenails try these.

rating 5.0. Where have you been all of my life? I know they are just nail clippers but, WOW! I am very pleased with the quality and construction. The brushed or matte finish is a very nice design feature. It will allow you to keep the clippers under control due to the non slip texture. If you are looking for some wonderful nail clippers, these are the one. I wish I gad seen these years ago.
rating 5.0. Throw away your "dime store" clippers Don't waste your time and money on cheapo clippers.  These have sharp blades, are made of strong stainless steel, and cut hard nails easily.

rating 5.0. Everyone needs one of these. "This is something I've wanted forever and didn't even know it existed. Now I don't have great crescent shaped chunks clipped out of my toenails. Good, sturdy mechanics too. Will purchase again when I lose this one! "
rating 5.0. Quality These work really well and feel hefty, like they are quality.

rating 5.0. so when I saw these I thought how perfect! All four of them loved them I'm always looking for stocking stuffers for my husband and three grown sons, so when I saw these I thought how perfect!  All four of them loved them, their sharp, strong, and easy to handle.
rating 5.0. Great clippers Great clippers. Bought for my husband for Christmas. He just used them tonight and said they were the best clippers he ever had! Very top quality.
rating 5.0. Five Stars Nice clippers. Straight edge. Sturdy. I would recommend. Worth spending a little extra to get a nice pair.
rating 5.0. Perfect for thick nails My husband has thick toe nails and so he loves this! So easy to use!

rating 5.0. The wide handle provides a good surface for control Works very well.  Spring pressure is adequate to open the clipper but not so strong that it diminishes sensitivity.  The wide handle provides a good surface for control.

current_product_reviews:
rating 5.0. Can't imagine a superior model of nail-clippers It's sometimes hard to tell from small web photos just how a product will look and feel for real. The amazon picture for this clipper did look quite appealing, but I can say that in real life the item's quality is even higher than I'd been expecting. Really excellently designed weight and shape for a man's medium-size hands; perfectly balanced tension against the blade-closing action; great surface texture for easy gripping and avoidance of dropping during use. I especially like the wider-than-usual outer end of the push-lever--it doesn't slip away from under your thumb.

But the best part is how this clipper cuts through any sort of nails "like butter"--smoothly, so so easily. I can now trim my nails in half the time it used to take, and I no longer need to "smooth out" small sharp corners of nail that I used to get because of how the the whole instrument (older, smaller clippers I had) would move during the cutting action. It's very hard to find *any* purely straight-bladed nail clippers (which I find far more versatile than the curved ones) either online or in stores, and among the few available on amazon, I am convinced these can't be beat. The "pro" in its name is entirely appropriate.

Note 1: When you first unpack this product, the gap between the two cutting edges might not seem as wide as you might like, particularly for larger toenails, but then it opens to a better size when you get the push-lever handle up.

Note 2: This is my unvarnished and uncompensated opinion. The company did send me a very polite email a few days after I got the product, urging me to write a review, but not for any sort of consideration. I recommend them without reservation.

input: rating 5.0. They Do Exactly What They Say They Will Do They do exactly what they say they will do. One of the white ends have broke off already, but its not essential to the product working. I have used them in my daughter's hair and they left her hair really curly. I would buy them again.
rating 2.0. Two Stars Doesn't work for my hair.

rating 1.0. Sorry but these don't work I was hoping for a way to add curl to my hair without using heat.

These are very slick and don't grab your hair at all. And when you get your hair wrapped around it and try to twist the roller to stay in place, they often fall down or out completely because of their weight. Plus they don't twist easily. They are too thick to hold up with bobby pins to keep them from falling. Essentially this was a waste of $$.

rating 1.0. These do not bend or hold well at all. Wouldn't stay ... These did not hold my hair at all without using bobby pins. I have thin fine hair and they constantly fell out, even with using hairspray. I would return it but by the time i was able to really give it a try, it was passed my my return option.

rating 5.0. knowing it's bad for my hair I've never tried these before and I'm always using flat irons, knowing it's bad for my hair. Listen...I use these overnight, sometimes for a few hours..I take them out and it looks as if I used the world's most PERFECT curler. These are my favorite things to use. Love em.

rating 2.0. I like the container and it's color-coded size curlers but they ... I like the container and it's color-coded size curlers but they barely keep my hair in place, no matter how thin or thick I set it.
rating 1.0. Didn't work for me Don't stay or lock well. I'm a licensed hairstylist for 20 years...

rating 4.0. the white caps come off very easy, even when you are not using them As previously written by other reviewers, the white caps come off very easy, even when you are not using them. Plus they do not hold my twisted hair in place very well. I regret buying this.

The customer service experience with this seller has been beyond wonderful. They are responsive and stand by their product. Even if I am not satisfied with what I bought, their service has been excellent.
rating 5.0. Satisfied with my purchase Can't wait to use my flexi rods. Came package just like in the image with all 42 flexi rods
rating 5.0. Five Stars Great work fine love them

rating 1.0. They were great at first but after the second use the white ... They were great at first but after the second use the white ends started falling out.  This is product is made in China so I should of known better.  I will never buy this product again.

rating 5.0. These Fun Rods Will Deliver Curls I've used these flexible curling rods several times now. First, I practiced wrapping my hair up a few times at home without waiting the required time to get the curls. (I was so excited to try it out when they arrived.) I also tried it with both wet and dry hair. The hair wrapping goes pretty quickly because there are no fasteners or clips to worry about. That was a key factor in my decision to try these. You just roll up the hair from the bottom up and then bend the long ends up over the curl to secure. And they do stay put, once you figure out which way to bend them. Wrap tightly and bend the tips over the hair closest to the scalp. There are a few videos on YouTube that can help show you how to wrap your hair using curling rods of any kind, if you want to see it in action.

The rods are long and have a nice, slightly grippy texture on the surface, which is good for gripping the hair a little. But it's not too grippy that it would prevent easy removal from the curled hair.

They have a bit of cushioning to them, too, which means there's a little bit of give to them when you curl. The cushioning is also handy if you sleep in them. In general, it's not great sleeping with stuff stuck to your head. I managed to avoid getting a headache when I slept in these, though, which surprised me.

The ends are covered with little caps that keep the wire inside, and they kind of pop out of the rods a little, but not off. You can easily pop them back into place without any problems.

There are 6 of each size/color in the pack (7 sizes!) and you can choose a differing variety every time you set your hair, for differing looks. I have shoulder length hair and liked the smaller ones for the shortest hair at the nape of the neck, and graduate to larger sizes toward the top of my head. But I have used larger ones all around as well. It depends what kinds of curls you're going for. You can also use larger or smaller sections of hair to wrap each rod, for more variations.

The design of the clear/blue carrying case was also a factor in my decision. I like the upright design, the little zipper cap, and the handle on top. It's compact for the quantity of rods it contains, which makes it easy to travel with, though the vertical design can be difficult for getting all the rods back in and fitting well. It helps to straighten out the rods before putting them back in the container. Not a big deal, just a small struggle if you're short on time.

I have gotten crazy curly results from these after sleeping in these the other night. I was so curly I didn't recognize myself in the mirror! These rods will deliver! The key is to wrap moistened, but not sopping wet hair, wrap tightly and consistently. Mind those pesky ends and make sure they are held under the hair in the right direction, and leave these rods in for several hours until the hair is all completely dry. Overnight is best, but I've seen results in just a couple hours with only slightly dampened hair.

I'm still learning how to best curl using these, but I'm very happy with my investment. I think you will enjoy playing with your hair using these.

rating 2.0. But for anything else they are pretty worthless. They do not stay in the hair ... These may work if you have long hair-shoulder length or longer.  But for anything else they are pretty worthless. They do not stay in the hair and so do not work.

rating 2.0. Don't hold tight This might be a little easier to apply however it looks messy and you have those things hanging everywhere on your head, there is not way you could use something to cover it like you would with smaller curlers, also if you have thin and straight hair like mine, it will not hold!

rating 5.0. Never burning my hair again. These work perfectly. I have waist length hair and I still have plenty of length to the rod left over. I don't use hair product when using these so they look a bit more natural. I can have them in for about an hour and I'm good to go. Of course the amount of time you keep them in affects the curls so that's all up to you. They're very flexible so you can tie them in just about any way. I love using the smaller rods for tighter curls.

rating 1.0. These rollers are the WORST. I wanted to be cheap and buy these ... I wish I could leave less than a one star review, but I can't.  These rollers are the WORST.  I wanted to be cheap and buy these because I thought, "Hey, they are ten dollars, they should work just fine." No, they do not "work just fine".  Find a different brand.  These rollers wouldn't even hold the twist shapes that I tried to do even before using them on my hair.
rating 5.0. Five Stars Great for quick curling without a curling iron. Love this product perfect for any hair type.

rating 5.0. So far great! My hair is already curly so I cant ... So far great!
My hair is already curly so I cant really say how it will work with someone with straight hair, but for me it keeps my curls in tact. That is pretty much what I wanted them. Like any curlers, the longer you leave them the more defined they'll be.

rating 4.0. I love these, I bought these so I don't put ... I love these, I bought these so I don't put heat in my hair anymore so I don't damage my hair anymore and have been using them for a month and my hair is so much more healthy and has grown almost 2 inches with weekly hair treatments. The only down fall to these is I can't leave them in over night or it will look like I have a really bad perm. I have to wash my hair wait for my hair to be almost dry then put them in for about a hour. Then the next two days I put them in, in my dry hair to fix up my curls for about 20 min. I use the bigger ones cause the little ones make to intense of curls. I have found not putting the curlers close to scalp is best and to leave about 3-4 inches away.

rating 2.0. Ohuhu Twist Flex Hair Curler It curls when it stays on!  The wiring inside that makes it bendable and stay bended isn't very sturdy.  I find the curlers falling off without even disrupting the rolled hair.  Very flimsy.

rating 1.0. Waste if money!!! These are not what I thought they were.  For one they bend but do not hold their form.  They will not stay in my hair!  No matter how much I try to bend them they just fall right out!  Don't waste your money!  Don't think they even deserve one star.

rating 1.0. One Star Waste of money. The wires in these rollers are very weak and do not stay bent, therefore the rollers slide out of my hair almost immediately.

Ohuhu gave me a partial refund without me having to return the product or request a refund after my one star review. At first I thought this was quite nice, but shortly there after I received an email asking for me to change my review. I acknowledged to them that I had received a partial refund but stood by my review that the product was inferior. They proceeded to email me six more times requesting me to change my review. I "unsubscribed" from their emails, ironic because I never subscribed in the first placed, and received yet another email from them pleading for a new review the next morning. I have since marked them as spam and I'm updating my review here so that no one else will have to deal with this sort of harassment. I will be avoiding Ohuhu products at all cost.
rating 2.0. Not for short hair Didn't stay in my short hair. Probably works well on someone with long hair.
rating 1.0. One Star It didnt work for me

rating 1.0. These so-called Flex Curlers actually Un-Flex I had already purchased some (other brand) of the thinner size of 'flex stick rollers' and they had worked great so I thought it would be good to have the Ohuhu set since it contained many different thicknesses. I tried the Ohuhu rollers immediately and realized that only the thinnest sizes of their rollers actually work.  The 4 thickest sizes will not hold a 'bend' at all.  Those immediately unfold and there goes your hair!  Also, for me anyway, all of these curlers rollers are much too long.  You don't need that much length of roller as you just have to deal with it overlapping too much.  I don't recommend them but I didn't want to write a bad review so I wrote the company telling them that I would like to give them a review privately (to help them improve the product) but I never heard back. I don't care about getting my money back, it's not that expensive.  But I want to let others know not to waste their money as there are other rods out there that actually work great.  This brand just isn't one of them.

rating 4.0. Don't be so quick to give up! My first time buying foam rollers was from Walmart, a pack of 18 conair rollers that came in 2 sizes for $15.. I was pretty disappointed when I saw this pack of 42 for $13 on amazon a few weeks after.

First, the sturdiness of these rods differ from the Conair ones I had purchased. They felt less firm, but found as you go down in size that changes. This can affect how well it stays in your hair, but I just use an elastic on the bottom part to keep my ends rolled.
Second, I have long hair so I found these to be a good length. Also, I combine the purple and blues together when needed, or the yellow and oranges (not very far in sizes and in case 6 isn't enough). I haven't tried the smaller sizes, but that's because I like bigger waves/curls.

I'm not much of a girly girl, so I found these perfect to do my hair the night before. Plus, no heat damage!! In the morning, I take them out, give my hair a few tosses and run my fingers through the curls and done. It may look a little silly at first, but give the hair a few moments to relax.

**Sleeping in them: I found the blues and purples to be the most comfortable, probably because they have the most cushion. Try not to roll to close to the scalp, some hairs might get rolled too tightly and are a pain in the ass when trying to sleep.

- For the girls not having success, you have to use it on damp hair! I've been rolling them in dry hair and then using a spray bottle to wet my hair then wrap them up in a scarf or shirt. I sleep in them and remove in the morning.

- I don't use hair spray, but the curls can last from one morning to the next! Of course, not perfectly but I like the way they look. I'm not a perfectionist at all, so I like the "effortless" beachy waves look.

rating 1.0. Poor quality, don't waste your money. I bought these twist-flex rods based on the high sales and reviews. But honestly, these are poor quality twist-flex rods... They do not hold in your hair. It is a complete waste of your money. Don't buy them.

rating 4.0. Works - can't speak to durability Instructions insufficient for the newbie - had to watch internet video to get the hair to stay on, but I probably could have figured it out with patience (I really don't do anything with my hair normally).  Not really sure they are an improvement over regular curlers with sleeves though!  First use had trouble getting curls close to the scalp, but was able to achieve a wave in just a couple of hours of wear from dry hair (fine, straight caucasian hair), although it didn't hold up to being brushed.  When you leave them in overnight or to dry from wet, they really do the job.  Slight chemical odor from the foam - expect that will go away.  Not sure how long they last, especially if you choose to use heat with them, but for the price I think they are good.
rating 5.0. Five Stars Easy to use.  Reasonable price.
rating 2.0. Two Stars The products not using friendly
rating 1.0. Don't Buy These Don't buy these. They don't stay in your hair.
rating 5.0. 5 Stars These work great! I have long thick hair and it curls great. Falls after a couple hours but still has a wavy relook to it.

rating 5.0. All of the colors except the two biggest ones stay twisted just fine. Some reviews made it seem like it's hard ... I read some complaints about them not staying twisted. All of the colors except the two biggest ones stay twisted just fine. Some reviews made it seem like it's hard to do, but it isn't hard at all. The two biggest ones stay in best if you tie it in a knot.  The curls are BEAUTIFUL! I love to curl my hair but I hate damaging it with heat. These are perfect.

rating 1.0. Dont sleep with them in These fall out and don't twist well. I was hoping I could sleep with these in and they're not only uncomfortable but they just fall out because they don't twist well at all. I'm sad I bought these. Putting my hair in a sock bun brings much better curls than these.

rating 4.0. Curly Hair These flexi-rods are essentially the same as those that you would purchase at your local beauty supply store. The thing I like about the Ohuhu pack is that you have seven varying sizes and colors. There are six of each size. It also comes with the storage bag, as shown, which is great for storage and if you need to travel with them. They run about $13, as of the date of this review. The price isn't bad at all.

These are great if you want spiral curls or to add curls to the ends of your hair. If you are looking to do a full-body style, start at the back of your neck (nape) and use the smaller rods, working your way up to the bigger rods when you get to the middle and top of your hair.

They are super easy to use and because they are soft, it is not too difficult to sleep in.

Of note, I was sent these flex-rods directly, at a discounted rate, in exchange for my honest opinion.

rating 5.0. It's not super comfortable to sleep with them in my hair but ... I was looking up hair ideas online and I saw a video where these rollers were used. I tried it and it really was as simple as it looked in the video. It's not super comfortable to sleep with them in my hair but it is worth it to wake up with curly hair.
rating 5.0. Worth it! These are great. There are many in the pack and they hold their shape well (creating nice curls). Nice gift for anyone with long hair too.

rating 5.0. I love them. I got a curl setting product to work with the twist flex rods and now get pretty curls that relax during the day. I put the curler by my scalp and wrap the hair around it then finish by putting the hair ends toward my scalp and turn the curler with hair closer to my scalp, made a loop of the twist flex (one half over the other) then bent that loop forward over the curls then brought the twist ends around the hair & loop to make a tight knot by my scalp. Try to get them toward the top of my head in front & sides so I can sleep without laying on a curler. I like the red in front, pink in the top back, and green ones I use along my neck line. My hair is still damp in places after 7 hours (I sleep in front of a box fan) so I just take them out and don't do anything with the curls until they dry completely then pull on the curls some. The curls last all day. I wash my hair every night but I have a feeling the curls could last longer.

Update: I now use no setting product just the rods. I use the green ones all over my head. I put the hair around the twist flex same way as before wrapping the hair around the whole twist flex rod then bend it in half putting the one half under the other, twist the ends together. So much easier and next day my hair is so loose and bouncy. I learned on tv yesterday to leave about 3 inches loose for the crimped style or double crimped style for a more relax style, so won't worry so much if the ends aren't twisted in.
rating 5.0. Beautiful curls in 10min! These are just ammmmazing. Life changing product!

rating 1.0. These are really terrible flexirods. Flexirods are supposed to be stiff These are really terrible flexirods. Flexirods are supposed to be stiff, these are super light and super flexible so they do not hold your hair.

rating 5.0. What I most liked about this package is that they have different sizes ... What I most liked about this package is that they have different sizes for different styles. I still need to practice a bit more with the directions in which to put them on to get the look I am going for. But over all I really enjoy the product.

rating 3.0. Not good ! The flexi rods are so flimsy they didn't work on my hair is bought rods better than this from Ross!

rating 1.0. my hair wouldn't hold a curl with these I have thick straight blond hair.  Bought these to try out for heatless curls.  My hair did not hold a curl -- it fell out immediately.  Your mileage may vary.

rating 4.0. I just bent the ends over the hair towards the center and it worked fine. Her curls came out great Purchased for DD, who did not expect much from these rods. However, she was pleasantly surprised, as was I, with the results. You must play with the rods to get them to stay bent. The directions say to tie them in a knot, but they were too short to do that for me. So, I just bent the ends over the hair towards the center and it worked fine. Her curls came out great. On the larger rods, the center popped out as I bent them. I hope it is not a problem later on. Overall, I like them and they worked great.

rating 5.0. Great for Natural Looking Curls without damaging heat These curlers are great! They are easy on the hair, no snagging or tangling. I wash my hair and dry it almost completely. Then, divide my hair into sections and start at the ends and roll my hair up as far as I can. A simple bend of the roller holds the hair in place. I repeat with each section. Once dry, unroll and enjoy very natural looking curls. These are so much better than using curling irons or heated rollers that dry and damage hair. I highly recommend these curlers.

rating 5.0. Best Curler Set Ever!!!! I love all the different different sized rollers included in this set. It's great for any size curl that I'm trying to achieve. Works best if you curl your hair while still damp and let it set until dry, then take them out. Then you better watch out for all the curls you are going to get. And those curls will last for days and days if you don't brush them all out.

rating 5.0. Great Product for Varying Styles I have tried many types of hair rollers over the years and these are definitely one of the best I have used. input: rating 5.0. Much more then I expected I have used this brush for about a week now and my teeth do feel cleaner. I have sensitive gums, so I was worried the more powerful vibrations of the brush would cause some damage but on the contrary, my gums feel healthier and stronger now. If you are looking for a good tooth brush, you will not be disappointed. I admit I was a bit hesitant to spend $80 on the Oral B S-200 but it was definitely worth it for me.
rating 5.0. sonic I love this toothbrush.  It certainly helps me to brush longer.  My husband is not keen on the sound however.
rating 5.0. Oral B Model S-200 Sonic Complete Rechargeable Power Toothbrush Have daughter with braces...her teeth never looked better.  Much improved from just using a standard toothbrush.

rating 5.0. Amazingly clean teeth for a very reasonable price... The sensations of this toothbrush are weirdly satisfying especially if you have never used a sonic toothbrush before. They key selling point is that our teeth have never felt quite so polished.  The price is great, the brush is simple and pleasant to use. Would recommend.
rating 2.0. Batteries are not replaceable Good toothbrush but the batteries are not replaceable so you need to replace the whole unit when they won't hold a charge anymore

rating 5.0. Good Toothbrush This toothbrush is much better than the Oral-B Crossaction Power that I used earlier. The head is nice and small and gets into hard to reach areas, the brush stand has no exposed metal (it's really cool from an engineering standpoint, they seem to be using a magnetic field to charge the battery), so it's pretty safe to leave by the sink.

After using the brush, my teeth feel smooth & clean, like after going to a dentist. It takes a little getting used to but the bristles are soft and don't hurt your gums.

I've been using the product only for a month, some other reviewers were disappointed with the battery life, I can't comment on that yet. However, I think Ni-Cd batteries are happier if you put them through full charge/discharge cycles, a full charge lasts a week. At least 250 full charge/discharge cycles is what I expect out of Ni-Cd's, charging once every week gives me about 250 weeks (little more than 4.5 years). I don't actually think I'll have the toothbrush that long, but the point is that I think the battery should be fine if you don't charge it everyday.

Also, this really shouldn't matter but this toothbrush is made in Germany.

rating 5.0. Very good electric toothbrush I have owned and used the Oral B Sonic Complete model S-200 for nearly three years. I should note that a lot of this review is probably applicable to the S-320 model as well.

When I first started using the toothbrush, it took a while to get used to the sonic vibrations in my mouth. It was a very unusual sensation--almost tickled--and made me laugh, but I soon got used to it after about a week. Interestingly, when my girlfriend first started using it she had the same experience, couldn't stop laughing.

I noticed immediately that my teeth felt much cleaner, very smooth. It was effortless to clean my teeth since the toothbrush did all the real work. One thing I noticed that was not advertised was that in addition to the 2-minute timer (three long pauses), the brush also indicates 30-second intervals (one short pause), which I found very helpful for timing even brushing around all my teeth.

I purchased replacement heads at regular intervals, and would recommend the soft heads. After about two years of using the brush, I noticed some small areas at the base of a few teeth that became VERY sensitive to the slightest pressure. My dentist informed me that this was due to gum recession (when the gums are pushed back from the tooth, exposing the more delicate and HIGHLY sensitive roots of the tooth) caused by vigorous brushing. I didn't think I was brushing vigorously at all, but I had been using the regular "clean" setting. After hearing that this discomfort was due to brushing I started using the "soft" setting and things returned to normal, and this lower setting still effectively cleaned my teeth.

Unfortunately, in the last few weeks the toothbrush would occasionally not turn on, and eventually started to spontaneously/randomly turn on for brief moments. Apparently this was its death cry. I'm not sure whether leaving the toothbrush on the charger all the time would over time lead to diminished battery life, but would recommend against storing it on the charger.

Overall, I really liked the sonic complete toothbrush, would buy it again, and highly recommend everyone use a sonic toothbrush.

Pros:
- superior teeth cleaning
- 2-minute and 30-second timers
- does not automatically turn off after two minutes like some other electronic toothbrushes
- long-lasting charge (took it on several two-week vacations and it still had more charge left when I returned home)
- storage for three additional heads in charging station*
- toothbrush head holder in charging station can be pulled out easily for routine cleaning*
- drainage holes in the bottom of each toothbrush head slot (no nasty buildup) and good ventilation allows heads to dry (no mildew/mold)*
- wall-mountable charging station*
- non-slip rubber bottom on charging station
- solid construction, heads stay on

Cons:
- no replacement battery (dead battery = bulky manual toothbrush)
- no carrying case (apparently the S-320 model does)
- very long & bulky (it was hard to fit into my toiletries travel case)
- no PDF/digital owner's manual (OK, so this might be nit-picking, but keeping the printed manuals for every electronics gadget is a pain!)

*It appears that the S-200 model no longer comes with the larger charging station with head storage, but the S-320 model does.

rating 4.0. Oral B Placing the order and receiving it were as simple as it should be.  No delays or hidden charges. The product is great - I've owned one before so was expecting just what I got.  I'd recommend both the product and using Amazon to purchase.

rating 5.0. Great Toothbrush For The Blind So you've read the reviews about how this is a great toothbrush that does the job and your teeth will be clean.  This review chooses to focus on the color scheme of this Oral-B unit.  I chose to rate this product with five stars.  If I had the option, I would specifically rate this a 4.9, less than a perfect score, solely because of the questionable color plastics chosen for this toothbrush.  To start it off, the front of the handle was made aquamarine.  This hideous color should not match any sane person's bathroom decor.  But if that wasn't bad enough, the back of the handle is a repugnant seafoam green color.  Between these varying shades of hideousness, a strip of cerulean blue flake plastic accents the toothbrush.  The brush head itself has a ring of royal blue around the neck, and the back of the brush head is cornflower blue.  Of course, the bristles themselves are yet another random shade of blue.  In conclusion, this is a very solid product that is held back by the fact that it looks like a hodgepodge of different color plastics, none of which come anywhere near matching or aethsetically pleasing.  Why can't they just make this toothbrush a simple black, white, or any solid color?  I recommend this product to anyone who wants clean teeth and can't decide between the color green or blue.

rating 5.0. Great Brush I bought this toothbrush about 2 years ago and have happily used it ever since. It leaves the teeth feeling clean and is not too bulky to hold. I find the timer buzz convenient and easy to ignore if you chose so. Can't see why people find it annoying.

Both my husband and myself use the same model - we just change brush heads. The brush head replacements are inexpensive and come with different colored rings. The battery holds charge forever, which is great for trips. We've taken it to 8-10 day trips without the charger, and used it both twice a day, and it still had charge. Highly recommended.

rating 5.0. Oral B Sonic The Dentist recommended this for my wife, I was worried she wouldn't like using it but with it's two speeds it's working great. Highly recomended and it also got the highest consumer reviews.

rating 5.0. Great purchase!!! You will notice whiter teeth within days, your teeth feel cleaner, gums feel tougher, and battery life is amazing.  It will go up to 2 weeks on one charge.  It has a built-in timer so you know how long to brush each quadrant of your mouth.  It also has 2 speeds for sensitivity.  Great purchase, a must have for anyone that is particular about oral hygiene.

rating 4.0. Nice toothbrush Got this a few weeks back when it was on sale. I can't compare it to other power toothbrushes as this is the first one I've owned. My dentist recommended the phillips brand but I chose this one over the price. I love it. If you are currently brushing your teeth with a normal toothbrush I would highly recommend upgrading to this one. It's amazing how clean it makes your teeth feel. I was skeptical at first - I just thought my dentist was trying to give me something I did not need. I was wrong I'm glad I bought this one.

It is easy to use and kind of tickles at first when using it but after about 3 or 4 times using it there is not weird feelings at all.

rating 5.0. Oral B S-200 is powerful / easy to reach into tight places This is my first sonic toothbrush and I am please with its performance.

Pros:
-powerful
-long handle makes it easy to reach into tight places
-operates app. 2 weeks on one charge (takes 12-14 hrs to fully charge) and the charger unit makes a convenient stand for the toothbrush
-charging unit serves as toothbrush stand (would be even better if the power cord was detachable from the charging base to allow for clutter-free storage for the toothbrush between charges)
-price; I had considered the Sonicare product but decided on this because of the huge price difference. I am satisfied.

Cons:
-difficult to complete a full brushing without tapping the power button (first tap cycles to low speed, then off, then full power)
-30 second timer is annoying - brush signals by going into a quick on-off sequence for a few seconds.

rating 5.0. Excellent toothbrush, especially for what I paid for it I was a little wary of buying the Oral-B S-200 because I'd had a Sonicare Advance 4100 for probably 3 years before. I was so used to it I thought I'd not really want the Oral-B. I am pleasantly surprised. It is slightly harsher - the brush with it is a little harsher on my teeth and gums, but not in a bad way. After using the Oral-B for about 3 weeks I am very happy that I made this purchase. It's light, does a great job cleaning, and should be fairly easy to find replacement brushes. Thanks, Amazon, for such a great deal!

rating 4.0. Oral B S-200 Sonic - nice I had bought a Sonicare toothbrush not too long ago as I had one that died. On a recent trip to the mountains of Colorado I misplaced the Sonicare and cannot find it. I bought this for a couple of reasons - it was less expensive than the Sonicare, I wanted to compare the two, and if I find the Sonicare I will have two different brushes.

So far I have been very pleased with the unit. Has many of the same features and the timer is a nice feature. Pros are that it seems to be as efficient as the Sonicare, easier to keep clean, and is lighter. Cons are that after the timer goes through it cycle it does not shut off the toothbrush. Also, the location of the on/off button is such that (for the way I use it) it is easy to change the setting on the brush or shut it off. Once you change it the timer resets itself.

All in all I would recommend this to anyone.

rating 4.0. S-200 review I have used this product over the past month and here are my findings:

Pros
1) Cleans teeth well, I have deep molars and it gets down in the crevices.
2) Refills are cheap enough ($5 dollar range) unlike some toothbrushes - low cost of ownership
3) Well sealed, doesn't seem to build up any toothpaste munge, and rinses off well
4) A second push on the power button when running goes into "slow" vibration mode - good for brushing your tongue without feeling like you're going to gag

Cons
1) 30 second pause - every 30 seconds it pauses with a little blip - gets kind of annoying
2) Packaging was kind of flimsy, and it doesn't come with a cover for the toothbrush head which is less sanitary in a bathroom setting, and will make it annoying to share across multiple people with nowhere to store the extra heads.

Overall
Good product, works well, seems to be standing up to normal use. If you're looking for a no-nonsense toothbrush at a reasonable price, this is it.

rating 5.0. Charged up about rechargeable toothbrush This was purchased as a gift for my father, who has Parkinson's Disease and was having trouble brushing his teeth. This item was a perfect gift, because the brush does all of the work, and his teeth are getting cleaner than before. I later ordered replacement heads, and now my mother shares the base and they are both able to use this to brush their teeth. It apparently wasn't too difficult to learn how to use, as my parents are both in their seventies and do not care for a lot of techno gadgets. As I was shopping for an electric toothbrush, the reviews on this model steered me toward it specifically because of its ease of use. It does need a spot to sit that is near an outlet for recharging. If you don't have adequate counter space, you will have to be a bit creative or flexible (or both) to make this work, but my folks are very pleased with how their teeth feel after using this brush. Initially, my mother said her gums bled a bit, but after a few days, that has stopped, indicating better gingival health! So, yes, when I am an empty nester and can utilize my counter space for this tool, I will purchase one, too! (Well, okay...maybe sooner.)

As I shopped around for this item, I found the best price here at Amazon. It was definitely worth the amount of $$.

WELL ORAL-B JUST LIKE PHILIPS CUSTOMER SERVICE STINKS..I CONTACTED THEM BECAUSE THIS TOOTHBRUSH STARTED RUNNING OUT OF POWER A FEW MONTHS AGO..ORAL-B STATED THAT THE TOOTHBRUSH SHOULD LAST BETWEEN 3-5 YRS, WELL MINE LASTED MAYBE 2 YRS 2MONTHS AFTER THE POWER PROBLEM STARTED WHEN I TOLD THEM THAT, THERE REMEDY WAS A $10.00 COUPON TOWARDS A NEW ONE...THIS IS UNACCEPTABLE SO I WENT ONLINE UTUBE FOUND SEVERAL VIDEOS ON HOW TO CHANGE THE BATTERY, AND I WAS ABLE TO DO IT AND MY TOOTHBRUSH IS BACK TO WORKING AGAIN, I WENT TO BATTERY PLUS STORE AND THEY CONNECTED THE 2 BATTERIES THAT I NEEDED FOR A FEW EXTRA BUCKS, IT COST ME ALL OF $9.00..SO THERE YOU HAVE; IT ORAL-B JUST WANTS YOU TO BUY A NEW TOOTHBRUSH..I'M TIRED OF BUYING THINGS THAT DON'T EVEN LAST THE TIME THEY ARE SUPPOSE TOO..IF ANYONE ELSE HAD THIS PROBLEM JUST CHECKOUT THE UTUBE VIDEOS ON CHANGE ORAL-B TOOTHBRUSH BATTERY..IT WORKS..YOU DO HAVE TO BE VERY CAREFUL WHEN OPEN TOOTHBRUSH MAKE SURE U DON'T BREAK THE VERY THIN COPPER WIRES, CUS IF THAT HAPPEN THEN YOU WILL NEED TO BUY A NEW ONE...

current_product_reviews: rating 5.0. Lasted almost 8 years with 2 uses per day This won't be much of a review. I bought this on October 17, 2007 here on Amazon and must've skipped reviewing it. The good news is: It has been used at least twice a day since October 17, 2007. The bad news: It just stopped charging yesterday, June 24, 2015. I'm back to buy a replacement one and thought I would post this review. I consider myself a fairly simple man and will be trying to find a new model with the features this one has. I'm already leaning towards the Oral-B Pro-3000. Very impressive lifespan on this one and I'll have to just hope my next one lasts as long as this one. I remember when I bought this one. I couldn't believe an electric toothbrush would make that much difference, but it has. I'm planning on overnight shipping the next one just so I don't have to wait to have my electric toothbrush. I follow a pretty basic mouth cleaning process of: Full two minutes with the electric toothbrush as soon as I get out of bed. Two to three light brushings in the afternoon to evening. Then I used the electric toothbrush before going to bed, followed by a once over with my wife's water irrigation pick. I'll probably regret saying this, but I'm 40 now and haven't had a cavity since my mid-20s and while I gave up on the gleaming white smile, I'm still very happy to have made it this far without any dental problems. I credit this toothbrush for a lot of my oral health.

rating 5.0. as i've had SO many bad experiences. i purchased the conair rollers almost as ... i've not had a chance to try these but they are identical to the mixed pack of conair rollers i bought at (shame) walmart. i've spent literally hundreds of dollars on devices to curl my undamaged, straight, thick hair, which i decided to grow out after years of frustration unable to find someone to cut it the way i asked. my hair won't hold a curl for anything, and i REALLY don't want to damage it with a perm, as i've had SO many bad experiences. i purchased the conair rollers almost as a joke, remembering slightly similar ones my mom had 50 years ago.
THESE ARE THE SAME THING. the sizes are smaller, greate for my uneven bits, (i used to wear the original dorothy hamill wedge. try getting THAT hair cut at my age, in this day and age [ yeah, i skated in the 70's, we all wore it, another story], but it's impossible to find someone who can actually do that cut, at least where i live (check out my yelp) and it's hell growing it out.
long story short, these are the same curlers as the name brand, which have worked AMAZINGLY (oh, honeys, skip that weird thing that is supposed to suck up your hair and curl it for you? for $200+ i had smelled like a perm, and had flat hair. every thing else burned me). i have sat 1) in the tub after shampoo, 2) late at night with dry hair watching tv, with and without product, 3) in the pool, catching rays, 4) stupidly mistaking a can of shave cream for mousse(!!) and still had wonderful results. i repeat, my hair is COARSE. STRAIGHT. i'm about to be 57, have very few silvers (dammit) but my hair has never behaved. i'm not good at it, but with the small rollers in my original kit, i've been able to put back in barrettes or combs and have it STAY. the three sizes i ordered here are all smaller and i'm rolling as i type. am already thrilled. THANK YOU!!!!

rating 5.0. An excellent product I have used it for several years. This is the only cream to significantly improve the damaged skin on my hands, and ditto for the cracked skin on my feet. Regular use! You must use it regularly and consistently. It absorbs well and quickly and is not greasy.

It has a somewhat medicinal smell (think Noxema), but it is not unpleasant & fades quickly if you are concerned about how you smell to others.

One important caveat for dog owners (I don't know about cats). Tea tree oil is poisonous to dogs. I don't know if the small concentration in this cream is enough to matter, but it would be prudent to not let a dog lick your hands for a while after applying this cream.

rating 5.0. Best Lashes for the Price For the price, these are beautiful lashes with a soft demi shape and wispy fringe. The lash band while black-and oddly reminiscent of thread-is quite thin and comfortable. I've also found them durable enough for more than one use, so long as I'm gentle and clean the band of glue.

Possible drawbacks: The lashes are handmade and, therefore, not perfectly identical. It's subtle and I think adds to the realism but others might not. Also the band doesn't curve enough for many Western eyes- I just press the ends together a bit while my lash glue is getting tacky.

Like another reviewer, I have Alopecia so I've gone through quite a few falsies. These have become a cheap staple for everyday wear while I prefer more expensive mink lashes with a clear band for going out. Cool thing is-when I disguise the band- they look as good as my $40 lashes they're just not as durable. I'd call that a win!

rating 5.0. Lightweight, comfortable, and looks natural These lashes are lightweight and look very natural if applied correctly. I trim them, cut them in half so I can usually get 2 uses from each pair, and apply the lashes under my natural lashes NOT on top. Since the band on these is not clear (it's a thin thread) I think applying under is the best way to camouflage and blend with my lashes. It also eliminates the need for heavy liner to blend the lash band and I can skip liner for a simple day look.  (Under application is easy to learn with a little practice and help from youtube tutorials.) I also like these lashes bc they are fanned and curled beautifully.  I don't care for the clumpy look, or overly dark color of other false lashes.  These are thin and delicate looking, like real hair, and the color is not too dark so they look natural.  I hope the price doesn't keep going up and they stay available on Prime which is another huge benefit, 10 great lashes for a great price shipped with Prime!

I will however see if the seller has other sizes and different sizes. I will definitely buy from this seller again. Very happy with my thrift purchase. Happy to see quality can still be had without breaking the bank.

UPDATE: not long after writing this review and daily use. Sadly several of the teeth started breaking off the combs. Plastic is bound to do that, give out. For $5 & such a large pack, they were bound to break. Just sad it happened so quickly. Overall, can’t beat $5 & the size. The combs still hold my hair even with a few of the teeth broken. rating 5.0. Different yes, I use it daily :) With cosmetics online it's difficult if you haven't seen the product first hand,choosing colors.

I swore I ordered a different color set than I received on this order. I haven't seen this Trio in store before.
The colors that I received, are a buff ivory (semi sheer) a taupe center (that I love) than is the bronzer which has orange undertones.

At first I cringed at the orange of the bronzer. Yet once applied they all blend well. It's a Fall / Winter combo for me.
I am enjoying the buff and taupe, very different from any blush, contour, this is new tones/colors compared to what I've used in the past.
A very natural effect. I have always been a fan of Physicans Formula. I am using it daily.

rating 4.0. Works on pale skin, sorta! I have to start by saying that I am a super pale girl! Therefore, my review might not be the best frame of reference (unless you are also a super pale person). The contour and bronzer shade are amazing! The contour specifically is the kind of shade that is neutral enough that it doesn't look out odd. It functions really well. A light hand is always a good idea. It is easy to build, as is the bronzer! This actually might be one of my most favorite bronzers. I 10/10 prefer matte over shimmer. I mean, its more orange on my skin than it would be on more fair than light skin, but its the kind of shade that can dual as a blush too. I like it, it brings the life back in me. The only thing is the highlight shade. I cannot use it for my face. It makes a decent light color for the eyes if I need it. Its just too dark on me and cannot do the job. Still, I don't regret buying this. The packaging is plastic, but really nice! It comes with a brush, but I used my own. Contour and bronzer blend out really easily too! Lastly, theres a little mirror which is always a bonus!

rating 4.0. Not ideal for very pale skin, but usable For the record, I am a longtime Physicians Formula wearer, and their products can be hit or miss. I am not being paid to leave a review, or representing anyone but myself here. This is the first time I tried PF's countouring powder, and I've used it for about three seasons. I like it. I don't love it.

I have very pale skin with pink undertones that is uber-sensitive and I have to be very careful about sun exposure. Anything can make me break out in hives or eczema, at any time, even if I used it daily for years. Thankfully, PF products have never done that to me, in almost a decade.

As for this product, the shades are where I take some issue. They worked best for my tone in summer, but now, in winter -- I'm shopping for a different product.

* The dark shade is really mahogany-like, so I have to blend, blend, blend in daylight or risk looking like I smeared mud on my face. It's good for shading jawline and cheekbones with a light touch at temples if you need it. Beware, though. If you are very pale, you want a very light touch with this one.

*The medium shade is very subtle and helps blend and soften the dark. It also works well as a matte bronzer, around sides of the nose, and wherever you need just a little definition. This is the best shade of the bunch, honestly. At least for my skin tone.

* The light shade is, frankly, worthless to me. It gives my skin a pasty, chalky look and looks heavy. Yuck. I occasionally use it in tiny spots as a concealer, but as a contour powder, it just looks like I put on way too much setting powder or something. Instead, I use my lightest shade of matte eyeshadow (also a PF product) and a higher-end illuminator for highlighting. Those results are beautiful, frankly, even on my 38 year old skin.

All in all, PF's contour colors are suitable for pale skin, but may be better on other slightly darker or warmer skin tones, or on summer tanned skin.

rating 5.0. This is my favorite sculpting product The matte sculpting trio is my daily go-to product. It does such a beautiful job of contouring my face, I don't even use blush anymore - I just let this bring my cheekbones out, brush a little highlight on the top, and it looks very natural and pretty.

rating 5.0. Yes! My hair can look like a magical unicorn tail! I have just about every hair styling tool you can think of...wands, traditional, wavers, everything. This was my first bubble wand. I have really long, really straight hair that fights curl and loves to go flat.

It took me a while to get the hang of how to wrap my hair around this wand for a great curl, but once I did, I was really impressed. It makes pretty waves all through the strand. Toward the top of your hair, it almost looks like finger waves. The best part is that the curls last a long, long time. Days! They relax in time, but they stay.

I love this curling iron. The waves can be as structured or loose as you want, depending on how long you hold the hair to the iron and on how thick of a strand you're curling.

Definitely a great purchase and the price was pretty good, too.

rating 5.0. I purchased this after reading the reviews and I'm glad I did I purchased this after reading the reviews and I'm glad I did. There are 2 distinct types of curls. rating 5.0. Hollywood hair at a discount price I really love this product.  I have long hair but not extremely long.  I like to curl or wave my hair several times a week so I have tried tons of different irons.  I like this  because it creates  a tighter wave...it's like a spiral curl and a wave got married and had a beautiful baby.  I find it looks best to spiral your hair around the skinnier sections between the bumps (not over the bumps).  For a fuller, more glamorous look, you can alternate the directions of the curls, or for a more wavy look do them all going away from your face. I found a short Bed Head youtube video for this product that I think is worth watching.  The heat dial just has low, medium and high not degrees and the cord is like mega, crazy long.  I think that's handy if you do other people's hair or if you want to keep your cat entertained while you primp.  For the price, especially, I think it's awesome.

rating 5.0. Perfect Nails This is the one of the best products for those that do their own nails at home and are not very good with applying acrylic freestyle.  Just mix your acrylic onto the brush and pat it into the nail form, once that is done, turn the form over and press it onto your own nail and hold.  Scrap any access of from sides, wait approx. 3 mins. until acrylic hardens, then pull the form off.  You will have a beautiful smooth acrylic nail and all that is left to do is just file the sides to shape the way you want.  Love, Love this product.

rating 5.0. Excellent for gel nail application also I used these to create a set of gel nails. I followed a tutorial on YouTube by JapanNailsandMore. I don't know her real name, but she's an experienced nail technician and cosmetologist who does great tutorial videos on YouTube. These forms are great for gel nail application, using clear builder gel (either non-soak off or soak-off) by IBD, and a UV light. You brush a small amount of the gel into the form and cure it on its own under the UV lamp for about 30 seconds, and then take it out. Then you brush more gel into the form and make sure it sticks to the sidewalls. You then place it over your finger and hold your finger under the lamp. You must hold the form down with another finger so as to prevent bubbling, at least for the first 10 seconds, then you can let go and let the lamp cure it for 2 minutes total. Once the lamp is turned off, remove your finger from the UV lamp and then pop the form off. It comes right off and the nail is PERFECT! So much easier than doing the sculptured nail forms. I am SOLD! Will never be without these again.

rating 5.0. If you want to cut back on nail salon visits try the forms They work exactly as they say. I have used these forms over and over and the results are always the same. The forms pop off easy and leave a smooth glossy acrylic nail. Can be used over acrylic nail tips or create the whole nail with acrylic powder. Comes with a mini cd with instructional videos. Strong, lasting, I would recommend using a clear powder. Very simple to do and will save on salon visits.
**When using these forms make sure to clean the nail bed well so the acrylic will adhere. If you don't the nail will lift around the nail bed and can cause injury if something is caught/snagged between the nail and your nail.
**If you are using just the acrylic (no nail tip) I would recommend you take a sharpie and draw a line across the outside of the nail to mark the desired length of the nail.

rating 5.0. Must have for do-it-yourselfers I have never successfully communicated the shape I want at a nail salon. Today I finally achieved the look I've been going for, and I didn't have to leave my house.
A few things to note:
1. I am not a professional but I have access to professionals and the supplies.
2. Any acrylic powder/liquid/primer works.
3. Make sure to use a good primer.
4. Watch the instructional video or YouTube. You do need to know how to work with acrylic and what to expect.
5. I have a steady hand and a lot of patience.
6. Get a good brush, and nail tips.
7. I do nail art for fun, typically using fake nails and creating sets. They don't last but 5 day tops. This solves that issue for me.
8. Cost: expect to spend about the same as two to three full sets initially to get everything you will need, but it will pay for itself in the long run. I went to Sally's Beauty Supply and grabbed a small kit, "no lift acrylic". Had no issues, set beautifully, and the forms popped off with ease.
9. Hold on for 20+ seconds then leave the form on it for at least 2 min. It will stay, I promise. You can scrape the excess off after the 20 seconds, it won't hardened too fast, and acrylic just peels off the skin anyway.

Couple of things I learned on my first try: if you press too hard sometimes too much squeezes out and can leave a little well, or negative spot on the nail. I found that waiting for it to cure, and then do the process again with the form, adding​ enough acrylic just to fill the hole and cover the nail in a thin layer so you don't have uneven spots. Honestly, it's a perfect product. I am beyond excited to wave goodbye to nail salons. Now I can do my little sister's nails for prom, and my friends can come over to get their nails done when their schedule allows it. I can do my nails how I want, where I want, and when I want.

input: rating 4.0. Worth the purchase The bleach didn't work for beans,  but the hair dye was very intense and brilliant.  I thought it would be muddy like the manic panic colors but it's much more vibrant.  I've had it in my hair for about a month now and it's still going strong.

rating 5.0. Worked perfectly for me! I purchased two kits, the Lusty Lavender and the Blue Envy, intending to dye blue and purple streaks into my hair. I probably should have known that I couldn't get streaks without foils, but that's my own failing. Other than that, though, the kits worked much better than I was expecting.

My hair is naturally a fairly light ash blonde, and it was highlighted a long time ago and never grew out, so I really only bleached it so the color would take well. I was surprised at the results of the bleach -- it pulled my hair to a lovely golden blonde that I might go back to after my color's faded out. It was strong, and the fumes didn't feel great on my eyes, but overall, that part of the kit wasn't lacking in the slightest.

The next day, I attempted to put in the purple and blue streaks. However, I didn't use enough dye, and I didn't leave them in for long enough, so they weren't dark enough for my liking. In addition, since I was doing it in stages, and I'd done two applications of bleach, I didn't have any gloves left to do the dyeing. DO NOT MAKE THAT MISTAKE. My hands were stained black, and the only things that made a significant dent in the color were nail polish remover and Veet hair remover. Wait until you have gloves. It was a horrendous mistake.

The color after the first application was more pink and cornflower blue, which wasn't exactly what I was looking for, so I went back and thoroughly dyed all of my hair but an inch or two at the top. I let it sit for about two hours, and then went to rinse it out -- a process which took nothing short of eternity. Two days after, I'm still rinsing out purple, but I expected that.

Once I finished the second application, I wound up with a very rich, dark, even, and dimensional color that's a mix of purple, blue, and indigo, lighter in some places, darker in others, which I think looks much more 'natural'. Great kit, although I do think they should include two pairs of gloves for people who dye their hair over two days.
rating 2.0. Color Does Not Last This color looks great...for about a week. Then it fades DRAMATICALLY :( Not long lasting at all, but if your looking for temporary color, give it a try!

rating 5.0. FUN!!! I decided to dye my hair for a Halloween event.  I got so many compliments and was told I should keep it permanent.  My hair is colored natural light blonde so I did not bleach it.  I did apply it twice because some areas were not as dark or even as I wanted. It was a lot of fun!  Be sure to put Vaseline around your face, ears, neck line, etc., however, if you do get it on your skin you can scrub it off with a buff puff in the shower.  It will continue to bleed when you shower even after the water has ran clear.  It will rub off on clothing if you sweat or your hair is wet.

rating 5.0. Here's a few tips... First off, I would recommend leaving the bleach in for at LEAST a half hour, and if your hair is even the slightest bit brown, leave in for about 40 minutes or more. I had dyed mine dark brown previously so I left it in for an hour. The longer you leave the color in, the more vibrant it will be. If you leave it in for the minimum time, it will look more blond. Also, if your hair is long, use ALL of the bottle. I would a little bit of bleach handy to wipe up the color from surfaces, as it will drip. Or, cover everything in newspapers or old towels. Make sure that you rinse the color out completely, and keep rinsing even when the water does run clear. I have gotten stains on pillows and blankets from not washing it out enough. Also, it would be better to buy color protecting shampoo/conditioner, as it will stay in longer. Overall, if you follow all the directions and use the tips I listed above, you will get a long-lasting, vibrant color.

rating 5.0. Works JUST like Special Effects Cupcake Pink Used this to get Pastel Pink (cotton candy colored) hair.
Works perfectly.
If you are wondering how I got it, instructions below.

First, bleach your hair to WHITE. Using toner helps. Then, after I rinsed my hair (use shampoo but no conditioner), I mixed a drop of dye with some cheap generic (WHITE) conditioner and mixed it. It needs to be a bit brighter than cotton candy. Then, cover your hair in the mix and let it sit for an hour or more, depending on how long you want it in. Apply heat every 20 minutes with a blowdryer. Then rinse. Your hair will not only be dyed, but it will be deep conditioned. :)

rating 5.0. Love It - Super Vibrant I've tried a couple of Splat colors now and have loved them.

Some reviews mention quick color fading. I have not experienced that at all. The first time I colored was with lusty lavender back in September it did fade a little and then stayed a purple-pink until I just recolored it with pink fetish this week (February). So five months later I still had color both on my natural color (gray and dark ash brown) and sections I bleached.

I did follow other reviews advice to leave the color on for 2-3 hours.

It does dye your skin something terrible. I've found facial toner helps remove it bug it does take a few days. Also, using Vaseline to protect skin around face, neck, and in the part helped a lot this time around. I was worried the Vaseline would prevent the hair from dying but it didn't seem to effect it.

Also, it gives GREAT grey coverage. I'm actually looking forward to going grey so I don't need to bleach my hair to get these great colors!!!

rating 5.0. 2/16/16 - This is truly amazing stuff! 2/16/16 - Eyelash Growth Serum for Long Eyelashes From Uptown Cosmeceuticals

This is truly amazing stuff!

First, let me say, no one has bribed me, coerced me, or given me freebies or a discount for my review. This is my honest experience since I received this product in January 2016.

I have always had short eyelashes. When I've used an eyelash curler in the past, my eyelashes looked nice to those standing directly in front of me, but from the side, my lashes looked unnaturally and abruptly turned upwards. I'm Japanese and have a very slim, below-the-crease eyelid, which means the upper eyelid area is substantially larger. With age, my upper eyelid has gotten heavier and a bit droopy, smothering my eyelashes. I always figured, "that's life."

Well, years ago my niece was going to try an eyelash growth serum, but was told she needed to see her eye doctor before they would give it to her. When she saw her eye doctor, she discovered that she had an eye problem that prevented her from being permitted to use the eyelash growth serum (not this particular eyelash growth serum. I think it must have been a prescription, back then. That's why she had to see her eye doctor before they'd give it to her.) Her eye medication had the same ingredient as the eyelash growth serum, so she could not use both. Several months ago, she told me about this and she said that her medication actually made her eyelashes grow.

A few weeks back, I was searching Amazon for mascara and I ran across these eyelash growth serums. I had no idea what the "active ingredient" was that made them work, so I had no idea what to buy, especially since there were so many to choose from. I suppose I could have done an "eeny, meeny, miney, moe", but I read the reviews on a few. There were a couple where the serum was to be applied directly to the lashes. Perhaps, they work; I don't know. But, what I do know is the hair, itself, is dead; its growth occurs within the hair follicle beneath the surface of the skin. Perhaps, the eyelash-applied serums are designed to slide back down the lashes to reach the skin and then seep into the hair follicle. It makes sense until you think about applying mascara--it would cover-up the serum. If the ultimate goal is for the serum reach the skin to bathe the hair follicle, why not apply it directly to the skin? So, this helped me with my selection.

When I reviewed this particular eyelash growth serum, most of the reviews were pretty good. The company, Uptown Cosmeceuticals made some fairly solid declarations: up to 25% thickness and length after two weeks, and 75% increase after six weeks of regular use. Of course, "up to" does not exclude 1% thickness & length, but the 25% & 75% measures were an impressive possibility. The clincher was the company's guarantee: if you're not completely satisfied, they'll refund your purchase. Another thing: all of the pictures--many reviewers provided pictures of the progress of their eyelash growth--a week, two weeks, three weeks, etc. Plus, so many reviewers stated that they saw increased lash length within a few days. I figured, what the heck; I've wasted more than $20 on useless stuff. I might as well try it.

I wish I had taken pictures, too. I know this sounds absurd, but I'd swear that my eyelashes grew after the first application ... I mean, a noticeable length. After four days, I could see them just peaking out from my upper eyelid. I received the serum on Jan 27th. Today is Feb 16th. My eyelashes are about 1/8" above my upper eyelid--no exaggeration. Now, when I use my eyelash curler, instead of a single abrupt bend in my eyelashes, I have enough lash to place several soft bends to give my lashes a natural curl. I'm truly amazed! My eyelashes were always so short. Now, I literally have something to flutter. There's something about eyelashes that makes a woman feel feminine.

After two weeks, when I knew there was growth, that it wasn't my imagination, I started applying the serum to my eyebrows. I have a relatively large, bald scar where I was hit on my brow-bone with a rock, and the outer edges of my eyebrows thinned out after I went through "the change." I was curious to see if it would help. It's only been a week, but there's definitely an improvement. It's not clear to me if there is new hair growth, but my existing brow hairs are definitely longer. The extra length covers the scar in my left brow so well, that a couple of times, I turned to my right brow to look for the scar--it wasn't as blatantly obvious as it has been for the last 40 years!

There is one recommendation I'd make to anyone who tries this serum:
After two weeks, I noticed that my lashes on the left seemed to be slighly longer than those on my right. I think this was because I was applying the serum to my left lash-line, first. Without re-dipping the brush into the serum, I'd apply the serum to my right lash-line. I think my right lashes weren't getting as much of the serum, as the left. So I started applying the serum to the right lash-line, first. Once my lashes appear equal in length, I'll alternate which lash-line I apply the serum first, or re-dip the brush to give each lash-line a full brush of the serum. One last idea: another reviewer said that once her lashes reached the length she desired, she reduced her application of the serum to once a week. I thought that was a good idea.

To anyone who purchases this serum because of my contribution to its accolodes, I hope it works as well for you as it has for me.

input: rating 5.0. Good stuff! Smells great. A coupleof tablespoons of water on a damp brush to shave my entire face. Very little odor, which as a healthcare pro, is important. The full fat lanolin leaves my face smooth and clean. Blends with a pea sise dollop of cream for the best of both worlds. Worth the price up front....ends up less expensive that buying cans.
rating 5.0. Five Stars Truly great soap. Very light scent, but the lather is amazing, as is the feel of your skin afterwards.

rating 5.0. I'm spoiled now. Knowing the softening effect lanolin has on skin, I had high hopes ordering this soap and was not disappointed.
The soap takes a few minutes of work to get a suitable lather, but I'm not in a hurry when getting ready to lay a straight razor against my throat.
The lather has a pleasant scent and does a great job of lubricating the skin.
I have found the soap easy to rinse off with no lingering film and leaves my face supple.
The soap cake was a bit larger than my shaving mug, requiring that I shave down the edges to fit and knead the flakes back in with a little warm water, but my mug runs a little on the small side.
Overall, I would recommend this soap to anyone interested in wet shaving, it performs well and the old-fashioned looking packaging actually makes for a neat decorative item if you're going for a Victorian Era look.

rating 5.0. An excellent soap, and much less tempermental than I thought it would be I've been trying out new shaving soaps, and this was the latest one after trying Tabac (really loved that one too). From all of the reviews I've seen here I thought it would be tempermental to work with, but it wasn't that bad at all. In fact, if you can get a good lather out of Williams shaving soap (and yes, it can be done), then lathering with Mitchell's Wool Fat is a snap by comparison.

The big thing to remember is that just like Williams and Tabac, this is a hard-milled, tallow-based soap, and unlike the glycerin-based soaps of Van Der Hagen or Colonel Conk, a tallow soap needs to be used with lots of water, and the hotter you can get that water, the better the lather and cushion will be.
The big secret to getting great lather when I use this soap-or any other tallow soap-is to fill the bowl it's in with boiling hot water until it's about 1/2inch over the soap and let it soak for at least 5 minutes before lathering. After that the soap is properly hydrated and will work very well with whatever brush you use in your daily routine, just be sure to dump out the water before you lather up.

Some people have compared the scent to Williams, and it definitely has a "clean" scent, but it's not nearly as "cleaning product-y" to my nose. My wife REALLY likes the scent a lot, too, so it's a winner in my book. Will definitely buy again.

rating 5.0. Best shaving soap I ever used. Wonderful shaving soap.  Lasts a long time and smells great.

rating 5.0. The best shave soap/creme I have ever used!!! The best shaving soap I have ever used. Hands down. I have been wet shaving with a safety razor for over ten years. I have been searching for the perfect shaving soap or creme for the same amount of time. I have used everything from King of Shaves, Proraso, Cremo Creme, etc, and nothing has been as comfortable as the Mitchell's Wool Fat Soap. Its an absolute joy to use. There are a few tricks to using this soap. If you want to see a good video review online, I recommend googling "Mantic,  Mitchell's Wool Fat Shaving Soap." This gentlemen provides great tips on how to get the most out of this shaving soap. For instance, for the best lather, you should use a "Boar Bristle Brush" which is stiffer than a badger brush. I have a "Super Badger Brush" which doesn't make near the lather that the stiffer, boar bristle brush makes during lathering. One last point, as other reviewers have pointed out, this soap does not have a strong scent, its just very subtle and clean. That was a plus for me. If you are looking for something with a stronger scent you may want to look elsewhere.

rating 5.0. Wet shavers swear by it for a reason. This is probably one of the best shaving soaps you'll ever find. It is not as difficult to lather as some say it is but you do have to work a bit longer if your water is on the harder side as is mine. But that doesn't stop me from making a really nice lather with this. I prefer to use a boar brush with soaps but badger works well too. When prepared properly it produces a very slick lather that protects the skin very well. I think one of the best qualities of this soap, however, is the feel of your skin after your shave. The lanolin in this soap gives a great post shave feel to your skin that I have not experienced with any other soap. As far as the scent goes, it is just kind of a classic soapy smell that is rather mild and doesn't seem to linger very long but it is very pleasant. I still think the Taylor of Old Bond Street sandalwood is a winner for one of the best scents but I still think MWF is a better performer. Many wet shavers swear by this soap for a reason. It is just one of the finest soaps you'll find anywhere.
rating 5.0. Why did they invent canned foam Excellent shaving soap. No problems lathering. Extremely slick. Not really sure why you can only find shave foam in pressure cans in the stores.

rating 5.0. What a soap! I have only used this a few times so far, but WOW so far I love it. I'm gifted with tough facial hair and skin that doesn't get along with shaving. Anyone lucky to have facial hair growing in different directions and can only shave with the grain understand this.
Strong smell which isn't unpleasant, it's a clean smell - no issues with lather but I have a water softener.
Each time I've used it I've been impressed with the ease of the shave, and the lack of razor rash. I will revisit this review based on how long the cake lasts, other reviews I've read state Mitchell's Wool Fat Soap lasts for some time so I'm curious to see how long this cake lasts me.

rating 5.0. Why this soap is not expensive Like other hard-milled soaps, it will last ten times longer than ordinary soaps, including "ordinary" shaving soap. Plus it smells great, light and subtle and the lather is amazing. Like my friend Harlan once said: "You don't always get what you pay for, but you never get MORE than you pay for." I paid a bit more for some castile hand soap a few months ago. I've been using the same bar to wash my hands several times a day for at least two months and it looks just about the same size. Sometimes money buys quality AND economy. Sometimes it just tells the world that you're a snob. But beware of false economies. As an added note, buying items made by slave-labor is a false economy.
