rating 5.0. Amazing product- worked for me! I have fair, sun-damage prone skin, minor rosacea and mild acne. I have only used a few times so far, and I am thrilled to see my skin is smoother looking and brighter already. Even if you're young, this product is a great alternative to expensive treatments at the spa. It did burn a bit at first but that subsided within a few minutes. You just leave it on--no washing off. I'll update as I get farther along. Soo happy! :)

rating 5.0. Amazing Product! I am absolutely thrilled with the results that I have gotten with this product in the short time that I have been using it. I am skeptical about most products that promise results in which acne or fine lines will improve dramatically with use but this is the only one that has delivered on those promises for me.

I am 36 years old and I have fair, sensitive, oily, extremely acne prone skin and it has been a problem for me for over 20 years. I have tried everything to keep my acne under control. Everything! But nothing has ever worked for long. My biggest problem is pores that clog like crazy and nothing I tried could keep them clear, so I would be broken out constantly or just ready to break out. It doesn't help that I am skin obsessed either ( touching and picking is a no-no, but I couldn't help it) so my skin always looked raw and sore. I also have scarring and and using makeup to help cover the damage only made me break out more, so I felt helpless everyday and depressed.

After going to a dermatologist, he said that I was going about this the wrong way entirely. I had been using benzoyl peroxide products and he said that for older skin this can aggravate the problem even more. Instead, he gave me a glycolic acid/ salicylic acid face wash and told me to try this product in addition to see if it helped at all to keep my pores unclogged and it really does! I used to have many blackheads and clogged pores and now they are drastically reduced. I look at my skin and see very few to none now and for me that is a miracle. I am losing the desire to constantly touch my face as well. This product makes your skin baby soft and helps to erase fine lines too. I also use it on my chest, where I also used to break out constantly, and that has helped enormously as well.

The price, benefits and promises that this product deliver are all reasons to buy this product. But you will keep using it after discovering how amazing it can be for your skin to be mostly clear almost all of the time if you have suffered with acne as I have. I am so so happy with the results I got.

When first using it your skin will tingle and feel burning, but that goes away. I only use it at night and I also mix the product with moisturizer to hydrate my skin at the same time. Also, you might break out a little until you have used the product for a few days as I did, but then I cleared up and have stayed clear. This truly is a miracle in a jar!

rating 5.0. AMAZING I've been using this product off and on for about a year now. I feel like it's a face lift in a bottle. I put it on before bed and wake up feeling like my skin is tight and my pores are small. My skin is so soft when I use it regularly. I love it. I use the Alpha Hydrox lotion on my back and it cleared up any blemishes I would get from time to time. I've never had perfect skin in my life until I tried this line of products. I will warn that if you get this product on sensitive areas, it can burn. I've gotten cold sores before if I accidentally get it on my lips. It can irritate sensitive skin so keep it away from those areas.

rating 5.0. Only a week and my skin is FABULOUS! I've only been using this product for a little over a week and can already see a marked difference in the condition of my skin.  I am in my upper 20's with combination skin, large pores on my nose and on the apples of my cheeks, and am prone to adult acne.  I'm starting to see very slight laugh lines and a few lines between my eyebrows.  After just a week, my skin is smoother, my pores are refined, and my fine lines are diminishing.  ONE WEEK!  My skin has not broken out and purged yet as others have warned.  My acne scars have faded.  My skin is not oily nor dry when using this product and I have not had any sensitivity issues (I usually have sensitive skin).  This has been a miracle product!  I have been using it morning and night after cleansing, and have no problems applying makeup over it as it absorbs well into the skin.  A little bit goes a long way.  If you're looking to try a glycolic acid product for minor resurfacing, this is a great place to start.  It's an excellent product for the price!
rating 2.0. Tough on skin This product really irritated my skin. I wasn't able to use it long enough to find out if it worked.

rating 3.0. No result I've been using this for almost 3 months but I haven't seen any change to my skin, except that the cheek areas on the sides of my nose are not as rough as they used to be. Other than that, my pores are still as enlarged as before, my age spots (and believe me, they are very small, and only three of them) are not improved and the texture of my skin is unchanged.

I like the consistency, though. It's very light and not greasy so I prefer it over Oil of Olay.
I probably won't buy again if I find a better exfoliating product.

rating 5.0. The only beauty cream you need. A friend told me that her facialist told her to always use AH every night. Since AHA by itself is hardly available at your standard drug store, I tried amazon and found this

This cream is wonderful.

All you need is to get facials periodically and use this cream at night and you will have smooth skin always.

I like to mix a little of this with my favorite moisterizer at night as my skin does well that way.

When I stop using this for whatever reason, it is obvious every time; rough crappy skin.

-Oh and I have very sensitive skin prone to breakouts and have had no issues ever with this product.

rating 5.0. The real deal With regular use of this 12% alpha hydroxy acid, you will have smoother, silkier skin in no time. There are so many skin products out there that are horrendously expensive but not nearly effective as this. "Souffle" perfectly describes the lightness of this cream. It just melts into your skin. You can wear this as a day or night cream.

I used Alpha Hydrox skin products for a long time. But, the big box store I went to stopped carrying the brand. I was delighted to find it again!

rating 5.0. Alpha Hydrox I love the way my face looks.  I am a 60 year old and thought I needed a Face lift.  The more I use this product - the better I feel about myself.  [[ASIN:B000G8LWZI Alpha Hydrox 12% Glycolic AHA Souffle-1.6 oz]]

rating 5.0. Seriously, the best skin product out there I get asked all the time what skin cream I use. This is a main staple in my daily routine. I use this, then sunscreen. That's it. It's just a fantastic product, and I could not be more pleased with it.

rating 5.0. Terrific! - Beware of packaging I am in my late 30s with fair skin and adult onset acne. I have started using the souffle about three months ago and have seen great results in not only significant reduction of break outs, but also in the way old scars and spots fade and become less visible. I should say that this product does take a couple of weeks to get used to as it did leave my skin stinging just slightly after application at first and I did see some redness. However, as the instruction leaflet says these do go away. Some people may find it helpful to use every other day at first, which is what I did. I now use the face wash from this line and the lotion which I use on my body every now and then, not daily. I do see that my skin looks brighter and more radiant. I am very happy with having finally found a product that works. Just do not forget to use a sunscreen!

UPD: The product no longer comes packaged as described. I attempted to receive what's pictured twice, and had to return it twice. The souffle used to come in a JAR, but only comes in a TUBE now. For those who don't like creams in a tube (I don't because a lot more of it is wasted as it is impossible to squeeze out exact amounts and pointless to get whatever little is left when the tube is almost empty) this is a point to consider. Amazon needs t change product description and picture. Switching to 10% cream from this line as it is available in a jar.

rating 5.0. Fantastic product, great price!! I'm a 37 year old Mom with oily fair skin. I've been trying for years to find a product that will clear up my skin.  My Mom owns a salon and day spa so I've tried over the counter, prescription and spa products. Nothing has been as effective as alpha hydrox lotion and/or the souffle.  Not only has my skin cleared up, but my fine lines have almost disappeared.  I just recently switched to the souffle and I find it to be even better than the alpha hydrox lotion.  I also use the retinol night rescue at night before I put on the souffle.  So that may be helping with the acne and fine lines as well.  The foaming face wash is a great mild cleanser too. However, I find it doesn't remove all of my eye makeup sometimes but probably because it is a mild cleanser.  The cost of these products is very reasonable too!!  Be warned, you will notice a tingling sensation after putting on the alpha hydrox lotion or souffle. This is normal and it means the AHA is working.  Also, be careful if you use a daily cleansers with exfoliating beads or other grainy ingredient.  The AHA is naturally taking off some of that superficial dead skin on the top layer. If you use an exfoliating cleanser daily plus the alpha hydrox lotion or souffle, you could be stripping away too much skin and your skin can become very irritated and possibly break out more. Best to use a mild cleanser when using AHAs and use an exfoliating type cleanser once a week.  Good luck and hope you like this product line is much as me!!

rating 5.0. I love it This lotion really helps keep my skin looking clear.  I have melasma and it is the best lotion I have found to keep it at bay - it even beats out prescription hydroquinone, which I have used for years.

current_product_reviews: rating 5.0. Who Ever Is Listening.......... I am 58. I have been accused of being my son's girlfriend -- solely on the basis of my skin. I have been using La Prairie Cellular Radiance for the last nine years. I am a registered nurse -- it was another RN who introduced me to La Prairie via a fabulous Nordstrom facial special. Many times, I have tried different, cheaper, well-known and less effective moisturizers; all with the result of minor to major breakouts. Then I saw a review of AHA Souffle on MakeUpAlley.com and sent for the cheapest jar. I started to use it after my shower and before my La Prairie Cellular Radiance Cream. What a miraculous cream. NEVER has my skin looked this fabulous. No wrinkles, my age spots are fading (they used to be cute little freckles), and acne scars are fading fast.

rating 5.0. This is my second bottle ( which my husband added to a gift packcage for me knowing how much i loved it) My skin is very dry I have used several high end moiturizers over the years, and by doing some research just found Insta Natural.

by the way:

rating 3.0. PUMP BROKEN, had to pry open and transfer to another jar. Yep...these pumps are really crappy make, as I have seen with other reviews...so bad, because I would have give 5 star for the moisturizer...but now, that it has been adulterated and had to be scooped into another old container, I feel weird...

Anyway, overall, it feels greasy, thick and heavy at first, BUT do not despair, in about 2 minutes it absorbes perfectly into your skin and feels clean, moisturized and never greasy, perfect base for make up.
Somehow did make my skin look healthier, plumper, and cause absolutely no breakouts...I did noticed some blackheads and pores on my nose filling up so it is possible that the oils kind of accumulate, but right after using exfoliator, I was fine again...

would buy again, but PLEASE, fix those damn pumps...I cannot run back to the post office every time...and I will not like keeping defective product again!

UPDATE 12/21/14
Very sad to report that now I have stopped using the cream alltogether. I will post update if anything changes.
Reasons: As I have mentioned in my first review, I noticed this cream is very heavy, but absorbs very well. However over past weeks of using it, it seems it has completely blocked my pores, in a very sneaky, slow, invisible manner. The problems started when l found painful bumps between my eyebrows and on my temples and huge hard pores on my chin. I do not use hair products, so it is not from that. I am a picker, so tried to relieve these pores by extracting the blackheads, and exfoliating, in hopes it would relieve them, as well as aid in removing the deposits this moisturizer might leave during the day. I tried this for a while only to find more sneaky blackheads and whiteheads forming in places I would have never had this problem before. See, I have a skin that sometimes has outbreaks but not in the typical oily/teenager spots, but since I have started using this cream, the areas of no problems before have become breeding grounds for families of nasty, hardened pores. Unfortunately they are very hard, painful and almost impossible to remove without damage...I have stopped using the moisturizer and now will wait it out. I guess I might do better with a gel based moisturizer.

rating 2.0. Disappointing. Didn't hydrate me. Disappointed. I tried this with combination of the Vitamin C Serum and it never hydrated me.

rating 5.0. Highly Recommended! I'm a Caucasian woman in my late fifties and I bought this product several weeks ago because I liked the natural ingredients and hoped they would nourish my skin. I neglected my skin until after menopause when I became distressingly aware of how rapidly I had aged.
In the course of the past 2 yrs. I have tried numerous products and been moderately satisfied.
I LOVE this cream!
In the past several weeks fine lines and wrinkles have gradually but distinctly diminished. But even more important and incredible is that the red, blotchy areas on my cheeks and forehead have faded until they are barely noticeable! I no longer use cover up or foundation at all!
Friends and family have commented on how great I look. At the pharmacy, I was talking to another woman in line about hot flashes and she thought I was too young for menopause! OMG!!!
I highly recommend this cream.

Secondly, it contains beeswax, a known comedogenic ingredient that doesn't belong in a facial moisturizer. rating 5.0. Fun and comfy! Thank you for your reviews . . . shopping made easy! Even with the reviews, these are a much better quality than expected. Choose a style that suits your mood. If you have a problem with the bands not staying in place, try a few quick fixes: place the bands  around your neck (necklace style) and fit one band at a time. Place the first band toward the front and slip the second band behind it. It can be secured with a fancy/plain bobby pin, too. If one band holds the hair in place and the second one just won't hold, a snip of the scissors is an easy quick fix. Tie the ends of the band you cut in a knot and see how that fits. Try using it over the very wide bands for a fun look. If all else fails, you have a shimmer band (necklace) to use with scarfs!

input: rating 5.0. Much more then I expected I have used this brush for about a week now and my teeth do feel cleaner. I have sensitive gums, so I was worried the more powerful vibrations of the brush would cause some damage but on the contrary, my gums feel healthier and stronger now. If you are looking for a good tooth brush, you will not be disappointed. I admit I was a bit hesitant to spend $80 on the Oral B S-200 but it was definitely worth it for me.
rating 5.0. sonic I love this toothbrush.  It certainly helps me to brush longer.  My husband is not keen on the sound however.
rating 5.0. Oral B Model S-200 Sonic Complete Rechargeable Power Toothbrush Have daughter with braces...her teeth never looked better.  Much improved from just using a standard toothbrush.

rating 5.0. Amazingly clean teeth for a very reasonable price... The sensations of this toothbrush are weirdly satisfying especially if you have never used a sonic toothbrush before. They key selling point is that our teeth have never felt quite so polished.  The price is great, the brush is simple and pleasant to use. Would recommend.
rating 2.0. Batteries are not replaceable Good toothbrush but the batteries are not replaceable so you need to replace the whole unit when they won't hold a charge anymore

rating 5.0. Good Toothbrush This toothbrush is much better than the Oral-B Crossaction Power that I used earlier. The head is nice and small and gets into hard to reach areas, the brush stand has no exposed metal (it's really cool from an engineering standpoint, they seem to be using a magnetic field to charge the battery), so it's pretty safe to leave by the sink.

After using the brush, my teeth feel smooth & clean, like after going to a dentist. It takes a little getting used to but the bristles are soft and don't hurt your gums.

I've been using the product only for a month, some other reviewers were disappointed with the battery life, I can't comment on that yet. However, I think Ni-Cd batteries are happier if you put them through full charge/discharge cycles, a full charge lasts a week. At least 250 full charge/discharge cycles is what I expect out of Ni-Cd's, charging once every week gives me about 250 weeks (little more than 4.5 years). I don't actually think I'll have the toothbrush that long, but the point is that I think the battery should be fine if you don't charge it everyday.

Also, this really shouldn't matter but this toothbrush is made in Germany.

rating 5.0. Very good electric toothbrush I have owned and used the Oral B Sonic Complete model S-200 for nearly three years. I should note that a lot of this review is probably applicable to the S-320 model as well.

When I first started using the toothbrush, it took a while to get used to the sonic vibrations in my mouth. It was a very unusual sensation--almost tickled--and made me laugh, but I soon got used to it after about a week. Interestingly, when my girlfriend first started using it she had the same experience, couldn't stop laughing.

I noticed immediately that my teeth felt much cleaner, very smooth. It was effortless to clean my teeth since the toothbrush did all the real work. One thing I noticed that was not advertised was that in addition to the 2-minute timer (three long pauses), the brush also indicates 30-second intervals (one short pause), which I found very helpful for timing even brushing around all my teeth.

I purchased replacement heads at regular intervals, and would recommend the soft heads. After about two years of using the brush, I noticed some small areas at the base of a few teeth that became VERY sensitive to the slightest pressure. My dentist informed me that this was due to gum recession (when the gums are pushed back from the tooth, exposing the more delicate and HIGHLY sensitive roots of the tooth) caused by vigorous brushing. I didn't think I was brushing vigorously at all, but I had been using the regular "clean" setting. After hearing that this discomfort was due to brushing I started using the "soft" setting and things returned to normal, and this lower setting still effectively cleaned my teeth.

Unfortunately, in the last few weeks the toothbrush would occasionally not turn on, and eventually started to spontaneously/randomly turn on for brief moments. Apparently this was its death cry. I'm not sure whether leaving the toothbrush on the charger all the time would over time lead to diminished battery life, but would recommend against storing it on the charger.

Overall, I really liked the sonic complete toothbrush, would buy it again, and highly recommend everyone use a sonic toothbrush.

Pros:
- superior teeth cleaning
- 2-minute and 30-second timers
- does not automatically turn off after two minutes like some other electronic toothbrushes
- long-lasting charge (took it on several two-week vacations and it still had more charge left when I returned home)
- storage for three additional heads in charging station*
- toothbrush head holder in charging station can be pulled out easily for routine cleaning*
- drainage holes in the bottom of each toothbrush head slot (no nasty buildup) and good ventilation allows heads to dry (no mildew/mold)*
- wall-mountable charging station*
- non-slip rubber bottom on charging station
- solid construction, heads stay on

Cons:
- no replacement battery (dead battery = bulky manual toothbrush)
- no carrying case (apparently the S-320 model does)
- very long & bulky (it was hard to fit into my toiletries travel case)
- no PDF/digital owner's manual (OK, so this might be nit-picking, but keeping the printed manuals for every electronics gadget is a pain!)

*It appears that the S-200 model no longer comes with the larger charging station with head storage, but the S-320 model does.

rating 4.0. Oral B Placing the order and receiving it were as simple as it should be.  No delays or hidden charges. The product is great - I've owned one before so was expecting just what I got.  I'd recommend both the product and using Amazon to purchase.

rating 5.0. Great Toothbrush For The Blind So you've read the reviews about how this is a great toothbrush that does the job and your teeth will be clean.  This review chooses to focus on the color scheme of this Oral-B unit.  I chose to rate this product with five stars.  If I had the option, I would specifically rate this a 4.9, less than a perfect score, solely because of the questionable color plastics chosen for this toothbrush.  To start it off, the front of the handle was made aquamarine.  This hideous color should not match any sane person's bathroom decor.  But if that wasn't bad enough, the back of the handle is a repugnant seafoam green color.  Between these varying shades of hideousness, a strip of cerulean blue flake plastic accents the toothbrush.  The brush head itself has a ring of royal blue around the neck, and the back of the brush head is cornflower blue.  Of course, the bristles themselves are yet another random shade of blue.  In conclusion, this is a very solid product that is held back by the fact that it looks like a hodgepodge of different color plastics, none of which come anywhere near matching or aethsetically pleasing.  Why can't they just make this toothbrush a simple black, white, or any solid color?  I recommend this product to anyone who wants clean teeth and can't decide between the color green or blue.

rating 5.0. Great Brush I bought this toothbrush about 2 years ago and have happily used it ever since. It leaves the teeth feeling clean and is not too bulky to hold. I find the timer buzz convenient and easy to ignore if you chose so. Can't see why people find it annoying.

Both my husband and myself use the same model - we just change brush heads. The brush head replacements are inexpensive and come with different colored rings. The battery holds charge forever, which is great for trips. We've taken it to 8-10 day trips without the charger, and used it both twice a day, and it still had charge. Highly recommended.

rating 5.0. Oral B S-200 is powerful / easy to reach into tight places This is my first sonic toothbrush and I am please with its performance.

Pros:

-long handle makes it easy to reach into tight places
-operates app. 2 weeks on one charge (takes 12-14 hrs to fully charge) and the charger unit makes a convenient stand for the toothbrush
-charging unit serves as toothbrush stand (would be even better if the power cord was detachable from the charging base to allow for clutter-free storage for the toothbrush between charges)
-price; I had considered the Sonicare product but decided on this because of the huge price difference. I am satisfied.

Cons:

rating 5.0. Excellent toothbrush, especially for what I paid for it I was a little wary of buying the Oral-B S-200 because I'd had a Sonicare Advance 4100 for probably 3 years before. rating 4.0. Oral B S-200 Sonic - nice I had bought a Sonicare toothbrush not too long ago as I had one that died. On a recent trip to the mountains of Colorado I misplaced the Sonicare and cannot find it. I bought this for a couple of reasons - it was less expensive than the Sonicare, I wanted to compare the two, and if I find the Sonicare I will have two different brushes.

So far I have been very pleased with the unit. Has many of the same features and the timer is a nice feature. Pros are that it seems to be as efficient as the Sonicare, easier to keep clean, and is lighter. Cons are that after the timer goes through it cycle it does not shut off the toothbrush. Also, the location of the on/off button is such that (for the way I use it) it is easy to change the setting on the brush or shut it off. Once you change it the timer resets itself.

All in all I would recommend this to anyone.

rating 4.0. it lightened up to a medium red color that I am fairly happy with. The product does have a sulfurous odor I have been dyeing my hair with dark Demi colors for two years, first a dark red, and then a blue/black. Considering that my hair was quite dark, it lightened up to a medium red color that I am fairly happy with. The product does have a sulfurous odor, but that seems par for the course with color removers and it did not leave my hair damaged.
rating 1.0. One Star Didn't work AND killen my hair

rating 5.0. Fantastic with a stench of excellence Let me start off by saying this is an excellent product if you can get past the smell. After looking up someone using it on youtube I had to try it. As someone who frequently takes the adventure down the hair dye abyss, removing hair dye with less damage sounded beyond appealing. So I was excited to try something other than bleach to remove the red I had recently dyed my hair. When I opened bottle one I was hit with a smell that made my stomach turn. I will be honest I usually pride myself on my strong stomach, but this stench (no other word for it) made me gag multiple times. Bottle two had a more pleasant smell but it was still not appealing to the nose. Mixing the two together was a smell close to vomit. But all in all stench aside it did remove the majority of the dye in the 20 minutes it recommends on the box. It did leave a residual smell on my hair that even shampoo and two conditioners couldn't remove. Before and immediately after using the Salerm Color Reverse

Update:
I have since used this again the bottles gave me about two treatments due to how thick my hair is, it did further remove more of the dye from my hair but it left it feeling highly dried out and weak.

rating 5.0. It works! Yup, it smells. However, ALL color reverse products smell like donkey poo. Nevertheless, after about three applications, it removed most of my boxed dyed black hair. Which is AMAZING. So, if you can get past the smell, this product does actually get the job done.

rating 5.0. Works good for product without bleach or ammonia Hair dyes work as deposit on your natural hair so if your natural hair has been bleached prior to coloring then chances are your natural hair is also dry & damaged under the color dye. Salermo Color Reverse removed most of the demi-permanent color I've been using. It works pretty good for having no bleach or ammonia-(however it does have an ammonia smell, so expect that). I'd recommend not using it more than twice depending the condition of your hair. For my hair, I'm using it just twice. The 2nd time should remove all of the rest of demi color in my hair. I'm very satisfied with this product.

My intent is to do an overall color much lighter but I had to remove the hair dye in order for the next color to hold. I'm very satisfied with the amount of color it removed.
rating 2.0. Two Stars Didn't do a great job. Won't repurchase.

rating 2.0. Didn't lift the color out. It did not lift the dye out at all. Not giving it 1 star because I'm not sure if it's Ion's fault for making such a sticky blue, or Salern's fault for not working. I bought the product because I watched it work on someone else with my own eyes, but it certainly did not lighten my hair, even after three uses.
rating 4.0. Pulls out a lot of color. Before and after looks really beautiful. Only one complaint is that it smells like like rotten eggs.

rating 5.0. AWESOME. 10 out of 10! I natural golden-blonde hair with a lot of beautiful natural highlights. I dyed it DARK red last july and the red was still stuck in my hair (it's January now). I had tried all natural methods first with no luck. I really didn't want to bleach my hair so I decided to give this a shot. It took a couple rounds, but I have my NATURAL BLONDE back and my hair feels absolutely great. I am so stoked about this!! Also, yes, it does stink a little but it's not as bad as people make it out to be as long as you don't shove your nose in the mixture haha.

rating 1.0. My hair is green!!! This looked promising at the beginning I did all of my research and seen nothing of what I went through except the very foul smell. I did everything I was supposed to then 3 whole hours from the time I finished washing and conditioning my hair my oldest daughter told me my hair was green in the back. I was very happy with the immediate color tht  it was after the red was out but now I look like shreck it left my hair as green as ever and now I have no clue where to go from here. I will never use this product again.

rating 4.0. My Detailed experience using this product! I waited to write a review till I used it(3 times). Yes it works. Yes it smells sulfurous, but it's not that horrible. I'm caucasian, not of ethnicity. Not course grey hair either. I have hard to dye hair (due to hormones) that always needs dyed twice to achieve desired color. Darkest brown. 1st use removed the color from the hair closest to my roots that was dyed the least. Heat Definitely NEEDS applied(which I tried the 2nd time). 20mins is NOT enough time, I went to 40mins (which I didn't try till the 3rd use. I was worried my hair would melt. It didn't & won't.) It turns the hair a red-ish auburn/orangey color depending on original shade. The 3rd time I used an ash blonde box dye after to neutralize the color remover instead of the 10 vol mixed with water. It gets darker after it's neutralized. It only lightened it to a medium brown on the darkest part of my hair. My hair still got dryed out. Not as bad as it would from stripping. So does works but it's not for everyone. I would recommend this product for a person who doesn't have any issues dying their hair. Or only needs a single dye job undone.

rating 1.0. Do NOT BUY! Item was received open and product was all over the box. Which made everything it touches smell rotten

rating 5.0. Smells a little but worth every penny! I only used this once and loved the results! I have had had bright red hair over my previous blonde balayage for about a year or so. We went in to bleach it out with over 40 bleach sitting for an hour and hardly any color lifted..... so I searched for an at home option to strip the red and not damage my hair. This worked amazingly! I did 30 minutes for my first treatment and all the red that was over the blonde is gone!!! Funny enough, I didnt put the stuff on my brown hair and the red is still on that but gone from the blonde!!! Will possibly use a second treatment to get the red out of the brown. My hair was a little smelly after but I used some peppermint oil diluted in water and sprayed it over, no smell left!!!!! And my hair is silky smooth without being dried out like bleach. Love this product and super recommend!! Showed before and after 1 use!

rating 5.0. It works I have had red hair (box dye) for a year and this stuff took majority of it out, first try, no problem. Since red is so ridiculously hard to get out, I am doing the reverse a 2nd try then recoloring. I advise you to read the instructions so no harm is done to your hair*

input: rating 5.0. This is way better than foil fingers This, being steam, for me, is the answer to the dilemma of damage done by harsh acetone to remove gel nails.  It's not perfect, and it takes a bit longer than they say, and the documentation is weak, BUT, I'll never go back.

rating 5.0. Worth Every Penny This is amazing.  I purchased the white one and it arrived exactly on time and early enough in the day that I was able to use it that same day.  I put my fingers in for about 5 minutes and when i pulled them out the polish was curling and peeling away where I had filed the top coat.  It was fantastic.  I did not need to use the cuticle pusher to remove the strips, I just pushed it with my thumb nail and it slip right off.  There were some some spots that needed more time, but I just put those fingers back into the acetone.  I love it that it comes with 2 extra gaskets and two extra baskets.  I love it!

rating 5.0. Better than soaking Works on acrylic nails without pealing off the top layer of nail or hurting my hands. I have only used once because I only remove my nails once a month or longer if I can, but it was much. Better than soaking or using foil or gloves. Both hands took me about 3 hours so I purchased a 2nd one so I could do each hand at the same time and I think I got the hang of getting the Acrylic off better towards the end so I hope to cut the time down to less than a hour next time.

It also came quickly

rating 2.0. Customer service is good. REVIEW UPDATE: I was contacted by Blue Top and they offered to either send me a new machine or to refund my purchase price. I opted for the refund. In return they have asked me to change my rating as it "has had a very bad effect on them." They also offered to send, at no cost to me, a few other items ranging from approximately $2.50 to $6.50 - I don't have the offer in front of me at the moment. I had no use for the items they were offering. They also followed-up SEVERAL times advising that, because they "had been successful at getting [me] a full refund" they would appreciate my changing my review rating. I was unable to respond to them as my computer was in the shop for a few days and then everything had to be reinstalled. So, I'm just getting back and up running. While I appreciate the refund SO VERY much, and I appreciate the customer service, I am unable to change my rating of the item itself. I can add a more positive seller rating, but the product itself, whether refunded or not, was still defective, in my opinion, and if someone else can use that information, I'd rather keep my opinion of the item itself honest.

--Would I buy another? No.
--Would I recommend it to friends or family? No.
--Would the item have been worth the cost if it had worked properly? Certainly.
--Was customer service helpful? Yes.
--Would I buy other items from this company based on the product? No.
--Would I buy other items from the company based on the customer service? Yes.

I hope that helps!

ORIGINAL REVIEW: Only used the steamer once so far, but it took me 20 minutes just to remove the gel from ONE fingernail. I had to fix the accent nail on one hand when I chipped the nail, but didn't have the same color that the salon used. So, I was taking the color off the accent nail on the other hand so that my accent nails would match. Anyway, the plastic insert that holds the acetone doesn't fit snugly in the machine. It's plastic, so when you put it in the steamer, it pops up just a little. I added a little pressure to push it back down, but as soon as I took my hands off of it, it popped back up again. So, I don't know if it can actually make enough contact with the machine to get warm enough to create enough steam or not. I did have to refill the reservoir twice for that one nail though, and the polish crumbled off... never got moist. Ended up having to file the rest off and then buffing the nail. The lights on the machine aren't consistent either. When I first turned it on, the H (Heat) light came on. When the S (Steam) light came on, I put one finger in and curled it around the part that it shows in videos on-line, and I rested the remainder of the fingers across the top of the steamer. I noticed that the light kept bouncing back and forth between the H to the S. I checked the cord/plug to make sure everything was tight. No issues there. So, I don't think that's normal and feel like I've wasted my money. I was wary about this product due to all the negative reviews, but thought I'd take my chances. $30+ is a lot of money to "waste." Disappointed.
rating 1.0. Too Hot I just received the unit and used it for the first time. It gets very very hot to the point that I can't keep my fingers in the unit.

rating 5.0. It removes dip powder nails too I bought this being a bit skeptical about removing other than gel polish. I was willing to take that chance vs drying out my fingertips with messy wraps and taking an hour.  This works!  I e-file off the shiny top coat, steam for 10 min. A beep will sound at 5 and 10 minutes.  I start using my steel pusher and also file off softened product.  I repeat this 2-3 times and my nails are product free, and not dried out!  The unit itself it pretty simple, but does a great job.

rating 4.0. Patience is the key EDIT 02/28/2019: As we all know there is no "quick" way of taking off gel nail polish. Also certain brands of polish come off easier than others.

ORIGINAL REVIEW: If I could give 3.5 stars I would. It takes quite a bit of time to soak off the gel. It is best for me to do the foil wrap and wait 10 to 15 minutes. It would take a good 15 minutes for the peel to start w/the machine and then again you continue to put your hand in and soak. Yes, I used acetone (actually from the paint store-it's cheaper than nail polish remover w/acetone).

Wipe your nails with alcohol, because your nails can't have any oil on it. Then for my basic steps I used my Sensational Nails products. rating 5.0. Easy and pretty! I wrote some tips to help you I never leave reviews but I almost didn't by this product because of all the negative reviews, my husband said for $12 just try it for yourself and I'm so glad I did! I can't for the life of me do the rope braids on my own but with this tool it took like 2 minutes. Some tips I can share with you I think he other reviewers must not have done is brush the hair your twisting really well, I used a little bit of gel in my daughters hair on each section to make it straight and stay together, and make sure the hairs at even lengths when clipping it in the grooves, I held the machine out and slowly pressed the button as if twirled her hair (be careful not to over twirl it I did it at 1st and had to redo it), then I just followed the arrow and turned the machine that way until it was as tight as I wanted it (again don't over twist it) and the picture was the finished product from my 1st time! There are probably some YouTube tutorials of people showing you how to use it but it really was great and easy, I loved the result.

current_product_reviews: rating 5.0. Very good for eyes irritations or eyes fatigue This product is called Optrex, it is a French brand that has specialized in eyes care for decades. This product is great if you need an eye bath; the 16oz bottle contains among other ingredients cornflower water that is known to relief eye irritations or fatigue for example.  If you use computers a lot then it is good to pour a little bit of it on a coton  to let it then rest on your eyes for 10 or 15 min before sleeping. It really relaxes you.  It is good in case of dust or particles in your eyes too. You need to know that once opened this product has a limited life of 4 weeks  and it should be conserved at a temp inferior to 77 F.

I use the conditioner very sparingly, basically only swiping it through my hair briefly then rinsing thoroughly. The heat therapy spray is nice, but softens my hair a bit too much so I use it very sparingly too... and I still use my own volumizing product to bring more body to my hair.

rating 4.0. ~ Moisturizing, Softening, Frizz-Taming ~ My hair is thick, wavy, and color-treated. This Premium Nature Argan Oil Shampoo, Conditioner, and Thermal Shield Set works well for my hair type. The Argan Oil provides the extra conditioning I need without weighing my hair down. Although the bottles are smaller than the ones they sell separately, I like that these come in a set, since they all work great together. I see that most reviewers love the scent of these. That is the only thing I don't particularly like. Although the scent is unisex, it smells a little too masculine for my preference, but that is just my own preference. I love the handy flip lids on the shampoo and conditioner and the sprayer is great for the thermal shield. A huge plus to me is when a product comes with tamper-proofing, which this set comes completely wrapped in plastic, so I know it has not been tampered with.

The shampoo is rich and opaque and, just from the creaminess, I knew this would work well on my hair. The clear shampoos are usually not conditioning enough (in general), but the creamy ones, like this, are great. The conditioner is the same, very thick and rich, perfect for my wavy, dry, color-treated hair. It may weigh finer hair down a bit much, unless used more sparsely. The thermal shield helps keep my waves from becoming frizzy. It is lightly conditioning and softening. It sprays evenly and just the right amount. I like these being sold in the set for trial purposes and then the ability to purchase them separate as well, once you run out or the smaller set sizes.

This is a nice set of wonderfully moisturizing, softening, and frizz-taming products that add shine, bounce, and softness.

rating 5.0. The curl of the Bella and Bear is GREAT. It curls quickly and the curl lasts I was skeptical because I've tried so many eyelash curlers since the original Shu-Uemera (sp?) was removed from the US.  All except for Tarte brand have been absolutely worthless.  The pads of the Tarte curler need to be replaced every 3 months and the curler itself gives out after 6 months, typically the "spring" mechanism snaps.  The curl of the Bella and Bear is GREAT.  It curls quickly and the curl lasts.  It seems to be very sturdy and it says it will last so time will tell but I am optimistic.  I have long eyelashes that are poker straight so one or two passes with this curler and a tip of  mascara and I'm done!  I love it it so much as a matter of fact,  that the next time I make a purchase from Amazon I will order another, just in case Bella and Bear stop making them!

rating 5.0. LOVE!! I've used so many different brands of eyelash curlers over the years and have been going back to the Revlon eyelash curler time and again for over 10 years. It recently broke so I decided I'd browse around for a new one on Amazon. With all the great reviews, I thought I'd give Bella & Bear a try. The first couple days I had a hard time getting this new tool to work with my eyes. When you've been using the same eyelash curler for over 10 years, using something different is obviously going to feel a little off. However, after 2-3 days, I finally adjusted to the new feel of this curler and it's been great in giving me beautiful curls. The thing I love most about this curler is that I never feel it tug or pull at my lashes as did my Revlon at times.

Overall, I am completely satisfied with this purchase. Like other reviewers commented, the curler comes in a very cute box!

rating 5.0. Great quality great vendor! Longtime lash curler, loving this product. It is very very well made, and love the control that it built into the device.

rating 4.0. The good, the bad and the ugly... My review consists of the good, the bad and the ugly. First,
the good - I finally found a curler that 'fits' my eyelid. Huh, you say? Well, it appears that most of the manufacturers believe I have golf balls instead of human eyes. The tool's shape is long and shallow enough of a curve to catch most of the eyelashes. It is comfortable to use, but takes a bit of practice. Once you are comfortable with the scissor motion (sorry, no other way to describe it) used to make the curl, you are rewarded with a nice, non-crimpy curl. The pad in this one is the best I have used. This tool also opens wide enough that even the most nearsighted or lush lashed amoungst us can get it close to the base of the upper lashes. The design over all is very well thought out. I recommend using an magnifying mirror if you have to use bifocals (I do). All that said, now...
the bad - there is no replacement pad and I am questioning how sanitary this is going to be in the long run. I agree completely with folks here who advise to REMOVE ALL EYE MAKEUP before use. Ain't nothing pretty about an eye infection. I am wondering out loud how to properly clean this curler to be certain it remains safe. If you are prone to sties and other types of infection, I would be asking the same questions. Which leads to...
the ugly - until you practice and are used to it (won't take long) the results are a bit odd looking. The black pad is plushier and looks to be made of silicone and not the usual hard rubber. I have found that the hard rubber pads are chiefly responsible for that weird ugly crimp you can get. Not so with this one. I mentioned before the squeezy scissor action your thumb and index finger have to use can be tricky. If you have coordination issues, well - things could get ugly.
SUGGESTIONS for use...wash your hands before use. I keep the little plastic clip to keep the tool closed. You could follow the advise of one person here to heat the tool with a dryer, but I think I may skip that. Use a makeup mirror to assist you. Practice when you have some time. Enjoy, Ladies - get out there and be lovely.

rating 5.0. Best Eyelash Curler Ever! I recently had wonderful luck with other products from the Bella and Bear line, so I decided to try this eyelash curler.  I have small eyes and have spent many, many years trying to find an eyelash curler that I could use effortlessly and without discomfort.  This eyelash curler is the only one I have ever found that actually curls my lashes easily and with no discomfort.  Anyone else like me that has tried them all . . . your search is definitely over!

I really wanted this to work for me, but it kept pinching the skin on my eyelid and beside my eyes. My eyes aren't huge, so I'm guessing my eyes aren't the right size or wide enough for this product? The company did follow through on their promise of a money back guarantee and upon return, my money was refunded. I'm grateful for that! What I eventually found that worked for me is one from Lancome in the department store. Not cheap though.

rating 1.0. Hurts I'm surprised at the good reviews. Maybe it's just me but the curler has little extended tips that actually hurt the skin when you try to get close to your lashes and it pinches.
I wanted this to work because I read you can heat this one with a blow dryer for longer lasting curls because its made of metal. But alas, I'm re-ordering the plastic Japonesque go curl. Even though they don't last long - I've gone through several - they allow you to get super close to your lash line without pinching and provide a nice curl.
FOLLOW UP: The company is really strange in their customer service response. The day after I posted my review they publicly posted a great reply on Amazon saying they would be happy to return my money. They then sent me a private email stating:

Rather than just leave such negative feedback simply contacting us and asking that we stop sending emails or even more simply just unsubscribing from Amazons email system would have solved this problem.

We are sorry that you feel that 3 simple emails warranted such negative feedback as you left but we do hope that you found our product satisfactory.

Have a nice New Year

rating 5.0. this eyelash curler is truly amazing. I have used so many cheap ones I order a ton of stuff on Amazon and never usually have time to write a review, but I feel the need to review this one product and their company.

First of all, this eyelash curler is truly amazing. I have used so many cheap ones, and have thrown them out or replaced them as needed. Some of them pinch my eyelids just a bit, or some curl the longer lashes but don't really curl the lashes towards the outer part of the eye. This baby does the job of curling ALL my lashes. I am Asian, therefore am not gifted in the eyelash department, but with this curler and a coat of mascara, my eyes pop! I am so happy with this curler, I have recommended it to all my friends!

Second, customer service: I wrote them on amazon asking if I could buy a replacement rubber pad, because mine had fallen off and I left it somewhere in Spain when I went traveling with my girlfriends last month. They responded promptly and told me I would be getting a brand new eyelash curler replacement! The rubber is not supposed to fall out that easily, so I needed a replacement.

I am so happy with this company, that I cannot wait to try out their other products! Thank you Bella and Bear!

rating 2.0. Don't fall for the gimick Eh, there is NOTHING special about this curler. I bought it a couple of months ago and should have returned it right away but thought if I just got used to it that it really would work as advertised. Now I just can't be bothered. Back to using my cheap Revlon curler. Works much better.

rating 2.0. Not for me This product is well built, nice to look at, and will last. However, it does not work for me. The top horizontal piece of the curler is too wide for it to fit comfortably under my brow bone. Still looking for a great curler.

rating 5.0. One of my favorite eyelash curlers I have tried many eyelash curlers and while most are sufficient and get the job done I can honestly say this is my favorite eyelash curler along with the whiplash curler (by crave naturals also available on amazon).

I read once on in a beauty magazine that eyelash curlers tend to wear out after about a year and a half. No way!! A good eyelash curler with proper care (not getting dropped or crushed in a makeup bag) should last a lifetime, the pads are another story...but not with this one!.
I have tried the famous Shu Uemura and while that did a very nice job of getting a natural curl I could tell statement about wearing out after a year and a half as the metal connection did not seem very strong. This curler however is rock solid in it's build! The metal is thick and reinforced as is the connection. Another thing I preferred over the Shu Uemura is that this is strongly spring loaded where as Shu Uemura isn't.

The finger holds are very comfortable and smooth plus, also importantly, the curler looks classy! The pad on this curler, as said by the company doesn't need to be replaced. This is impressive as you can tell by looking at it and feeling it the pad is thick and does not have a lot of give to it but is still gentle enough not to harm your lashes.

As for curling. I get the curler as close to the roots as possible by about half opening my eye and the no pinch design allows you to do this easily. Then I hold for about 7 seconds occasionally pumping the curler. I then raise my eyebrows and move the curler a little farther down my lashes and pinch for a second for two while bend the curler backwards and raising the handle up a bit. The main issue with certain eyelash curlers is the over curled and bent lashes effect which also isn't good for your lashes. This curler, when not over pressed, gives a natural curler that is noticeable on it's own without even using mascara.

And a shout out to the wonderful company Bella and Bear with their wonderful customer service and wonderful products that they wholeheartedly back up! Can't go wrong here.

rating 3.0. Good but not great It's pretty good, I'm not really sure if it's the best product I've ever used though, I needed a new eyelash curler and this gets the job done but you would be fine using a cheeper one.

UPDATE
It looks like Bella and Bear have backed down on the claim that they had made in the past that the pad *never needs replacing" which makes me wonder if a. The quality of the product has gone down. Or b. they have decided to be honest with their buyers. Also they now include a travel bag for your lash curler which is a nice touch (too bad they did not have that when I bought mine last month) all that being said I still enjoy using my curler but you still could just as easy get the same results with a cheeper one.

rating 5.0. From Drab to Gorgeous in Seconds I just discovered Bella and Bear and I absolutely LOVE their products! I have to say, Bella and Bear is an amazing Brand and I hope they will be adding more products soon. When I bought this product I was skeptical to the quality. After all, it is inexpensive. However, after reading all the glowing reviews, I decided to buy it. The 'Eyelash Curler: Eye & Mighty' came within two days and all of the packaging was secured very well. The packaging is extremely cute and durable. The eyelash curler looks exactly like the picture (silver metal with smooth red handles) and does a fabulous job at curling eyelashes. My lashes seem to have a mind of their own, pointing at what seems in every direction imaginable. This product tames my lashes and adds a little lift before I even put mascara on. This eyelash curler keeps my lashes neat while doubling the size instantaneously. I had my sister use this product and she loves it almost as much as I do. She has naturally long, curly lashes and has never had a need for eyelash curlers; but after using this product, her lashes are even more beautiful than before.I definitely recommend buying this product.

rating 5.0. Great Product if You Have Nickel Allergy I recently developed a nickel allergy. Most eyelash curlers contain nickel. This one does not. I am so happy to have found this eyelash curler which does not irritate my eyelids, that I am purchasing an 2nd curler to leave in my travel bag.

rating 2.0. Wanted it to work for me... I've been using this curler for the last few weeks & as much as I wanted to love it, I don't. The curler itself is very well made, it is durable, the spring works great, & the finger grips make it easy to grip without fear of it slipping. The only design flaw that I've found is that the rubber pad that curls my lashes already has a slit in it from where the metal & rubber meet. The other issue which is why it doesn't work for me is the curve of the actual curler. It is very wide which, but the curve of the curler is too strait for my eyes. I have almond shaped eyes & I guess a slightly smaller eye socket. In order to get all of my lashes in the curler I have to press the curler into my eyelid & then adjust the angle to get them in a few times to get the curler close enough to the base of my lashes. If I only had to press the curler lightly on my eyelid that would be one thing, but I have to really press & almost flatten out my eyelid for it to work. Even after that I get maybe a creased "curl". My lashes are quite long & bone strait & even pumping the curler a few times up my lashes doesn't give much of a curl. By the time I put on mascara they are almost strait again. I've been using curlers for 20 some odd years now & honestly the drug store ones work better for me personally.

If you have short lashes & more oval eyes this may be a great curler for you & I do recommend it for the quality. Good luck!

rating 5.0. Great Product! Now that I'm getting older, I need help making my eyes appear more open. By using this eyelash curler I could actually see a difference!
Using the eyelash curler was easy and the little rubber pieces ensured I didn't pinch my skin.
The thing that really impressed me was the attention to detail this company, Bella and Bear, provides. The curler comes in a pretty fabric case and has instructions for how to get the best results. There's also a story about how the company got its name.
I'm impressed with Bella and Bear's follow up. I received an email asking if the product was working and asking me to write a review. This email felt more personal than the others, and they offered a discount on my next purchase.
Let's face it, there are plenty of eyelash curlers out there. Bella and Bear impressed me with the product, the price, the packaging and the follow up. That, in my humble opinion, is a winning combination.

rating 5.0. Different yes, I use it daily :) With cosmetics online it's difficult if you haven't seen the product first hand,choosing colors.

I swore I ordered a different color set than I received on this order. I haven't seen this Trio in store before.
The colors that I received, are a buff ivory (semi sheer) a taupe center (that I love) than is the bronzer which has orange undertones.

At first I cringed at the orange of the bronzer. Yet once applied they all blend well. It's a Fall / Winter combo for me.
I am enjoying the buff and taupe, very different from any blush, contour, this is new tones/colors compared to what I've used in the past.
A very natural effect. I have always been a fan of Physicans Formula. I am using it daily.

rating 4.0. Works on pale skin, sorta! I have to start by saying that I am a super pale girl! Therefore, my review might not be the best frame of reference (unless you are also a super pale person). The contour and bronzer shade are amazing! The contour specifically is the kind of shade that is neutral enough that it doesn't look out odd. It functions really well. A light hand is always a good idea. It is easy to build, as is the bronzer! This actually might be one of my most favorite bronzers. I 10/10 prefer matte over shimmer. I mean, its more orange on my skin than it would be on more fair than light skin, but its the kind of shade that can dual as a blush too. I like it, it brings the life back in me. The only thing is the highlight shade. I cannot use it for my face. It makes a decent light color for the eyes if I need it. Its just too dark on me and cannot do the job. Still, I don't regret buying this. The packaging is plastic, but really nice! It comes with a brush, but I used my own. Contour and bronzer blend out really easily too! Lastly, theres a little mirror which is always a bonus!

rating 4.0. Not ideal for very pale skin, but usable For the record, I am a longtime Physicians Formula wearer, and their products can be hit or miss. I am not being paid to leave a review, or representing anyone but myself here. This is the first time I tried PF's countouring powder, and I've used it for about three seasons. I like it. I don't love it.

I have very pale skin with pink undertones that is uber-sensitive and I have to be very careful about sun exposure. Anything can make me break out in hives or eczema, at any time, even if I used it daily for years. Thankfully, PF products have never done that to me, in almost a decade.

As for this product, the shades are where I take some issue. They worked best for my tone in summer, but now, in winter -- I'm shopping for a different product.

* The dark shade is really mahogany-like, so I have to blend, blend, blend in daylight or risk looking like I smeared mud on my face. It's good for shading jawline and cheekbones with a light touch at temples if you need it. Beware, though. If you are very pale, you want a very light touch with this one.

*The medium shade is very subtle and helps blend and soften the dark. It also works well as a matte bronzer, around sides of the nose, and wherever you need just a little definition. This is the best shade of the bunch, honestly. At least for my skin tone.

* The light shade is, frankly, worthless to me. It gives my skin a pasty, chalky look and looks heavy. Yuck. I occasionally use it in tiny spots as a concealer, but as a contour powder, it just looks like I put on way too much setting powder or something. Instead, I use my lightest shade of matte eyeshadow (also a PF product) and a higher-end illuminator for highlighting. Those results are beautiful, frankly, even on my 38 year old skin.

All in all, PF's contour colors are suitable for pale skin, but may be better on other slightly darker or warmer skin tones, or on summer tanned skin.

rating 5.0. This is my favorite sculpting product The matte sculpting trio is my daily go-to product. It does such a beautiful job of contouring my face, I don't even use blush anymore - I just let this bring my cheekbones out, brush a little highlight on the top, and it looks very natural and pretty.

rating 4.0. Top 3 Contour for Ghostly Pale Skin I'm about as fair as they come. That makes finding contour powders difficult.  The center color in this palette is in my top three favorites.  I like that it's very cool.  It edges towards a slightly purple/grey taupe without being so purple/grey that I have to be careful.  It goes on sheer,but really nicely pigmented.  If I want it to be a little warmer, I'll blend it with the lightest shade of Profusion Cosmetics contour powder. It's also a great ghostly contour if wanting a cool taupe without the purple/grey undertone. The Profusion one is just a skosch warmer.  I don't use the outside powders normally. The bronzer color could work for eyeshadow, I guess. The highlight is too dark for me and is chalky looking on my skin.  If they ever make the center color as a contour all by itself, I'd be really happy.  The  one thing I don't like about this product is the container. Mine won't open all the way without lifting the bottom up and it drives me nuts because I can't just open it and sit it on my vanity.  It will close if I try.  Other than that, it's probably the cheapest contour that works great for really pale skin, and it blows every luxury brand I've tried out of the water.  Two thumbs up!

rating 3.0. Simple and compact palette I like how portable this is to take with me in my makeup bag when traveling.  The contour is subtle enough so I don't look like a clown.  I am not a makeup superhero, so I am sure I could be better at contouring and highlighting.  The matte finish is nice, but I wish the highlighter was more pronounced. with a little shimmer.  Each color is very compacted, so it takes a little bit of pressure to get powder.  I don't use the middle color at all, since it is a muddly color.  There is a small brush that comes with this setj, which is nice.  It has soft bristles and a nice angle.  There is also a nice size mirror considering this is small.

rating 2.0. It doesn't "peel" like advertised. It does help prevent nail polish on ... It doesn't "peel" like advertised. It does help prevent nail polish on your skin, but Elmer's glue would work better and cheaper

rating 1.0. I paid for "CITY" brand, but received something called "WayGo."  This is false advertising. I saw all the negative reviews, but thought I would try it anyway... less than $3 and free shipping wasn't going to break the bank.  However, all the negative reviews are accurate!  It takes a long time to dry (in the photo below, the picture was taken at about 5 minutes), and when it peels off it's inconsistent.  The first time it didn't come off all in one piece.  The second time it came off in one large piece and one tiny piece.  I would say it peels off like a thick layer of Elmer's glue peeled off when I was in elementary school.  I paid for "CITY" brand, but received something called "WayGo."  This is false advertising.  If CITY brand has changed its name, it should be updated in the description.  I agree that the smell is pleasant... but I'm not buying it for the smell.  I'm buying it protect my nails when doing nail art.  The first bottle of latex peel off nail protector was by a company called "PUEEN" and while it is more expensive (approximately $9 a bottle) it works like a dream!  I would highly recommend that to anyone who doesn't have a latex allergy.  Bottom line, unless you plan to wash WayGo off your fingers with soap and water after your manicure is completed, I would avoid this like the plague.  There, I said it.  LOL  Also, if it helps anyone else to know, I paid for this on 12/1/16 and received it on 1/6/17.

rating 5.0. I Like It I thought the more you spent, the better the product was supposed to be, but after dropping heavy dollars for average results/things, I decided to go to what has always worked for me. Neutorgena and Olay have been the pinnacle of my beauty regime since last fall. A couple months ago, I got into doing this 10 step beauty regime to keep away break outs and get rid of these pores and dark spots.

I use this as a makeup remover and it is amazing; it smells great, it feels light and washes off smooth and does a good job as a regular cleansing. I double cleanse with a face wash after though and it leaves my face refreshed and clean (Less dirt comes off after I tone when I use them together). I've nearly finished my first bottle and have ordered another. This will definitely be a permanent part of my regime now.

rating 5.0. Who knew oil cleansers would treat acne? I have suffered with acne most of my life. I did the bacitracin as a child and then once puberty was over, my acne cleared up quite nicely.  However, I do tend to get adult acne from time to time which is not a big deal but I have hyper pigmentation which means I have spots all over my face from the acne.  I have tried many different products to prevent acne, to not over dry my skin, to cleanse my face without harsh chemicals and was at my wits end.  I read an article online about a lady that started using moisturizing oil to help without break outs and I was intrigued.  It seems like putting oil on your face would break you out more but I was wrong. I have been using Neutrogena Ultra Light Cleansing Oil for about 2 months (same bottle) and I am beyond satisfied. It is very light but cleanses my face without over drying.  My face feels soft and full of moisture after I use it.  I have also noticed less break outs since using this product. I haven't noticed any change in my glow but other people have noted that my face seemed more vibrant. I definitely suggest this product!

rating 5.0. Wonderfully light and silky, does a good job, smells wonderful too! I have used more expensive facial cleansing oils and not been nearly as happy with them as I am with this product.  First, it is much silkier and lighter than other oils I have used, so that it is much easier to use, absorbs better, and doesn't leave oily spots on my vanity.  One of the nicest things about it is the pleasant smell; smells a little bit like apples.  The other oils I have used did not have a pleasant smell at all; smelled like cooking oil.  And on top of all that, the price on this oil is significantly less expensive than what I was using. Why would I ever go back to paying more when I can buy this oil for less and be much happier with it?  Can't recommend it highly enough.  Just fyi; I am in my fifties, have dry skin, always looking for anti-aging and moisturizing products, but even someone with younger skin would be happy since the oil is so light.

rating 3.0. It's an okay inexpensive oil cleanser. Not top of ... It's an okay inexpensive oil cleanser.  Not top of the line, but does the job.  Removes waterproof mascara, which is a big plus.

rating 3.0. "Light" describes the degree of cleansing, not the oil texture... I have used other oils to oil cleanse in the past, but I wanted one that had an emulsifying agent to help the oil rinse off cleanly. This oil seemed like a good place to start, since Neutrogena has a good reputation and this was a very affordable choice.

It does a good job of removing my (lightly applied) foundation, but when it comes to liquid lipcolor, mascara, and eyeliner, this stuff just doesn't really work. It feels nice, and the emulsification is really cool. Unfortunately it's not so great as a cleanser. So now I'm back to oil cleansing with a different oil, and using one pump of this to get all the oil off.

rating 5.0. Great face wash, doesn't hurt my eyes like some other cleansing oils I have been using the Burt's bees cleansing oil and decided to try the neutrogena one. It is great for removing blackheads and getting make up off!! would definitely recommend as an every day face wash.

rating 2.0. Not for me I have used DHC's Cleansing Oil for years and like it fine, but wondered it there might be something out there just as good at a better price, so decided to try the Neutrogena Ultra Light Cleansing Oil.  The smell is awful (reminds me of kid's cough syrup) and I just don't care for the way it feels and removes my makeup.  The bottle is much smaller than my DHC oil, so it really isn't any more cost-effective.  I simply don't see it as better or equal to what I'm currently using, so I won't purchase it again.

rating 5.0. My new favorite cleansing oil. I never would have predicted it, but I like this better than the DHC.  It's not as thick (not a good or bad thing, just an observation) and has a light, almondy scent.  Those are the two main differences I noticed right away.  It feels really good on my face, dissolves my eye makeup without burning my eyes, and washes off completely without leaving a film.  I like that it comes in a small bottle, because I like being able to use things up before they sit for too long and get old.  The pump works really well.  I've been using this morning and night for a few weeks and love that my face doesn't have that tight, dry feeling after washing.  I did like the DHC, but at 1/4 the price (yay, subscribe and save!) I'll definitely be using this from now on.

current_product_reviews: rating 4.0. My skin seems to like this product... I've only used this a few times so far, but so far, so good.  It does a great job removing make-up gently...including eye make-up.  Since it's oil based, I was worried it would break me out.  I'm 41, but my skin is very sensitive and I'm still prone to break-outs, particularly in my t-zone area.  Thankfully, so far, my skin seems to be liking this product.  I do think it's a little pricey for the size of the bottle you receive, but overall, I'm pleased.
other_product_reviews: rating 4.0. Beautiful, but lightly pigmented. The color is beautiful and it provides the perfect pop of brightness with a more neutral, smokey eye...BUT!  (You knew there was a but coming, right?)  It's not super pigmented and you have to go over it several times to get a good pop.  Since it's lighter, it's not ideal for a winged eye either.  I usually just flick it out on the edge a little, but don't do a full-on wing.  I'd say the color trends more toward violet (with red undertones), than purple (with blue). It doesn't bleed or run, and for the price, I think it's a good choice, if you're looking for a little spark with your eye make-up routine.

input: rating 5.0. Amazon has a great price, so I figured it was worth trying I'm a yoga teacher and I'm barefoot all day long. As a result, I have some nasty, dry feet. When I found out about this, I was sold on the idea. Amazon has a great price, so I figured it was worth trying. I've only used it once so far, but already my feet feel and look a million times better. One reviewer said to do it five days in a row to see the best results and that's what I'm planning to do. So happy to have this in my life now! I'll definitely be going longer between pedicures!

rating 5.0. This one is hands down the best I've used Over the years I have used many electronic foot files and have mostly preferred the ones with plugs.  This one is hands down the best I've used, making quick work of my callouses. Highly recommended.  Make sure you give it a full charge before using for the first time.

rating 5.0. Best of.... Hands down, this is the best for keeping your feet smooth. I'm on my feet all day at work and am also a runner so my feet take a beating. I've tried other brands but because they run on batteries they tended not to do well, and another one that the shape makes it not be able to get a smooth surface. So on went the hunt. So I like red so one day I put red in and up this showed. Very reasonable price and it's rechargeable. Got it, let it charge, and gave it a shot. Super smooth feet in no time flat. No heat or pulling at the skin like I had with others. I followed up with my Manuka honey cream for the body and I was so happy. Definitely earned my recommendation!

rating 5.0. Completely Sold!!! Excellent product! Love this product!!! Works beautifully. I ordered one for me and one for my sister. We both love it and can't praise it enough. Most were too weak and some worked but batteries needed to be replaced too often. Some were awkward to hold and some wore down almost immediately. This product is beyond expectation!!!  We have already recommended it to many friends and family members.

rating 5.0. LOVE, LOVE THIS PRODUCT! I purchased this product after severely cutting my heel with one of those blades used to cut the calluses from your feet. My husband had warned me of the dangers of using blades, and I've cut my feet several times. I read many reviews on this product and others before purchasing. It came in 2 days and had a charge when it arrived.  I keep it plugged in so it's ready whenever my husband or I are needing to use it. I'm very pleased with this product, and very happy that I purchased this.  It works very well and does a great job, and I use it at least once a week to keep my feet really soft. My husband also uses it about once a week also, to keep his feet smooth.  He was shot in the foot and the bullet fragments are still in his foot, and he gets calluses where the fragments are in his foot. I also purchased the extra rollers, but haven't had to change the original one out yet.  Those who complained about this product must not have used it properly.  When using don't press down thinking that extra pressure will help, it's not designed to be used like that.  Just lightly touch the area you want to treat and the file will definitely work well. For those who have a lot of hard calluses, you might want to either soak your feet first to soften the skin,  or use it everyday for a couple of days until you get the best results.  You don't want to over do it because you may cause your feet to be sore. I'm very happy with my purchase and highly recommend this wonderful gadget to anyone.  It will definitely save you a lot of money on getting pedicures elsewhere!

rating 5.0. How I got my feet in fab shape This thing is an essential step I took in getting my feet back to tip top shape. Being a dancer, my poor feet were doomed to have calluses. I do my best to avoid that at all costs, but I couldn't stop it. I ended up having a couple small ones and one decently thick one. This thing is amazing, especially for the smaller ones. Let's go right into a pro's and con's list.

pro's:
- Safely grinds away the skin
- Long battery life
- Can still be used while charging
- Long life for the stone (I got mine in February and still haven't needed a replacement with relatively frequent use)
- Strong motor that doesn't lag when touched to the skin (unless of course you're smashing it up against yourself which if you are.. stop.)

con's
- Take's a long time, especially for larger calluses (we will revisit this issue in a second)
- Does (of course) produce a fine, skin powder that is pretty gross if you think too hard about it. So don't think too hard about it.

Ok so now you've read the pro's and con's list and you might be wondering, "Adina, didn't you say you had a larger callus? Did you just end up spending days and weeks and years just to shave it off?"
The answer is no. While this tool was extremely helpful, I am much too impatient for that. As I mentioned, this foot file does take forever, but don't hate on it for that! You don't want a foot file that is too powerful or else it may take skin off in chunks rather than that lovely skin powder we discussed and thus your skin will no longer be soft and smooth which is, to my understanding, our goal.
Back to the issue of my larger callus. Here are the steps I took to remove that bad boy.

I started off just using the same method I used for the littler ones which was file for about an hour every other day in hopes that I could one day grind it down to the bottom. I don't like using this for much longer in only one spot as the skin begins to feel quite warm and I can't imagine that is good for you. After finishing up any areas that concern you, thickly apply O'Keeffe's [[ASIN:B0002QB9NE O'Keeffe's for Healthy Feet, Foot Cream 3.2oz]] and throw on some thick cotton socks. I'm sure this method would eventually work for the larger one too, but who has time for that? So I took an extra step with the large one and used Dr. Scholl's Medicated Callus remover [[ASIN:B00099XL30 Dr. Scholl's Callus Removers, Medicated, 6 pads]]. I put one on for 48 hours and when I took it off, the callused skin was very white and soft, but still there. I figured it was a bust but I went to the water park the next day and the mixture off water and walking around barefoot made most of the now-white and soft callus just come off. (Don't worry, it didn't come off in the water and I was able to throw the skin away properly) I'm sure you don't need to go to the water park to achieve the same goal, just give yourself a good foot soak and then scrub it away!
There was still a bit of callused skin underneath what came off but I was able to easily come back in with this foot file and finish it off! Now my feet are baby soft just in time for summer!

input: rating 5.0. It works! I just can't believe that this makes true magic when it comes to whitening teeth.  I could tell a difference and I don't smoke nor drink coffee but wow what a difference on my husband.  He used it three or four nights in a row brushing two minutes, per instructions, and it is amazing stuff.  I highly recommend following the directions to the T, because I was so excited I opened it and made a mess, but if you're careful and use the dark towel by the sink as recommended you'll be fine.  Even when it splatter everywhere, as I said I didn't read the directions to spit out as close to drain as possible, because it is natural it was a breeze to clean up with a wet cloth.  Make sure you floss after use because I cleaned my gums and black residue was still in gums even after brushing so just FYI.  I can honestly say conservatively speaking, this little jar will last me and my husband using once a week for at least a year, you don't need a lot.  Comparing the price of whitening treatments and how painful they're (if sensitive teeth) this is a no brainer, I didnt feel any difference on my teeth, sensitivity wise, at all.  Love will recommend to friends.
