rating 5.0. Unbelievable! Ok... this tasteless, scentless *black* powder looks intimidating.  But don't let the looks fool you... it works.  And I mean works well.  It has helped lessen my sensitivity and leaves my teeth feeling like right after a trip to the dental hygiene chair for a cleaning.  Yes, you will see black when brushing.. and yes... there really is no taste or smell to it (even though it says "mint")... but the results are absolutely amazing.  NO GMO, NO poisonous fluoride... just a couple of safe ingredients.  The way mother nature intended.  Why support giant corporations and chemical companies when there is someone out there doing it right without all of the things we don't need in our bodies?  Try this product.  I believe you will be glad you did!

rating 5.0. This is the best teeth whitener on the market WOW! Buy this now. And while you're at it buy a few more for the people you love. This maybe the single most impressive product I have ever bought. I have been chewing tobacco and drinking 2 pots of coffee for 20 years, and this immediately whitened my teeth to almost perfect color. You dont get any fake looking white, you get natural teeth looking white.

For those of you having trouble with the "mess" give me a break. Even if you get a little on the counter, it will clean it better than you would have anyways. Just put a napkin under the jar. Tap the top, and sides of rim a few times with your toothbrush and slowly unscrew the cover. It's really not a big deal. When you are spitting during brushing make sure you get your head fairly low (to avoid splash). And remeber even if you do splash a bit it's no big deal the clay will actually help clean your sink anyways.

This stuff is simply amazing. Please support this product and keep them in buisness.

rating 5.0. Whiter Teeth! This stuff is great!  Noticeable improved whiteness after the first use!  I love the clean senstation afterwards.  I do not make a paste out of it.  I just dip my dry brush in and then run the bristles under a slow stream (so that the black powder does not fly everywhere).  Removes surface stains very successfully!

rating 5.0. Has multi uses This is just something I wanted to try out for whiter teeth, since mine are too sensitive for the chemical whiteners on the market or even a good dental whitening. After the first couple days use my teeth were shinier & slightly not as stained. I'm not expecting dramatic results over night.
One big issues I had with my gum was an abscess that was hard getting rid of with antibiotics (8 days, 1500 mg a day) but after 3 days of packing this on the gum where the abscess was it cleared up (after the antibiotics ran out & the pain was still there). I wasn't expecting this product to clear out the rest of a gum infection, but it did.

Some of the others uses I have found for this are as a facial wash (quarter size amount of castor oil & a tsp of charcoal make a good facial), for insect stings (gets rid of the itching), cuts, the list goes on.

I thought the price was expensive at first, but compared to some of the other brands this one has less ingredients, also a better quality of them. I'm not disappointed.

To use this as a toothpaste is very messy & I still have not mastered how not to get it all over the sink or myself when brushing, but that's ok. It does clean up easy. There is no taste or smell, just a gritty feeling to the charcoal.

Don't expect miracles over night though, because as with all natural products they lack the chemicals that provide extreme results in a short amount of time. Natural products take time to work with repeated uses.

rating 5.0. This is really good stuff. After one use This is really good stuff.  After one use, there was an immediate improvement.  It wasn't CRAZY or SUPER white but it felt amazing and my teeth were whiter, especially my K9s (or as my wife says, my fangs). It does make a HUGE mess so hold your breath when you bring the toothbrush up to your mouth.  I also recommend placing a paper towel or kleenex under the container to help prevent a mess. I use it about once a week and I should probably use it more.  I drink a lot of dark things (coffee, soda, and so on) and more regular use would have a big impact.  For a few days after I got it I was using it once a day and the results were really dramatic.  You'll love this but you have to be careful of the mess.  It won't stain anything but it can be a huge pain to clean up.

rating 3.0. 5 Magic Mud tips UPDATE: Reduce from 5 stars to 3 after using 1/3 to 1/2 of a jar during a 5 month period:

I believe this product diminished stains and lightened my tooth color. However, the powder molded, so I had to throw away at least half the jar.

Go ahead. Try it. What have you got to lose? Here are five Magic Mud tips as you get going:
1) Take selfies. See before & after for yourself.
2) Give it time. Don't expect pearly whites over night. Keep using.
3) Brush your front teeth, which people see.
4) Waterpik afterwards to get stubborn grit gone from between teeth.
5) Wet wipes take care of any minor messes.
One last note: Magic Mud is tasteless. Orange and mint are in the ingredient list, but, as advertised, they don’t contribute flavor.

If you do wish to try this type of tooth powder, there are now other companies that sell similar products for much cheaper. Maybe activated charcoal will whiten your smile, but it didn't do anything for mine.

rating 5.0. Happy with improved gum results! This is my second unit I purchased for the kids. I wanted to try it on trial basis and see if my oral hygiene would improve and it has.
My dental hygienist highly recommend a oral spray so I chose this unit. With regular flossing, waterpik and final step brushing in this order, I have noticed an overall improvement, gums no longer bleed and a reduction of morning and bad breath. In the reservoir I added about 2oz of Hydrogen peroxide, a cap full of crest gum care mouthwash and spray away. My gums have adjusted and now spraying at the highest settings at 10! So far the units have continued to do their purpose and very happy.
Update, However it is not a substitute for flossing just another step to improve oral hygiene and care.

rating 5.0. Very good. I am an actual purchaser of this product and my review is genuine. The water pick is well made and works well. I attached it to the bathroom sink by removing the aerator with a pair of pliers. Then installed the water diverter by hand. Attached the holder assembly to the wall with adhesive.
It works flawlessly and no leaks. The water pressure is good, but not as strong as the electric motorized water picks I own. But does a decent job of cleaning my mouth. I recommend this product. Happy with purchase.

pros: no need for electricity.
no need to keep filling the water container.
well made. no leaks in connections.
cons: some people might feel the water pressure is no strong as an electric motorized unit.
not as portable as an electric unit.

rating 4.0. Great alternative from battery operated products This product is a beast. Scared the living daylights out of me when I was using the pick cleaner the first time. Water came put super strong so I shouldn't have even turned the faucet on all the way. The water pick literally flew out of my hand and went berserk in my bathroom. I digress, Faucet operated; batteries not necessary. Product itself can use a slight cosmetic makeover because the sticker makes it look cheap. Hung it on the wall adjacent to our faucet and the adhesive is holding up well. But the hose tends to keep getting in the way when not in use.

rating 4.0. Not as good as the Water Pik since it doesn't have pulsating ... It's okay. Not as good as the Water Pik since it doesn't have pulsating flow. The hose keeps splitting and blowing off. Each time it does I have to cut it off past the split and reattach it to the outlet. Eventually the hose will be to short to use.

The seller contacted me and issued a credit since I had trouble with it. For the price it is a good product and worth the money. It's not that big of a deal to replace the tubing. You can get replacement tubing at any local hardware store.

rating 4.0. So far very happy with purchase Decided to give this a try instead of spending $80.00 on a WaterPik.  I stumbled upon this while searching for a WaterPik to purchase.  This product arrived very quickly, with a few days from ordering.  Installation was super easy, although very little guidance provided by manufacturer.  This product works great so far without any leaks.  The control valve is solid metal and very well made.  When turning control from faucet to flosser there is no struggle.  The switch is adjustable so you can control water pressure while making sure the water temp a ture is appropriate.  Will update review after further uses.  Very satisfied with this product, would recommend so far.

rating 4.0. It works just fine. Happy I figured out how to install in ... It works just fine.  Happy I figured out how to install in my shower, but you have to buy several parts and it may not have the final aesthetics that some would find ok.

rating 5.0. Great Solution for Older Dad Dad had to get artificial teeth (he's 93). After the dentist put in four posts, he made two removable bridges of teeth. But, he said if Dad takes the teeth out and puts them back in often he will need new posts in a few years -- not something he wants to go through again. So, to be able to keep the teeth clean, clean easily in the crevices and under the bridges we got this for him. He can clean everything well, brush the teeth, and only has to take out the bridge once a week for more thorough cleaning.

rating 5.0. Top-Notch Product. This item was delivered today and I couldn't be happier.  I used to have one hooked up in the shower and it wasn't working quite right anymore, so I ordered one for the sink.  Was super easy to hook up and works great.  Used it on my teeth and gums and am totally satisfied with the performance.  You can adjust the pressure of the water jet by adjusting your faucet handle.  Turning the lever on the unit itself doesn't adjust the spray strength.  If you're looking for a great product, this is it.  I love these flossers that hook up to the water source - never run out of water while flossing.  That's the drawback to the tank units that I never liked.

rating 1.0. Big disappointment. I bought one of these type water flosses ... Big disappointment. I bought one of these type water flosses a few years ago. Don't remember the manufacturer. It worked well until a week ago, so I ordered this one. It fit my sink okay, but the spray was considerably weaker than my old one. I felt that it did not dislodge residue as well as my old one. So, I cannibalized the parts I needed to repair my old one and I was back in business.

I just want a powerful stream of water to clean my teeth. The extra goodies don't matter. This system did not provide that function to my satisfaction.

rating 5.0. Jet is the perfect name for this pick! I love this! The price was great and it is super easy to install. I didn't need to bother with the rack or the other heads. It comes with 3 identical water spout heads and 3 identical water spout brush heads. I assume this is so that more than 1 person can use the product without having to share heads. We have electric tooth brushes and I don't mind sharing the spout head with my husband, so we really only use 1 spout head.

I was unsure if this would work in my bathroom sink. However, it was easy to remove the original head and connect this one. The faucet still works as it did before (actually the pressure is better). With very little stream the water pick is powerful enough to fully clean the teeth. It really is a jet of water!

The only downside is that if you don't turn the knob downward (downward for water coming down from the faucet, up for water going up to the pick) and you turn the sink on, you will have the jet of water shoot out and probably hit the ceiling. Yes, it is that powerful, but feels fine on the gums. It is much quicker and I believe does a better job than flossing. No more floss picks for me! I don't know why I didn't get something like this years ago!

rating 5.0. Great item wonderful price Bought this for my 92+ year old dad, who was having trouble controlling a regular stand alone dental irrigator. Plus it was hard for him to fill the water reservoir all the time. This solves both problems & I'm even thinking of getting one for myself & as xmas gifts for the whole family. So easy to install.

rating 4.0. It's easy to get too much force (ouch) It's much easier to use than a traditional free-standing water flosser, which can be a pain to keep clean. Just be sure the flow is adjusted properly. It's easy to get too much force (ouch). It's not very attractive, but it's not horrible looking. I live alone, so I don't use the holder that comes with it. I just wrap the tubing over a single Command hook above the faucet. Installation took maybe 2 minutes. I'm very satisfied with it.

rating 5.0. Metal where it needs to be. I rarely rate and review products when requested. This one was particularly good. this is my second connect to water faucet dental pick. The first unit I had for a year and a half. The entire unit was plastic and broken. This dental spa is metal where it needs to be. It also includes water brushes. Which allow you to pick during and after brushing without having to chance attachments. This is a really nice feature.

My only complaint is that perhaps the whole is slightly too small and with higher pressures makes the stream too concentrated. It's stings slightly it if it's up too high.

My one recommendation to the designers; have your engineer figure out how to have the flow to the pick be dependent on the presence of water pressure. My wife likes to turn off the faucet and not touch the valve to the pick. So when I go to wash my hands the next time, I turn on the faucet only to have my own little bellagio fountain show all over the counter. It's a good practice joke, but also a forgetful habit for some.

If there was some kind of quick disconnect for either the hose at the valve or for the valve itself, like the kind on a portable dishwasher hose, so you could get the hose out of the way entirely when not in use, it would be completely perfect. It does a great job of cleaning between my teeth. And what is with the name? Is "Smagreho" even a real word? ;-P

rating 5.0. The best of the 3 types I've tried I've now had 3 types of water picks and this is the best so far. The first was an actual water pik. Its power level was too high for me and it was inconvenient to use. It cluttered the sink, had a clunky coiled hose and needed ac power to run. I stopped using it after a few months even though my mouth felt clean. When my pockets Reached 4-5 and the dentist suggested additional visits I bought my second unit. This one you filled with water and it had a charging base so no hoses and it was easy to use. The ease of use and the clean feeling I got by using made it enjoyable to use. I continued using it daily for about 1.5 years. My red gums went away and most pockets reduced by 1-2 so the dentist and I were really impressed. But the pump got slower and slower so I bought this one. This connects directly to the faucet and provides an unlimited supply of water. The pressure is adjustable using the faucet valve so you have better control compared to the other units. There is no power or tank to fill. Just insert in your mouth and turn on. It seems well made so I think it will last a long time.

This is to inform those considering purchase of this item that if their bath sink fawcets are the standard 3/4" outlet, they will be unable to connect this item until the manufacture corrects this design error. rating 5.0. This thing is like a pressure-washer for your mouth This thing is like a pressure-washer for your mouth! I have to run the sink at half strength. It really does a great job of cleaning your gums and between your teeth. A good price, too; and it should pay for itself in a couple years, since I won't have to buy bags of flossers anymore. And it's way less of a hassle than other water-flossers, since it doesn't require electricity or a manually refilled resevoir.

UPDATE: I recently moved, and this is not nearly as powerful at my new place as it was at my old, because (as it turns out) the maximum throughput of this waterflosser is dependent on the maximum throughput of your sink. However, it still works well, and the low price, unlimited water, choice of water temperature, and lack of need for electricity makes this still better than an electric waterflosser.

rating 1.0. Does not fit recessed aerator faucets. This Seller needs to tell prospective Buyers that this product DOES NOT FIT the newer designer faucets that have recessed aerators, which are the most popular faucets now days. These are not like simple the aerators on other faucets. It's recessed and screws up inside the faucet tip. Standard aerators are just screwed on to the end of the faucet tip and can easily be removed by unscrewing them with a pair of pliers. My Delta faucet needs a special key/wrench to remove the aerator that you can purchase on Amazon, but it's likely this product won't fit anyway, based on the other reviews I read.

I really wanted this product to work, because I have a Shower Breeze (purchased on Amazon) attached to my shower head, and I love it. This product looked identical but made for sink faucets. The Seller needs to make an adaptor that fits recessed aerator faucets since none are available. I returned this item.

rating 5.0. Great oral irrigator - read this for a list of the adaptor sizes I purchased this SMAGREHO oral irrigator in March 2018. The water diverter is metal, as is one of the adaptors. The rest of the adaptors are white plastic. All of the adaptors have male threads on both sides. The threads on the diverter are female, and the threads in your faucet which your old aerator screwed in to need to be female as well. The metal adaptor fit our old (1970's / 1980's) Moen faucets.

I measured the outside diameter of the threaded part of each of the adaptors - the part that screws into your faucet. Here are the diameters: 0.68-0.69", 0.76-0.77", 0.83-0.84", 0.92" (this is the metal one), and 1.07-1.08". The other side of each of the adaptors (the side that screws into the diverter) measures 0.83-0.84".

I am impressed by the high-quality silicone gaskets that came with the unit. I experienced no leaks.

My faucets are the type where you pull the handle upward to turn it on. This makes it tricky to precisely adjust the water pressure, so the pressure is high enough to wash out food particles, but not so high it is painful to use. I suspect that facets which have handles that you rotate would give easier / more precise control of the water pressure.

rating 5.0. Lightweight, comfortable, and looks natural These lashes are lightweight and look very natural if applied correctly. I trim them, cut them in half so I can usually get 2 uses from each pair, and apply the lashes under my natural lashes NOT on top. Since the band on these is not clear (it's a thin thread) I think applying under is the best way to camouflage and blend with my lashes. It also eliminates the need for heavy liner to blend the lash band and I can skip liner for a simple day look.  (Under application is easy to learn with a little practice and help from youtube tutorials.) I also like these lashes bc they are fanned and curled beautifully.  I don't care for the clumpy look, or overly dark color of other false lashes.  These are thin and delicate looking, like real hair, and the color is not too dark so they look natural.  I hope the price doesn't keep going up and they stay available on Prime which is another huge benefit, 10 great lashes for a great price shipped with Prime!

rating 5.0. The fat This is a classic. Having tried a few hard soaps and creams and also having heard that "the fat" has a cult-like following, I had to try it.
The smell is nothing short of awesome. Kind of a powdery linen clean smell. And it's a pervasive smell too, that will carry with you after your shave.
But the magic of MWF is the lanolin, a natural wax that the sheep exude to protect their wool... The original Mitchell noticed that wool shearers' hands were always soft and supple, and thankfully decided to try a shaving product based in it.
You will notice the lanolin effect right after your shave as you're rinsing off your face. It will be smooth and moist and will feel awesome.
MWF can be tough to lather, but after a few tries and watching a few videos online you'll get the hang of it. For best results try skipping the bowl/scuttle and just face-lather. It'll create a luscious, moisturizing, delicious smelling lather that is like no other.

rating 4.0. some soaps its fine, others are not so apparently I'm one of those people that has difficulty lathering it up correctly. I do have hard water but with a softener...some soaps its fine, others are not. i definitely have to work for this one! I've only been using it for a few days now, with an EJ DB89 and Polisilver Super iridium blades, and its a nice shave. I'll have to try it with some other blades too...I occasionally swap between Astra and Feathers as well.
Using this setup the razor does glide nicely. I'm mixed on the scent however... its soapy, which I don't mind, but tends to linger longer than I like. I do prefer it to other scents like bay rum and sandalwood... they are too 'old man'-ish for my tastes, even though they are a favorite with others. If you're used to those and not a soapy/clean scent, this might not be to your liking. Lastly, I have a feeling this puck is going to last a VERY long time! I'll give it a few more weeks before deciding if it'll have a permanent place in my rotation but so far I think its a yes.

rating 5.0. Great soap for shaving This is just fantastic. I loved opening it up and smelling the very masculine, subtle clean aroma of the soap. There are reviewers that say it can be hard to get a later. I don't find that to be a problem at all. I work some into my Parker silver tip badger hair brush and then get a lather up in a bowl. My only regret is not getting more than one of these. At the rate I use it, almost daily, the one puck will last at least a year. That's pretty good price/use ratio. I've tried a lot of other soaps and really like the Razo Rock but the Mitchell's will outlast all of the others. Also, the clean smell won't interfere with any aftershave or cologne you put on like some of the other scented soaps out there.

rating 4.0. Creamy and Moisturizing I've read a lot of good reviews about Mitchell's Wool Fat Soap, so I had to at least give it a try. It is definitely worth the money. It is a very rich, thick lather that carries a bunch of moisture to your face which makes for a nice shave,

I also bought the wood Kingsley Shave Soap Bowl and the Mitchell's fits in it perfectly.

rating 5.0. Smells great and great value! My husband loves this soap! It makes shaving easier, leaves his face feeling moisturized and doesn't dry his skin out, and it smells great too. You can't beat it for the price!

rating 5.0. works very well After reading all the reviews, I thought I would have to spend some time building the foam and I was wrong.  Placed the soap in a KFC dish (where it fit well and was free to boot) and a about five twirls off the brush on the soap and rubbed the brush on the face.  Started working it on the face and in about a minute had an excellent foam.  Off to shaving and it left the face very clean and gave a very good shave.  I was surprised as this was my first time with a soap; have used creams or croaps before.

rating 5.0. Lather Galore This soap lathers easier and better than any soap I've used.  I read through a bunch of soap recommendations on a shaving blog and this one seemed to be the most commonly recommended so I decided to give it a shot.  I have hard water so lathering soaps has always been a challenge but not with this soap.  Definitely a soap everyone should try at least once because it just might change your view on shaving soaps.

rating 5.0. last name:ever, first name:greatest Simple,effective,delightful. I searched high and low for a shave soap I
would enjoy daily. Many other products have certain aspects that I enjoy, but
nothing has everything all in one like this product. All 5 senses are appeased with this
product. The smell is clean,simple and mild enough to not overpower other treatments (aftershave/cologne)
It leaves the skin feeling well nourished and protects well with my daily straight shave.
The lather is unparalleled, it does not disipate as it sits on the face. Works best with badger brushes.
What is truly amazing is how long it lasts. I have been using for 6 months and it still looks brand

for blades, styptic stick,etc..

rating 5.0. Why did they invent canned foam Excellent shaving soap. No problems lathering. Extremely slick. Not really sure why you can only find shave foam in pressure cans in the stores.
rating 5.0. definitely a favorite. leaves face very smooth. will not dry out or burn skin even if on face for awhile. mainly has a soap aroma with little fragrance. definitely a favorite.

rating 5.0. Best Shave Soap I Have Ever Used. First let me say this; I use this soap with creams, and never alone. I've never been a fan of shaving with any soaps alone. However, when you combine a soap with a cream I believe the result is always better than either one by theirselves.
In the past I would change up the soap vs cream combinations frequently, but since the first time I used Mitchell's I've never used another soap.
They claim there is something about the natural oils found in the wool that make it super slick, and that's very evident when you use it.
I have even used it with creams that I thought gave a horrible shave, but had an awesome smell and it made them better.
For example;
TOBS, great smell, but not slick at all. Razor burn and cuts every time I used it before. Mix it with Mitchell's, and just like that. Best of both worlds.

rating 5.0. What a soap! I have only used this a few times so far, but WOW so far I love it. I'm gifted with tough facial hair and skin that doesn't get along with shaving. Anyone lucky to have facial hair growing in different directions and can only shave with the grain understand this.
Strong smell which isn't unpleasant, it's a clean smell - no issues with lather but I have a water softener.
Each time I've used it I've been impressed with the ease of the shave, and the lack of razor rash. I will revisit this review based on how long the cake lasts, other reviews I've read state Mitchell's Wool Fat Soap lasts for some time so I'm curious to see how long this cake lasts me.
Other products I've used: Col Conk Bay Rum - primary shave soap since I went back to DE safety razor. Doesn't last so long but i
it's cheap and does the trick
L'Ocitane (bought from retail store) - hated it, produced tons of lather but never held up and the shave
was uncomfortable, no lubrication and very drying to the face and
I have oily skin. I would use this stuff to wash my hands, terrible as
a shaving soap for me. Some love it, didn't work for me.

rating 1.0. It also had a really bad lather. Please do not buy this soap This soap gave me skin irritation on my neck and burned for hours. It also had a really bad lather. rating 5.0. Glad I purchased I've read on some shaving blogs that MWF has like a cult following. I don't belong to any cults(haha) but love this soap. rating 5.0. What a product! Mitchell's wool fat shaving soaps have been around for a long time, and it's easy to see why. This review is based on results using a safety razor. Soaking the puck for 10 minutes prior to shaving, then loading the brush upside down and face lathering seems to work the best with this soap. Lathering correctly with MWF soap causes the blade to effortlessly glide across the face, minimizing irritation. This also leaves the face feeling soft and healthy after the shave. I rarely use anything other than a moisturizer after shaving. Wonderful product!

rating 5.0. As a new shaver, this is amazing. Bought this at the recommendation of many online forums for it's newb-friendly lather, and will probably stick with it for a long time to come. Lathers even with hard water, smells like an old barber shop (not to strong, just a nice light fragrance you would expect from shaving creams). This is a Face-Lathering soap, and you may need to soak it in warm, but not hot, water while you soak your brush for the best results. Thoroughly impressed. Lanolin may cause unwanted ingrown hairs for some, so keep an eye on your skin.

rating 5.0. Awesome lather This stuff is AWESOME.  I've tried a few other shaving soaps from various online shaving shops, with mixed success. rating 5.0. Wool Fat I switched to wet shaving with shaving soap at the beginning of the year and have tried three or four other shaving soaps. Others that I have tried have been D Harris, Col Conks, Geo Thumper, and Edwin Jagger. GFT and DH are probably the same soap works the same, CC only lasts me about 3-4 weeks. EJ needs a lot of water to create a lather.

rating 5.0. The best shave soap/creme I have ever used!!! The best shaving soap I have ever used. Hands down. I have been wet shaving with a safety razor for over ten years. I have been searching for the perfect shaving soap or creme for the same amount of time. I have used everything from King of Shaves, Proraso, Cremo Creme, etc, and nothing has been as comfortable as the Mitchell's Wool Fat Soap. Its an absolute joy to use. There are a few tricks to using this soap. If you want to see a good video review online, I recommend googling "Mantic,  Mitchell's Wool Fat Shaving Soap." This gentlemen provides great tips on how to get the most out of this shaving soap. For instance, for the best lather, you should use a "Boar Bristle Brush" which is stiffer than a badger brush. I have a "Super Badger Brush" which doesn't make near the lather that the stiffer, boar bristle brush makes during lathering. One last point, as other reviewers have pointed out, this soap does not have a strong scent, its just very subtle and clean. That was a plus for me. If you are looking for something with a stronger scent you may want to look elsewhere.

rating 5.0. Awesome, non drying, lightly scented, manly soap Awesome, non drying, lightly scented, manly soap. I love this stuff. This has replaced all of my other shaving soaps. I have tried to use other soaps, but once you have taken this soap for a drive there is no going back.

rating 5.0. Smells nice and works up a slick lather while hydrating your face Works really well providing a slick smooth lather for your razor to carve through with minimal resistance.

I find it easiest to face lather. Normally it takes me about a minute or two. I know the water is very soft here and it's also in the tropics so most of the time the ambient temperature is close to body temperature which may help. All I do is soak the brush in warm water, squeeze some water out and twirl it on the soap for 15-20 seconds. I don't add any water to the soap or prepare it in any other way. Lather then starts building as soon as I put the brush on my face just adding a little water for the right consistency.

I bought a cheap $2-3 individual sized lidded souffle bowl from the supermarket. The puck was ever so slightly smaller so I got the cheese grater out and took a little off the bottom. I jammed the shavings down the sides with a spoon and now the puck is one with the bowl with no water running down the sides.

+Smells clean and mild (while in use - smell doesn't linger)
+Leaves your face soft and hydrated
+Reduces irritation
+Good value for money - a little goes a long way.
rating 5.0. Great smell Lovely soap, bought it for my husband, he likes shaving more now and he smells great!

rating 4.0. great soap but pricey I've used wool fat shaving soap for years and what I like about it is how nice it leaves my skin for the rest of the day. You have to work to get a lather going, and a boar brush works better than a badger but I like boar better anyways. So it's stir, add a little water, stir, more water, repeat until you get the lather you want. I sometimes find wool fat shaving soap for a cheaper price than Mitchel's but this is a good value. However, it is a little too large to fit in the bottom of a typical Old Spice shaving mug, so I found a bigger shaving mug. Maybe it's just more for the money, so I'm OK with it. Some people are allergic to wool fat (lanolin), so beware. It's also a very hard soap, so it lasts. For me, it gives a nice shave and leaves my skin baby soft for the day.

rating 5.0. this is not hard to lather you just dont know how yet;) this is a great soap. infact the best one I've ever tried. Pros: feels great, nice light smell, real nice non slip shave, you get to say wool fat allot. i find this pairs well with a Feather blade in my DE razor

Cons: a little expensive but should last you about six months; takes experience to get a good lather, your skin might not react well to lanolin

Ok so for the lather... when it first got this soap my fore arm was getting huge from trying to work a perfect lather. Hopefully it can save you some trial and error. You don’t need to face lather. Soak the puck and your brush in warm to hot water (hard or soft), take shower, and dump out water from soap. One shake of the brush and then work the puck gently. Now transfer the froth into a separate bowl and work it until it peaks. Should take less than two minutes. I do this with a silvertip brush no problem at all. you don’t need to be an expert barber to work a good lather.

Soak the puck, drain the puck, and play with the amount of water and soap you use until you figure it out. If it took you too long to get a perfect almost whipped cream lather, adjust your technique.

rating 5.0. Very nice I have been using Vanerhagen soap with no complaints. I just wanted to try a new soap. I like the scent, it lathers well and the razor glides over my skin.

3-17-17

rating 5.0. Amazing but a little thin This is soap is absolutely fantastic! It is by far the best performing shave soap that I have used thus far. My only "complaints" would be that the pick was a bit thinner than I expected. Other than that, I have nothing but rave reviews for this product.

current_product_reviews:
rating 5.0. THE Standard in premium shaving I've tried at least a dozen shave soaps, maybe two dozen. MWF is my go-to, standard. I leave it in a shave bowl in a drawer. I rinse my brush and then swirl the dripping wet brush on top of the soap. In about 20 seconds, you have phenomenal lather. Wet and repeat for more water if needed. Lather around your face further as needed. I often do this, then take a shower and repeat the lathering when I get out of the shower, and then shave.

I have found a few artisan soaps that lather as good, but none better. Those artisan soaps often come in a variety of delightful fragrances, but I don't care that much about fragrance. MWF doesn't have much of any fragrance. In fact after having wet it and let it dry a few dozen times, there is no discernible scent of any kind. If you like the smell of MWF (or any other shave soap), keep it in a covered container between uses. I'd suggest for those who like fragrance in their shave soap to just put MWF in a covered container and sprinkle in a little of their favorite cologne a few times and keep it covered and you'll have a $40 artisanal shave soap!

I like the ingredients. It's tallow based which I prefer. There's lanolin in it which I like. Although it's not a glycerin based soap, there is a little glycerin in the ingredients - it doesn't seem to be a large amount, so if you really like glycerin, get some vegetable glycerin on Amazon and sprinkle it on top of the soap or add it to your pre-shave. It doesn't have shea butter in it, so I sometimes keep an open puck of shea butter shave soap in a shave dish next to MWF and occasionally reach down with my brush and lather from both.

I've seen some reviews that say that it doesn't lather all that well in "hard" water. We have moderately hard water in my city and I've never had trouble at all.

Finally, it's cheap! I lather before showering; after showering, and usually a second time while shaving, and my brush is full of lather when I finish and my single puck will last for maybe 300 shaves?? so maybe 4 cents a shave??

Also,

rating 5.0. Very nice! I received 3 authentic Salux bath sheets (wash cloth just doesn't sound right). The package says made in Japan and the tag on the cloth says made in japan. I love these thing! They take a little soap a long LONG way and dry very quickly. They can be machine washed but should be hung to dry.

For those who have not heard of these, they are not a terry towel wash cloth. They are very long (in length) exfoliating cloths made from nylon and polyester. They are a lot tougher than the ones you will find at a dollar store/ beauty supply store and last a lot longer. Also they can be very rough when you first use one, in short, it feels like a good back scratching. I don't recommend using it on you face or privets as it can be too rough.

rating 5.0. Salux is the best thing since sliced bread I love these cleansing cloths! I've been using them for about 2 years now and at this point, can't live without one in my shower. I don't feel nearly as clean using a washcloth as I do using the Salux cloth. I used to use nylon poufs but they can't hold a candle to my Salux; they just don't exfoliate as well. I often rinse it and lightly scrub my skin as I rinse off lather and my skin feels even cleaner that way. Another great feature is the long length, I can clean my back easily.

Maybe it's because of the large amount of suds created, but I can smell my shower gels a LOT more using my Salux cloth than a washcloth. I love using scented shower gels and my Salux makes them more noticeable to me.

It's great for the gym too, I roll it in a towel to dry and leave it hanging in my locker. It dries almost 100% that way so no worries about a funky smelling washcloth in the locker or gym bag.

I wash mine weekly in the laundry with towels, using hot water and oxiclean, and they come out great. Mine last about a year before they get a little too ratty. I buy them in threes so I have some in the linen closet at all times.

One thing that they were not great for was using with greasy skin scrubs. I ruined one using it with a scrub for dry skin, the oil never really came out of it, even after washing in hottest water.

rating 5.0. This one is a keeper! Love this scrubber. The best out of many I've tried through the years and I'm using it exclusively for several years now since I came across it. This one is a keeper. Just make sure that you get the original and not a cheap imitation. I've ordered a dozen of them, although each one will easily last you for months with regular / daily use. Gave a couple to my mum and sis. Not too harsh, not too soft, just right. It does a great job exfoliating the surface dead skin cells away; the scrub also helps with (blood) circulation, leaving at the end of your wash the largest body organ (your skin!) feeling really clean and rejuvenated! Makes ample of foam with a small amount of soap on it and maintains it well throughout your wash. A couple of little tips: Wet thoroughly the scrubber before applying soap on it (use soap sparingly on it, a little amount goes a long way with this scrubber as I've happily discovered - you save on soap too!) After you apply soap on the scrubber, shut your shower off during the scrubbing process, otherwise the running water can and will readily and easily wash the foaming soap away from the scrubber. Turn on water when ready to rinse. That saves on water too! A win - win scenario! What else can you possibly ask for?...  :)

rating 5.0. Fantastic Find I read about these cloths on Makeupalley where they are nearly universally praised. After reading the reviews there, I bought one on eBay about a year ago. rating 1.0. Dangerous product, unethical seller When I first saw this, I thought this would be the answer to my hair-drying prayers. Big mistake. The first time I attached this to my Conair dryer, it got so hot that it actually burned my skin. And I mean BURNED it. When my husband tried to remove it, it broke into pieces. A few days later, my hairdresser asked what had "fried" my hair. "If it burned your skin," she said, "imagine what it did to your hair."
Then the fun began with the vendor. I filed a request to return it, and they sent me 2 screens of legalese doubletalk about how they weren't the manufacturer, only the seller, so they weren't responsible, so they wouldn't refund my money. I mean, the word "Unbreakable" is in the name of the product - but when it broke during normal use, mayabeauty didn't see a problem with that! If this isn't a defective product, what is? Be aware that anything you buy from this seller is strictly 'buyer beware' - don't expect them to stand behind it. This product will burn you - and so will the seller.

rating 2.0. You get what you pay for... There are many components to this product making me wonder how they can manufacture it for so cheap.  But, in the end, the product has to work.  The liner actually went on really smoothly.  Directions state to put several coats which I did.  It took a long time to dry (even with a blow dryer to speed up the process), and the liner never lost its glossy look.  The eyelashes stuck and went on easily, but the bond is very light.  I wouldn't trust going out into even the slightest breeze wearing these.  I've seen reviews of the original (name brand) product, and the girls could tug on the lashes or aim a blow dryer at them and they stayed put.  Not so here.  Add to that, the eyelashes are ridiculously big.  They aren't even slightly natural looking.  It was fun to try but not good enough magnetism nor natural looking enough to wear out in the real world.  Not a complete loss as I have a mirror and eyelash applicator that I can continue to use.  Won't use the eyeliner as it's just too shiny.

rating 5.0. Easy to apply This is a cute set! The lashes are a bit big for me but you can easily trim them to the size most suitable to you. The liner goes on smoothly and easily and holds the lashes in place well. I'm always a bit afraid of the glue of other lash sets so this was a perfect compromise. Overall very pleased with these, I don't wear them daily, just for special occasions and these are perfect for that as they're dramatic.

rating 5.0. Great product. Very pleasantly surprised! These had some iffy reviews so I wasn't sure what to expect. The box was slightly bent from shipping but the product inside was undamaged. The eyeliner bottle looks a little cheap but the eyeliner inside is smooth and has a thin brush that I felt was very easy to apply the product with. The liner and lashes worked exactly as described. Someone said that they felt like they had to put the eyeliner on pretty thick to make them stay, but I tried on my hand and one generous sweep was enough for them to stick to. I put two thin coats on my eyes and the lashes attached like magic. Cleanup is easy too. When I washed it off my hand it only took water. That being said, I wouldn't recommend wearing these in the rain! They are very dramatic eyelashes so you may need to trim them but the width was perfect for my eyelids.

Update: they stayed comfortably attached all evening at dinner party and we're easy to remove at the end of the night. The eyeliner basically peels off and rinses off cleanly.

current_product_reviews: rating 4.0. Did not survive hot summer days inside the car So I live in desert climate and one of my challenges (poor me, I know!) is that (a) I want to leave a lip balm in my car at all times, in case I forget to bring one from home, and (b) it gets veeeeery hot inside my car during summer times when the car is parked. Usually, if I leave lip balms in the car, they just melt all over the place or, if they stay inside their casing, their consistency changes in a way that they cannot be applied properly anymore to the lips. So I was excited to discover Duke Cannon lip balm, which promises to resist high temperatures. Alas, this one did not survive my car, either. It did not melt out of its case but the texture still disintegrated and made it impossible to properly apply the lip balm. I suppose it gets hotter than 125 degrees inside my car. On the bright side, after this one failed, I tried another Duke Cannon lip balm and that one survived the heat in the car! Here's the link: https://smile.amazon.com/gp/product/B01JDF9WEM/ref=oh_aui_detailpage_o07_s01?ie=UTF8&psc=1

rating 5.0. Actually lasts 2weeks+ better than regular dye I NEVER write reviews but this one is so worth it since the reviews on this product are varying hopefully this can help someone decide. I have been doing my brows for almost 15 years, so I am very particular about them. I’ve tried almost every brow product, from pencils to pomade and even wonderbrow (which sucks don’t get it). I’ve also dyed my brows with regular hair dye before and stopped because it only lasted a few days and not worth the continuous effort. Well let me tell you this product is still on today and it’s been two weeks! It was quite dark the first few days and then I had to use my Anastasia dip brow pomade to lightly outline them but I only have to use like half the amount of product I normally do. My eyebrow hairs are very light naturally and my hair is dark, I’m so happy to say that they finally match! And it looks so natural, I could go without the pomade if I had to. One tip though, leave it on for 20 mins or more. I love that it’s individually packaged too, this will last me months. I wish I had before pictures so you can see how bad my natural brows are lol but I attached a pic of them right now on week 2 with some pomade and as you can see the actual hairs are still quite dark. (I’m growing them out right now so don’t hate on the strays) anyway sorry for the long review but if you’re like me then I’m sure you read all of them. There are no cons, I love everything about what this did and the longevity of it is amazing. Do yourself a favor and try this out, I would reccomend it to everyone who colors in their brows!

rating 5.0. LOVE this product! First time DIY eyebrow tint and SO easy to use!! rating 5.0. Eyebrow game changer Uh, hello, this product is dope AF. I have suffered my whole life from pretty sparse and invisible colored brows. It's weird, because my hair is pretty dark brown, but my brows look like fishing line and I look like an alien with no makeup on them. I was snooping around the internet doing some research on microblading, which is basically like tattooing on your eyebrows, but it's pretty expensive, and only lasts a few years, and also I don't really trust other people to semi-permanently choose the shape of my brows. I stumbled across this product on Instagram I think and decided to give it a shot.

If you regularly draw on your eyebrows, then applying this will be a breeze. If you have dark hair and wear your brows dark, then you don't really even have to worry too much about how long you keep this product on. I usually do 3 applications at about 7 minutes at a time each time I dye my brows.

I noticed in other reviews that there was some discrepancy on how long this product lasts, and I think I figured out where the difference is coming from. If you have pretty full and nicely shaped brows, but they are light colored and you want them darker, and you are only using this product to dye the ACTUAL HAIRS of your eyebrows, a single application of this will last for weeks. HOWEVER, if you have thin, sparsely furred brows and are using this product to dye the SKIN under and around your actual brow hairs (like drawing or tattooing would do) then this will really only last between 2 and 4 days depending on what you're putting on your face and what kind of activities (like sweating) you are doing.

Either way it is still worth it. It is so nice to wake up in the morning with eyebrows instead of looking like Bob Geldoff in The Wall. I also get about 3 applications from each little "pill" so this product should last a good long while before I have to reorder. Overall very good purchase that I would recommend to anyone who wants darker, fuller brows.

rating 5.0. Salux is the best thing since sliced bread I love these cleansing cloths! I've been using them for about 2 years now and at this point, can't live without one in my shower. They make my shower gels lather perfectly and they scrub my skin w/o being too hard on it. I use less shower gel and get better results. I don't feel nearly as clean using a washcloth as I do using the Salux cloth. I used to use nylon poufs but they can't hold a candle to my Salux; they just don't exfoliate as well. I often rinse it and lightly scrub my skin as I rinse off lather and my skin feels even cleaner that way. Another great feature is the long length, I can clean my back easily.

Maybe it's because of the large amount of suds created, but I can smell my shower gels a LOT more using my Salux cloth than a washcloth. I love using scented shower gels and my Salux makes them more noticeable to me.

It's great for the gym too, I roll it in a towel to dry and leave it hanging in my locker. It dries almost 100% that way so no worries about a funky smelling washcloth in the locker or gym bag.

I wash mine weekly in the laundry with towels, using hot water and oxiclean, and they come out great. Mine last about a year before they get a little too ratty. I buy them in threes so I have some in the linen closet at all times.

One thing that they were not great for was using with greasy skin scrubs. I ruined one using it with a scrub for dry skin, the oil never really came out of it, even after washing in hottest water.
rating 1.0. One Star Too rough to use as face cloth

rating 1.0. Way Too Scratchy For Humans!! Uh, definitely blew this purchase based on reading previous reviews! Maybe the fabric was thinner or softer in earlier versions, but these things are rough! Extremely scratchy on my rough face, so no way it was usable on a woman's softer skin. BTW, these are certainly large pieces of fabric intended to be used to scrub your back solo, but I was raw after just a few scrubs to the point I had to stop altogether. Examination in the mirror confirmed scratches on my back, and I'm not a softy! I decided to cut up one of the cloths and use it on my face so I could control the pressure. Another fail! These things are just too harsh for me. Bottom line, they're going into the rag bin for use on non-delicate surfaces that need scrubbing!! Oh, and not Made in Japan!! Another BS product made in the land of cheap crap. Extremely disappointing!

rating 4.0. Great for Smooth Skin Cons:

I have sensitive skin and this cloth can be too abrasive if used with any pressure on some parts of my skin. So, lathering up and then using the cloth also helps me to only use this abrasive cloth where I need it.

Pros:
Great for those little bumps.

Fantastic at exfoliation for shaving, waxing, any hair removal, etc.

Helps with ingrown hairs! But be careful not to press too hard when using it. Go over multiple times at a lighter pressure rather than pressing too hard, which can have the opposite effect and actually cause ingrown hairs.

Do recommend, just not for the reasons I originally purchased.

rating 5.0. This one is a keeper! Love this scrubber. The best out of many I've tried through the years and I'm using it exclusively for several years now since I came across it. This one is a keeper. Just make sure that you get the original and not a cheap imitation. I've ordered a dozen of them, although each one will easily last you for months with regular / daily use. Gave a couple to my mum and sis. Not too harsh, not too soft, just right. It does a great job exfoliating the surface dead skin cells away; the scrub also helps with (blood) circulation, leaving at the end of your wash the largest body organ (your skin!) feeling really clean and rejuvenated! Makes ample of foam with a small amount of soap on it and maintains it well throughout your wash. A couple of little tips: Wet thoroughly the scrubber before applying soap on it (use soap sparingly on it, a little amount goes a long way with this scrubber as I've happily discovered - you save on soap too!) After you apply soap on the scrubber, shut your shower off during the scrubbing process, otherwise the running water can and will readily and easily wash the foaming soap away from the scrubber. Turn on water when ready to rinse. That saves on water too! A win - win scenario! What else can you possibly ask for?...  :)

rating 3.0. I wanted to love these, but the smells.... I love a relaxing bath in fall and winter, and I purchased these bath salts about six months ago. Some pros are that I like the milky options, they make the bath feel a little more luxurious, and the placebo effect of the milkiness makes me think the salts are actually doing something significant (I dont really think they are, but that's not really the reason I use them anyway). The colors are kind of fun, although the yellow looks a little too similar to dehydrated urine. The huge con for me is the smells are so overtly chemical. They smell like bad laundry detergent. The smell is heavily concentrated when you first pour, as it fills the air of your bathroom with its harsh chemical smell. This subsides a little after a while, but the fake synthetic chemical smell doesn't really relax me. I was looking for something to smell gentle and natural, like the descriptions evoke (I used the camera feature on Google Translate to decipher them). I wouldn't buy these again--I think I'm looking for something a bit more sophisticated in scent.

rating 5.0. Box distortion. 😂 Ok. My star is going to be a bit off because I only used it once and I'm already reviewing it. I've just received it yesterday and used it last night because I couldn't wait! I got the box on time, but was rather disappointed by the bend up box. Even though the product itself is fine. Haha. But I love taking my products with my box, ya know? Girls gotta shoot and review for dem girlfriends! Anyways right to the product, I loveeeeed it butttttt~ There wassss something I didn't like. I didn't like how it didn't lather up well or my hair was too dry and ate everything as soon as it touched the product. Which means I had to squeeze out a lot- and it's a small bottle compare to those typical jar of hair masque. But man~ as soon it was on my hair, my hair felt super duper soft like as I've described to my friends a very light squishy pillow! I rinsed and blow dried it. The usual but wow, I had not much of my usual annoying tangles and boy, was my hair super straight. (Natural hair) the next day, my hair feels ok, not as dry and frizzy. So I can't wait to use more of this product and let it work its magic!

(Oh btw, my hair has been straightened, bleached, highlighted, dyed several times- pretty much a very dry dead frizzy mess and I feel like I have more hope now)

Oh oh! And I loved the design of the product! It's so chic! 😍💕

rating 5.0. My favorite hair product - EVER. I absolutely adore this product. This is the ONLY hair mask / conditioning treatment that actually works on my hair. It makes my split ends feel soft and healthy again, and the rest of my hair is soft and manageable as well, and lasts the rest of the day! Some products feel like the wear off after a while, or doesn't enhance the softness at all. This product makes me want to touch my hair all day. I can easily run my fingers through it or brush my hair without it getting caught at the ends. I know this product is costly, but a little goes a long way.

I received all three products (the shampoo, conditioner, the mask) in a subscription box to try, and while all three products seem to work well together, the mask honestly works so well on its own that I don't even feel like I need the Oribe Gold Lust shampoo or conditioner. I used this product for two months last year until I ran out of my sample sizes. I saved up enough money and just purchased another bottle, and even after ONE use, I can feel such an improvement in my hair already. rating 5.0. Feel It Working I'd used the Alpha Hydrox brand in the past, always with great results, but never the 12% solution. Here's the thing: When you put this lotion on you can actually feel it working. There's a mild tingle/burning sensation. And within minutes you can detect a discernible difference in your skin's appearance. If you're looking for a product that tones your skin, reduces fine lines and wrinkles, and fades dark circles under the eyes, look no further. This stuff is inexpensive, a jar lasts a long time, and it works absolute wonders.

rating 5.0. Highly recommended. Even though this is advertised for fine lines, I use it for acne (because of its high AHA content).  It clears skin up within 6 weeks and evens out tone.  I'm not wasting my money on anything else now that I know this product is out there, and is very affordable.

rating 5.0. Great stuff!! Glycolic Acid is the one ingredient that you want your facial products to have. If you have problem skin such as acne, uneven skin tone, oily skin, dry skin or wrinkles you can count on glycolic acid to fix those problems. I bought this product because glycolic acid was recommended for my skin by my skin care specialist many years ago. I have uneven skin texture, oilyness and acne. I bought some very pricey glycolic acid from her and it did work wonders but the price was way too much for me ($50.00 a jar!). So ever since I have been searching for something similiar and I think I have found it with the "Alpha Hydroxy" line. And who can beat that price?? When I say I searched for similiar products, I am not kidding. I have thrown out and gave away (not to mention stored away) so many products that didn't work it is crazy.

One thing you must understand is that you have got to be faithful with this stuff. Don't skimp and don't skip treatments unless you have a skin reaction. First you got to get your pores open and a simple pot of steaming water with a towel wrapped over your head should do the trick. Buy the facial wash and wash your face gently. Slather this souffle on and go to bed. You should feel some tingle and that is good because that means you got some open pores and this souffle is sinking in. If you don't feel the tingle, don't give up on this product! Keep using this every day and you should see some difference in a week or so.

I can't believe the difference in my skin in only two weeks. And the amazing part is that I could see a difference in my skin texture within the first few days. The climate here is very dry and my skin was suffering from being too dry also. This product fixed that. AMAZING! My acne along the jawline is also clearing up a little at a time. But it is going away and that is a huge relief because this product is working. The fine lines on my forehead are also slowly disappearing.

One more thing about using this product for acne. I was watching a infomercial about a new skin care line that is supposed to be better than ProActive (which I have tried and it didn't work). I wanted to see what the main ingredient was because they did not use benzoyl peroxide. I couldn't believe it when they said glycolic acid was their main ingredient. And here we are getting the same benefits with the "Alpha Hydroxy" line without having to be bothered with orders over the phone, trial memberships and pushing more products that we don't want or need.

Glycolic acid can be pricey so to be getting a jar for this price is a bargain. Don't let that cheap price fool you....this stuff works.

I get seduced (ha ha who doesn't) by cosmetic claims and I try the products but I seem to always come back to this one as my stand by. The other products are more expensive and actually don't work as well for me.  I am going to stick with this one from now on. The price is right and it does what it says.  What more can you ask.

I am skeptical about most products that promise results in which acne or fine lines will improve dramatically with use but this is the only one that has delivered on those promises for me.

I am 36 years old and I have fair, sensitive, oily, extremely acne prone skin and it has been a problem for me for over 20 years. I have tried everything to keep my acne under control. Everything! But nothing has ever worked for long. My biggest problem is pores that clog like crazy and nothing I tried could keep them clear, so I would be broken out constantly or just ready to break out. It doesn't help that I am skin obsessed either ( touching and picking is a no-no, but I couldn't help it) so my skin always looked raw and sore. I also have scarring and and using makeup to help cover the damage only made me break out more, so I felt helpless everyday and depressed.

After going to a dermatologist, he said that I was going about this the wrong way entirely. I had been using benzoyl peroxide products and he said that for older skin this can aggravate the problem even more. Instead, he gave me a glycolic acid/ salicylic acid face wash and told me to try this product in addition to see if it helped at all to keep my pores unclogged and it really does! I used to have many blackheads and clogged pores and now they are drastically reduced. I look at my skin and see very few to none now and for me that is a miracle. I am losing the desire to constantly touch my face as well. This product makes your skin baby soft and helps to erase fine lines too. I also use it on my chest, where I also used to break out constantly, and that has helped enormously as well.

The price, benefits and promises that this product deliver are all reasons to buy this product. But you will keep using it after discovering how amazing it can be for your skin to be mostly clear almost all of the time if you have suffered with acne as I have. I am so so happy with the results I got.

When first using it your skin will tingle and feel burning, but that goes away. I only use it at night and I also mix the product with moisturizer to hydrate my skin at the same time. Also, you might break out a little until you have used the product for a few days as I did, but then I cleared up and have stayed clear. This truly is a miracle in a jar!

rating 5.0. AMAZING I've been using this product off and on for about a year now. I feel like it's a face lift in a bottle. I put it on before bed and wake up feeling like my skin is tight and my pores are small. My skin is so soft when I use it regularly. I love it. I use the Alpha Hydrox lotion on my back and it cleared up any blemishes I would get from time to time. I've never had perfect skin in my life until I tried this line of products. I will warn that if you get this product on sensitive areas, it can burn. I've gotten cold sores before if I accidentally get it on my lips. It can irritate sensitive skin so keep it away from those areas.

rating 5.0. Only a week and my skin is FABULOUS! I've only been using this product for a little over a week and can already see a marked difference in the condition of my skin.  I am in my upper 20's with combination skin, large pores on my nose and on the apples of my cheeks, and am prone to adult acne.  I'm starting to see very slight laugh lines and a few lines between my eyebrows.  After just a week, my skin is smoother, my pores are refined, and my fine lines are diminishing.  ONE WEEK!  My skin has not broken out and purged yet as others have warned.  My acne scars have faded.  My skin is not oily nor dry when using this product and I have not had any sensitivity issues (I usually have sensitive skin).  This has been a miracle product!  I have been using it morning and night after cleansing, and have no problems applying makeup over it as it absorbs well into the skin.  A little bit goes a long way.  If you're looking to try a glycolic acid product for minor resurfacing, this is a great place to start.  It's an excellent product for the price!

I like the consistency, though. It's very light and not greasy so I prefer it over Oil of Olay.
I probably won't buy again if I find a better exfoliating product.

rating 5.0. Like, the maximum extent to which you should use ... This is a must-have bath item. It's essential to exfoliate your skin if you want healthy, smooth skin. I used to have kp (keratosis pilaris) aka those small chicken bumps that appear on your arms. This definitely helped with that. The important thing to remember is to not overuse it. Like, the maximum extent to which you should use this wash cloth is every other day. It'll actually irritate your skin if you use it too much and end up doing more harm than good. One cloth lasts a very long time (I've been using one for 6 months). The first use will probably hurt, but the cloth gets less intense with more use. After around 5 washes, it because the perfect exfoliating cloth! Buy the set of three, keep one, and gift the other two to your friends!! I always recommend this to all my friends.

rating 5.0. Marvelous! These things are great, full stop. I have tried and thrown away many kinds of scrubbing cloths and pads, either because they were ineffective or too irritating, or because they became unsanitary in the damp environment of the shower room. These cloths have just the perfect level of abrasiveness to exfoliate without irritation (they feel stimulating rather than scratchy). The mesh-like fabric rinses completely clean and drains and dries quickly. I bunch up the soapy cloth to scrub arms, legs and feet (they are wonderful for scrubbing feet) and use it at full length to wash my back and neck. I purchased the set of three so I can rotate them and run them through the laundry weekly, but I think that if you wash the cloth out thoroughly after every use, with fresh soap, and hang it where it will dry completely, you might not have to launder it at all. My skin feels like silk after using these for just one week: all the flakiness is gone. I have paid a lot of money for full-body exfoliation at spas, and this cloth did the job just as well. (I received what appears to be the authentic Japanese product. The packages are labeled at the top with the name of a distributor based in Hawaii.)

current_product_reviews: rating 5.0. It's almost a misnomer to call it a wash cloth. Seeing these as a recommended item on another product page, I did some research, quickly became intrigued, and decided to give them a try. After the first shower (and with every successive shower), all I can say is wow. The nylon material itself is strong, flexible, and durable (the one I'm using is fine after 2 months). The design/weave of the material allows me to build up a good lather, even with low-lather body washes. Unlike a loofah or bath puff, the length of the wash cloth gives the ability to reach difficult areas, like the full length of the back. Finally, the level of exfoliation is just enough: my skin (which can be pretty sensitive) definitely felt smooth and exfoliated, but there was no irritation or sensitivity, which can sometimes occur with more harsh exfoliating products. Considering what this wash cloth can do, don't waste your money on scrubs or loofahs.

rating 4.0. Curl activator creates bouncy curls but not gentle on fragile hair I am a white woman with longish hair past my shoulders. On a good day it's like Shirley Temple. On a bad day it's like Pattie LaBelle circa 1985. It looks great when freshly shampooed, but shampoo destroys my hair, as does conditioner, as does the dozens of other products (including homemade) I have tried before finally settling on plain water rinse, as infrequently as possible (one a week in the winter), which leaves my hair a tangled mess and nary a curl in sight. So I tried using Bouncy Curl as a conditioner, knowing that I can't leave any styling product on my hair as it's too fragile. After rinsing my hands, I put a dollop on my palm and lightly spread it through my hair, rinsing it off immediately. Then I let my hair dry naturally.

It does just what it says. Once my hair was dry (using a tee shirt instead of a terry cloth towel to minimize breakage), I had bouncy curls, and scrunching gave them more lift. The curls lasted a couple of days but then I re-activated them with my clothing steamer (since my hair, unlike most curly girls, loves humidity). It's now been a 6 days since I used the product and I still have bouncy curls.

The only reason I gave it four stars instead of five is that I did have some breakage when I used a pick to comb through it when dry. There are some ingredients my hair doesn't like that causes breaking (but that is my fussy hair - it doesn't like anything, not even plain coconut oil). I'm going to try Kinky Curly custard, as I checked their ingredient list and I think my hair should be okay with it.

Bouncy Curls probably works even better as a styling product but I was using it more like a co-wash. It does activate the curl and I imagine it works even better on African or mixed-chicks hair. But it works pretty well on European hair. Try it if you have curly hair that isn't prone to breakage.

Overall it’s cut my wing application in half and looks much better than if I freehand it!

Till finally, I tried this, just recently, I bought a couple of and based on some positive reviews so I decided to give this a try. I am a certified purchaser and not in anyway affiliated with this company. Here is my review, I am absolutely blown away, I haven't finished the first bottle yet and I saw the very positive effect...my arms have dramatic effect, they are so white and smooth..I am so happy with the results. I am very sensitive to products but this didn't irritate my skin. I will stick to this lotion, Advise if you decided to try this (your result may not be the same as mine), try this advice. once you put the lotion on your skin, arms and legs wrap them  with "glad wrap" or clear plastic and let this stay over night, By doing this the skin will get soaked to the lotion cause the lotion is trapped by the plastic wrap...you would see the result instantly...believe me.  I learned this  from my long time dermatologist using plastic wrap whenever you put lotion or cream to maximize the potency of the ingredients.I did this using Shouvy lorion.. Thank you Shouvy, your product is keeping its promise. The best so far. !!! Excellent....my skin is whiter...and I mean whiter!!! .

I have acne and severe stretch marks around my stomach, waist, bottocks, underarms, shoulder, and inner thighs with facial scarring which is very depressing. I have tried using tretinoin mixed with lotions I've used from the past but have not used them for long because of the fact Retin-A is very expensive and can take months before noticing true results so I stopped mixing my prescription; saving it for my face instead. Anyways, I always remind myself not to allow my skin issues to stress me out because I know stressing won't solve anything but trying out effective skin products will ☺️ Right now I am using glycolic acid mixed with a vitamin c cream (I purchased from a cosmetic vendor because I am testing out my own product I am getting ready to release) at bedtime along with Shouvy's Kojic acid and vitamin c lotion during the day. I use Fair & White's Exclusive Whitening Soap. I decided not to purchase the soap because it's tok string for my skin. I hope the combination of products I am using will work together effectively to solve most of my skin issues. I am giving my review 5 stars because as I said, I do not know how this lotion will work gor me. I will definitely drop a star or two if I don't notice any changee in my skin

Note: This lotion smells really nice. It has the scent of a very expensive designer purfume. I will try to reach out to the company to let them know that guys are trying out the lotion too and not just females so hopefully they will tone down the scent.

rating 4.0. Very impressed I was skeptical as to how this lotion would perform. The lotion itself has a pleasant smell and goes on very light. While it's not the most moisturizing in terms of making your skin feel hydrated, I feel like it does what it says it will do. I used it only on my arms due to a "farmer's tan" and initially I saw a huge improvement on a scar then I started to notice how my skin color was fading and evening out. I am not dark but am olive toned with a slight tan. I am very happy with this lotion and would recommend it to anyone looking to even out their skin tone with some added bonuses of fading scars.

rating 5.0. Amazing results, takes at least 6-8 weeks Ordered with Shouvy whitening body lotion, both arrived together. The soap lathers really well, and smells something like artificial grape flavoring. The smell is subtle. I used both products (soap first, then lotion right after that shower) consecutively, and began to see progress in the first 9 days (again, because I was using both products daily). By the end of using it every-other-day for 2 months consecutively, I saw a noticeable difference. I’m back to a much lighter skin tone and my skin almost seems to glow with brightness. While using both Shouvy products, I also used zinc-oxide sunscreen and other sun protection measures to guarantee results. It's a really good product, at least for olive skin tones like mine. I honestly love this product!

rating 5.0. That said it's best used after having exfoliated with a Kojic acid soap It has all the ingredients that are proven to work for skin lightening back to it's original tone.  If it does not' work for you then you must be trying to lighten skin beyond its own natural base color.  No product on the public market is going to make your skin lighter than the color you have naturally.  The product will help speed up the erasing of tan lines and sun exposure provided you keep those areas covered up and away from the sun.  So yes it gradually works.  It's taken a month to soften that tan line on my lower legs from wearing capri slacks in the sun.  That said it's best used after having exfoliated with a Kojic acid soap.

input:
rating 4.0. Fantastic for a blended tight/loose curl look Definitely works. Will curl my hair like no other tool I've tried. Long as in REALLY long wand which is awesome for us long haired women. BUT the advertising that you can have either tighter or looser curls by using selective sections of the wand - not so much. Unless you have some serious time to spend carefully wrapping then moving each little piece back to the larger knobs your hair ends up in the valleys, giving a tighter curl, which I don't always want.

So for a breeze through my hair to get fabulous curls yes it's awesome. Will have to purchase a larger sized wand to get the looser curls looks I sometimes want.

rating 4.0. I will keep using it. Nice results. So far, it seems to work just fine -- I just need to practice. After the 3rd time curling my hair I seem to have gotten the hang of it. It's going to be time consuming - You have to work with very small chunks of hair to get it to curl well. By the time I'd curled 1/2 my head I was really sick of it and my shoulder was hurting. But you can't walk around with 1/2 of your hair curled. I think that's just a matter of getting used to having to hold your arm up for long periods of time.
I really like the glove that came with this! I just bought another curling iron that said it came with a glove and when I opened the box, this freak thing with 3 fingers fell out of the box! No, this comes with a proper glove that stretches to fit your hand. Although I rarely need to use the glove with this because of the design.
4 stars because this doesn't have an auto shut off. It also doesn't have an on/off switch. On means it's plugged in. Off is when it isn't plugged in.

rating 4.0. Love I love how this was so inexpensive but definitely did the job!

rating 5.0. Best curler ever! Best curling iron I ever bought! My friends want to borrow it all the time. I have naturally curl hair. When I straighten it, the first couple days it looks great, then it starts to wave. So instead of fighting it, I use this to add more texture and great curls throughout. I don't ususally curl every strand of hair, just a few underneath and then around the top. It adds a great textured look and makes my hair look styled verses day three waves. You should try it!! It's easy to just wrap hair and go. The curls obviously depend on how much hair you grab. Underneath I do large chunks just to add wave and so it doesn't make my hair puffy. Top, about 1 inch sections random to get a tighter curl. Stay beautiful!!

current_product_reviews: rating 5.0. Love this wand! I'm not going to lie and say using this particular wand was not time consuming...it definitely was, but the results are worth it.  I have very long hair, so wrapping was a bit of a struggle because the wand is only so long.  I was able to wrap around both the balls and the indents, giving a unique curl effect.  Works best to physically unwrap the strand of hair you're curling rather than pulling downward as with a traditional clipless wand.  Any reviews stating this wand does not give curl either has too short of hair, isn't using a high enough heat setting, or is completely doing it wrong.  You really need to use your right hand over your head to curl the left and vice versa for the right.  The black cool tip should be pointing downward as you curl.  There are some informative videos on YouTube for how to curl with this wand.  Anyhow, I'm pleased with this wand and look forward to many great looks from it.

rating 5.0. Your hair with withstand gale force winds! :) I've tried many products for "extreme" hairstyles like liberty spikes and nothing comes close to the performance of Got2b Glued Blasting Freeze Spray. I've now used it exclusively for over 3 years and purchase it in multi-packs for team events.

I've spiked hair as long as 18-24 inches and, if you can find a creative way to sleep with your hair spiked so high, your style will last a few days, even if you're dancing in the wind.

Tips for use: Brush or comb hair. Depending on the desired style, it's often helpful to part hair into segments for spikes and using coated hair ties to keep the segments apart. Apply spray to clean, dry hair. Spray Got2b Glued liberally (your hair should feel wet) first to the roots in small segments and dry with a hair dryer on a cool setting to establish the "base" of your style. Use your hands to shape it as your dry. (For some of the longer hair, it can become a four-hand task, so get some assistance from a friend.) Once your shape is set, spray to the tips of your hair in small segments and dry each segment before doing the next. When you've spiked all segments, give your entire style one more heavy coating and dry with the dryer. For liberty spikes or other styles where you want the tips to stay tidy, apply a small amount of Got2b Ultra Glued Invisible Styling Gel (smile.amazon.com/Got2b-Ultra-Invincible-Styling-6-Ounce/dp/B004FQK7DA/) to the tips only and dry once more with the dryer.

Clean-up is also easy. Use a gentle shampoo and warm water.
